papers.title: Discovery of Nonlinear Dynamics with Automated Basis Function Generation
papers.summary: Discovering governing equations from observational data remains a fundamental challenge in scientific modeling, particularly when the underlying mathematical structure is unknown. Traditional sparse identification methods like SINDy excel at discovering parsimonious models but require researchers to specify candidate basis functions a priori, a limitation that often leads to model failure when critical terms are omitted or when systems exhibit unconventional dynamics. Purely symbolic regression approaches offer unlimited flexibility but struggle with noise sensitivity and frequently produce overly complex, unstable equations. We present AutoSINDy, a hybrid Discovery-then-Solve framework that combines the exploratory power of symbolic regression with the robust sparsity-promoting capabilities of SINDy. Our method operates in three stages: (1) PySR-based symbolic regression discovers candidate functional forms from bootstrapped data chunks; (2) a curation pipeline decomposes, expands, and filters these expressions using collinearity analysis to construct a minimal yet comprehensive library; and (3) SINDy identifies sparse governing equations from this custom-tailored library. Extensive experiments across canonical nonlinear systems demonstrate that AutoSINDy consistently recovers ground-truth equations even under high observational noise, achieving a ground-truth recovery rate of 92.8% across all trials. Compared with standard SINDy using enriched libraries and standalone symbolic regression, AutoSINDy achieves higher predictive accuracy, superior generalization to unseen trajectories, and substantially lower symbolic complexity.

ai.tldr: 提出AutoSINDy混合框架，自动生成基函数并通过稀疏识别发现非线性动力学方程。
ai.motivation: 传统稀疏识别方法需要预定义基函数，遗漏关键项会导致模型失败；纯符号回归对噪声敏感且易产生复杂不稳定方程。
ai.method: 三阶段流程：PySR符号回归从数据块发现候选形式；共线性分析筛选构建精简库；SINDy稀疏识别得到控制方程。
ai.result: 在典型非线性系统上，高噪声下真实方程恢复率达92.8%，预测精度和泛化能力优于标准SINDy和纯符号回归。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户自动发现数据背后的非线性动力学方程，无需手动指定基函数。

idea_spark.idea: 将符号回归的探索性与稀疏回归的鲁棒性结合，实现端到端的方程发现。
idea_spark.risk: 符号回归阶段可能产生冗余或过拟合表达式，需精心设计筛选流程。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受SINDy对简洁模型的偏好和符号回归灵活性的启发，取两者之长。

en.tldr: Proposes AutoSINDy, a hybrid framework that automatically generates basis functions and discovers nonlinear dynamics equations via sparse identification.
en.motivation: Traditional sparse identification requires predefined basis functions, risking model failure when key terms are omitted; pure symbolic regression is noise-sensitive and often yields overly complex, unstable equations.
en.method: Three-stage pipeline: PySR symbolic regression discovers candidate forms from bootstrapped data chunks; collinearity analysis curates a minimal library; SINDy identifies sparse governing equations.
en.result: On canonical nonlinear systems, achieves 92.8% ground-truth recovery rate under high noise, with better predictive accuracy and generalization than standard SINDy and standalone symbolic regression.
en.help_to_user: Helps users automatically discover nonlinear dynamics equations from data without manually specifying basis functions.

idea_spark.idea: Combine the exploratory power of symbolic regression with the robustness of sparse regression for end-to-end equation discovery.
idea_spark.risk: Symbolic regression may produce redundant or overfitted expressions, requiring careful curation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by SINDy's preference for parsimony and symbolic regression's flexibility, leveraging the strengths of both.

papers.title: The finite expression method for turbulent dynamics with high-order moment recovery
papers.summary: Turbulent dynamical systems are characterized by nonlinear interactions and stochastic effects that generate coupled statistical quantities, such as non-zero higher-order moments, which are difficult to capture from data with accuracy. We propose a two-stage data-driven modeling framework that combines symbolic regression with generative models to jointly identify the governing dynamics and predict their key statistical quantities. In Stage I of the framework, the Finite Expression Method (FEX) is adopted to discover closed-form expressions of the deterministic dynamics, recovering nonlinear interaction terms and external forcing without predefined libraries. In Stage II, generative models are introduced to learn the residual stochastic components as a refined correction to the model error from the Stage I approximation, enabling accurate characterization of higher-order statistics. Theoretical analysis establishes the consistency of the symbolic estimator and quantifies the estimation error in terms of data size and numerical discretization. The model performance is verified through detailed numerical experiments on the stochastic triad models across multiple regimes, demonstrating that the framework successfully recovers interaction terms and forcing expressions, and accurately predicts statistical moments up to order five. These results highlight the potential of integrating interpretable symbolic discovery with data-driven stochastic modeling for complex turbulent systems.

ai.tldr: 提出两阶段数据驱动框架，结合符号回归与生成模型，发现湍流动力学闭式表达式并准确预测高阶统计矩。
ai.motivation: 湍流动力系统的高阶统计矩难以从数据中准确捕获。
ai.method: 第一阶段用有限表达式方法发现确定性动力学闭式表达式；第二阶段用生成模型学习残差随机分量以修正模型误差。
ai.result: 在随机三元组模型上成功恢复相互作用项和强迫表达式，并准确预测五阶以内的统计矩。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种可解释的符号发现与数据驱动随机建模结合的湍流系统建模方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将符号回归与生成模型结合，分别处理确定性和随机成分，提升高阶矩预测精度。
idea_spark.risk: 生成模型可能过拟合或难以泛化到未见过的湍流状态。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受有限表达式方法在符号回归中的成功启发，将其与生成模型互补用于随机动力学建模。

en.tldr: Proposes a two-stage data-driven framework combining symbolic regression and generative models to discover closed-form expressions of turbulent dynamics and accurately predict high-order statistical moments.
en.motivation: High-order statistical moments in turbulent dynamical systems are difficult to capture accurately from data.
en.method: Stage I uses the Finite Expression Method to discover closed-form deterministic dynamics; Stage II uses generative models to learn residual stochastic components as a correction.
en.result: Successfully recovers interaction terms and forcing expressions on stochastic triad models, and accurately predicts statistical moments up to order five.
en.help_to_user: Provides an interpretable symbolic discovery approach integrated with data-driven stochastic modeling for complex turbulent systems.

idea_spark.idea: Combine symbolic regression and generative models to handle deterministic and stochastic components separately, improving high-order moment accuracy.
idea_spark.risk: Generative models may overfit or fail to generalize to unseen turbulent regimes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the success of the Finite Expression Method in symbolic regression, complemented by generative models for stochastic dynamics.

papers.title: HS-FNO: History-Space Fourier Neural Operator for Non-Markovian Partial Differential Equations
papers.summary: Neural operators provide fast surrogate models for time-dependent partial differential equations, but their standard autoregressive use usually assumes that the instantaneous field $u(t,\cdot)$ is a complete state. This assumption fails for delay equations, distributed-memory systems, and other non-Markovian dynamics: two trajectories may agree at time $t$ and nevertheless have different futures because their histories differ. We introduce the History-Space Fourier Neural Operator (HS-FNO), a neural operator for delay and memory-driven PDEs formulated on the lifted state $u_t(θ,x)=u(t+θ,x)$, $θ\in[-τ,0]$. The key computational step is to decompose one history-state update into a learned predictor for the newly exposed future slice and an exact shift-append transport for the portion of the history window already known from the previous state. This avoids learning deterministic history coordinates, reduces the learned output dimension, and enforces the natural discrete history update. We test HS-FNO on five benchmark families covering delayed reaction--diffusion, spatial epidemiology, nonlocal neural-field dynamics, delayed waves, and distributed-memory closures. Across ten random seeds, HS-FNO attains the lowest aggregate one-step, history-space, and rollout errors among the principal baselines. The largest gain occurs in autoregressive prediction, where aggregate rollout error decreases from $0.241$, $0.188$, and $0.185$ for current-state, lag-stack, and unconstrained history-to-history operators, respectively, to $0.094$. The same model uses fewer parameters than unconstrained history prediction. These results indicate that enforcing the discrete shift structure of history-state evolution is an effective inductive bias for non-Markovian PDE surrogate modeling.

ai.tldr: 提出历史空间傅里叶神经算子（HS-FNO），通过显式编码历史状态更新的移位-追加结构，有效处理非马尔可夫偏微分方程，在多个基准上取得更低的预测误差。
ai.motivation: 标准神经算子假设瞬时场为完整状态，无法处理延迟方程、分布式记忆系统等非马尔可夫动力学，导致预测失效。
ai.method: 将历史状态提升为函数 u_t(θ,x)=u(t+θ,x)，利用学习预测器处理新暴露的未来切片，并通过精确移位-追加传输已知历史窗口，减少学习维度并强制离散历史更新。
ai.result: 在五个基准族（延迟反应扩散、空间流行病学、非局部神经场动力学、延迟波、分布式记忆闭包）上，HS-FNO 在一步、历史空间和 rollout 误差上均优于基线，rollout 误差从 0.241 降至 0.094。
ai.help_to_user: 为需要处理非马尔可夫动力学的 PDE 代理建模提供了一种高效且参数更少的解决方案。

idea_spark.idea: 将历史窗口的确定性传输与未来预测分离，可减少学习负担并提升长期预测稳定性。
idea_spark.risk: 对历史窗口长度 τ 敏感，过短可能丢失记忆，过长增加计算成本。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受离散时间动力系统中状态更新自然结构的启发，将物理先验（移位-追加）融入神经网络设计。

zh.motivation: 标准神经算子假设瞬时场为完整状态，无法处理延迟方程、分布式记忆系统等非马尔可夫动力学，导致预测失效。

zh.result: 在五个基准族（延迟反应扩散、空间流行病学、非局部神经场动力学、延迟波、分布式记忆闭包）上，HS-FNO 在一步、历史空间和 rollout 误差上均优于基线，rollout 误差从 0.241 降至 0.094。
zh.help_to_user: 为需要处理非马尔可夫动力学的 PDE 代理建模提供了一种高效且参数更少的解决方案。

idea_spark.risk: 对历史窗口长度 τ 敏感，过短可能丢失记忆，过长增加计算成本。

en.tldr: Proposes the History-Space Fourier Neural Operator (HS-FNO) that explicitly encodes the shift-append structure of history-state updates to effectively handle non-Markovian PDEs, achieving lower prediction errors across multiple benchmarks.
en.motivation: Standard neural operators assume the instantaneous field is a complete state, failing for delay equations, distributed-memory systems, and other non-Markovian dynamics, leading to inaccurate predictions.
en.method: Lifts the state to a history function u_t(θ,x)=u(t+θ,x), uses a learned predictor for the newly exposed future slice, and applies an exact shift-append transport for the known history window, reducing learned dimensions and enforcing discrete history updates.
en.result: On five benchmark families (delayed reaction-diffusion, spatial epidemiology, nonlocal neural-field dynamics, delayed waves, distributed-memory closures), HS-FNO achieves the lowest one-step, history-space, and rollout errors among baselines, reducing rollout error from 0.241 to 0.094.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient and parameter-sparse solution for surrogate modeling of PDEs with non-Markovian dynamics.

idea_spark.idea: Separating deterministic transport of the history window from future prediction reduces learning burden and improves long-term prediction stability.
idea_spark.risk: Sensitive to the history window length τ; too short may lose memory, too long increases computational cost.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the natural structure of state updates in discrete-time dynamical systems, embedding physical priors (shift-append) into neural network design.

papers.title: Bridging Spectral Operator Learning and U-Net Hierarchies: SpectraNet for Stable Autoregressive PDE Surrogates
papers.summary: Neural operators for time-dependent PDEs face a structural tension: spectral architectures (FNO and descendants) inherit exponential rollout-error growth from their one-step Lipschitz constant, while hierarchical U-Net operators trade resolution invariance for multi-scale detail. We introduce SpectraNet, an autoregressive neural operator that composes truncated spectral convolutions inside a U-Net hierarchy with a Residual-Target Spectral Block trained under a Semigroup-Consistency Loss. The residual-target parametrization replaces L^T stability blow-up with linear T*delta drift, and the spectral path's parameter count is Theta(L w^2 M^2), independent of grid N. Under a single unified protocol against 16 published neural-operator baselines on Navier-Stokes nu=1e-5 at 64x64, SpectraNet reaches test relative L2 = 0.0822 at 2.04M parameters -- 2.33x fewer than canonical FNO at ~20% lower error -- and wins five of six rows in a cross-PDE comparison against FNO (NS at nu in {1e-4, 1e-3}, PDEBench Shallow-Water 2D and Diffusion-Reaction, with the Active-Matter row going to FNO inside its seed spread). Trained from scratch at native 128^2 under the same protocol, SpectraNet improves to 0.0724 while FNO regresses to 0.3080. Free rollout stays bounded for T=100 where FNO diverges across all 200 test trajectories. On consumer CPU at B=1, SpectraNet runs sub-200ms while the full-attention Transformer that wins raw L2 pays ~60x latency; we do not claim to beat that Transformer on raw L2, only to dominate the lightweight (<=5M parameter, sub-200ms CPU) Pareto frontier. Source code: https://github.com/Enrikkk/spectranet

ai.tldr: 提出SpectraNet，将谱算子学习与U-Net层次结构结合，实现稳定的自回归PDE代理模型。
ai.motivation: 现有谱算子（如FNO）在时间依赖PDE中因单步Lipschitz常数导致指数级误差增长，而U-Net算子虽能捕捉多尺度细节但缺乏分辨率不变性。
ai.method: 在U-Net层次内嵌入截断谱卷积，并采用残差目标谱块和半群一致性损失训练。
ai.result: 在Navier-Stokes 64×64网格上，SpectraNet以2.04M参数达到测试相对L2=0.0822，比FNO参数少2.33倍、误差低约20%；在128²网格上训练，误差进一步降至0.0724，而FNO退化为0.3080；自由滚动在T=100时保持有界，FNO则发散。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种轻量级（≤5M参数、CPU<200ms）且稳定的自回归PDE代理方案，适用于资源受限场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将谱算子的全局特性与U-Net的多尺度层次结构融合，以抑制误差累积。
idea_spark.risk: 在Active-Matter等复杂PDE上性能可能不如全注意力Transformer，且谱截断可能限制高频细节。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从FNO的误差增长问题出发，借鉴U-Net的层次化设计来稳定自回归过程。

zh.motivation: 现有谱算子（如FNO）在时间依赖PDE中因单步Lipschitz常数导致指数级误差增长，而U-Net算子虽能捕捉多尺度细节但缺乏分辨率不变性。

idea_spark.idea: 将谱算子的全局特性与U-Net的多尺度层次结构融合，以抑制误差累积。
idea_spark.risk: 在Active-Matter等复杂PDE上性能可能不如全注意力Transformer，且谱截断可能限制高频细节。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从FNO的误差增长问题出发，借鉴U-Net的层次化设计来稳定自回归过程。

en.tldr: Proposes SpectraNet, combining spectral operator learning with U-Net hierarchies for stable autoregressive PDE surrogates.
en.motivation: Spectral operators (e.g., FNO) suffer exponential rollout-error growth due to one-step Lipschitz constant, while U-Net operators trade resolution invariance for multi-scale detail.
en.method: Embeds truncated spectral convolutions inside a U-Net hierarchy with a Residual-Target Spectral Block trained under a Semigroup-Consistency Loss.
en.result: On Navier-Stokes 64×64, SpectraNet achieves test relative L2=0.0822 with 2.04M parameters, 2.33× fewer than FNO at ~20% lower error; at 128², improves to 0.0724 while FNO regresses to 0.3080; free rollout stays bounded for T=100 where FNO diverges.
en.help_to_user: Provides a lightweight (≤5M parameters, CPU<200ms) and stable autoregressive PDE surrogate suitable for resource-constrained settings.

idea_spark.idea: Fuse global spectral properties with U-Net's multi-scale hierarchy to suppress error accumulation.
idea_spark.risk: May underperform full-attention Transformers on complex PDEs like Active-Matter, and spectral truncation may limit high-frequency details.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by FNO's error growth issue, leveraging U-Net's hierarchical design to stabilize autoregression.

papers.title: EnergyLens: Interpretable Closed-Form Energy Models for Multimodal LLM Inference Serving
papers.summary: As large language models span dense, mixture-of-experts, and state-space architectures and are deployed on heterogeneous accelerators under increasingly diverse multimodal workloads, optimising inference energy has become as critical as optimizing latency and throughput. Existing approaches either treat latency as an energy proxy or rely on data-hungry black-box surrogates. Both fail under varying parallelism strategies: latency and energy optima diverge in over 20% of configurations we tested, and black-box surrogates require hundreds of profiling samples to generalize across model families and hardware. We present EnergyLens, which uses symbolic regression as a structure-discovery tool over profiling data to derive a single twelve-parameter closed-form energy model expressed in terms of system properties such as degree of parallelism, batch size, and sequence length. Unlike black-box surrogates, EnergyLens decouples tensor and pipeline parallelism contributions and separates prefill from decode energy, making its predictions physically interpretable and actionable. Fitted from as few as 50 profiling measurements, EnergyLens achieves 88.2% Top-1 configuration selection accuracy across many evaluation scenarios compared to 60.9% for the closest prior analytical baseline, matches the predictive accuracy of ensemble ML methods with 10x fewer profiling samples, and extrapolates reliably to unseen batch sizes and hardware platforms without structural modification, making it a practical, interpretable tool for energy-optimal LLM deployment.

ai.tldr: 提出EnergyLens，通过符号回归从少量性能数据中推导出可解释的闭式能量模型，用于多模态大语言模型推理服务的能量优化。
ai.motivation: 现有方法将延迟作为能量代理或依赖数据密集的黑盒代理，在多样化并行策略下延迟与能量最优解不一致（超过20%配置），且黑盒代理需要大量采样才能泛化。
ai.method: 使用符号回归作为结构发现工具，从性能数据中推导出包含12个参数的闭式能量模型，解耦张量并行与流水线并行贡献，分离预填充与解码能量。
ai.result: 仅需50个性能测量样本，EnergyLens在配置选择Top-1准确率达88.2%（基线60.9%），预测精度与集成机器学习方法相当但采样量减少10倍，并能可靠外推到未见过的批量大小和硬件平台。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解并优化大语言模型推理服务的能量消耗，无需大量数据或黑盒模型，提供可解释的物理意义和行动指导。

idea_spark.idea: 将符号回归与领域知识结合，自动发现简洁且可解释的物理模型，替代黑盒代理。
idea_spark.risk: 符号回归可能陷入局部最优，且模型形式需人工验证物理合理性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用符号回归从少量数据中提取可解释模型，启发其他领域（如网络、存储）的能量或性能建模。

zh.tldr: 提出EnergyLens，通过符号回归从少量性能数据中推导出可解释的闭式能量模型，用于多模态大语言模型推理服务的能量优化。
zh.motivation: 现有方法将延迟作为能量代理或依赖数据密集的黑盒代理，在多样化并行策略下延迟与能量最优解不一致（超过20%配置），且黑盒代理需要大量采样才能泛化。
zh.method: 使用符号回归作为结构发现工具，从性能数据中推导出包含12个参数的闭式能量模型，解耦张量并行与流水线并行贡献，分离预填充与解码能量。
zh.result: 仅需50个性能测量样本，EnergyLens在配置选择Top-1准确率达88.2%（基线60.9%），预测精度与集成机器学习方法相当但采样量减少10倍，并能可靠外推到未见过的批量大小和硬件平台。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解并优化大语言模型推理服务的能量消耗，无需大量数据或黑盒模型，提供可解释的物理意义和行动指导。

idea_spark.idea: 将符号回归与领域知识结合，自动发现简洁且可解释的物理模型，替代黑盒代理。
idea_spark.risk: 符号回归可能陷入局部最优，且模型形式需人工验证物理合理性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用符号回归从少量数据中提取可解释模型，启发其他领域（如网络、存储）的能量或性能建模。

en.tldr: Proposes EnergyLens, which uses symbolic regression to derive an interpretable closed-form energy model from few profiling samples for multimodal LLM inference serving.
en.motivation: Existing methods treat latency as an energy proxy or rely on data-hungry black-box surrogates; latency and energy optima diverge in over 20% of configurations, and black-box surrogates require hundreds of samples to generalize.
en.method: Uses symbolic regression as a structure-discovery tool over profiling data to derive a single twelve-parameter closed-form energy model that decouples tensor and pipeline parallelism contributions and separates prefill from decode energy.
en.result: Fitted from as few as 50 profiling measurements, EnergyLens achieves 88.2% Top-1 configuration selection accuracy (vs. 60.9% for baseline), matches ensemble ML methods with 10x fewer samples, and extrapolates reliably to unseen batch sizes and hardware.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand and optimize energy consumption of LLM inference serving with an interpretable, sample-efficient model.

idea_spark.idea: Combine symbolic regression with domain knowledge to automatically discover concise, interpretable physical models, replacing black-box surrogates.
idea_spark.risk: Symbolic regression may get stuck in local optima, and the model form requires manual validation of physical plausibility.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using symbolic regression to extract interpretable models from few data points inspires similar modeling in other domains like networking or storage.

papers.title: Kernel Learning of PDE Solution Operators
papers.summary: A kernel-based approach for the learning of the solution operator of general nonhomogeneous partial differential equations (PDEs) is proposed. The method incorporates physical priors, typically encoded through the PDE operator, into a kernel ridge regression framework, and employs a regularization-based formulation to construct an operator learner. This yields a closed-form estimator that is independent of the input functions that determine the underlying PDE. From the perspective of regularization theory, the resulting estimator induces a well-defined operator that links input and output spaces, which contain the functions that define a Dirichlet problem and its solution, respectively. Consequently, it effectively shifts from a PDE solver to an operator-based solver. In contrast to standard supervised learning methods, it does not rely on paired input--output training data and enables systematic extrapolation beyond observed regimes.   A full error analysis is conducted, providing convergence rates for the operator-based solver under suitable choices of regularization parameters. Extensive numerical experiments, including Darcy flow and Helmholtz equations, demonstrate that the proposed method achieves high accuracy and efficiency across a range of problem settings, and compares favorably with operator learning approaches in both approximation quality and computational cost.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于核的方法学习非齐次偏微分方程的解算子，通过物理先验和正则化框架得到闭式估计器，无需配对训练数据，并进行了完整的误差分析和数值验证。
ai.motivation: 传统监督学习方法需要大量配对输入-输出数据，且难以外推；而基于物理的算子学习能利用PDE结构，提高泛化能力。
ai.method: 将PDE算子编码为物理先验，融入核岭回归框架，通过正则化构建算子学习器，得到与输入函数无关的闭式估计器。
ai.result: 在Darcy流和Helmholtz方程等数值实验中，该方法在精度和计算成本上均优于现有算子学习方法，并提供了收敛率理论保证。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何用核方法高效学习PDE解算子，并指导在类似问题中应用物理先验和正则化策略。

idea_spark.idea: 将物理先验通过核函数嵌入，实现无需配对数据的算子学习，可能启发新的无监督或半监督PDE求解范式。
idea_spark.risk: 核方法对高维问题可能面临计算复杂度挑战，且物理先验的编码方式需针对不同PDE调整。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合物理信息与核方法的思路，可推广至更一般的科学计算任务，如逆问题、参数估计等。

en.tldr: A kernel-based method for learning solution operators of nonhomogeneous PDEs is proposed, incorporating physical priors via kernel ridge regression to yield a closed-form estimator without paired training data, with full error analysis and numerical validation.
en.motivation: Standard supervised methods require paired input-output data and struggle with extrapolation; leveraging PDE structure through physical priors can improve generalization.
en.method: Physical priors encoded via the PDE operator are integrated into a kernel ridge regression framework, using a regularization-based formulation to construct an operator learner that yields a closed-form estimator independent of input functions.
en.result: Numerical experiments on Darcy flow and Helmholtz equations show high accuracy and efficiency, outperforming existing operator learning methods in both approximation quality and computational cost, with theoretical convergence rates.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to efficiently learn PDE solution operators using kernel methods, and guides application of physical priors and regularization strategies in similar problems.

idea_spark.idea: Embedding physical priors via kernel functions enables operator learning without paired data, potentially inspiring new unsupervised or semi-supervised PDE solving paradigms.
idea_spark.risk: Kernel methods may face computational challenges in high dimensions, and encoding physical priors requires adaptation for different PDEs.
idea_spark.inspiration: The combination of physical information and kernel methods can be extended to broader scientific computing tasks, such as inverse problems and parameter estimation.

papers.title: Generalization Error Bounds for Picard-Type Operator Learning in Nonlinear Parabolic PDEs
papers.summary: Operator learning for partial differential equations (PDEs) aims to learn solution operators on infinite-dimensional function spaces from finite-resolution data. In this setting, it is important for the learned model to be discretization-invariant, or resolution-robust, and to reflect PDE-specific structure. It is therefore natural to ask how such structure should be encoded in the model architecture, hypothesis class, or learning procedure. In this paper, we study operator learning for solution operators of nonlinear parabolic PDEs based on Duhamel--Picard iteration. We formulate Picard iteration as an abstract state-transition model and present a theoretical framework for Picard-type operator learning. We derive implementation-agnostic generalization error bounds that separate the implementation error from the estimation error associated with the abstract state-transition model induced by Picard iteration. A key consequence is that increasing the Picard depth reduces the Picard truncation error without causing an unbounded growth of the entropy-based estimation error. We also extend the analysis to long-time prediction by rolling out the same learned local model over successive time blocks. Finally, we illustrate the theory for nonlinear heat equations on the torus using a Picard-type Fourier neural operator as a concrete implementation.

ai.tldr: 本文针对非线性抛物型偏微分方程的解算子学习，基于Duhamel-Picard迭代提出了Picard型算子学习框架，推导了与实现无关的泛化误差界，并证明了增加Picard深度可降低截断误差而不会导致熵估计误差无界增长。
ai.motivation: 现有算子学习方法缺乏对PDE特定结构的显式编码，且难以保证离散化不变性；需要理论框架来理解如何将PDE结构融入模型架构和假设类。
ai.method: 将Picard迭代形式化为抽象状态转移模型，分离实现误差与估计误差，推导泛化误差界；并扩展至长时间预测的滚动策略。
ai.result: 增加Picard深度可降低截断误差，且熵估计误差不会无界增长；以非线性热方程为例，用Picard型傅里叶神经算子验证理论。
ai.help_to_user: 为设计具有理论保证的PDE算子学习模型提供了泛化误差分析工具和架构设计指导。

idea_spark.idea: 将Picard迭代与注意力机制结合，使模型能自适应调整迭代深度以平衡截断误差与估计误差。
idea_spark.risk: 理论假设可能对实际复杂PDE（如强非线性或混沌系统）不成立，导致误差界过于乐观。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受Duhamel原理启发，将时间积分转化为迭代学习，类似思想可用于其他时间依赖的物理系统建模。

en.tldr: This paper studies operator learning for solution operators of nonlinear parabolic PDEs based on Duhamel-Picard iteration, formulates Picard iteration as an abstract state-transition model, derives implementation-agnostic generalization error bounds, and shows that increasing Picard depth reduces truncation error without unbounded growth of entropy-based estimation error.
en.motivation: Existing operator learning methods lack explicit encoding of PDE-specific structure and discretization invariance; a theoretical framework is needed to understand how to incorporate PDE structure into model architecture and hypothesis class.
en.method: Formalizes Picard iteration as an abstract state-transition model, separates implementation error from estimation error, derives generalization error bounds, and extends to long-time prediction via rollout.
en.result: Increasing Picard depth reduces truncation error without unbounded growth of entropy-based estimation error; theory is illustrated for nonlinear heat equations using a Picard-type Fourier neural operator.
en.help_to_user: Provides generalization error analysis tools and architectural guidance for designing theoretically grounded PDE operator learning models.

idea_spark.idea: Combine Picard iteration with attention mechanisms to adaptively adjust iteration depth for balancing truncation and estimation errors.
idea_spark.risk: Theoretical assumptions may fail for complex PDEs (e.g., strongly nonlinear or chaotic systems), leading to overly optimistic error bounds.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by Duhamel's principle, converting time integration into iterative learning; similar ideas can be applied to modeling other time-dependent physical systems.

papers.title: FLUX: Geometry-Aware Longitudinal Flow Matching with Mixture of Experts
papers.summary: Many biological systems evolve through continuous local dynamics while switching between latent regimes defined by learning, stimulus context, internal state, or developmental stage. These processes are often observed only as unpaired longitudinal snapshots: the same cells, neurons, or animals are not tracked as matched trajectories, even though population states are sampled across successive stages. This creates two coupled challenges. First, trajectories must respect curved low-dimensional manifolds embedded in high-dimensional biological measurements. Second, the model must identify when the transport mechanism itself changes. We introduce FLUX (FLow matching for Unpaired longitudinal data with miXture-of-experts), a geometry-aware longitudinal flow-matching framework for joint transport modeling and unsupervised regime discovery. FLUX learns a data-dependent metric from pooled labeled and unlabeled observations, uses that metric to construct geometry-aware conditional paths between adjacent marginals, and decomposes the resulting velocity field into sparse expert vector fields selected by a Straight-Through Gumbel-Softmax router. Across manifold controls, a regime-switching Lorenz system, widefield cortical calcium imaging during associative learning, and embryoid body single-cell differentiation, FLUX reconstructs longitudinal transport while recovering interpretable regime structure. Ablations show that mixture-of-experts routing alone is insufficient: FLUX without geometric learning can fit local transport but fails or weakens regime discovery when regimes are encoded in local dynamics. These results suggest that geometry-aware velocity decomposition provides a general strategy for discovering latent biological state transitions from unpaired longitudinal snapshots.

ai.tldr: 提出FLUX框架，结合几何感知流匹配与专家混合模型，从非配对纵向快照中重建生物系统连续动态并发现潜在状态转换。
ai.motivation: 生物系统在连续动态中切换潜在状态，但观测数据仅为非配对纵向快照，需同时处理高维弯曲流形和传输机制变化。
ai.method: 学习数据依赖度量构建几何感知条件路径，用直通Gumbel-Softmax路由器将速度场分解为稀疏专家向量场。
ai.result: 在流形控制、洛伦兹系统、皮层钙成像和类胚体分化数据上，成功重建纵向传输并恢复可解释状态结构。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需配对轨迹即可发现生物状态转换的通用方法，适用于单细胞、神经科学等纵向快照数据。

idea_spark.idea: 将专家混合路由与数据驱动度量学习结合，自动识别动态变化的关键时间点。
idea_spark.risk: 对高维稀疏数据可能过拟合，且路由选择的可解释性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受生物系统多阶段动态启发，将流形约束与稀疏专家机制融合，实现无监督状态发现。

en.tldr: FLUX combines geometry-aware flow matching with mixture of experts to reconstruct continuous dynamics and discover latent regime transitions from unpaired longitudinal snapshots.
en.motivation: Biological systems evolve through latent regimes but are observed only as unpaired snapshots, requiring simultaneous handling of curved manifolds and changing transport mechanisms.
en.method: Learns a data-dependent metric to construct geometry-aware conditional paths and decomposes the velocity field into sparse expert vector fields via Straight-Through Gumbel-Softmax routing.
en.result: On manifold controls, Lorenz system, cortical calcium imaging, and embryoid body data, FLUX reconstructs longitudinal transport and recovers interpretable regime structure.
en.help_to_user: Provides a general strategy for discovering biological state transitions from unpaired longitudinal data without requiring matched trajectories.

idea_spark.idea: Combine mixture-of-experts routing with data-driven metric learning to automatically identify critical time points of dynamic changes.
idea_spark.risk: May overfit on high-dimensional sparse data, and interpretability of routing choices needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by multi-stage biological dynamics, integrating manifold constraints with sparse expert mechanisms enables unsupervised state discovery.

papers.title: Quantitative Sobolev Approximation Bounds for Neural Operators with Empirical Validation on Burgers Equation
papers.summary: Neural operators have emerged as a powerful tool for learning mappings between infinite-dimensional function spaces. However, their approximation properties in Sobolev norms remain poorly quantified, even though these norms control both function values and derivatives and are the natural metrics for PDE well-posedness, stability, and generalization. We develop a functional-analytic framework for operator learning in Sobolev spaces and connect it to the numerical behavior of Fourier Neural Operators (FNOs) on a prototypical PDE. First, for a continuous nonlinear operator $\mathcal{G}: H^{s}(D)\to H^{t}(D')$ with $s > d/2$ and inputs restricted to a compact subset of $H^{s}(D)$, we prove that $\mathcal{G}$ can be uniformly approximated in $H^{t}$-norm by a neural operator with $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^{-d/s})$ trainable parameters. This yields an explicit complexity--error relation of the form $\|\mathcal{G}-\mathcal{G}_θ\|_{H^{t}} \lesssim C N^{-s/d}$. We then study the one-dimensional viscous Burgers solution operator $\mathcal{G}: u_{0}\mapsto u(\cdot,1)$ on a bounded $H^{1}$-ball and train FNOs with an $H^{1}$-loss. Across a sweep of model sizes, we obtain test $H^{1}$-errors down to $\mathcal{O}(10^{-7})$ and relative errors of order $10^{-3}$, with predictions accurately matching both solutions and spatial derivatives on held-out data. A log-log plot of Sobolev error versus parameter count exhibits an approximate power law $\|\mathcal{G}-\mathcal{G}_θ\|_{H^{1}} \approx C N^{-α}$ with empirical exponent $α\approx 1.4$, and long-horizon training reveals optimization instabilities in large FNOs, providing quantitative evidence that Sobolev-space approximation theory meaningfully predicts neural-operator scaling behavior.

ai.tldr: 本文为神经算子在Sobolev范数下的逼近建立了理论界，并通过Burgers方程上的FNO实验验证了幂律标度行为。
ai.motivation: 神经算子在无限维函数空间映射学习中表现优异，但其在同时控制函数值和导数的Sobolev范数下的逼近性质缺乏量化分析。
ai.method: 发展泛函分析框架，证明连续非线性算子可由参数数为O(ε^{-d/s})的神经算子在H^t范数下一致逼近；对一维粘性Burgers方程解算子，训练带H^1损失的FNO并测量误差。
ai.result: 测试H^1误差低至10^{-7}，相对误差约10^{-3}；Sobolev误差与参数数呈幂律关系，经验指数α≈1.4；大FNO出现优化不稳定。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解神经算子在Sobolev空间中的逼近能力及其与PDE求解精度的关系。

idea_spark.idea: 利用Sobolev逼近界指导FNO的架构设计，例如自适应调整频率模式数以平衡精度与计算成本。
idea_spark.risk: 理论界依赖于输入紧致性和算子光滑性假设，实际PDE可能不严格满足；大模型优化不稳定可能限制可扩展性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将Sobolev范数作为损失函数可能提升神经算子对导数敏感问题的泛化能力。

en.tldr: This paper establishes theoretical Sobolev approximation bounds for neural operators and validates the power-law scaling behavior via FNO experiments on Burgers equation.
en.motivation: Neural operators excel at learning mappings between infinite-dimensional function spaces, but their approximation properties in Sobolev norms—which control both function values and derivatives—remain poorly quantified.
en.method: Develops a functional-analytic framework proving that a continuous nonlinear operator can be uniformly approximated in H^t norm by a neural operator with O(ε^{-d/s}) parameters; for the 1D viscous Burgers solution operator, trains FNOs with H^1 loss and measures errors.
en.result: Test H^1 errors as low as 10^{-7}, relative errors around 10^{-3}; Sobolev error vs. parameter count follows a power law with empirical exponent α≈1.4; large FNOs exhibit optimization instabilities.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the approximation capability of neural operators in Sobolev spaces and its relation to PDE solution accuracy.

idea_spark.idea: Use Sobolev approximation bounds to guide FNO architecture design, e.g., adaptively adjusting frequency modes to balance accuracy and computational cost.
idea_spark.risk: The bounds rely on input compactness and operator smoothness assumptions that may not hold for real PDEs; large-model optimization instability may limit scalability.
idea_spark.inspiration: Incorporating Sobolev norms as loss functions may improve generalization of neural operators for derivative-sensitive problems.

papers.title: jNO: A JAX Library for Neural Operator and Foundation Model Training
papers.summary: jNO (jax Neural Operators) is a JAX-native library for neural operators and foundation models with unified support for both data-driven and physics-informed training. Its core design is a tracing system in which domains, model calls, residuals, supervised losses, and diagnostics are written in one symbolic language and compiled into one optimization pipeline. This allows users to move between operator regression, mesh-aware residual evaluation, and PDE-constrained training without restructuring the surrounding code. jNO also supports multi-model compositions, fine-grained control at parameter level (model, optimizer, and learning rate), hyperparameter tuning, and JAX-native workflows for translated PDE foundation-model families. The source repository is available at https://github.com/FhG-IISB/jNO.

ai.tldr: jNO是一个基于JAX的神经网络算子与基础模型训练库，统一支持数据驱动和物理信息训练，通过追踪系统将域、模型调用、残差、监督损失和诊断编译为单一优化流水线。
ai.motivation: 现有神经网络算子库在数据驱动与物理信息训练之间切换时需重构代码，缺乏统一高效的训练框架。
ai.method: 设计一个符号追踪系统，将域、模型调用、残差、监督损失和诊断用统一语言描述并编译为单一优化流水线，支持多模型组合、参数级精细控制、超参数调优及JAX原生工作流。
ai.result: 用户可在算子回归、网格感知残差评估和PDE约束训练之间无缝切换，无需重构代码，并支持基础模型族的高效训练。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员和工程师快速构建和训练神经网络算子及基础模型，尤其适用于涉及PDE的科学计算任务。

idea_spark.idea: 将符号追踪系统扩展到多物理场耦合问题，自动生成联合优化流水线。
idea_spark.risk: 符号编译可能引入额外开销，对极大规模模型或复杂PDE系统性能需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受JAX函数式编程和自动微分启发，将科学计算中的不同组件符号化并统一编译。

en.tldr: jNO is a JAX-native library for neural operators and foundation models, unifying data-driven and physics-informed training via a tracing system that compiles domains, model calls, residuals, supervised losses, and diagnostics into a single optimization pipeline.
en.motivation: Existing neural operator libraries require code restructuring when switching between data-driven and physics-informed training, lacking a unified and efficient training framework.
en.method: Design a symbolic tracing system that describes domains, model calls, residuals, supervised losses, and diagnostics in one language and compiles them into a single optimization pipeline, supporting multi-model compositions, fine-grained parameter control, hyperparameter tuning, and JAX-native workflows.
en.result: Users can seamlessly switch between operator regression, mesh-aware residual evaluation, and PDE-constrained training without code restructuring, and efficiently train foundation model families.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers and engineers quickly build and train neural operators and foundation models, especially for scientific computing tasks involving PDEs.

idea_spark.idea: Extend the symbolic tracing system to multi-physics coupled problems, automatically generating joint optimization pipelines.
idea_spark.risk: Symbolic compilation may introduce overhead; performance for extremely large models or complex PDE systems needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by JAX's functional programming and automatic differentiation, symbolizing and unifying different components in scientific computing.

papers.title: Inpainting physics: self-supervised learning for context-driven fluid simulation
papers.summary: Neural surrogate models for computational fluid dynamics (CFD) are typically trained as forward operators that map explicit problem specifications, such as geometry and boundary conditions, to solution fields. This ties the model to the conditioning variables seen during training and limits reuse under boundary-condition shifts or local geometry changes. We propose to reformulate steady CFD inference as an inpainting problem: instead of training on explicit boundary conditions, we learn a self-supervised prior over velocity fields and impose boundary constraints only during inference by fixing known regions such as inlet, outlet or unchanged regions from previous simulations. To scale this idea to large 3D meshes, we introduce a local neighbourhood tokeniser that represents high-resolution velocity fields as compact spatial latent tokens and train latent flow-matching and masked-autoencoder models on these tokens. On intracranial aneurysm hemodynamics, our method reconstructs full velocity fields from sparse boundary context, outperforms supervised neural surrogates under boundary-condition and dataset shift and enables local geometry editing by reusing unchanged simulation context. These results suggest that viewing CFD inference as context-conditioned inpainting can turn neural surrogates from task-specific predictors into reusable flow priors.

ai.tldr: 将稳态计算流体动力学推理重新表述为图像修复问题，通过自监督学习速度场先验，并在推理时施加边界约束，实现可重用的流先验。
ai.motivation: 传统神经代理模型作为前向算子，受限于训练时的边界条件，难以应对边界条件变化或局部几何编辑。
ai.method: 提出局部邻域分词器将高分辨率速度场表示为紧凑空间潜在令牌，训练潜在流匹配和掩码自编码器模型，在推理时通过固定已知区域施加边界约束。
ai.result: 在颅内动脉瘤血流动力学中，从稀疏边界上下文重建完整速度场，在边界条件和数据集偏移下优于监督代理模型，并支持局部几何编辑。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种将CFD推理视为上下文条件修复的新视角，使神经代理模型成为可重用的流先验，适用于边界条件变化或几何编辑场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将物理模拟的边界条件视为图像修复中的掩码区域，利用自监督学习从稀疏观测中推断完整场。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量自监督预训练数据，且局部邻域分词器可能对网格拓扑敏感，需验证泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受图像修复中上下文条件生成启发，将物理模拟转化为条件生成问题，突破传统前向模型的限制。

en.tldr: Reformulates steady CFD inference as an inpainting problem, learning a self-supervised prior over velocity fields and imposing boundary constraints only during inference, enabling reusable flow priors.
en.motivation: Traditional neural surrogates are trained as forward operators tied to explicit boundary conditions, limiting reuse under shifts or local geometry changes.
en.method: Introduces a local neighbourhood tokeniser to represent high-resolution velocity fields as compact latent tokens, trains latent flow-matching and masked-autoencoder models, and imposes boundary constraints by fixing known regions during inference.

en.help_to_user: Provides a new perspective on CFD inference as context-conditioned inpainting, turning neural surrogates into reusable flow priors for varying boundary conditions or geometry edits.

idea_spark.idea: Treat boundary conditions in physical simulation as masked regions in inpainting, using self-supervised learning to infer full fields from sparse observations.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on high-quality self-supervised pretraining data; local neighbourhood tokeniser may be sensitive to mesh topology, requiring validation of generalization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by context-conditioned generation in image inpainting, transforming physical simulation into a conditional generation problem to overcome limitations of forward models.

papers.title: CATO: Charted Attention for Neural PDE Operators
papers.summary: Neural operators have emerged as powerful data-driven solvers for PDEs, offering substantial acceleration over classical numerical methods. However, existing transformer-based operators still face critical challenges when modeling PDEs on complex geometries: directly processing over massive mesh points is computationally expensive, while operating in raw discretization coordinates may obscure the intrinsic geometry where physical interactions are more naturally expressed. To address these limitations, we introduce the Charted Axial Transformer Operator (CATO), a geometry-adaptive and derivative-aware neural operator for PDEs on general geometries. Instead of applying attention directly in the physical coordinate system, CATO learns a continuous latent chart that maps mesh coordinates into a learned chart space, where chart-conditioned axial attention efficiently captures long-range dependencies with reduced computational cost. In addition, CATO introduces a derivative-aware physics loss for steady-state PDEs that jointly supervises solution values, mesh-consistent gradients, and an auxiliary flux-like field, improving physical fidelity and reducing oversmoothing. We further provide a theoretical approximation result showing that, under a favorable chart, charted axial attention can represent low-rank axial solution operators with controlled error, and that small chart perturbations induce bounded approximation degradation. CATO achieves the best performance across all evaluated datasets, yielding an average improvement of approximately 26.76\% over the strongest competing baselines while reducing the number of parameters by 81.98\%. These results highlight the effectiveness of learning geometry-adaptive charts and derivative-aware physical supervision for accurate and efficient PDE operator learning.

ai.tldr: 提出CATO，一种几何自适应且导数感知的神经算子，用于一般几何上的偏微分方程求解。
ai.motivation: 现有基于Transformer的神经算子在复杂几何上处理海量网格点计算昂贵，且原始离散坐标可能掩盖内在几何结构。
ai.method: 学习连续潜在图表将网格坐标映射到图表空间，在该空间应用图表条件轴向注意力以高效捕获长程依赖；引入导数感知物理损失联合监督解值、网格一致梯度和辅助通量场。
ai.result: 在所有评估数据集上取得最佳性能，平均提升约26.76%，参数量减少81.98%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过几何自适应图表和导数感知物理损失提升偏微分方程神经算子学习的精度与效率。

idea_spark.idea: 将图表条件轴向注意力与导数感知损失结合，为几何自适应神经算子设计提供新思路。
idea_spark.risk: 图表学习的质量高度依赖数据，可能对噪声或稀疏网格敏感；理论保证基于有利图表假设，实际中图表可能不理想。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受微分几何中图表概念的启发，将物理坐标映射到学习到的图表空间以简化注意力计算并保留几何信息。

en.tldr: Proposes CATO, a geometry-adaptive and derivative-aware neural operator for solving PDEs on general geometries.
en.motivation: Existing transformer-based operators are computationally expensive on massive mesh points and raw coordinates may obscure intrinsic geometry.
en.method: Learns a continuous latent chart mapping mesh coordinates to a chart space, where chart-conditioned axial attention captures long-range dependencies efficiently; introduces derivative-aware physics loss jointly supervising solution values, mesh-consistent gradients, and an auxiliary flux-like field.
en.result: Achieves best performance on all evaluated datasets with an average improvement of ~26.76% and 81.98% parameter reduction.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how geometry-adaptive charts and derivative-aware physics loss improve accuracy and efficiency in neural PDE operator learning.

idea_spark.idea: Combining chart-conditioned axial attention with derivative-aware loss offers a new design for geometry-adaptive neural operators.
idea_spark.risk: Chart quality heavily depends on data and may be sensitive to noise or sparse grids; theoretical guarantees assume favorable charts which may not hold in practice.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the concept of charts in differential geometry, mapping physical coordinates to a learned chart space to simplify attention computation while preserving geometric information.

papers.title: Stable Long-Horizon PDE Forecasting via Latent Structured Spectral Propagators
papers.summary: Long-horizon forecasting of time-dependent partial differential equations (PDEs) is critical for characterizing the sustained evolution of physical systems. While neural operators have emerged as efficient surrogates, they typically learn implicit finite-time transitions from discrete observations. When deployed autoregressively, such propagators often suffer from rapid error accumulation and dynamic drift. To address this, we propose a neural forecasting framework that reformulates PDE rollout as learning a Structured Spectral Propagator (SSP) in a propagation-oriented latent space. Following an analysis-propagation-synthesis design, our framework: (i) maps physical states into a shared, time-consistent spatial representation; (ii) projects this space into a compact propagation state to isolate recurrent dynamics from fine-grained spatial details, thereby decoupling reconstruction fidelity from rollout regularity; and (iii) evolves retained spectral modes using a frequency-conditioned linear backbone complemented by a nonlinear spectral closure to account for truncated interactions. This explicit structuring endows the propagator with a strong inductive bias for coherent modal evolution. Extensive experiments demonstrate that SSP significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, reducing relative $L_2$ errors by up to 48.9% and exhibiting improved stability in temporal extrapolation beyond the supervised horizon.

ai.tldr: 提出一种结构化谱传播器（SSP），在面向传播的隐空间中学习PDE的长期滚动预测，显著降低误差并提升稳定性。
ai.motivation: 现有神经算子自回归预测时误差累积快、动态漂移严重，难以实现稳定的长时间PDE演化预测。
ai.method: 采用分析-传播-合成框架：将物理状态映射到时间一致的共享空间，再投影到紧凑传播状态以解耦重建与滚动规律，最后用频率条件线性主干加非线性谱闭合演化保留的谱模式。
ai.result: 在多个PDE基准上，SSP将相对L2误差降低最多48.9%，并在监督时域外展现出更好的外推稳定性。
ai.help_to_user: 若您需要长时间预测物理系统演化（如流体、气候），SSP提供了更稳定、更准确的神经算子方案。

idea_spark.idea: 将物理系统的模态演化显式建模为频率条件线性过程加非线性修正，可启发更通用的隐空间动力学学习框架。
idea_spark.risk: 对高度非线性或混沌系统，谱截断可能丢失关键模式，导致预测发散。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴经典谱方法（如傅里叶、小波）的结构化先验，为神经网络提供强归纳偏置，是提升长期预测稳定性的有效路径。

idea_spark.idea: 将物理系统的模态演化显式建模为频率条件线性过程加非线性修正，可启发更通用的隐空间动力学学习框架。
idea_spark.risk: 对高度非线性或混沌系统，谱截断可能丢失关键模式，导致预测发散。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴经典谱方法（如傅里叶、小波）的结构化先验，为神经网络提供强归纳偏置，是提升长期预测稳定性的有效路径。

en.tldr: Proposes a Structured Spectral Propagator (SSP) that learns PDE rollout in a propagation-oriented latent space, significantly reducing error and improving stability.
en.motivation: Existing neural operators suffer from rapid error accumulation and dynamic drift in autoregressive long-horizon PDE forecasting.
en.method: An analysis-propagation-synthesis framework: maps physical states to a shared time-consistent space, projects to a compact propagation state to decouple reconstruction from rollout regularity, and evolves retained spectral modes via a frequency-conditioned linear backbone with nonlinear spectral closure.
en.result: SSP reduces relative L2 error by up to 48.9% on multiple PDE benchmarks and demonstrates improved stability beyond the supervised horizon.
en.help_to_user: If you need long-term forecasting of physical systems (e.g., fluid dynamics, climate), SSP offers a more stable and accurate neural operator approach.

idea_spark.idea: Explicitly modeling modal evolution as a frequency-conditioned linear process with nonlinear corrections can inspire more general latent dynamics learning frameworks.
idea_spark.risk: For highly nonlinear or chaotic systems, spectral truncation may miss critical modes, leading to prediction divergence.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging structured priors from classical spectral methods (e.g., Fourier, wavelet) provides strong inductive biases for neural networks, a promising path to improve long-term forecasting stability.

papers.title: Kernel-based linear system identification using augmented Krylov subspaces
papers.summary: We propose a novel Krylov subspace method for estimating the finite impulse response (FIR) of a one-dimensional linear time-invariant systems. The method approximates the system's FIR using a kernel-based formulation combined with hyperparameter selection based on maximum likelihood estimation (MLE), which requires repeated evaluation of two terms: The data fit $\boldsymbol{y}^{\top} (λ\boldsymbol{I} + \boldsymbol{A})^{-1} \boldsymbol{y}$ and the model complexity $\log(\det (λ\boldsymbol{I} + \boldsymbol{A}))$, where $\boldsymbol{A}$ is a certain positive semidefinite matrix that admits fast matrix--vector products and $λ> 0$ is a regularization parameter. Instead of approximating these two quantities separately, we jointly approximate them using a single augmented Krylov subspace for $\boldsymbol{A}$. One major benefit of augmentation is that we obtain accelerated convergence when approximating the data fit quadratic form, through implicit preconditioning. Thanks to the shift invariance of Krylov subspaces, the extracted approximations can be used to evaluate the MLE objective for many values of $λ$ at little additional cost. We derive error bounds for the approximations, reflecting the benefits of augmentation demonstrated through multiple numerical experiments.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于增广Krylov子空间的核方法，用于估计一维线性时不变系统的有限脉冲响应。
ai.motivation: 现有方法在估计有限脉冲响应时，需要重复计算数据拟合项和模型复杂度项，计算成本高。
ai.method: 通过构建增广Krylov子空间联合近似这两个量，利用子空间的平移不变性实现多正则化参数的高效评估。
ai.result: 推导了近似误差界，数值实验表明增广方法加速了数据拟合二次型的收敛。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种高效的系统辨识算法，特别适用于需要扫描正则化参数的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将数据拟合和模型复杂度联合近似，利用隐式预处理加速收敛。
idea_spark.risk: 增广子空间维度选择可能影响近似精度，需平衡计算量与准确性。

en.tldr: Proposes a kernel-based method using augmented Krylov subspaces to estimate the finite impulse response of one-dimensional linear time-invariant systems.
en.motivation: Existing methods require repeated evaluation of data fit and model complexity terms, leading to high computational cost.
en.method: Jointly approximates both terms via a single augmented Krylov subspace, leveraging shift invariance for efficient multi-λ evaluation.
en.result: Derives error bounds and demonstrates accelerated convergence for the data fit quadratic form through numerical experiments.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient system identification algorithm, especially useful when scanning multiple regularization parameters.

idea_spark.idea: Jointly approximate data fit and model complexity using implicit preconditioning to accelerate convergence.
idea_spark.risk: Choice of augmented subspace dimension may affect approximation accuracy, requiring a trade-off between computation and precision.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the success of Krylov subspaces in solving large linear systems, applied here to kernel methods in system identification.

papers.title: M$^3$: Reframing Training Measures for Discretized Physical Simulations
papers.summary: Neural surrogate models for physical simulations are trained on discretized samples of continuous domains, where the induced empirical measure leads to uneven supervision, biasing optimization and causing spatial inconsistencies in physical fidelity. To mitigate this measure-induced bias, we propose M$^3$ (Multi-scale Morton Measure), a scalable framework that balances training measures by partitioning space according to physical variation and allocating supervision across multiple scales. Applied to three industrial-scale datasets with diverse discretizations, M$^3$ consistently improves predictions in the continuous physical domain, achieving up to 4.7$\times$ lower error in large-scale volumetric cases. These gains persist under aggressive subsampling (160M $\rightarrow$ 16M $\rightarrow$ 1.6M points), where M$^3$-trained models outperform those trained on higher-resolution data, reducing physics-weighted relative $L_2$ error by 3--4$\times$ and the corresponding MSE by up to 13$\times$. These results highlight data distribution as a key factor in operator learning and position M$^3$ as a scalable, data-efficient approach for physically consistent modeling.

ai.tldr: 提出M³框架，通过多尺度Morton度量平衡离散化物理模拟中的训练度量偏差，提升连续域预测精度。
ai.motivation: 物理模拟的神经代理模型在离散化样本上训练时，经验度量导致监督不均匀，优化偏向且物理保真度空间不一致。
ai.method: M³（多尺度Morton度量）根据物理变化划分空间，跨多个尺度分配监督，平衡训练度量。
ai.result: 在三个工业级数据集上，M³持续改善连续物理域预测，误差降低高达4.7倍；在激进子采样下，M³训练的模型优于高分辨率数据训练的模型，物理加权相对L2误差降低3-4倍，MSE降低高达13倍。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过调整数据分布来提升物理模拟代理模型的精度和效率。

idea_spark.idea: 利用Morton曲线和物理变化自适应采样，实现多尺度监督分配，平衡训练度量。
idea_spark.risk: 框架依赖物理变化的空间划分，可能对复杂动态系统适应性有限；多尺度计算开销需评估。

en.tldr: Proposes M³ framework to balance training measure bias in discretized physical simulations via multi-scale Morton measure, improving continuous-domain predictions.
en.motivation: Neural surrogate models for physical simulations trained on discretized samples suffer from uneven supervision due to empirical measure, biasing optimization and causing spatial inconsistencies.
en.method: M³ (Multi-scale Morton Measure) partitions space according to physical variation and allocates supervision across multiple scales to balance training measures.
en.result: On three industrial-scale datasets, M³ consistently improves predictions in the continuous physical domain, achieving up to 4.7× lower error; under aggressive subsampling, M³-trained models outperform those trained on higher-resolution data, reducing physics-weighted relative L2 error by 3–4× and MSE by up to 13×.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how adjusting data distribution can improve accuracy and efficiency of surrogate models for physical simulations.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage Morton curves and physics-aware adaptive sampling for multi-scale supervision allocation to balance training measures.
idea_spark.risk: Framework relies on spatial partitioning based on physical variation, which may have limited adaptability to complex dynamic systems; multi-scale computational overhead needs evaluation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Optimizing supervision signals from a data distribution perspective inspires continuous modeling of discretized data in other fields like computer graphics and geographic information systems.

papers.title: Hierarchical Multi-Fidelity Learning for Predicting Three-Dimensional Flame Wrinkling and Turbulent Burning Velocity
papers.summary: High-fidelity experimental characterization of turbulent premixed flames remains limited by the cost and complexity of advanced diagnostics, particularly under elevated pressures and intense turbulence where measurements of coupled flame morphology and burning dynamics are sparse. Here, we develop a hierarchical multi-fidelity neural network framework (MuFiNNs) to address this challenge by integrating sparse high-fidelity experimental data with structured low-fidelity representations encoding dominant physical trends. The framework combines hierarchical low-fidelity construction with nonlinear multi-fidelity correction to learn coupled geometric and reactive flame behavior while recovering discrepancies that simplified models alone cannot capture. The methodology is applied to expanding turbulent premixed flames to predict three-dimensional flame wrinkling dynamics and turbulent mass burning velocity across varying fuels, pressures, and turbulence intensities. Using experimentally informed low-fidelity trend models with sparse high-fidelity measurements, MuFiNNs accurately reconstruct observed flame behavior, enable interpolation across unseen operating conditions, and demonstrate robust extrapolation beyond the training domain. Importantly, the framework remains effective in noisy, weakly structured, or experimentally inaccessible regimes where conventional data-driven approaches often fail. These results show that hierarchical multi-fidelity learning provides a scalable and physically grounded strategy for predictive combustion modeling in data-limited regimes. More broadly, this work establishes multi-fidelity scientific machine learning as a practical framework for extracting physically meaningful predictive models from sparse experiments, particularly for instability-dominated and turbulence-sensitive reactive flows where high-fidelity data acquisition is demanding.

ai.tldr: 提出分层多保真神经网络框架，融合稀疏高保真实验数据与低保真趋势模型，预测湍流预混火焰的三维褶皱和燃烧速度。
ai.motivation: 高保真实验表征湍流预混火焰成本高、数据稀疏，尤其在高压强湍流下难以获取耦合形态与燃烧动力学数据。
ai.method: 构建分层多保真神经网络（MuFiNNs），通过低保真构造与非线性多保真校正学习几何与反应耦合行为，并恢复简化模型无法捕捉的偏差。
ai.result: 准确重建火焰行为，实现未见过工况的内插和训练域外的稳健外推，在噪声、弱结构或实验不可达区域仍有效。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种在数据有限条件下预测复杂燃烧现象的实用方法，可推广至其他湍流敏感反应流。

en.tldr: Proposes a hierarchical multi-fidelity neural network framework that integrates sparse high-fidelity experimental data with low-fidelity trend models to predict three-dimensional flame wrinkling and turbulent burning velocity in premixed flames.
en.motivation: High-fidelity experimental characterization of turbulent premixed flames is costly and data-sparse, especially under elevated pressures and intense turbulence where coupled morphology and burning dynamics are hard to measure.
en.method: Develops hierarchical multi-fidelity neural networks (MuFiNNs) combining low-fidelity construction with nonlinear multi-fidelity correction to learn coupled geometric and reactive flame behavior and recover discrepancies missed by simplified models.

en.help_to_user: Provides a scalable, physically grounded strategy for predictive combustion modeling in data-limited regimes, applicable to other turbulence-sensitive reactive flows.

idea_spark.idea: Incorporate physics-informed constraints (e.g., conservation laws) to improve low-fidelity models, or use active learning to optimize high-fidelity data collection.
idea_spark.risk: Construction of low-fidelity models relies on domain knowledge; large trend deviations may degrade correction performance; extrapolation robustness needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by multi-fidelity scientific machine learning, combining sparse high-fidelity data with low-fidelity physical trends for data-efficient prediction.

papers.title: A Call to Lagrangian Action: Learning Population Mechanics from Temporal Snapshots
papers.summary: The population dynamics of molecules, cells, and organisms are governed by a number of unknown forces. In the last decade, population dynamics have predominantly been modeled with Wasserstein gradient flows. However, since gradient flows minimize free energy, they fail to capture important dynamical properties, such as periodicity. In this work, we propose a change in perspective by considering dynamics that minimize a population-level action under a damped Wasserstein Lagrangian. By deriving the corresponding Hamiltonian equations of motion, we formalize Wasserstein Lagrangian Mechanics, a structured class of second-order dynamics that encompasses classical mechanics, quantum mechanics, and gradient flows. We then propose WLM as the first algorithm that learns these second-order dynamics from observed marginals, without specifying the Lagrangian. By directly learning the population mechanics, WLM can both forecast and interpolate unseen marginals, and outperforms existing gradient flow and flow matching methods across a wide range of dynamics, including vortex dynamics, embryonic development, and flocking.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于阻尼Wasserstein拉格朗日量的群体力学学习方法，能从时间快照中学习二阶动力学，无需指定拉格朗日量。
ai.motivation: 现有Wasserstein梯度流模型只能描述自由能最小化，无法捕捉周期性等重要动力学特性。
ai.method: 推导阻尼Wasserstein拉格朗日量对应的哈密顿运动方程，形式化Wasserstein拉格朗日力学，并设计算法直接从观测边缘分布学习二阶动力学。
ai.result: 在涡旋动力学、胚胎发育和群体运动等多种动力学中，优于梯度流和流匹配方法，能预测和插值未见边缘分布。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需显式建模力场即可从快照数据学习群体运动规律的新框架。

idea_spark.idea: 将拉格朗日力学与Wasserstein几何结合，为学习非保守动力学提供新范式。
idea_spark.risk: 对高维或噪声较大的边缘分布，学习稳定性可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从经典力学中的最小作用量原理出发，将群体动力学视为二阶系统，启发更通用的物理信息学习框架。

en.tldr: Proposes a method to learn population mechanics from temporal snapshots using a damped Wasserstein Lagrangian, without specifying the Lagrangian explicitly.
en.motivation: Existing Wasserstein gradient flow models only minimize free energy and fail to capture important dynamical properties like periodicity.
en.method: Derives Hamiltonian equations of motion from a damped Wasserstein Lagrangian, formalizes Wasserstein Lagrangian Mechanics, and develops an algorithm to learn second-order dynamics directly from observed marginals.

idea_spark.risk: Learning stability may degrade for high-dimensional or noisy marginal distributions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Drawing from the principle of least action in classical mechanics, treating population dynamics as second-order systems inspires more general physics-informed learning frameworks.

papers.title: Intervention-Based Time Series Causal Discovery via Simulator-Generated Interventional Distributions
papers.summary: We propose SVAR-FM (Structural VAR with Flow Matching), a framework for time series causal discovery that treats a physics-based simulator as a mechanical realization of Pearl's do operator. Clamping a variable inside the simulator physically severs confounding paths, producing interventional data by construction. Conditional Flow Matching then learns the nonlinear interventional conditionals. Theoretically, we prove that the full structural VAR becomes identifiable under a coverage condition on the simulator-clampable variables, and derive an end-to-end error bound that decomposes into Monte Carlo, simulator fidelity, and Flow Matching terms. A sign-flip corollary predicts that when simulator accuracy falls below a threshold, the estimated causal effect reverses sign. Empirically, a benchmark across four scientific domains confirms that SVAR-FM recovers the correct causal sign where observational methods produce sign-reversed estimates due to confounding. A case study in ultrafast laser physics verifies the sign-flip prediction by physically varying the accuracy level of a first-principles quantum solver: the low-accuracy setting reverses the causal sign, while the high-accuracy setting recovers the correct direction (R-squared = 0.983, zero bias).

ai.tldr: 提出SVAR-FM框架，利用物理模拟器实现Pearl的do算子，通过Flow Matching学习干预条件分布，实现时间序列因果发现。
ai.motivation: 观测数据中的混杂因素常导致因果方向估计错误，而真实干预实验成本高或不可行。
ai.method: 将物理模拟器作为do算子的机械实现，夹持变量切断混杂路径生成干预数据，再用条件流匹配学习非线性干预条件分布。
ai.result: 在四个科学领域基准测试中，SVAR-FM正确恢复因果符号，而观测方法因混杂产生符号反转；超快激光物理案例验证了符号翻转预测。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需真实干预实验即可从时间序列数据中推断因果方向的方法。

idea_spark.idea: 结合模拟器与生成模型，将模拟器视为可干预的因果机制，实现低成本因果发现。
idea_spark.risk: 模拟器精度不足时因果符号可能反转，需谨慎评估模拟器保真度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用模拟器的物理可干预性替代真实实验，为因果推断开辟新路径。

en.tldr: Proposes SVAR-FM, a framework that uses a physics-based simulator as a mechanical realization of Pearl's do operator and Flow Matching to learn interventional conditionals for time series causal discovery.
en.motivation: Confounding in observational data often leads to incorrect causal direction estimates, while real interventional experiments are costly or infeasible.
en.method: Treats a physics-based simulator as a mechanical do-operator: clamping a variable severs confounding paths to generate interventional data, then uses Conditional Flow Matching to learn nonlinear interventional conditionals.
en.result: Across four scientific domains, SVAR-FM recovers correct causal signs where observational methods produce sign-reversed estimates; a ultrafast laser physics case study verifies the sign-flip prediction with R-squared = 0.983 and zero bias under high-accuracy simulator.
en.help_to_user: Provides a method to infer causal direction from time series data without requiring real interventional experiments.

idea_spark.idea: Combine simulators with generative models, treating the simulator as an intervenable causal mechanism for low-cost causal discovery.
idea_spark.risk: Insufficient simulator accuracy may reverse causal sign, requiring careful fidelity assessment.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging the physical intervenability of simulators as a substitute for real experiments opens new avenues for causal inference.

papers.title: Don't Fix the Basis -- Learn It: Spectral Representation with Adaptive Basis Learning for PDEs
papers.summary: Spectral neural operators achieve strong performance for PDE learning, but rely on fixed global bases that limit their ability to represent spatially heterogeneous and multiscale dynamics. We propose Adaptive Basis Learning (ABLE), a framework that learns data-dependent spectral representations instead of relying on predefined bases. ABLE constructs a spatially adaptive Parseval frame via a learned ancillary density, enabling the operator to act in a lifted spectral space while preserving invertibility and maintaining $O(N\log N)$ complexity through FFT-based implementation. This shifts the source of expressivity from spectral coefficients to the representation itself, allowing the model to capture localized structures and non-translation-invariant interactions more efficiently. ABLE integrates seamlessly into existing neural operator architectures as a drop-in replacement for spectral layers. Across a range of benchmarks ABLE improves accuracy over strong baselines, with the largest gains in regimes characterized by sharp gradients and multiscale behavior. Moreover, augmenting existing models (e.g., U-FNO, HPM) with ABLE further enhances their performance, demonstrating its role as a general and complementary spectral refinement. Our results highlight that the data-driven choice of representation, rather than operator complexity alone, is a key bottleneck in neural operator design. By learning the basis itself, ABLE provides a principled and efficient framework for improving spectral methods in PDE learning.

    "method": "通过学习辅助密度构建空间自适应的Parseval框架，在提升谱空间中操作算子，保持可逆性并利用FFT实现O(N log N)复杂度。",
    "result": "在多个基准上优于强基线，尤其在陡峭梯度和多尺度场景中提升显著；作为即插即用模块可增强现有模型。",
    "help_to_user": "提供一种通用且高效的谱细化方法，可替代现有神经算子中的谱层，提升对局部结构和非平移不变交互的建模能力。",
    "idea_spark": {
      "transferable": "该自适应基学习思想可迁移至其他依赖固定基的深度学习领域，如信号处理、图像压缩或物理信息网络。",
      "idea": "将自适应基学习与物理约束结合，例如在损失函数中嵌入偏微分方程残差，实现无监督或半监督的谱表示学习。",
      "risk": "学习数据依赖的基可能引入过拟合风险，尤其在训练数据有限或分布外推场景中。",
      "inspiration": "受谱方法中基函数选择重要性的启发，思考在深度学习模型中动态调整表示空间而非仅优化系数。"
    }
  },
  "en": {
    "tldr": "Proposes Adaptive Basis Learning (ABLE) framework that learns data-dependent spectral representations instead of fixed global bases, improving accuracy and efficiency for PDE learning.",
    "motivation": "Fixed global bases limit spectral neural operators' ability to represent spatially heterogeneous and multiscale dynamics.",
    "method": "Constructs a spatially adaptive Parseval frame via a learned ancillary density, enabling operator action in a lifted spectral space with invertibility and O(N log N) complexity via FFT.",
    "result": "Outperforms strong baselines across benchmarks, with largest gains in sharp gradient and multiscale regimes; serves as a drop-in enhancement for existing models like U-FNO and HPM.",
    "help_to_user": "Provides a principled and efficient spectral refinement that can replace spectral layers in neural operators, improving modeling of localized structures and non-translation-invariant interactions.",
    "idea_spark": {
      "transferable": "The adaptive basis learning idea can transfer to other deep learning domains relying on fixed bases, such as signal processing, image compression, or physics-informed networks.",
      "idea": "Combine adaptive basis learning with physical constraints, e.g., embedding PDE residuals in the loss function, for unsupervised or semi-supervised spectral representation learning.",
      "risk": "Learning data-dependent bases may introduce overfitting risk, especially with limited training data or out-of-distribution extrapolation.",
      "inspiration": "Inspired by the importance of basis choice in spectral methods, consider dynamically adjusting the representation space in deep learning models rather than only optimizing coefficients."
  }
}

papers.title: Discrete Flow Matching: Convergence Guarantees Under Minimal Assumptions
papers.summary: Flow Matching has recently emerged as a popular class of generative models for simulating a target distribution $μ_1$ from samples drawn from a source distribution $μ_0$. This framework relies on a fixed coupling between $μ_0$ and $μ_1$, and on a deterministic or stochastic bridge to define an interpolating process between the two distributions. The time marginals of this process can then be approximately sampled by estimating the transition rates, or more generally the generator, of its Markovian projection. This framework has recently been extended to the case of discrete source and target distributions, under the name Discrete Flow Matching (DFM). However, theoretical guarantees for such models remain scarce. In this paper, we study two DFM models on $\mathbb{Z}_m^d = \{0,\ldots,m-1\}^d$, sampled through time discretization, and derive non-asymptotic associated bounds for both of them. In contrast to previous work, we establish non-asymptotic bounds in Kullback--Leibler divergence for the early-stopped version of the target distribution. We also derive explicit convergence guarantees in total variation distance with respect to the true target distribution. Importantly, these bounds rely only on an approximation error assumption, relaxing standard score assumptions used in earlier works, while also yielding improved dependence on the vocabulary size $m$ and the dimension $d$.

ai.tldr: 本文研究了离散流匹配（DFM）模型在Z_m^d上的收敛性，给出了非渐近KL散度和全变差距离的保证，仅依赖近似误差假设。
ai.motivation: 离散流匹配模型缺乏理论保证，现有工作依赖较强的分数假设，本文旨在放松这些假设并建立更一般的收敛性结果。
ai.method: 分析了两种离散流匹配模型的时间离散化采样过程，推导了早期停止目标分布的非渐近KL散度界和全变差距离界。
ai.result: 获得了依赖于词汇大小m和维度d的改进收敛界，且仅需近似误差假设，无需标准分数假设。
ai.help_to_user: 为离散生成模型的收敛性分析提供了更宽松的理论框架，有助于设计更可靠的离散流匹配算法。

idea_spark.idea: 利用早期停止策略和近似误差假设，可以避免对分数函数的强假设，从而简化理论分析并可能提升实际性能。
idea_spark.risk: 近似误差假设在实际中可能难以验证，且早期停止可能影响生成样本的质量。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受连续流匹配收敛性分析的启发，将非渐近界技术扩展到离散设置，并放松假设条件。

en.tldr: This paper studies the convergence of Discrete Flow Matching (DFM) models on Z_m^d, providing non-asymptotic bounds in KL divergence and total variation distance under only an approximation error assumption.
en.motivation: DFM models lack theoretical guarantees; existing work relies on strong score assumptions. This work aims to relax these assumptions and establish more general convergence results.
en.method: Analyzes two DFM models with time discretization, deriving non-asymptotic KL divergence bounds for the early-stopped target distribution and total variation distance bounds to the true target.
en.result: Obtains improved convergence bounds depending on vocabulary size m and dimension d, requiring only an approximation error assumption instead of standard score assumptions.
en.help_to_user: Provides a more relaxed theoretical framework for convergence analysis of discrete generative models, aiding in designing more reliable DFM algorithms.

idea_spark.idea: Using early stopping and approximation error assumptions avoids strong assumptions on score functions, simplifying theoretical analysis and potentially improving practical performance.
idea_spark.risk: The approximation error assumption may be hard to verify in practice, and early stopping could degrade sample quality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by convergence analysis of continuous flow matching, extending non-asymptotic bounds to the discrete setting while relaxing assumptions.

papers.title: Regret Analysis of Guided Diffusion for Black-Box Optimization over Structured Inputs
papers.summary: Guided-diffusion black-box optimization (BO) has shown strong empirical performance on structured design problems such as molecules and crystals, but its regret behavior remains poorly understood. Existing BO regret analyses typically rely on maximum information gain, non-pretrained surrogate models, or exact acquisition maximization -- assumptions that break down in modern diffusion -- BO pipelines, where pretrained diffusion models serve as powerful priors over valid structures and acquisition maximization is replaced by approximate sampling over astronomically large discrete spaces. We develop a first certificate-based expected simple-regret framework for guided-diffusion BO that avoids maximum-information-gain bounds, RKHS assumptions, and exact acquisition maximization. The central quantity in our analysis is mass lift: the increase in probability mass assigned to near-optimal designs relative to the pretrained generator. This view explains how exponential-looking finite-budget convergence and polynomial acceleration can all arise from the same mechanism. We also give practical diagnostics for estimating search exponents from finite candidate pools and a proposal-corrected resampling construction that provides a fully certified sampler instance.

ai.tldr: 提出首个基于证书的期望简单遗憾框架，用于分析引导扩散黑箱优化在结构化输入上的遗憾行为，核心概念是质量提升。
ai.motivation: 现有黑箱优化遗憾分析依赖最大信息增益、非预训练代理模型或精确采集最大化，这些假设在现代扩散-黑箱优化流程中不成立。
ai.method: 开发了基于证书的期望简单遗憾框架，引入质量提升概念，并给出有限候选池的搜索指数估计和提议校正重采样构造。
ai.result: 解释了指数型有限预算收敛和多项式加速如何源于同一机制，并提供完全认证的采样器实例。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解引导扩散黑箱优化的理论保证，并提供实用诊断工具。

idea_spark.idea: 将质量提升与贝叶斯优化中的信息论指标结合，设计更高效的采集函数。
idea_spark.risk: 理论框架可能依赖特定假设（如预训练生成器质量），实际应用需验证鲁棒性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从概率质量分配角度重新思考黑箱优化，启发基于生成先验的搜索策略设计。
