en.tldr: Proposes a decentralized novelty detection framework that controls global false discovery rate via exchanging quantized surrogate models, without sharing raw data.
en.motivation: Existing novelty detection methods often require centralized data or sharing raw data, posing privacy and bandwidth bottlenecks, and struggle with heterogeneous composite null distributions.
en.method: Each agent independently learns a non-conformity score function, exchanges its low-precision quantized representation, and proves that evaluating data against quantized composite scores preserves conditional exchangeability.
en.result: Theoretical proof provides rigorous finite-sample guarantees for global FDR control; synthetic data experiments show the approach maintains competitive statistical power while drastically reducing communication cost.
en.help_to_user: Helps users perform novelty detection with controlled false discovery rate in privacy-sensitive and bandwidth-limited decentralized settings.

idea_spark.idea: Quantize non-conformity scores for exchange, leveraging conditional exchangeability to guarantee global FDR control without sharing raw data.
idea_spark.risk: Quantization precision may affect statistical power; robustness of conditional exchangeability assumption under heterogeneous data distributions needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combines model compression with statistical inference to provide efficient and privacy-preserving anomaly detection for distributed systems.

papers.title: Path-Coupled Bellman Flows for Distributional Reinforcement Learning
papers.summary: Distributional reinforcement learning (DRL) models the full return distribution, but existing finite-support or quantile-based methods rely on projections, while recent flow-based approaches can suffer from \emph{boundary mismatch} at the flow source or from \emph{high-variance} bootstrapping when current and successor noises are independent. We propose Path-Coupled Bellman Flows (PCBF), a continuous-time DRL method that learns return distributions with flow matching using \textbf{source-consistent Bellman-coupled paths}: the current path starts from the required base prior at $t{=}0$, reaches the Bellman target at $t{=}1$, and maintains a pathwise affine relation to the successor flow at intermediate times (without requiring time-$t$ marginals to satisfy a distributional Bellman fixed point for all $t$). PCBF couples current and successor return flows through shared base noise and uses a $λ$-parameterized control-variate target: $λ{=}0$ recovers an unbiased sample Bellman target, while $λ{>}0$ trades controlled bias for variance reduction. Experiments on analytically tractable MRPs, OGBench, and D4RL show improved distributional fidelity and training stability, and competitive offline RL performance.

ai.tldr: 提出路径耦合贝尔曼流（PCBF），一种连续时间分布强化学习方法，通过源一致的贝尔曼耦合路径学习回报分布，解决了边界不匹配和高方差自举问题。
ai.motivation: 现有分布强化学习方法存在投影误差或高方差自举问题，影响分布保真度和训练稳定性。
ai.method: PCBF 使用共享基噪声耦合当前与后继回报流，并通过 λ 参数化的控制变量目标在偏差与方差间权衡。
ai.result: 在解析 MRP、OGBench 和 D4RL 上实验表明，PCBF 提升了分布保真度和训练稳定性，离线 RL 性能具有竞争力。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种更稳定、保真度更高的分布强化学习算法，适用于需要完整回报分布建模的决策任务。

idea_spark.idea: 利用共享噪声源和中间时间点的仿射关系，避免逐时间步的分布贝尔曼不动点约束，简化训练。
idea_spark.risk: λ 参数的选择可能影响偏差-方差权衡，需要调参；连续时间方法在实际离散控制任务中的实现复杂度较高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从流匹配和贝尔曼方程的结合中获得启发，将分布学习转化为连续时间路径优化问题。

en.tldr: Proposes Path-Coupled Bellman Flows (PCBF), a continuous-time distributional RL method that learns return distributions via source-consistent Bellman-coupled paths, addressing boundary mismatch and high-variance bootstrapping.
en.motivation: Existing distributional RL methods suffer from projection errors or high-variance bootstrapping, degrading distributional fidelity and training stability.
en.method: PCBF couples current and successor return flows through shared base noise and uses a λ-parameterized control-variate target to trade bias for variance reduction.
en.result: Experiments on analytic MRPs, OGBench, and D4RL show improved distributional fidelity, training stability, and competitive offline RL performance.
en.help_to_user: Provides a more stable and faithful distributional RL algorithm suitable for tasks requiring full return distribution modeling.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage shared noise sources and affine relations at intermediate times to avoid per-time-step distributional Bellman fixed-point constraints, simplifying training.
idea_spark.risk: The λ parameter requires tuning for bias-variance trade-off; continuous-time methods may increase implementation complexity in discrete control tasks.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by combining flow matching with Bellman equations, reframing distribution learning as a continuous-time path optimization problem.

papers.title: Generating Symmetric Materials using Latent Flow Matching
papers.summary: Tackling the task of materials generation, we aim to enhance the previously proposed All-atom Diffusion Transformer (ADiT) by introducing SymADiT, a symmetry-aware variant. To do so, we use a representation of materials based on Wyckoff positions. We follow ADiT and perform generative modelling in latent space, adapted to our symmetry-aware representation. By forcing the output of the generative model to adhere to the symmetry restrictions imposed by the generated crystal's space group and each atom's Wyckoff-position, the generated materials exhibit more realistic symmetry properties. We benchmark our method against both symmetry-aware and symmetry-agnostic models for materials generation and show competitive performance, generating stable, symmetric materials with a simple Transformer architecture.

ai.tldr: 提出SymADiT，通过引入Wyckoff位置表示和对称约束，在潜在空间中生成具有真实对称性的材料。
ai.motivation: 现有材料生成模型常忽略晶体对称性，导致生成结构不真实。
ai.method: 基于Wyckoff位置表示材料，在潜在空间中进行流匹配生成，并强制输出符合空间群和Wyckoff位置的对称性约束。
ai.result: 与对称感知和对称无关的模型相比，SymADiT生成了稳定且对称性更真实的材料，性能具有竞争力。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户生成符合晶体对称性的新材料结构，适用于材料设计。

idea_spark.idea: 将对称性约束直接融入潜在扩散模型的解码过程，而非仅在后处理中施加。
idea_spark.risk: 过度约束可能限制生成多样性，需平衡对称性与结构新颖性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受晶体学中Wyckoff位置分类的启发，将离散对称性信息编码为生成模型的硬约束。

idea_spark.risk: 过度约束可能限制生成多样性，需平衡对称性与结构新颖性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受晶体学中Wyckoff位置分类的启发，将离散对称性信息编码为生成模型的硬约束。

en.tldr: Proposes SymADiT, a symmetry-aware variant of ADiT, using Wyckoff positions and symmetry constraints in latent space to generate realistic symmetric materials.
en.motivation: Existing material generation models often ignore crystal symmetry, leading to unrealistic structures.
en.method: Represents materials via Wyckoff positions, performs latent flow matching, and enforces symmetry constraints from space group and Wyckoff positions on the output.
en.result: SymADiT generates stable, symmetric materials with competitive performance against both symmetry-aware and symmetry-agnostic baselines.
en.help_to_user: Helps users generate new material structures that respect crystal symmetry, useful for materials design.

idea_spark.idea: Incorporate symmetry constraints directly into the decoding process of latent diffusion models rather than post-processing.
idea_spark.risk: Over-constraining may reduce diversity; a balance between symmetry and novelty is needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by crystallographic Wyckoff position classification, encoding discrete symmetry information as hard constraints in generative models.

papers.title: Analytic Continuation Between Real- and Imaginary-Time Quantum Dynamics and the Fundamental Instability of Inverse Reconstruction
papers.summary: We develop a unified spectral-semigroup framework that connects real-time and imaginary-time quantum dynamics through analytic continuation. Within this formulation, evolution is expressed as an exponential reweighting of spectral components generated by a single operator $\mathcal{G}$, placing unitary and dissipative dynamics on equal footing within a common spectral structure. The mapping naturally induces a nonlocal fractional operator in time, giving rise to a contractive semigroup governed by a square-root spectral deformation and identifying imaginary-time evolution as an effective fractional low-pass filter. While exponential attenuation suppresses high-frequency components, the inverse transformation remains systematically controllable within a well-defined spectral window. In this regime, stable reconstruction of low-energy and coarse-grained dynamical features is achieved, establishing a predictive relation between imaginary-time evolution and recoverable information. This leads to a quantitative description of a bandwidth-resolved asymmetry between forward propagation and inverse recovery. Across systems with continuous and discrete spectra, few-level coherence, and non-Hermitian generators, we demonstrate that spectral structure governs reconstruction fidelity in a unified manner. In particular, non-Hermitian and open-system settings reveal that irreversibility emerges as a geometry- and scale-dependent feature of the spectrum, tied to both damping and eigenstate non-orthogonality. These results recast analytic continuation as a structured, scale-dependent filtering process with quantifiable and systematically accessible reconstruction limits, providing a unified perspective on the interplay between dynamics, spectral geometry, and information recovery.

ai.tldr: 提出了统一谱-半群框架，通过解析延拓连接实时与虚时量子动力学，揭示了逆重构的根本不稳定性。
ai.motivation: 实时间与虚时间量子动力学之间的解析延拓长期存在逆重构不稳定的根本问题，缺乏统一的理论框架来理解其内在机制。
ai.method: 构建谱-半群框架，将演化表示为谱分量的指数重加权，引入非局部分数阶时间算子，形成平方根谱变形的压缩半群，并分析带宽分辨的不对称性。
ai.result: 指数衰减抑制高频分量，但在特定谱窗内逆变换可控，可实现低能及粗粒化动力学特征的稳定重构；非厄米与开放系统中不可逆性表现为谱的几何与尺度依赖特征。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解解析延拓作为结构化、尺度依赖的滤波过程，以及重构极限的量化描述。

idea_spark.idea: 利用分数阶时间算子的低通滤波特性，设计基于谱窗的自适应重构算法，以在噪声环境下稳定提取低能动力学信息。
idea_spark.risk: 谱窗的选择可能依赖先验知识，且非厄米系统的谱几何复杂性可能导致重构精度对参数敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将不可逆性视为谱几何与尺度依赖特征，启发在开放量子系统中利用谱结构设计可控的量子信息恢复协议。

zh.tldr: 提出了统一谱-半群框架，通过解析延拓连接实时与虚时量子动力学，揭示了逆重构的根本不稳定性。
zh.motivation: 实时间与虚时间量子动力学之间的解析延拓长期存在逆重构不稳定的根本问题，缺乏统一的理论框架来理解其内在机制。
zh.method: 构建谱-半群框架，将演化表示为谱分量的指数重加权，引入非局部分数阶时间算子，形成平方根谱变形的压缩半群，并分析带宽分辨的不对称性。
zh.result: 指数衰减抑制高频分量，但在特定谱窗内逆变换可控，可实现低能及粗粒化动力学特征的稳定重构；非厄米与开放系统中不可逆性表现为谱的几何与尺度依赖特征。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解解析延拓作为结构化、尺度依赖的滤波过程，以及重构极限的量化描述。

idea_spark.idea: 利用分数阶时间算子的低通滤波特性，设计基于谱窗的自适应重构算法，以在噪声环境下稳定提取低能动力学信息。
idea_spark.risk: 谱窗的选择可能依赖先验知识，且非厄米系统的谱几何复杂性可能导致重构精度对参数敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将不可逆性视为谱几何与尺度依赖特征，启发在开放量子系统中利用谱结构设计可控的量子信息恢复协议。

en.tldr: A unified spectral-semigroup framework connects real- and imaginary-time quantum dynamics via analytic continuation, revealing the fundamental instability of inverse reconstruction.
en.motivation: The analytic continuation between real- and imaginary-time quantum dynamics suffers from fundamental instability in inverse reconstruction, lacking a unified theoretical framework to understand its intrinsic mechanism.
en.method: A spectral-semigroup framework is developed, expressing evolution as exponential reweighting of spectral components, introducing a nonlocal fractional time operator that yields a contractive semigroup with square-root spectral deformation, and analyzing bandwidth-resolved asymmetry.
en.result: Exponential attenuation suppresses high-frequency components, but within a well-defined spectral window the inverse transformation is controllable, enabling stable reconstruction of low-energy and coarse-grained dynamics; irreversibility in non-Hermitian and open systems emerges as a geometry- and scale-dependent spectral feature.
en.help_to_user: Provides a structured, scale-dependent filtering perspective on analytic continuation and quantifiable reconstruction limits.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage the low-pass filtering property of the fractional time operator to design adaptive spectral-window-based reconstruction algorithms for stable extraction of low-energy dynamics under noise.
idea_spark.risk: Selection of the spectral window may rely on prior knowledge, and the spectral geometry complexity in non-Hermitian systems could make reconstruction accuracy sensitive to parameters.
idea_spark.inspiration: Viewing irreversibility as a geometry- and scale-dependent spectral feature inspires the design of controllable quantum information recovery protocols in open systems using spectral structure.

papers.title: Analysis of a three-dimensional fluid flow in rotating cylinders
papers.summary: Subject of consideration is the modelling and analysis of a capillary-driven three-dimensional rimming-flow problem. We present the derivation of a fourth-order quasilinear degenerate-parabolic partial differential equation for the height $h > 0$ of a fluid film coating the inner wall of a cylinder that rotates around a horizontal axis. The equation arises from a rescaled Navier-Stokes system for thin fluid films by means of a lubrication approximation and accounts for the physical effects of rotation, surface tension and gravity. The effect of the latter is measured by a non-dimensional parameter $0 \leq δ\ll 1$.   We characterise the structure of the steady states depending on the ratio $\ell$ of the cylinder length to its radius. In the absence of gravity ($δ=0$), in the case $\frac{\ell}π \notin \mathbb{Z}$, steady states are unique. For $0 < δ\ll 1$, steady states are shown to be locally unique for any $\ell$. These steady states are stable for $\ell < π$, while they are unstable for $\ell > π$.   Furthermore, in the absence of gravity, for all $\ell > 0$, we show that there exists a manifold of time-periodic solutions. In the critical case $\ell = π$, we study the dynamics of the solutions close to the manifold of periodic orbits in the critical case $\ell = π$ on the large time scale $τ= δ^2 t$. It turns out that in the time scale $τ$ this dynamics can be approximated by a system of ordinary differential equations.

ai.tldr: 本文研究旋转水平圆柱内壁毛细驱动薄膜流动的三维建模与分析，推导出四阶退化抛物型偏微分方程，并分析了稳态结构、稳定性及时间周期解。
ai.motivation: 理解旋转圆柱内薄液膜的动力学行为，特别是毛细力、旋转、表面张力和重力共同作用下的流动规律。
ai.method: 采用润滑近似从Navier-Stokes系统推导出薄膜高度的四阶退化抛物型方程，并通过参数分析研究稳态唯一性、稳定性及周期解流形。
ai.result: 无重力时稳态在ℓ/π非整数时唯一；弱重力下对任意ℓ局部唯一；ℓ<π时稳态稳定，ℓ>π时不稳定；无重力时存在时间周期解流形，临界ℓ=π时动力学由常微分方程组近似。
ai.help_to_user: 提供旋转圆柱内薄膜流动的严格数学分析框架，可用于涂层工艺、旋转机械润滑等工程问题的理论指导。

idea_spark.idea: 考虑引入非均匀旋转速度或表面张力梯度，研究其对稳态稳定性和周期解的影响。
idea_spark.risk: 模型假设薄膜很薄且忽略惯性项，可能不适用于高转速或厚膜情形。
idea_spark.inspiration: 临界长度ℓ=π处的动力学简化提示，类似的分岔现象可能出现在其他流体系统中，值得探索。

en.tldr: This paper models and analyzes a capillary-driven three-dimensional rimming flow on the inner wall of a rotating horizontal cylinder, deriving a fourth-order degenerate parabolic PDE and studying steady states, stability, and time-periodic solutions.
en.motivation: To understand the dynamics of thin liquid films inside rotating cylinders under the combined effects of capillarity, rotation, surface tension, and gravity.
en.method: A lubrication approximation reduces the Navier-Stokes system to a fourth-order degenerate parabolic PDE for film height; steady-state structure, stability, and periodic solution manifolds are analyzed via parameter ratios.
en.result: Without gravity, steady states are unique when ℓ/π is not an integer; with weak gravity, they are locally unique for any ℓ. Steady states are stable for ℓ<π and unstable for ℓ>π. Without gravity, a manifold of time-periodic solutions exists; at critical ℓ=π, dynamics on a slow time scale is approximated by ODEs.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous mathematical framework for thin-film flows in rotating cylinders, useful for coating processes and lubrication in rotating machinery.

idea_spark.idea: Investigate the effect of non-uniform rotation or surface tension gradients on steady-state stability and periodic solutions.
idea_spark.risk: The model assumes very thin films and neglects inertia, which may fail for high rotation speeds or thicker films.
idea_spark.inspiration: The critical length ℓ=π suggests a bifurcation point; similar phenomena may occur in other fluid systems and deserve exploration.

papers.title: Counting subgraphs in bounded-size Achlioptas processes
papers.summary: Achlioptas processes such as the Bohman--Frieze process are much harder to analyse than the classical Erdős--Rényi process, due to the dependence between edges added at different stages. This dependence means that most analysis so far is dynamic, often based on the differential equation method. In the Erdős--Rényi case there is an alternative static approach, pioneered by Erdős, Rényi and Bollobás, based on evaluating the expectation (and higher moments) of various subgraph counts, and using this to study the component structure. Here we show that this latter approach can be applied (with some complications) to the Bohman--Frieze process. For example, we are able to show that the expected number $μ_{k,t,n}$ of $k$-vertex tree components after $tn$ steps satisfies (essentially) $μ_{k,t,n}=c_{k,t}n(1+O(k/\sqrt{n}))$. Our method gives a very complicated formula for $c_{k,t}$, which seems to be unusable. However, since $c_{k,t}$ does not depend on $n$, we may use recent results obtained by the differential equation method and branching process analysis to find the asymptotics of $c_{k,t}$ as $k\to\infty$. The latter results also give a formula for $μ_{k,t,n}$ of the form $c_{k,t}n$ plus an error term, with a much more usable description of $c_{k,t}$ but a much worse error term. We combine the best of both worlds to prove a number of new results about the process near criticality. In particular, we obtain extremely sharp bounds on the size of the largest non-giant component near criticality, including the limiting distribution of its fluctuations.

ai.tldr: 本文展示了如何将Erdős–Rényi过程中的静态子图计数方法应用于Bohman–Frieze过程，从而在临界点附近获得关于最大非巨型组件大小的极紧界。
ai.motivation: Achlioptas过程（如Bohman–Frieze过程）由于不同阶段添加的边之间存在依赖关系，比经典Erdős–Rényi过程更难分析。现有分析多为动态方法（如微分方程法），而静态子图计数方法在Erdős–Rényi情况下非常成功，本文旨在将后者推广到Bohman–Frieze过程。
ai.method: 通过评估各种子图计数的期望和高阶矩，推导出k顶点树组件数量的期望表达式，并结合微分方程法和分支过程分析的结果，得到渐近行为。
ai.result: 得到了临界点附近最大非巨型组件大小的极紧界，包括其波动的极限分布。
ai.help_to_user: 为分析具有依赖性的随机图过程提供了一种新的静态分析框架，有助于理解临界行为。

idea_spark.idea: 结合静态期望计算和动态渐近分析，可以同时获得精确的常数项和紧的误差界。
idea_spark.risk: 常数项c_{k,t}的表达式可能非常复杂，难以直接使用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用不同方法的互补优势（一个提供精确常数但误差大，另一个提供简单描述但误差小）来获得最佳结果。

en.tldr: This paper shows how to apply the static subgraph counting method from Erdős–Rényi processes to Bohman–Frieze processes, yielding extremely sharp bounds on the size of the largest non-giant component near criticality.
en.motivation: Achlioptas processes (e.g., Bohman–Frieze) are harder to analyze than classical Erdős–Rényi processes due to edge dependencies across stages. Existing analyses are mostly dynamic (e.g., differential equation method), while the static subgraph counting approach is successful for Erdős–Rényi. This work extends that approach to Bohman–Frieze processes.
en.method: Evaluates expectations and higher moments of various subgraph counts, derives the expected number of k-vertex tree components, and combines results from differential equation method and branching process analysis to obtain asymptotics.
en.result: Obtains extremely sharp bounds on the size of the largest non-giant component near criticality, including the limiting distribution of its fluctuations.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new static analysis framework for random graph processes with dependencies, aiding in understanding critical behavior.

idea_spark.idea: Combining static expectation calculations with dynamic asymptotic analysis yields both precise constants and tight error bounds.
idea_spark.risk: The constant c_{k,t} may have a very complicated formula that is hard to use directly.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging complementary strengths of different methods (one gives exact constants but large error, the other gives simple description but small error) to achieve the best results.

papers.title: Computation of Set Tolerances with Applications to the Minimum Spanning Tree Problem
papers.summary: The regular set tolerance is an important term in sensitivity analysis. For combinatorial sum problems, e.g., the Traveling Salesman Problem, Shortest Path Problem and Minimum Spanning Tree Problem, it determines how much the sum of the costs of the elements of a set can be increased while ensuring that all current optimal solutions remain optimal. The regular set lower tolerance determines how much the sum of the costs of the elements of a set can be decreased while ensuring that the objective value of the optimal solution is not changed. We investigate a general method for computing regular (upper and lower) set tolerances in combinatorial sum problems. For the upper tolerance, we present a linear programming approach, and for the lower tolerances, three linear programming approaches, where the last two are novel and lead to recursive procedures for computation of the lower tolerances of all subsets of the given ground set. Furthermore, we give new upper bounds for set lower tolerances. For both upper and lower tolerances, we give an exact formula for sets of cardinality 2 and 3. Finally, we consider the computation of tolerances for the Minimum Spanning Tree Problem, give a formula for single tolerances, a lower bound for regular set upper tolerances and an exact formula for regular set lower tolerances.

ai.tldr: 本文研究组合和问题中正则集合容差的通用计算方法，并应用于最小生成树问题。
ai.motivation: 正则集合容差是灵敏度分析中的重要概念，但现有计算方法有限，需要更通用的方法。
ai.method: 提出线性规划方法计算上容差，以及三种线性规划方法计算下容差，其中后两种是新颖的递归方法。
ai.result: 给出了集合下容差的新上界，以及基数为2和3的集合的精确公式；针对最小生成树问题给出了单容差公式、正则集合上容差的下界和正则集合下容差的精确公式。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解组合优化问题中解对成本变化的敏感程度，并提供了计算容差的具体方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将集合容差概念与机器学习中的特征重要性或模型鲁棒性分析相结合，评估特征集变化对模型输出的影响。
idea_spark.risk: 递归方法在大规模集合上可能计算复杂度高，需要优化或近似策略。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从集合容差的精确公式出发，启发设计更高效的近似算法或启发式方法用于实际应用。

en.tldr: This paper investigates a general method for computing regular set tolerances in combinatorial sum problems and applies it to the Minimum Spanning Tree Problem.
en.motivation: Regular set tolerance is important in sensitivity analysis, but existing computation methods are limited, necessitating a more general approach.
en.method: Proposes a linear programming approach for upper tolerances and three linear programming approaches for lower tolerances, with the latter two being novel recursive procedures.
en.result: Provides new upper bounds for set lower tolerances, exact formulas for sets of cardinality 2 and 3, and specific formulas for the Minimum Spanning Tree Problem including single tolerances, a lower bound for regular set upper tolerances, and an exact formula for regular set lower tolerances.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the sensitivity of optimal solutions to cost changes in combinatorial optimization problems and provides concrete methods for computing tolerances.

idea_spark.idea: Combine the concept of set tolerances with feature importance or model robustness analysis in machine learning to evaluate the impact of feature set changes on model output.
idea_spark.risk: Recursive methods may have high computational complexity for large sets, requiring optimization or approximation strategies.
idea_spark.inspiration: The exact formulas for set tolerances inspire the design of more efficient approximation algorithms or heuristics for practical applications.

papers.title: PDEAgent-Bench: A Multi-Metric, Multi-Library Benchmark for PDE Solver Generation
papers.summary: PDE-to-solver code generation aims to automatically synthesize executable numerical solvers from partial differential equation (PDE) specifications. This task requires not only understanding the mathematical structure of PDEs, but also selecting appropriate discretization schemes and solver configurations, and correctly implementing the resulting formulations in finite-element method (FEM) libraries. Existing code generation benchmarks mainly evaluate syntactic correctness, or success on predefined test cases. To our knowledge, there is currently no publicly available benchmark specifically for PDE-to-solver code generation, and general-purpose code benchmarks do not fully capture the unique challenges of numerical PDE solution, such as ensuring solver accuracy, efficiency, and compatibility with professional FEM libraries. We introduce PDEAgent-Bench, to the best of our knowledge, the first multi-metric, multi-library benchmark for PDE-to-solver code generation. PDEAgent-Bench contains 645 instances across 6 mathematical categories and 11 PDE families, with common FEM libraries for DOLFINx, Firedrake, and deal.II. Each instance provides an agent-facing problem specification, a reference solution on a prescribed evaluation grid, and case-specific accuracy and runtime targets. PDEAgent-Bench adopts a staged evaluation framework in which generated solvers must sequentially pass executability, numerical accuracy, and computational efficiency checks. Experiments with representative LLMs and code agents show that models can often produce runnable code, but their pass rate drops substantially once accuracy and efficiency requirements are enforced. These results indicate that current agents remain limited in producing numerically reliable and efficient PDE solvers, and that PDEAgent-Bench provides a reproducible testbed grounded in the practical requirements of numerical PDE solving.

ai.tldr: 提出了首个多指标、多库的偏微分方程求解器代码生成基准PDEAgent-Bench，包含645个实例，覆盖6个数学类别和11个PDE族，使用DOLFINx、Firedrake和deal.II库。
ai.motivation: 现有代码生成基准主要评估语法正确性或预定义测试用例，缺乏专门针对PDE求解器代码生成的基准，无法全面捕捉数值求解的独特挑战（如精度、效率和库兼容性）。
ai.method: 构建包含645个实例的基准，每个实例提供面向智能体的问题描述、参考解、精度和运行时间目标，采用分阶段评估框架：可执行性、数值精度和计算效率。
ai.result: 代表性LLM和代码智能体常能生成可运行代码，但加入精度和效率要求后通过率大幅下降，表明当前智能体在生成数值可靠且高效的PDE求解器方面仍有限。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员系统评估和提升LLM在PDE求解器代码生成方面的能力，提供可复现的测试平台。

idea_spark.idea: 结合物理信息神经网络（PINN）与传统FEM库，生成混合求解器代码，兼顾精度和效率。
idea_spark.risk: 多库兼容性可能增加代码复杂度，导致生成失败率上升；精度目标设定需谨慎以避免过拟合。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从基准中识别常见失败模式（如离散化方案选择错误），设计针对性微调数据或提示策略。

idea_spark.idea: 结合物理信息神经网络（PINN）与传统FEM库，生成混合求解器代码，兼顾精度和效率。
idea_spark.risk: 多库兼容性可能增加代码复杂度，导致生成失败率上升；精度目标设定需谨慎以避免过拟合。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从基准中识别常见失败模式（如离散化方案选择错误），设计针对性微调数据或提示策略。

en.tldr: Proposes PDEAgent-Bench, the first multi-metric, multi-library benchmark for PDE solver code generation, with 645 instances across 6 mathematical categories and 11 PDE families, using DOLFINx, Firedrake, and deal.II.
en.motivation: Existing code generation benchmarks mainly evaluate syntactic correctness or predefined test cases, lacking a dedicated benchmark for PDE solver generation that captures unique challenges like accuracy, efficiency, and library compatibility.
en.method: Constructs a benchmark of 645 instances, each with an agent-facing problem specification, reference solution, accuracy and runtime targets, using a staged evaluation framework: executability, numerical accuracy, and computational efficiency.
en.result: Representative LLMs and code agents often produce runnable code, but pass rates drop substantially when accuracy and efficiency requirements are enforced, indicating current agents are limited in generating numerically reliable and efficient PDE solvers.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers systematically evaluate and improve LLM capabilities in PDE solver code generation, providing a reproducible testbed.

idea_spark.idea: Combine physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) with traditional FEM libraries to generate hybrid solver code that balances accuracy and efficiency.
idea_spark.risk: Multi-library compatibility may increase code complexity, leading to higher generation failure rates; accuracy targets need careful setting to avoid overfitting.
idea_spark.inspiration: Identify common failure modes from the benchmark (e.g., discretization scheme selection errors) and design targeted fine-tuning data or prompting strategies.

papers.title: A Stochastic Hybrid Automaton for Smartphone Battery Dynamics: Electro-Thermal Coupling and First-Passage Time-to-Empty Estimation
papers.summary: Smartphone time-to-empty (TTE) is difficult to predict because shutdown is governed not only by remaining charge, but also by instantaneous power capability under temperature-, aging-, and load-dependent voltage sag. We develop a stochastic hybrid automaton for smartphone battery dynamics that couples a first-order Thevenin equivalent-circuit model with a lumped thermal model and a stochastic user-activity process. The continuous state includes state of charge, polarization voltage, and battery temperature; user behavior is represented as a piecewise deterministic Markov process switching among idle, social/web, video, gaming, and weak-signal modes. Shutdown is formulated as a first-passage event when terminal voltage crosses a cutoff threshold or when requested power exceeds the instantaneous feasibility envelope.   The model captures a voltage-collapse mechanism that simple Coulomb-counting or linear discharge models miss: cold temperature or battery aging increases internal resistance, so high-power bursts can drive terminal voltage below cutoff even when substantial charge remains. Monte Carlo simulation yields a full TTE distribution rather than a single countdown, allowing lower-tail risk to be quantified by the 5th percentile. Sensitivity analysis identifies ambient temperature, internal resistance, weak-signal radio penalty, and screen brightness as major drivers of premature shutdown risk. These results motivate practical user guidance and an operating-system-level resistance-aware throttling policy that limits peak power in the power-limited regime. The framework provides a physically grounded, risk-aware approach for explaining and extending usable smartphone battery life under real-world uncertainty.

ai.tldr: 提出一种随机混合自动机模型，耦合电热特性和随机用户行为，用于预测智能手机电池的首次通过时间到空（TTE），并量化低温或老化导致的电压崩溃风险。
ai.motivation: 传统库仑计数或线性放电模型无法捕捉温度、老化、负载变化导致的电压崩溃，导致TTE预测不准确。
ai.method: 构建随机混合自动机，包含一阶Thevenin等效电路模型、集总热模型和随机用户活动过程（空闲、社交/网页、视频、游戏、弱信号模式），将关机定义为终端电压低于截止阈值或请求功率超过瞬时可行包络的首次通过事件。
ai.result: 蒙特卡洛模拟得到完整TTE分布，5%分位数量化低尾风险；敏感性分析识别环境温度、内阻、弱信号无线电损耗和屏幕亮度为主要风险因素。
ai.help_to_user: 提供基于物理的、风险感知的TTE预测，指导用户行为（如避免低温高功率使用）并支持操作系统级电阻感知限流策略，延长实际可用电池寿命。

idea_spark.idea: 结合实时传感器数据（温度、电流、用户活动）在线更新模型参数，实现动态TTE预测和自适应功率管理。
idea_spark.risk: 模型复杂度较高，实时部署可能面临计算资源限制；用户行为随机性建模的准确性依赖大量数据。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将电池寿命预测从确定性模型转向随机过程，并引入首次通过时间概念，为不确定性下的系统可靠性评估提供新思路。

en.tldr: A stochastic hybrid automaton coupling electro-thermal dynamics and stochastic user activity is proposed to predict smartphone battery time-to-empty (TTE), capturing voltage collapse due to cold temperature or aging.
en.motivation: Simple Coulomb-counting or linear discharge models fail to capture voltage collapse caused by temperature, aging, and load variations, leading to inaccurate TTE predictions.
en.method: A stochastic hybrid automaton is developed, integrating a first-order Thevenin equivalent-circuit model, a lumped thermal model, and a stochastic user-activity process (idle, social/web, video, gaming, weak-signal modes). Shutdown is defined as a first-passage event when terminal voltage drops below cutoff or requested power exceeds the instantaneous feasibility envelope.
en.result: Monte Carlo simulation yields a full TTE distribution, with the 5th percentile quantifying lower-tail risk. Sensitivity analysis identifies ambient temperature, internal resistance, weak-signal radio penalty, and screen brightness as major drivers of premature shutdown.
en.help_to_user: Provides physically grounded, risk-aware TTE predictions to guide user behavior (e.g., avoiding high-power usage in cold conditions) and supports OS-level resistance-aware throttling to extend usable battery life.

idea_spark.idea: Combine real-time sensor data (temperature, current, user activity) to update model parameters online for dynamic TTE prediction and adaptive power management.
idea_spark.risk: Model complexity may limit real-time deployment due to computational constraints; accuracy of stochastic user activity modeling relies on extensive data.
idea_spark.inspiration: Shifting battery life prediction from deterministic models to stochastic processes with first-passage time concepts offers a new approach for reliability assessment under uncertainty.

papers.title: An optimal control problem for Stokes-Cahn-Hilliard-Oono equations with regular potential
papers.summary: This article discusses an optimal control problem for a phase field model of two immiscible incompressible fluid flow, incorporating surface tension effects. The optimal control problem is defined with a $L^2$-cost functional and subject to the constraints governed by a system of coupled Stokes-Cahn-Hilliard-Oono equations. In this model, fluids are separated by a dynamic diffuse interface of finite width. We investigate the optimality condition of a given control. Initially, we establish the existence of an optimal solution for the coupled optimal control problem. Subsequently, we derive the optimality condition with respect to the corresponding adjoint system.

ai.tldr: 本文研究了不可混溶不可压缩两相流相场模型的最优控制问题，其中包含表面张力效应，并基于Stokes-Cahn-Hilliard-Oono方程组建立了最优性条件。
ai.motivation: 为具有扩散界面的两相流系统提供数学上严格的最优控制框架。
ai.method: 定义L2代价泛函，证明最优解存在，并通过伴随系统推导最优性条件。
ai.result: 建立了耦合最优控制问题的最优解存在性及伴随系统形式的最优性条件。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解相场流体控制问题的数学理论，特别是最优性条件的推导。

idea_spark.idea: 将最优控制与相场模型结合，可设计界面追踪或流体混合的精确控制策略。
idea_spark.risk: 伴随系统求解可能计算成本高，且正则势假设可能限制实际应用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将类似最优控制框架应用于多相流或复杂流体系统的工程优化。

zh.tldr: 本文研究了不可混溶不可压缩两相流相场模型的最优控制问题，其中包含表面张力效应，并基于Stokes-Cahn-Hilliard-Oono方程组建立了最优性条件。

idea_spark.risk: 伴随系统求解可能计算成本高，且正则势假设可能限制实际应用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将类似最优控制框架应用于多相流或复杂流体系统的工程优化。

en.tldr: This paper studies an optimal control problem for a phase field model of two immiscible incompressible fluid flows with surface tension, governed by Stokes-Cahn-Hilliard-Oono equations, and derives optimality conditions.
en.motivation: To provide a mathematically rigorous optimal control framework for two-phase flow systems with diffuse interfaces.
en.method: Define an L2-cost functional, prove existence of an optimal solution, and derive optimality conditions via the adjoint system.
en.result: Established existence of an optimal solution and optimality conditions in terms of the adjoint system for the coupled control problem.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the mathematical theory of optimal control for phase-field fluid problems, especially derivation of optimality conditions.

idea_spark.idea: Combining optimal control with phase field models enables precise control strategies for interface tracking or fluid mixing.
idea_spark.risk: Solving the adjoint system may be computationally expensive, and the regular potential assumption may limit practical applications.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires applying similar optimal control frameworks to engineering optimization of multiphase or complex fluid systems.

papers.title: Probability-Flow Distillation: Exact Wasserstein Gradient Flow for High-Fidelity 3D Generation
papers.summary: Score Distillation Sampling (SDS) and its variants have been widely used for text-to-3D generation by distilling 2D image diffusion priors. However, the standard SDS objective is prone to severe mode collapse, frequently yielding over-smoothed and over-saturated results. Although recent advancements, such as Score Distillation via Inversion (SDI), mitigate these artifacts and produce visually sharper models, they ultimately fail to faithfully capture the full target distribution. In this work, we show that the bottleneck limiting the sampling capacity of SDI stems from its reliance on the posterior mean estimator, which is mathematically equivalent to a single-step Euler approximation of the deterministic reverse DDIM trajectory. To address this, we propose a naturally motivated extension termed Probability-Flow Distillation (PFD). We establish that PFD corresponds exactly to a Wasserstein gradient flow, thereby inducing principled distribution-matching dynamics. Finally, we show that PFD can synthesize 3D assets with fine-grained, high-fidelity details and achieve improved quality compared to existing methods.

ai.tldr: 提出概率流蒸馏（PFD）方法，解决分数蒸馏采样（SDS）的模式崩溃问题，实现高保真3D生成。
ai.motivation: 标准SDS目标易导致模式崩溃，产生过平滑和过饱和结果；现有改进方法（如SDI）虽能缓解伪影，但无法忠实捕捉完整目标分布。
ai.method: 将PFD建立为精确的Wasserstein梯度流，从而引入有原则的分布匹配动力学。
ai.result: PFD能合成具有精细高保真细节的3D资产，质量优于现有方法。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过分布匹配改进3D生成质量，避免模式崩溃。

idea_spark.idea: 利用确定性逆DDIM轨迹的欧拉近似作为瓶颈，提出基于概率流的精确梯度流替代方案。
idea_spark.risk: 计算复杂度可能较高，且对初始分布选择敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从SDI的后验均值估计等价于单步欧拉近似这一观察出发，启发更精确的分布匹配方法。

zh.tldr: 提出概率流蒸馏（PFD）方法，解决分数蒸馏采样（SDS）的模式崩溃问题，实现高保真3D生成。
zh.motivation: 标准SDS目标易导致模式崩溃，产生过平滑和过饱和结果；现有改进方法（如SDI）虽能缓解伪影，但无法忠实捕捉完整目标分布。

idea_spark.idea: 利用确定性逆DDIM轨迹的欧拉近似作为瓶颈，提出基于概率流的精确梯度流替代方案。
idea_spark.risk: 计算复杂度可能较高，且对初始分布选择敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从SDI的后验均值估计等价于单步欧拉近似这一观察出发，启发更精确的分布匹配方法。

en.tldr: Proposes Probability-Flow Distillation (PFD) to address mode collapse in Score Distillation Sampling, achieving high-fidelity 3D generation.
en.motivation: Standard SDS objective suffers from mode collapse, yielding over-smoothed and over-saturated results; recent improvements like SDI mitigate artifacts but fail to capture the full target distribution.
en.method: Establishes PFD as an exact Wasserstein gradient flow, inducing principled distribution-matching dynamics.
en.result: PFD synthesizes 3D assets with fine-grained, high-fidelity details and achieves improved quality over existing methods.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how distribution matching improves 3D generation quality and avoids mode collapse.

idea_spark.idea: Identifies the bottleneck as the single-step Euler approximation of deterministic reverse DDIM trajectory, and proposes an exact gradient flow alternative via probability flow.
idea_spark.risk: May have high computational complexity and sensitivity to initial distribution.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the observation that SDI's posterior mean estimator is equivalent to a single-step Euler approximation, motivating a more accurate distribution matching method.

papers.title: RigidFormer: Learning Rigid Dynamics using Transformers
papers.summary: Learning-based simulation of multi-object rigid-body dynamics remains difficult because contact is discontinuous and errors compound over long horizons. Most existing methods remain tied to mesh connectivity and vertex-level message passing, which limits their applicability to mesh-free inputs such as point clouds and leads to high computational cost. Efficiently modeling high-fidelity rigid-body dynamics from mesh-free representations, therefore, remains challenging. We introduce RigidFormer, an object-centric Transformer-based model that learns mesh-free rigid-body dynamics with controllable integration step sizes. RigidFormer reasons at the object level and advances each object through compact anchors; Anchor-Vertex Pooling enriches these anchors with local vertex features, retaining contact-relevant geometry without dense vertex-level interaction. We propose Anchor-based RoPE to inject anchor geometry into attention while respecting the unordered nature of objects and anchors: object-token processing is permutation-equivariant, and the mean-pooled anchor descriptor is invariant to anchor reindexing while preserving shape extent. RigidFormer further enforces rigidity by projecting updates onto the rigid-body manifold using differentiable Kabsch alignment. On standard benchmarks, RigidFormer outperforms or matches mesh-based baselines using point inputs, runs faster, generalizes to unseen point resolutions and across datasets, and scales to 200+ objects; we also show a preliminary extension to command-conditioned articulated bodies by treating body parts as interacting object-level components.

ai.tldr: 提出RigidFormer，一种基于Transformer的物体中心模型，从点云等无网格输入高效学习多物体刚体动力学。
ai.motivation: 现有基于网格的刚体模拟方法依赖顶点级消息传递，计算成本高且无法处理点云等无网格输入。
ai.method: 采用物体级锚点表示，通过Anchor-Vertex池化融合局部几何特征，利用Anchor-based RoPE保持置换等变性，并通过可微Kabsch对齐强制刚体约束。
ai.result: 在标准基准上超越或匹配基于网格的基线，速度更快，泛化到未见点分辨率及跨数据集，可扩展至200+物体。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种高效、可扩展的无网格刚体动力学模拟方案，适用于点云输入和长时程预测。

idea_spark.idea: 将刚体动力学学习分解为锚点几何编码与刚体流形投影，避免密集顶点交互。
idea_spark.risk: 对高度非凸或复杂接触场景，锚点表示可能丢失局部细节。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用Transformer的置换等变性处理无序物体集合，结合可微优化保证物理一致性。

zh.motivation: 现有基于网格的刚体模拟方法依赖顶点级消息传递，计算成本高且无法处理点云等无网格输入。

zh.result: 在标准基准上超越或匹配基于网格的基线，速度更快，泛化到未见点分辨率及跨数据集，可扩展至200+物体。
zh.help_to_user: 提供一种高效、可扩展的无网格刚体动力学模拟方案，适用于点云输入和长时程预测。

idea_spark.idea: 将刚体动力学学习分解为锚点几何编码与刚体流形投影，避免密集顶点交互。
idea_spark.risk: 对高度非凸或复杂接触场景，锚点表示可能丢失局部细节。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用Transformer的置换等变性处理无序物体集合，结合可微优化保证物理一致性。

en.tldr: RigidFormer is an object-centric Transformer model that learns mesh-free rigid-body dynamics from point clouds with controllable step sizes.
en.motivation: Existing mesh-based methods rely on vertex-level message passing, which is computationally expensive and incompatible with mesh-free inputs like point clouds.
en.method: It uses object-level anchors enriched by Anchor-Vertex Pooling, Anchor-based RoPE for permutation-equivariant attention, and differentiable Kabsch alignment to enforce rigidity.
en.result: Outperforms or matches mesh-based baselines on standard benchmarks, runs faster, generalizes to unseen point resolutions and across datasets, and scales to 200+ objects.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient and scalable mesh-free approach for rigid-body dynamics simulation, suitable for point cloud inputs and long-horizon prediction.

idea_spark.idea: Decompose rigid-body dynamics learning into anchor geometry encoding and rigid manifold projection, avoiding dense vertex interactions.
idea_spark.risk: Anchor representation may lose local details for highly non-convex or complex contact scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage Transformer's permutation equivariance to handle unordered object sets, combined with differentiable optimization for physical consistency.

papers.title: Stochastic curve shortening flow driven by a transport-type pure jump Lévy noise
papers.summary: We study the existence and uniqueness, the regularity, and the long-time behavior of strong solutions to stochastic curve shortening flow driven by a transport-type pure jump Lévy noise. To obtain the existence and uniqueness of strong solutions, we transform the equation into its equivalent Itô-type stochastic partial differential equation via a transport equation, and apply the monotone method with Lyapunov-type conditions. The obstacles to investigate the long-time behavior are the weak dissipativity and singularity inherent in the equation. To this end, we establish an improved regularity and prove that these solutions converge pathwise to zero at an exponential rate.

ai.tldr: 研究由传输型纯跳跃Lévy噪声驱动的随机曲线缩短流的强解存在唯一性、正则性及长时间行为。
ai.motivation: 探索随机曲线缩短流在非高斯噪声驱动下的数学性质，填补该领域理论空白。
ai.method: 通过传输方程将原方程转化为等价的Itô型随机偏微分方程，并利用单调方法与Lyapunov型条件证明强解存在唯一性；通过改进正则性证明解以指数速率路径收敛到零。
ai.result: 证明了强解的存在唯一性、正则性以及指数速率路径收敛到零。
ai.help_to_user: 为随机曲线缩短流在非高斯噪声下的理论分析提供严格数学框架。

idea_spark.idea: 考虑将传输型噪声替换为其他非高斯噪声类型，或研究解在有限时间内的爆破行为。
idea_spark.risk: 弱耗散性和奇异性可能使长时间行为分析复杂化，需更精细的正则性估计。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用传输方程转化技巧将非Itô型噪声转化为Itô型，为处理类似噪声提供新思路。

zh.tldr: 研究由传输型纯跳跃Lévy噪声驱动的随机曲线缩短流的强解存在唯一性、正则性及长时间行为。
zh.motivation: 探索随机曲线缩短流在非高斯噪声驱动下的数学性质，填补该领域理论空白。
zh.method: 通过传输方程将原方程转化为等价的Itô型随机偏微分方程，并利用单调方法与Lyapunov型条件证明强解存在唯一性；通过改进正则性证明解以指数速率路径收敛到零。
zh.result: 证明了强解的存在唯一性、正则性以及指数速率路径收敛到零。
zh.help_to_user: 为随机曲线缩短流在非高斯噪声下的理论分析提供严格数学框架。

idea_spark.idea: 考虑将传输型噪声替换为其他非高斯噪声类型，或研究解在有限时间内的爆破行为。
idea_spark.risk: 弱耗散性和奇异性可能使长时间行为分析复杂化，需更精细的正则性估计。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用传输方程转化技巧将非Itô型噪声转化为Itô型，为处理类似噪声提供新思路。

en.tldr: We study existence, uniqueness, regularity, and long-time behavior of strong solutions to stochastic curve shortening flow driven by transport-type pure jump Lévy noise.
en.motivation: To fill theoretical gaps in stochastic geometric flows under non-Gaussian noise.
en.method: Transform the equation into an equivalent Itô-type SPDE via a transport equation, then apply monotone method with Lyapunov-type conditions; improve regularity to prove pathwise exponential convergence.
en.result: Proved existence, uniqueness, regularity, and pathwise exponential convergence to zero of strong solutions.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous mathematical framework for stochastic curve shortening flow under non-Gaussian noise.

idea_spark.idea: Consider replacing transport-type noise with other non-Gaussian noises, or study finite-time blow-up behavior.
idea_spark.risk: Weak dissipativity and singularity may complicate long-time analysis, requiring finer regularity estimates.
idea_spark.inspiration: The transport equation transformation technique converts non-Itô noise into Itô type, offering a new approach for similar noises.

keywords_raw: transport-type pure jump l vy noise

papers.title: Multiplayer parallel repetition without dependency-breaking and anchoring variables: monotonic, concave amplification
papers.summary: We obtain quantitative estimates on the decay of the multiplayer optimal value under parallel repetition. In comparison to a previous work of the author in 2025 (arXiv: 2508.09380) which sought to generalize dependency-breaking and anchoring variables from two-player Quantum games, being able to establish quantitative estimates on the decay of the optimal value of a multiplayer game under parallel repetition is of interest to establish under different assumptions. Specifically, independently of the dependency-breaking and anchoring variables that have previously been employed to remove correlations from entangled information shared between Alice and Bob (hence removing dependencies), monotonic concave functions can be used in place of such variables to obtain rates of decay on the optimal value. The game-theoretic setting with two players was first analyzed with monotonic concave functions by Lanzenberger and Maurer. For $q_i , x_i > 0$ $\forall 1 \leq i \leq N$ where $N > 0 $ is the total number of players we adddress an open question raised in their work regarding potential generalizations of two-player monotonic concave functions, through amplification functions of the form $Ψ_{\textit{Mult}} \equiv Ψ= N - \underset{1 \leq i \leq N}{\prod} \mathrm{exp} \big[ - q_i x_i \big]$, which in the multiplayer game-theoretic setting have more intricate combinatorial structures.

ai.tldr: 本文研究了多人博弈在并行重复下最优值的衰减，提出使用单调凹放大函数替代依赖打破和锚定变量，并推广了两人情形的结果。
ai.motivation: 建立多人博弈在并行重复下最优值衰减的定量估计，并推广两人博弈中单调凹函数的方法。
ai.method: 引入形如 Ψ = N - ∏ exp(-q_i x_i) 的放大函数，利用单调凹函数的性质分析多人博弈的复杂组合结构。
ai.result: 获得了多人博弈最优值在并行重复下衰减的定量估计，无需依赖打破和锚定变量。
ai.help_to_user: 为多人博弈的并行重复分析提供了一种不依赖特殊变量的通用方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将单调凹函数用于分析多人博弈中信息相关性的衰减。
idea_spark.risk: 放大函数的具体形式可能对博弈结构敏感，通用性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从两人博弈的单调凹函数推广到多人情形，启发其他多体系统的衰减分析。

en.tldr: This paper studies the decay of the optimal value in multiplayer games under parallel repetition, using monotonic concave amplification functions instead of dependency-breaking and anchoring variables, generalizing the two-player case.
en.motivation: To obtain quantitative estimates on the decay of the multiplayer optimal value under parallel repetition and to generalize the monotonic concave function approach from two-player games.
en.method: Introduces amplification functions of the form Ψ = N - ∏ exp(-q_i x_i) and leverages properties of monotonic concave functions to handle the intricate combinatorial structure of multiplayer games.
en.result: Quantitative decay estimates for the optimal value of multiplayer games under parallel repetition are obtained without relying on dependency-breaking or anchoring variables.
en.help_to_user: Provides a general method for analyzing parallel repetition in multiplayer games that does not require special variables.

idea_spark.idea: Use monotonic concave functions to analyze the decay of information correlations in multiplayer games.
idea_spark.risk: The specific form of the amplification function may be sensitive to the game structure, requiring further validation of generality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Generalizing two-player monotonic concave functions to multiplayer settings inspires decay analysis in other many-body systems.

papers.title: Reinforcement learning for inverse structural design and rapid laser cutting of kirigami prototypes
papers.summary: Kirigami is an increasingly useful fabrication method to produce shape-programmable metamaterial structures. However, inverse design remains difficult because deployment is nonlinear, and feasible cut layouts must satisfy discrete compatibility rules, avoid overlap, and map one target shape to valid designs. We present RL-Kirigami, an inverse design framework that combines optimal-transport conditional flow matching (OT-CFM) with reinforcement learning to generate compatible ratio fields for compact reconfigurable parallelogram quad kirigami. A marching decoder enforces global geometric compatibility, and Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) aligns the generator with nondifferentiable rewards for silhouette matching, feasibility, and ratio-field regularity. Across procedurally generated target shape instances, a single sample from the pretrained OT-CFM prior reached $94.2%$ sIoU and outperformed solver baselines while reducing forward simulator evaluations from hundreds to 1. GRPO improved accuracy to $94.91%$ sIoU and, with regularity included, reduced $\mathrm{TV}(\mathbf{x})$ from 0.95 to 0.81 while maintaining $94.83%$ sIoU. Generated layouts were exported to DXF and laser-cut in $50~μ\mathrm{m}$ polymeric sheets to produce deployable prototypes in $8.0 \pm 1.0$ minutes per part. These results support a manufacturing-aware inverse design workflow for deployable kirigami metamaterials under hard geometric feasibility constraints.

ai.tldr: 提出RL-Kirigami框架，结合最优传输条件流匹配与强化学习，实现可重构平行四边形四边折纸结构的逆向设计，并快速激光切割原型。
ai.motivation: 折纸结构逆向设计困难，因展开非线性、切割布局需满足离散兼容性规则且避免重叠。
ai.method: 使用最优传输条件流匹配生成比率场，通过行进解码器保证几何兼容性，并用组相对策略优化对齐非可微奖励。
ai.result: 单样本达到94.2% sIoU，GRPO提升至94.91% sIoU，正则化后TV从0.95降至0.81，每件激光切割约8分钟。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种制造感知的逆向设计流程，可快速生成可部署的折纸超材料原型。

idea_spark.idea: 将强化学习与生成模型结合，处理非可微约束，实现高效逆向设计。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖预训练生成模型，对新形状泛化能力可能有限；激光切割精度影响原型性能。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受折纸艺术启发，将数学兼容性规则与制造约束融入设计流程。

en.tldr: Proposes RL-Kirigami, combining optimal-transport conditional flow matching with reinforcement learning for inverse design of reconfigurable parallelogram quad kirigami, enabling rapid laser-cut prototypes.
en.motivation: Inverse design of kirigami is difficult due to nonlinear deployment, discrete compatibility rules, and overlap avoidance.
en.method: Uses OT-CFM to generate ratio fields, a marching decoder for geometric compatibility, and GRPO to align with non-differentiable rewards.
en.result: Single sample achieves 94.2% sIoU; GRPO improves to 94.91% sIoU; with regularization, TV drops from 0.95 to 0.81; laser cutting takes ~8 minutes per part.
en.help_to_user: Provides a manufacturing-aware inverse design workflow for rapidly producing deployable kirigami metamaterial prototypes.

idea_spark.idea: Combine reinforcement learning with generative models to handle non-differentiable constraints for efficient inverse design.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on pretrained generative models; generalization to novel shapes may be limited; laser cutting precision affects prototype performance.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by kirigami art, integrating mathematical compatibility rules and manufacturing constraints into the design pipeline.

papers.title: Local Private Information Retrieval: A New Privacy Perspective for Graph-Based Replicated Systems
papers.summary: We rethink the definition of privacy in multi-server, graph-replicated private information retrieval (PIR) systems, and introduce a novel setting where the user's privacy is governed by the servers' storage structure. In particular, while retrieving a message from a server, the user is concerned with hiding their desired message index from the server, only if the server stores the corresponding message. We coin this privacy requirement as local user privacy and the resulting PIR problem as local PIR on the graph. Our goal is to measure the gain in communication efficiency of local PIR, compared to that of canonical PIR, by establishing its capacity, i.e., the maximum number of message symbols retrieved, per downloaded symbol. To this end, we observe a remarkable gain in the local PIR capacity of graphs, that are disjoint union of distinct graphs, which is multiplicative, compared to the PIR capacity, when the individual graphs are identical. For connected graphs, we propose schemes to establish capacity lower bounds for edge-transitive and bipartite graphs, which are greater than the best-known PIR capacity bounds. Finally, we derive the exact local PIR capacity for the cyclic graph, and the path graph with an odd number of vertices.

ai.tldr: 提出了一种基于图复制系统的本地隐私信息检索（PIR）新定义，用户仅对存储目标消息的服务器隐藏索引，并分析了该设置下的通信效率增益。
ai.motivation: 传统PIR假设用户对所有服务器隐藏查询索引，但在图复制系统中，服务器仅存储部分消息，因此需要更贴合实际存储结构的隐私定义。
ai.method: 定义本地用户隐私和本地PIR问题，通过容量（每下载符号可检索的消息符号数）衡量通信效率，并针对不同图结构（如不相交并图、边传递图、二分图、循环图、路径图）推导容量上下界。
ai.result: 不相交并图的本地PIR容量相比传统PIR有乘法增益；对连通图（边传递图、二分图）提出方案得到优于已知PIR容量下界的下界；精确推导了循环图和奇数顶点路径图的本地PIR容量。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解在图复制分布式系统中如何设计更高效的隐私保护查询协议，以及不同图结构对通信开销的影响。

idea_spark.idea: 将图结构视为隐私边界，设计自适应查询策略，使服务器仅在其存储范围内承担隐私保护责任，从而降低整体通信开销。
idea_spark.risk: 本地隐私可能泄露用户查询模式（如频繁访问某服务器存储的消息），需结合差分隐私等机制进一步保护。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受图复制系统存储结构启发，重新思考隐私与效率的权衡，未来可探索动态图或异构存储下的本地PIR方案。

en.tldr: Proposes a new definition of privacy for graph-based replicated PIR systems, where users hide their desired message index only from servers that store that message, and analyzes the communication efficiency gain.
en.motivation: Traditional PIR assumes hiding the query index from all servers, but in graph-replicated systems servers store only subsets of messages, necessitating a storage-aware privacy definition.
en.method: Defines local user privacy and local PIR, measures communication efficiency via capacity (message symbols retrieved per downloaded symbol), and derives capacity bounds for various graph structures (disjoint unions, edge-transitive, bipartite, cyclic, path graphs).
en.result: Local PIR capacity for disjoint unions of identical graphs shows multiplicative gain over canonical PIR; for connected graphs (edge-transitive, bipartite) proposed schemes yield lower bounds exceeding known PIR capacity; exact capacity derived for cyclic and odd-vertex path graphs.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how to design more efficient privacy-preserving query protocols in graph-replicated distributed systems and the impact of different graph structures on communication overhead.

idea_spark.idea: Treat graph structure as privacy boundary and design adaptive query strategies where servers are only responsible for privacy within their stored messages, reducing overall communication cost.
idea_spark.risk: Local privacy may leak user query patterns (e.g., frequent access to a server's stored messages), requiring additional protection like differential privacy.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by storage structure of graph-replicated systems to rethink the trade-off between privacy and efficiency; future work could explore dynamic graphs or heterogeneous storage.

papers.title: Teaching LLMs to See Graphs: Unifying Text and Structural Reasoning
papers.summary: Using Large Language Models (LLMs) to process graph-structured data is an active research area, yet current state-of-the-art approaches typically rely on multi-step pipelines with Graph Neural Network (GNN) encoders that compress rich textual attributes into solitary tokens, creating a significant semantic bottleneck. In this paper, we introduce the Graph Transformer Language Model (GTLM), a novel architecture that enables pretrained LLMs to natively process graph topologies while entirely eliminating this compressive bottleneck. GTLM is exceptionally parameter-efficient: by injecting graph-aware attention biases directly into the LLM's attention modules, it introduces only 0.015% additional parameters relative to the base model. We theoretically prove that our bidirectional attention prefix preserves node permutation equivariance while maintaining exact backward compatibility with the pretrained base model. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that a 1B-parameter GTLM matches or exceeds the performance of 7B-parameter state-of-the-art models on standard Text-Attributed Graph benchmarks, while significantly surpassing baselines on GraphQA. Finally, we demonstrate that GTLM attention heads implicitly learn to simulate message passing, explaining its superior performance on algorithmic tasks. This paradigm shift enables true algorithmic reasoning within LLMs and provides a scalable foundation for next-generation GraphRAG and relational deep learning.

ai.tldr: 提出GTLM架构，让预训练LLM原生处理图拓扑，消除语义瓶颈，仅增加0.015%参数，1B模型性能媲美7B模型。
ai.motivation: 现有方法用GNN编码器压缩文本属性为单一token，造成语义瓶颈，限制LLM对图结构数据的处理能力。
ai.method: 在LLM注意力模块中注入图感知注意力偏置，设计双向注意力前缀保持节点置换等变性，且与预训练模型完全向后兼容。
ai.result: 1B参数GTLM在标准文本属性图基准上匹配或超越7B模型，在GraphQA上显著领先，注意力头隐式学习模拟消息传递。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种高效、可扩展的图推理方法，适用于GraphRAG和关系深度学习任务。

idea_spark.idea: 利用双向注意力前缀保持置换等变性，同时实现与预训练模型的兼容，为图推理提供新范式。
idea_spark.risk: 在超大规模图上，注意力偏置的计算开销可能成为瓶颈，需进一步优化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受GNN消息传递机制启发，将图结构信息直接编码到LLM注意力中，实现原生图推理。

zh.tldr: 提出GTLM架构，让预训练LLM原生处理图拓扑，消除语义瓶颈，仅增加0.015%参数，1B模型性能媲美7B模型。
zh.motivation: 现有方法用GNN编码器压缩文本属性为单一token，造成语义瓶颈，限制LLM对图结构数据的处理能力。

zh.result: 1B参数GTLM在标准文本属性图基准上匹配或超越7B模型，在GraphQA上显著领先，注意力头隐式学习模拟消息传递。
zh.help_to_user: 提供一种高效、可扩展的图推理方法，适用于GraphRAG和关系深度学习任务。

idea_spark.idea: 利用双向注意力前缀保持置换等变性，同时实现与预训练模型的兼容，为图推理提供新范式。
idea_spark.risk: 在超大规模图上，注意力偏置的计算开销可能成为瓶颈，需进一步优化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受GNN消息传递机制启发，将图结构信息直接编码到LLM注意力中，实现原生图推理。

en.tldr: Proposes GTLM architecture enabling pretrained LLMs to natively process graph topologies, eliminating semantic bottleneck with only 0.015% extra parameters; 1B model matches 7B models.
en.motivation: Current methods use GNN encoders that compress rich textual attributes into single tokens, creating a semantic bottleneck that limits LLM graph processing.
en.method: Injects graph-aware attention biases into LLM attention modules, designs bidirectional attention prefix to preserve node permutation equivariance and maintain backward compatibility.
en.result: 1B-parameter GTLM matches or exceeds 7B models on Text-Attributed Graph benchmarks, significantly surpasses baselines on GraphQA; attention heads implicitly learn to simulate message passing.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient and scalable approach for graph reasoning, applicable to GraphRAG and relational deep learning.

idea_spark.idea: Uses bidirectional attention prefix to maintain permutation equivariance while ensuring compatibility with pretrained models, offering a new paradigm for graph reasoning.
idea_spark.risk: Computational cost of attention biases may become a bottleneck on very large graphs, requiring further optimization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by GNN message passing, directly encodes graph structure into LLM attention for native graph reasoning.

papers.title: Double Criticality for a Hardy-Rellich Biharmonic Heat Equation in an Exterior Domain
papers.summary: We study the existence and nonexistence of weak solutions to an inhomogeneous semilinear biharmonic heat equation in an exterior domain, involving a singular Hardy--Rellich potential, a weighted nonlinearity $|x|^σ|u|^{p}$, and a positive source term $f(x)$. We identify two distinct critical regimes governing the behavior of solutions. More precisely, we first determine a Fujita-type critical exponent that separates nonexistence from existence. We then show that, in the supercritical range, a second critical exponent arises in terms of the decay exponent of the source, in the sense of Lee and Ni. Our results extend the recent work \cite{Tobakhanov} by considering a singular Hardy--Rellich potential and a weighted nonlinearity, leading to a different critical behavior.

ai.tldr: 研究外部区域中含Hardy-Rellich奇异势的半线性双调和热方程弱解的存在性与不存在性，识别出两个不同的临界指数。
ai.motivation: 扩展Tobakhanov的工作，考虑奇异势和加权非线性，揭示不同的临界行为。
ai.method: 通过分析Fujita型临界指数和Lee-Ni型第二临界指数，区分解的存在与不存在区域。
ai.result: 确定了两个临界指数：一个Fujita型指数分隔存在与不存在，另一个指数依赖于源项的衰减速率。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解奇异势和加权非线性对双调和热方程解的影响，为相关偏微分方程研究提供临界条件。

en.tldr: We study existence and nonexistence of weak solutions to a semilinear biharmonic heat equation with a Hardy-Rellich potential in an exterior domain, identifying two distinct critical exponents.
en.motivation: Extend Tobakhanov's work by incorporating a singular Hardy-Rellich potential and weighted nonlinearity, leading to different critical behavior.
en.method: Analyze Fujita-type critical exponent and a second Lee-Ni type exponent based on source decay rate.
en.result: Two critical regimes: a Fujita exponent separating existence from nonexistence, and a second exponent depending on source decay.
en.help_to_user: Provides critical conditions for solutions of biharmonic heat equations with singular potentials, aiding PDE research.

idea_spark.idea: Investigate the effect of more general singular potentials (e.g., inverse power) on critical exponents in exterior domains.
idea_spark.risk: Singular potentials may reduce solution regularity, requiring refined function space analysis.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining Fujita and Lee-Ni approaches can systematically study multi-critical exponent problems.

papers.title: Locational Pricing for Generative-AI Services via Token-Flow Market Clearing
papers.summary: GenAI services are in an early yet fast expanding phase. Providers compete on model capability and service quality, while the underlying infrastructure remains expensive and heterogeneous across regions, workloads, and compute assets. If these services diffuse into routine daily use, the relevant engineering problem becomes not only better models but also efficient dispatch on a geographically distributed AI service infrastructure. To address this, we formulate a network-constrained token-flow market that clears AI workloads across compute nodes and communication links. The baseline model is a linear program that co-optimizes routing and processing subject to compute-capacity and bandwidth constraints; its dual variables define location- and workload-specific marginal service prices. We further introduce a transfer-aware extension that prices data movement in physical units and isolates bandwidth congestion rents. In a 5-node U.S. case study, the transfer-aware model uncovers four saturated backbone links and raises total operating cost by 2.7\% relative to the token-equivalent baseline, while tightening the chatbot latency limit from 100~ms to 15~ms increases one locational price by 117\%. A 20-node scale-up exhibits the same merit-order dispatch logic and becomes infeasible once demand exceeds aggregate capacity. These results suggest that locational pricing is a useful organizing principle for operating an emerging AI service infrastructure and, over time, for designing competitive markets around it.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于令牌流的网络约束市场模型，为生成式AI服务制定位置定价，并通过案例验证其有效性。
ai.motivation: 生成式AI服务快速扩张，但基础设施昂贵且地理分布不均，需要高效的调度和定价机制。
ai.method: 构建线性规划模型，联合优化路由与处理，利用对偶变量定义位置和工作负载特定的边际服务价格，并引入传输感知扩展。
ai.result: 5节点案例显示传输感知模型发现4条饱和骨干链路，总成本上升2.7%；延迟收紧使某位置价格飙升117%；20节点扩展验证了边际调度逻辑。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解生成式AI服务中位置定价的原理及其对成本和延迟的影响。

idea_spark.idea: 结合实时网络状态动态调整AI服务价格，实现负载均衡和资源高效利用。
idea_spark.risk: 模型依赖精确的网络和计算容量数据，实际部署中数据获取和隐私保护可能成为挑战。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将电力市场中的节点定价思想引入AI服务领域，启发跨学科的市场设计研究。

en.tldr: Proposes a network-constrained token-flow market model for locational pricing of generative AI services, validated through case studies.
en.motivation: Generative AI services are expanding rapidly, but infrastructure is expensive and geographically heterogeneous, requiring efficient dispatch and pricing.

papers.title: Robust Multi-Agent LLMs under Byzantine Faults
papers.summary: Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly collaborate over peer-to-peer networks to improve their reliability. However, these same interactions can also become a source of vulnerability, as unreliable or Byzantine agents may sway neighboring agents toward incorrect conclusions and degrade overall system performance. Existing methods rely on leader-based coordination or self-reported confidence, both of which are susceptible to adversarial manipulation. We study decentralized LLM multi-agent systems (LLM-MAS) and propose Self-Anchored Consensus (SAC), a fully decentralized iterative filter-and-refine protocol in which agents iteratively exchange responses, locally evaluate and filter unreliable messages, and refine their own outputs. We present $(F{+}1)$-robustness conditions for the communication graph that ensure honest agents preserve and propagate reliable information despite Byzantine influence. Experiments on mathematical and commonsense reasoning benchmarks show that SAC effectively suppresses Byzantine influence and consistently improves performance across diverse communication topologies, whereas prior methods degrade under adversarial conditions.

ai.tldr: 提出一种完全去中心化的迭代过滤与精炼协议，使LLM智能体在拜占庭故障下仍能达成稳健共识。
ai.motivation: 现有基于领导者或自报置信度的方法易受对抗操纵，需要一种去中心化、抗拜占庭故障的LLM多智能体协作方案。
ai.method: Self-Anchored Consensus (SAC)：智能体迭代交换响应、本地评估并过滤不可靠消息、精炼自身输出，并给出通信图的(F+1)-鲁棒性条件。
ai.result: 在数学和常识推理基准上，SAC有效抑制拜占庭影响，在不同通信拓扑下持续提升性能，而先前方法在对抗条件下性能下降。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何设计去中心化、抗拜占庭故障的LLM多智能体系统。

idea_spark.idea: 将SAC与图神经网络结合，动态调整过滤阈值以应对未知攻击模式。
idea_spark.risk: 在极端稀疏或高度动态的通信图中，(F+1)-鲁棒性条件可能难以满足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从分布式系统容错理论（如PBFT）中借鉴，但针对LLM的语义不确定性进行适配。

idea_spark.inspiration: 从分布式系统容错理论（如PBFT）中借鉴，但针对LLM的语义不确定性进行适配。

en.tldr: Proposes a fully decentralized iterative filter-and-refine protocol enabling LLM agents to achieve robust consensus under Byzantine faults.
en.motivation: Existing leader-based or self-reported confidence methods are vulnerable to adversarial manipulation; a decentralized, Byzantine-resilient LLM multi-agent collaboration scheme is needed.
en.method: Self-Anchored Consensus (SAC): agents iteratively exchange responses, locally evaluate and filter unreliable messages, refine their own outputs, with (F+1)-robustness conditions on the communication graph.
en.result: On math and commonsense reasoning benchmarks, SAC effectively suppresses Byzantine influence and consistently improves performance across diverse topologies, while prior methods degrade under adversarial conditions.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to design decentralized, Byzantine-resilient LLM multi-agent systems.

idea_spark.idea: Combine SAC with graph neural networks to dynamically adjust filtering thresholds against unknown attack patterns.
idea_spark.risk: In extremely sparse or highly dynamic communication graphs, the (F+1)-robustness condition may be difficult to satisfy.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws from distributed system fault tolerance theory (e.g., PBFT) but adapts to the semantic uncertainty of LLMs.

papers.title: CIVeX: Causal Intervention Verification for Language Agents
papers.summary: A valid tool call is not necessarily a valid intervention. Tool-using language agents are guarded by schema validators, policy filters, provenance checks, state predictors, and self-verification, yet such safeguards do not certify that a state-changing action has an identifiable causal effect. In confounded workflows, the action that looks optimal in observational logs can reduce utility when executed.   We introduce CIVeX, a causal intervention verifier that maps proposed actions to structural causal queries over a committed action-state graph, checks identifiability, and returns one of four auditable verdicts: EXECUTE, REJECT, EXPERIMENT, or ABSTAIN. Execution requires an assumption-scoped causal certificate carrying graph commitments, an identification argument, a one-sided lower confidence bound (LCB), provenance, and risk limits.   On Causal-ToolBench (1,890 instances, 7 seeds), CIVeX yields zero observed false executions across moderate and adversarial confounding. Under adversarial confounding it reaches 84.9% accuracy and 81.1% of oracle utility (+2.23 vs +2.76) and is the only non-oracle method whose constrained utility under a zero-false-execution constraint exceeds the AlwaysAbstain floor. On IHDP and ZOZO Open Bandit (real production logs with uniform-random ground truth), CIVeX matches Oracle correct-execution within 0.1pp and cuts per-execute false-execution by >=50x over naive baselines. A chain-of-thought LLM verifier (Claude Opus, Sonnet) cuts false-execution by an order of magnitude over a terse baseline, yet under adversarial confounding Opus's utility falls to 74% of CIVeX's. Intervention identifiability, not action validity, is the missing primitive for reliable tool use.

ai.tldr: 提出CIVeX，一个因果干预验证器，通过结构因果查询确保语言代理的工具调用具有可识别的因果效应，在多种基准上实现零误执行。
ai.motivation: 现有工具调用安全机制仅验证动作有效性，无法保证状态改变动作具有可识别的因果效应，导致混淆工作流中看似最优的动作实际降低效用。
ai.method: 将提议动作映射到动作-状态图上的结构因果查询，检查可识别性，返回EXECUTE、REJECT、EXPERIMENT或ABSTAIN四种可审计裁决，并附带因果证书。
ai.result: 在Causal-ToolBench上实现零误执行，对抗混淆下准确率84.9%，效用达Oracle的81.1%；在真实生产日志中误执行降低50倍以上。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户确保语言代理调用的工具动作具有可验证的因果效应，避免因混淆导致的效用损失。

idea_spark.idea: 结合因果图与置信下界，在零误执行约束下最大化效用，为安全关键应用提供可审计保证。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖因果图假设的准确性，错误图结构可能导致错误裁决；计算开销可能影响实时性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从因果推断中的可识别性理论出发，将工具调用安全从语法验证提升至因果验证层面。

en.tldr: Proposes CIVeX, a causal intervention verifier that ensures tool calls by language agents have identifiable causal effects via structural causal queries, achieving zero false executions across benchmarks.
en.motivation: Existing tool-call safeguards only verify action validity, not whether state-changing actions have identifiable causal effects, causing seemingly optimal actions to reduce utility in confounded workflows.
en.method: Maps proposed actions to structural causal queries over an action-state graph, checks identifiability, and returns one of four auditable verdicts (EXECUTE, REJECT, EXPERIMENT, ABSTAIN) with a causal certificate.
en.result: Zero false executions on Causal-ToolBench; 84.9% accuracy and 81.1% of oracle utility under adversarial confounding; >=50x reduction in false executions on real production logs.
en.help_to_user: Helps users ensure that tool calls by language agents have verifiable causal effects, avoiding utility loss due to confounding.

idea_spark.idea: Combines causal graphs with lower confidence bounds to maximize utility under a zero-false-execution constraint, providing auditable guarantees for safety-critical applications.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on accurate causal graph assumptions; incorrect graphs may lead to wrong verdicts; computational overhead may affect real-time performance.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws from identifiability theory in causal inference to elevate tool-use safety from syntactic validation to causal verification.

papers.title: Mistake-Bounded Language Generation
papers.summary: We investigate the learning task of language generation in the limit, but shift focus from the traditional time-of-last-mistake metric of a generator's success to a new notion of "mistake-bounded generation." While existing results for language generation in the limit focus on guaranteeing eventual consistency, they are blind to the cumulative error incurred during the learning process. We address this by shifting the goal to minimizing the total number of invalid elements output by a generation algorithm. We establish a formal reduction to the Learning from Correct Demonstrations framework of Joshi et al. (2025), enabling a general recipe for deriving mistake bounds via weighted update rules. For finite classes, we provide an algorithm that simultaneously achieves an optimal last-mistake time of $\mathsf{Cdim}(L)$ and a mistake bound of $\lfloor \log_2 |L| \rfloor$, whereas for the non-uniform setting of countably infinite streams of languages, we prove a fundamental trade-off: achieving logarithmic mistakes $O(\log i)$ necessarily precludes convergence guarantees established in prior work. Finally, we show that our framework can be extended to accommodate noisy adversaries and guarantee mistake bounds that scale with the adversary's suboptimality.

ai.tldr: 提出“错误有界生成”概念，将语言生成学习目标从最终一致性转向最小化累积无效输出总数。
ai.motivation: 现有语言生成极限学习只保证最终一致性，忽略学习过程中的累积错误。
ai.method: 形式化归约到正确示范学习框架，通过加权更新规则推导错误界；对有限类设计最优算法，对无限流证明对数错误与收敛保证的权衡。
ai.result: 有限类上同时达到最优最后错误时间和对数错误界；无限非均匀流中实现对数错误会牺牲收敛保证；框架可扩展至噪声对手。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解语言生成中错误累积的量化分析，以及不同场景下的理论界限。

idea_spark.idea: 将错误有界生成与强化学习中的遗憾最小化结合，设计自适应生成策略。
idea_spark.risk: 理论假设可能偏离实际生成分布，噪声对手模型需验证现实可行性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受在线学习错误界启发，将语言生成视为持续学习过程而非单次任务。

en.tldr: Proposes 'mistake-bounded generation', shifting language generation learning from eventual consistency to minimizing total invalid outputs.
en.motivation: Existing language generation in the limit ignores cumulative errors during learning, focusing only on eventual consistency.
en.method: Formal reduction to Learning from Correct Demonstrations framework with weighted update rules; optimal algorithm for finite classes; trade-off proof for countably infinite streams.
en.result: Simultaneous optimal last-mistake time and logarithmic mistake bound for finite classes; logarithmic mistakes preclude convergence guarantees in non-uniform infinite streams; extension to noisy adversaries.
en.help_to_user: Provides theoretical understanding of cumulative error quantification in language generation and trade-offs across settings.

idea_spark.idea: Combine mistake-bounded generation with regret minimization in reinforcement learning for adaptive generation strategies.
idea_spark.risk: Theoretical assumptions may deviate from real generation distributions; noisy adversary model needs empirical validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by online learning mistake bounds, treating language generation as a continual learning process rather than a single task.

papers.title: Normal approximation of the numbers of isolated edges and isolated 2-stars in uniform simple graphs with given vertex degrees
papers.summary: We consider the configuration model and the uniform simple graph with given degree sequence $\boldsymbol{d}=\left(d_i\right)_{i=1}^n$. We derive quantitative bounds for the errors in (i) joint normal-Poisson approximation to the numbers of isolated edges, isolated 2-stars, self-loops and double edges in the configuration model, and (ii) normal approximation to the numbers of isolated edges and isolated 2-stars conditioned on that the configuration model is simple. The latter provides the first finite sample normal approximation results for the uniform simple graph with given vertex degrees. To achieve this, we develop a new Stein's method for joint normal-Poisson approximation and a new coupling approach to sums of indicators, which may be of independent interest.

ai.tldr: 本文针对给定度序列的均匀简单图，推导了孤立边和孤立2-星数量的正态近似误差界，并提出了新的联合正态-泊松近似Stein方法和指标耦合技术。
ai.motivation: 现有研究缺乏对给定度序列均匀简单图中孤立边和孤立2-星数量的有限样本正态近似结果，需要填补这一空白。
ai.method: 利用配置模型，结合新开发的联合正态-泊松近似Stein方法和指标耦合技术，推导误差界。
ai.result: 首次给出了均匀简单图中孤立边和孤立2-星数量的有限样本正态近似结果，并提供了定量误差界。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解给定度序列随机图模型中局部结构（孤立边和2-星）的统计分布，为网络分析提供理论支撑。

idea_spark.idea: 将Stein方法扩展到联合正态-泊松分布，并设计新的耦合策略处理条件事件。
idea_spark.risk: 方法复杂度较高，可能难以直接应用于大规模图或非均匀度序列。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从配置模型的条件化出发，启发对更复杂图模型（如随机块模型）中局部结构的近似研究。

en.tldr: This paper derives quantitative error bounds for normal approximation of the numbers of isolated edges and isolated 2-stars in uniform simple graphs with given degree sequences, using a new Stein's method for joint normal-Poisson approximation and a novel coupling approach.
en.motivation: Existing work lacks finite-sample normal approximation results for isolated edges and isolated 2-stars in uniform simple graphs with given vertex degrees.
en.method: Using the configuration model, combined with a newly developed Stein's method for joint normal-Poisson approximation and a coupling approach for sums of indicators.
en.result: First finite-sample normal approximation results for isolated edges and isolated 2-stars in uniform simple graphs, with quantitative error bounds.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the statistical distribution of local structures (isolated edges and 2-stars) in random graph models with given degree sequences, providing theoretical support for network analysis.

idea_spark.idea: Extend Stein's method to joint normal-Poisson distributions and design new coupling strategies to handle conditional events.
idea_spark.risk: The method is complex and may be difficult to apply directly to large-scale graphs or non-uniform degree sequences.
idea_spark.inspiration: Conditioning on the configuration model inspires research on approximations of local structures in more complex graph models, such as stochastic block models.

papers.title: Robust Learning Meets Quasar-Convex Optimization: Inexact High-Order Proximal-Point Methods
papers.summary: Robust learning aims to maintain model performance under noise, corruption, and distributional shifts, which are prevalent in modern machine learning applications. This work shows that examples of robust learning problems can be formulated as (strongly) quasar-convex optimization problems, which admit a benign landscape with no saddle points. We then propose HiPPA, an inexact high-order proximal-point method that employs a model-value gap to control the inexactness of subproblem solutions. Notably, we prove global convergence of HiPPA to global minima and establish that it attains a (local) linear or superlinear convergence rate, depending on the regularization order and inexactness control. Our numerical experiments on robust feature-alignment distillation indicate strong empirical performance of HiPPA and results consistent with our theoretical findings.

ai.tldr: 本文证明鲁棒学习问题可建模为拟凸优化，并提出一种不精确高阶近端点方法HiPPA，实现全局收敛与超线性收敛。
ai.motivation: 鲁棒学习在噪声、污染和分布偏移下保持模型性能，但现有优化方法缺乏理论保证。
ai.method: 将鲁棒学习问题转化为强拟凸优化，设计基于模型值间隙的不精确高阶近端点方法HiPPA。
ai.result: HiPPA全局收敛到全局最小值，并达到线性或超线性收敛率；在鲁棒特征对齐蒸馏任务上表现优异。
ai.help_to_user: 为鲁棒学习提供理论严谨的优化框架，指导实际中如何选择正则化阶次与不精确度控制。

idea_spark.idea: 利用模型值间隙作为自适应停止准则，减少子问题求解开销。
idea_spark.risk: 高阶方法计算成本高，实际部署需权衡精度与效率。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将非凸鲁棒问题转化为拟凸优化，启发设计更高效的全局优化器。
