en.tldr: This paper shows robust learning problems can be formulated as quasar-convex optimization and proposes HiPPA, an inexact high-order proximal-point method with global and superlinear convergence.
en.motivation: Robust learning maintains performance under noise and shifts, but existing optimization methods lack theoretical guarantees.
en.method: Formulate robust learning as strongly quasar-convex problems; design HiPPA using a model-value gap to control subproblem inexactness.
en.result: HiPPA converges globally to global minima with linear or superlinear rates; strong empirical performance on robust feature-alignment distillation.
en.help_to_user: Provides a theoretically grounded optimization framework for robust learning, guiding choices of regularization order and inexactness control.

idea_spark.idea: Use model-value gap as an adaptive stopping criterion to reduce subproblem solving cost.
idea_spark.risk: High-order methods incur high computational cost; practical deployment requires balancing accuracy and efficiency.
idea_spark.inspiration: Transforming non-convex robust problems into quasar-convex optimization inspires designing more efficient global optimizers.

papers.title: Sparse Signal Recovery using Log-Sum Regularization and Adaptive Smoothing
papers.summary: We study sparse signal recovery from noisy linear observations using nonconvex log-sum regularization. The log-sum penalty reduces the shrinkage bias of $\ell_1$ regularization and more closely approximates the $\ell_0$ regularization, but its nonconvexity can make reconstruction algorithms unstable. To mitigate this instability, we use an adaptive smoothing strategy that determines the smoothing parameter so that the scalar proximal operator remains continuous. Using this proximal operator, we formulate the approximate message passing (AMP) algorithm and derive the corresponding state evolution (SE) recursion. The fixed point of the SE recursion predicts the final mean squared error (MSE) and, in the noiseless limit, the exact-recovery phase transition. To further investigate finite-dimensional reconstruction behavior, we implement an alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM) algorithm. In the noiseless setting, we find that the empirical success boundary of ADMM closely agrees with the SE-predicted phase transition. In the noisy setting, we observe that AMP closely follows the SE prediction, whereas ADMM qualitatively reproduces the SE-predicted dependence of the final MSE on the regularization parameter. A comparison with $\ell_1$ regularization shows that log-sum regularization is beneficial in low-density or high-measurement-rate regimes, whereas $\ell_1$ regularization remains preferable at higher densities and lower measurement rates.

ai.tldr: 提出使用对数求和正则化与自适应平滑策略实现稀疏信号恢复，通过AMP和ADMM算法验证了其优于L1正则化的性能。
ai.motivation: L1正则化存在收缩偏差，而L0正则化虽理想但计算困难；对数求和正则化更接近L0，但非凸性导致不稳定。
ai.method: 采用自适应平滑策略使标量近端算子连续，基于此构建AMP算法并推导状态演化递归，同时实现ADMM算法进行有限维验证。
ai.result: 无噪声下ADMM的成功边界与SE预测的相变一致；有噪声下AMP紧跟SE预测，ADMM定性复现SE预测的MSE依赖关系；对数求和正则化在低密度或高测量率时优于L1。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种非凸正则化稀疏恢复方法，并给出了理论分析与算法实现，帮助用户在不同场景下选择正则化策略。

idea_spark.idea: 将自适应平滑与AMP结合，或许能用于压缩感知中的在线或流式数据恢复。
idea_spark.risk: 非凸优化可能陷入局部最优，且AMP的SE推导依赖于特定假设（如高斯观测矩阵），实际应用需谨慎。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受L1与L0折衷启发，可探索其他非凸惩罚（如SCAD、MCP）与自适应平滑的组合。

zh.tldr: 提出使用对数求和正则化与自适应平滑策略实现稀疏信号恢复，通过AMP和ADMM算法验证了其优于L1正则化的性能。

idea_spark.idea: 将自适应平滑与AMP结合，或许能用于压缩感知中的在线或流式数据恢复。
idea_spark.risk: 非凸优化可能陷入局部最优，且AMP的SE推导依赖于特定假设（如高斯观测矩阵），实际应用需谨慎。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受L1与L0折衷启发，可探索其他非凸惩罚（如SCAD、MCP）与自适应平滑的组合。

en.tldr: Proposes log-sum regularization with adaptive smoothing for sparse signal recovery, validated via AMP and ADMM algorithms, showing advantages over L1 regularization.
en.motivation: L1 regularization suffers from shrinkage bias, while L0 is ideal but intractable; log-sum is closer to L0 but its nonconvexity causes instability.
en.method: Uses adaptive smoothing to ensure continuity of the scalar proximal operator, formulates AMP with state evolution, and implements ADMM for finite-dimensional verification.
en.result: In noiseless case, ADMM success boundary matches SE phase transition; in noisy case, AMP follows SE closely, ADMM reproduces SE-predicted MSE dependence; log-sum outperforms L1 at low density or high measurement rate.
en.help_to_user: Provides a nonconvex regularization approach for sparse recovery with theoretical analysis and algorithms, aiding users in choosing regularization strategies for different scenarios.

idea_spark.idea: Combining adaptive smoothing with AMP may enable online or streaming recovery in compressed sensing.
idea_spark.risk: Nonconvex optimization may get stuck in local minima, and AMP's SE derivation relies on specific assumptions (e.g., Gaussian measurement matrices), requiring caution in practice.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the trade-off between L1 and L0, exploring other nonconvex penalties (e.g., SCAD, MCP) with adaptive smoothing is promising.

papers.title: Improving search efficiency via adaptive acquisition function selection in discrete black-box optimization
papers.summary: In discrete-variable black-box optimization, the number of candidate solutions grows combinatorially, while each evaluation is often expensive. Therefore, it is important to identify promising solutions efficiently within a limited number of trials. Bayesian Optimization of Combinatorial Structures (BOCS), an existing parametric method, works effectively when only a small amount of data is available. However, as the number of observations increases, BOCS tends to repeatedly propose points that have already been evaluated, which leads to search stagnation. A random-point addition strategy has been proposed to address this issue when an evaluated point is proposed, but it cannot sufficiently exploit information from promising data obtained so far. In this study, we propose a hybrid method that uses BOCS as the main search framework and generates alternative unevaluated points using a Gaussian process only when search stagnation is detected. In the Gaussian-process-based component, multiple Lower Confidence Bound (LCB) acquisition functions are adaptively selected to dynamically control the balance between exploitation and exploration. Numerical experiments using fully connected Quadratic Unconstrained Binary Optimization (QUBO) and Higher-order Unconstrained Binary Optimization (HUBO) as black-box functions show that the proposed method finds solutions with better objective values than the conventional random-point addition method in both settings. Additional analyses show that its effectiveness comes from selecting points that promote search progress within Hamming-distance neighborhoods, rather than simply adding low-energy points near promising solutions. Experiments with sparse surrogate models for quantum annealer applications further suggest the importance of retaining near-fully connected representational capacity.

ai.tldr: 提出一种混合方法，在离散黑箱优化中通过自适应选择采集函数提高搜索效率，避免搜索停滞。
ai.motivation: 现有BOCS方法在数据增多时易重复提出已评估点导致搜索停滞，随机加点策略未能充分利用已有信息。
ai.method: 以BOCS为主框架，检测到搜索停滞时用高斯过程生成替代未评估点，并自适应选择多个LCB采集函数以平衡探索与利用。
ai.result: 在QUBO和HUBO问题上，所提方法比随机加点法找到更优解；有效性源于在汉明距离邻域内选择促进搜索进展的点，而非简单添加低能点。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过自适应采集函数选择提升离散黑箱优化的搜索效率。

idea_spark.idea: 结合参数模型与高斯过程，在检测到停滞时动态切换采集函数，可能适用于更广泛的组合优化场景。
idea_spark.risk: 高斯过程组件可能增加计算开销，且对稀疏代理模型的表示能力有要求。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从量子退火器应用中稀疏代理模型需保留近全连接表示能力，启发未来研究关注模型容量与搜索效率的平衡。

en.tldr: Proposes a hybrid method that improves search efficiency in discrete black-box optimization by adaptively selecting acquisition functions to avoid search stagnation.
en.motivation: Existing BOCS tends to repeatedly propose evaluated points as data increases, causing stagnation; random-point addition fails to exploit promising data sufficiently.
en.method: Uses BOCS as main framework and generates alternative unevaluated points via Gaussian process when stagnation is detected, adaptively selecting multiple LCB acquisition functions to balance exploration and exploitation.
en.result: On QUBO and HUBO problems, the proposed method finds better solutions than random-point addition; effectiveness comes from selecting points that promote search progress in Hamming-distance neighborhoods rather than simply adding low-energy points.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how adaptive acquisition function selection can enhance search efficiency in discrete black-box optimization.

idea_spark.idea: Combining parametric models with Gaussian processes and dynamically switching acquisition functions upon stagnation may apply to broader combinatorial optimization scenarios.
idea_spark.risk: The Gaussian process component may increase computational cost and requires sufficient representational capacity in sparse surrogate models.
idea_spark.inspiration: The finding that sparse surrogate models need near-fully connected capacity for quantum annealer applications inspires future work on balancing model capacity and search efficiency.

papers.title: Equilibrium Residuals Expose Three Regimes of Matrix-Game Strategic Reasoning in Language Models
papers.summary: Large language models can score well on named game-theory benchmarks while failing on the same strategic computation once semantic cues are removed. We show this gap with procedurally generated zero-sum matrix games: a model that recognizes familiar games drops to 34%, 18%, and 2% success on anonymous $2{\times}2$, $3{\times}3$, and $5{\times}5$ payoff matrices. The benchmark separates semantic recall, learned approximate Nash computation, and an output-interface bottleneck that limits scale. Training only on $2{\times}2$ and $3{\times}3$ games, supervised fine-tuning raises unseen $5{\times}5$--$7{\times}7$ success from 2% to 61%, while exploitability-reward training averages 37% with high seed variance. We prove that the exploitability residual is $2$-Lipschitz in payoff perturbations, unlike discontinuous vertex-returning LP equilibrium selectors, explaining why residual training can transfer under payoff shifts even when formatting instability limits mean performance. A dominated-action padding experiment provides causal evidence: trained models solve $3{\times}3$ games embedded in much larger matrices, while random-padded controls fail and dense $12{\times}12$ games remain near failure. Procedural evaluation is therefore necessary for measuring strategic reasoning, and residual rewards expose a real but format-limited route to approximate equilibrium computation.

ai.tldr: 大型语言模型在去除语义线索的匿名零和矩阵游戏中表现急剧下降，但通过监督微调和可剥削性奖励训练可提升泛化能力。
ai.motivation: 现有基准测试依赖语义识别，无法准确衡量模型的战略推理能力，需要程序化生成的无语义游戏来暴露真实缺陷。
ai.method: 程序化生成2×2至5×5零和矩阵游戏，比较语义识别与匿名矩阵上的表现；采用监督微调、可剥削性奖励训练及主导动作填充实验。
ai.result: 匿名矩阵上成功率从语义识别时的34%降至2%（5×5）；监督微调使5×5-7×7成功率升至61%；可剥削性奖励训练平均37%但方差大；主导动作填充证明模型能解决嵌入大矩阵中的小游戏。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解LLM战略推理的局限性，并提供提升泛化能力的训练方法。

idea_spark.idea: 利用可剥削性残差的Lipschitz连续性设计更稳定的奖励信号，避免格式不稳定性。
idea_spark.risk: 训练方差大，且对密集大矩阵仍近乎失败，泛化边界有限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 程序化生成与残差奖励结合，可构建更鲁棒的推理评估框架。

zh.tldr: 大型语言模型在去除语义线索的匿名零和矩阵游戏中表现急剧下降，但通过监督微调和可剥削性奖励训练可提升泛化能力。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解LLM战略推理的局限性，并提供提升泛化能力的训练方法。

idea_spark.idea: 利用可剥削性残差的Lipschitz连续性设计更稳定的奖励信号，避免格式不稳定性。
idea_spark.risk: 训练方差大，且对密集大矩阵仍近乎失败，泛化边界有限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 程序化生成与残差奖励结合，可构建更鲁棒的推理评估框架。

en.tldr: Large language models drop sharply on anonymous zero-sum matrix games when semantic cues are removed, but supervised fine-tuning and exploitability-reward training improve generalization.
en.motivation: Existing benchmarks rely on semantic recognition and fail to measure true strategic reasoning; procedurally generated games without semantics expose the gap.
en.method: Procedurally generated 2×2 to 5×5 zero-sum matrix games; comparison of semantic vs. anonymous performance; supervised fine-tuning, exploitability-reward training, and dominated-action padding experiments.
en.result: Success on anonymous matrices drops from 34% to 2% (5×5); supervised fine-tuning raises 5×5–7×7 success to 61%; exploitability-reward training averages 37% with high variance; padding shows models solve embedded small games.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand limitations of LLM strategic reasoning and provides training methods to improve generalization.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage Lipschitz continuity of exploitability residual for more stable reward signals, mitigating formatting instability.
idea_spark.risk: High training variance and near-failure on dense large matrices limit practical applicability.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining procedural generation with residual rewards can build more robust reasoning evaluation frameworks.

papers.title: Graph Computation Meets Circuit Algebra: A Task-Aligned Analysis of Graph Neural Networks for Electronic Design Automation
papers.summary: EDA problems are graph-structured, but not all graph-structured problems call for the same GNN computation. We argue that successful GNN-for-EDA methods are those whose propagation, aggregation, and supervision align with the native algebra of the target task. Concretely: static timing analysis is a max-plus/min-plus recurrence on a topologically ordered DAG, structurally aligned with asynchronous DAG-GNNs; placement is governed by hypergraph wirelength and density penalties and is exploited by differentiable placers rather than by message-passing GNNs alone; routing congestion is a sparse demand-supply field over a layout grid; switching-activity propagation is a probabilistic recurrence on a directed netlist; IR drop is a linear system on the power-delivery network; and analog symmetry extraction is a discrete constraint-prediction problem on schematic graphs. Through these task-by-task alignments we (i) review the GNN architectural toolkit relevant to circuits, (ii) formalize how circuit graphs differ from generic graphs (directed, heterogeneous, multi-scale, with sequential and clock structure), (iii) characterize where current methods succeed and where the algebra-architecture mismatch limits them, and (iv) identify failure modes--stage leakage, proxy-to-signoff gap, calibration, and design-distribution shift--that we believe are likely to dominate the next phase of work. We position the paper as a GNN-for-EDA, task-aligned analysis rather than a comprehensive AI-for-chip-design survey. Continuous SE(3)-equivariant geometric GNNs are usually mismatched to Manhattan digital layout, and LLM-for-RTL, HLS, and RL/diffusion-based topology generation are outside our scope.

ai.tldr: 本文提出GNN-for-EDA方法成功的关键在于其传播、聚合和监督与目标任务的代数结构对齐，并逐任务分析了静态时序分析、布局、布线拥塞、开关活动传播、IR压降和模拟对称性提取等EDA问题。
ai.motivation: EDA问题具有图结构，但不同图结构问题需要不同的GNN计算，现有方法缺乏任务对齐的系统分析。
ai.method: 通过逐任务对齐分析，形式化电路图与通用图的差异（有向、异构、多尺度、含时序和时钟结构），并识别当前方法的成功与失败模式。
ai.result: 总结了GNN架构工具包，指出了代数-架构不匹配导致的阶段泄漏、代理到签核差距、校准和设计分布偏移等失败模式。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员理解GNN在EDA中的适用边界，指导选择或设计任务对齐的GNN架构。

idea_spark.idea: 开发自动检测代数-架构不匹配的工具，或设计可自适应调整传播/聚合规则的GNN。
idea_spark.risk: 过度强调代数对齐可能限制GNN的通用性，且EDA任务代数形式化本身可能不唯一。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受电路代数启发，可探索将GNN的传播规则显式参数化为代数运算（如max-plus、线性系统）。

en.tldr: This paper argues that successful GNN-for-EDA methods align propagation, aggregation, and supervision with the native algebra of the target task, and provides a task-by-task analysis covering static timing analysis, placement, routing congestion, switching activity propagation, IR drop, and analog symmetry extraction.
en.motivation: EDA problems are graph-structured but not all require the same GNN computation; existing methods lack a systematic task-aligned analysis.
en.method: Through task-by-task alignment, formalizes how circuit graphs differ from generic graphs (directed, heterogeneous, multi-scale, with sequential and clock structure) and identifies success and failure modes.
en.result: Summarizes the GNN architectural toolkit, and identifies failure modes such as stage leakage, proxy-to-signoff gap, calibration, and design-distribution shift due to algebra-architecture mismatch.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers understand the applicability boundaries of GNNs in EDA and guides the selection or design of task-aligned GNN architectures.

idea_spark.idea: Develop tools to automatically detect algebra-architecture mismatches, or design GNNs that adaptively adjust propagation/aggregation rules.
idea_spark.risk: Overemphasis on algebraic alignment may limit GNN generality, and the formalization of EDA task algebra may not be unique.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by circuit algebra, one could explore explicitly parameterizing GNN propagation rules as algebraic operations (e.g., max-plus, linear systems).

papers.title: MeTime: An R package for reproducible longitudinal metabolomics data analysis
papers.summary: MeTime is an opensource R package for reproducible analysis of longitudinal metabolomics data. It builds upon a central S4 container, metime_analyser, that stores multiple datasets, associated metadata and analysis outputs, enabling unified handling of complex longitudinal studies. Analyses are constructed by piping modular functions, beginning with data transformations (mod_), followed by calculations (calc_), and optional meta-analysis (meta_), so entire workflows remain transparent and easy to modify. MeTime wraps numerous existing methods within a consistent interface, including sample and metabolite distributions, correlation and distance matrices, dimensionality reduction (PCA, UMAP, tSNE), random forest imputation and feature selection via Boruta, eigenmetabolites and WGCNA based clustering, conservation index analysis, regression models (linear, mixed effects, and generalized additive), and partial correlation networks. By retaining all intermediate results and provenance within the container, MeTime facilitates iterative exploration and ensures reproducible reporting via automatically generated HTML and PDF outputs. Comprehensive user guides, case studies and reference documentation accompany the package, making MeTime a versatile platform for longitudinal omics workflows.

ai.tldr: MeTime是一个开源R包，用于可重复的纵向代谢组学数据分析，通过S4容器统一管理多数据集、元数据和分析结果。
ai.motivation: 纵向代谢组学研究需要可重复、透明且易于修改的分析流程，现有工具缺乏统一的数据容器和模块化工作流。
ai.method: 基于S4容器metime_analyser，通过管道式模块函数（mod_、calc_、meta_）构建分析流程，集成多种统计和机器学习方法。
ai.result: 支持数据变换、相关性分析、降维、随机森林插补、特征选择、聚类、回归模型和偏相关网络，自动生成HTML/PDF报告。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户以可重复的方式分析纵向代谢组学数据，简化复杂工作流的管理和报告生成。

idea_spark.idea: 开发一个跨组学的统一分析框架，共享数据容器和模块化函数，支持多组学纵向整合。
idea_spark.risk: 不同组学数据结构和预处理需求差异大，统一框架可能牺牲特定组学的优化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴MeTime的容器化思路，构建一个可扩展的纵向多组学分析平台。

en.tldr: MeTime is an open-source R package for reproducible longitudinal metabolomics data analysis, using an S4 container to unify multiple datasets, metadata, and analysis outputs.
en.motivation: Longitudinal metabolomics studies require reproducible, transparent, and easily modifiable analysis workflows; existing tools lack a unified data container and modular pipeline.
en.method: Built on the S4 container metime_analyser, the analysis is constructed by piping modular functions (mod_, calc_, meta_), integrating numerous statistical and machine learning methods.
en.result: Supports data transformations, correlation analysis, dimensionality reduction, random forest imputation, feature selection, clustering, regression models, and partial correlation networks, with automatic HTML/PDF report generation.
en.help_to_user: Helps users perform reproducible analysis of longitudinal metabolomics data, simplifying management of complex workflows and report generation.

idea_spark.idea: Develop a cross-omics unified analysis framework sharing the data container and modular functions for integrated longitudinal multi-omics studies.
idea_spark.risk: Different omics data structures and preprocessing requirements may compromise optimization for specific omics.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by MeTime's container approach to build an extensible longitudinal multi-omics analysis platform.

papers.title: Approximation of Degenerate Hyperbolic Equations with Interior Degeneracy and Applications to Controllability
papers.summary: In this paper, we establish the existence of solutions for a particular class of degenerate hyperbolic equations. Following this, we approximate these degenerate equations by employing a sequence of uniformly hyperbolic equations. Notably, this specific approximation result has remained unexplored in the existing body of literature. Ultimately, we utilize this approximation framework to derive controllability results for the original degenerate hyperbolic equations, marking what could potentially be the inaugural investigation into higher-dimensional degenerate hyperbolic equations.

ai.tldr: 本文研究了一类内部退化双曲型方程的解的存在性，并通过一致双曲型方程序列逼近退化方程，进而推导出可控性结果。
ai.motivation: 现有文献尚未探索内部退化双曲型方程的逼近结果，且高维退化双曲型方程的可控性研究尚属空白。
ai.method: 首先证明退化方程解的存在性，然后构造一致双曲型方程序列进行逼近，最后利用逼近框架推导可控性。
ai.result: 获得了退化双曲型方程的可控性结果，可能是高维退化双曲型方程的首个可控性研究。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解内部退化双曲型方程的逼近方法和可控性分析。

idea_spark.idea: 利用一致双曲型方程逼近退化方程，从而简化可控性分析。
idea_spark.risk: 逼近的收敛性可能依赖于特定的退化结构，通用性需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将退化问题转化为非退化问题的逼近，是处理退化PDE的通用思路。

en.tldr: This paper establishes the existence of solutions for a class of degenerate hyperbolic equations with interior degeneracy, approximates them by uniformly hyperbolic equations, and derives controllability results.
en.motivation: The approximation result for such degenerate equations has not been explored, and controllability of higher-dimensional degenerate hyperbolic equations is new.
en.method: Prove existence of solutions, then approximate via a sequence of uniformly hyperbolic equations, and finally use the approximation to obtain controllability.
en.result: Controllability results for the original degenerate hyperbolic equations are obtained, possibly the first for higher-dimensional cases.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand approximation methods and controllability analysis for degenerate hyperbolic equations.

idea_spark.idea: Approximate degenerate equations by uniformly hyperbolic ones to simplify controllability analysis.
idea_spark.risk: Convergence may depend on specific degeneracy structure; generality needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Transforming degenerate problems into non-degenerate approximations is a general strategy for degenerate PDEs.

papers.title: Safe Exploration for Nonlinear Processes Using Online Gaussian Process Learning
papers.summary: This paper proposes a safe data-driven control framework for nonlinear systems with partially known dynamics. The method ensures stability and constraint satisfaction during online learning, assuming only a stabilizable linear approximation of the process is available. Unmodeled nonlinear dynamics are captured by a Gaussian process residual learned in real time. Safety is enforced through a probabilistic control-invariant set derived from Lyapunov theory, guaranteeing high-probability stability. A convex quadratic program computes control inputs that maximize information gain while respecting probabilistic safety constraints. The framework provides finite-sample safety guarantees and allows adaptive expansion of the invariant set as uncertainty decreases. Numerical results validate the approach, demonstrating safe and informative exploration under model uncertainty: the safe set expands by about 30% while the Gaussian process root-mean-square error drops from 1.11 to 0.03.

ai.tldr: 提出一种安全数据驱动控制框架，用于部分已知动力学的非线性系统，通过在线高斯过程学习未建模非线性，确保探索过程中的稳定性和约束满足。
ai.motivation: 非线性系统在线学习时，传统方法难以同时保证安全性和探索效率，需要一种能处理模型不确定性的安全控制框架。
ai.method: 基于Lyapunov理论构建概率控制不变集，通过凸二次规划求解最大化信息增益的控制输入，同时满足概率安全约束。
ai.result: 数值实验表明，安全集扩大约30%，高斯过程均方根误差从1.11降至0.03，验证了安全且信息丰富的探索。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种可证明安全的在线学习控制方法，适用于对安全性要求高的非线性过程，如机器人或化工过程。

idea_spark.idea: 将高斯过程残差学习与Lyapunov稳定性理论结合，实现自适应安全集扩展。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖线性近似和概率保证，可能在实际强非线性或噪声环境下保守性过高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受安全强化学习中屏障函数启发，但采用更易计算的二次规划实现。

idea_spark.inspiration: 受安全强化学习中屏障函数启发，但采用更易计算的二次规划实现。

en.tldr: A safe data-driven control framework for nonlinear systems with partially known dynamics, using online Gaussian process learning to ensure stability and constraint satisfaction during exploration.
en.motivation: Online learning for nonlinear systems faces challenges in balancing safety and exploration; existing methods lack rigorous guarantees under model uncertainty.
en.method: Constructs a probabilistic control-invariant set via Lyapunov theory and solves a convex quadratic program to maximize information gain while respecting safety constraints.
en.result: Numerical results show a 30% expansion of the safe set and a reduction in Gaussian process RMSE from 1.11 to 0.03, demonstrating safe and informative exploration.
en.help_to_user: Provides a provably safe online learning control method for safety-critical nonlinear processes, such as robotics or chemical processes.

idea_spark.idea: Integrates Gaussian process residual learning with Lyapunov stability theory for adaptive safe set expansion.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on linear approximations and probabilistic guarantees, which may be overly conservative under strong nonlinearities or noise.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by barrier functions in safe reinforcement learning, but uses a computationally simpler quadratic programming approach.

papers.title: FlowADMM: Plug-and-play ADMM with Flow-based Renoise-Denoise Priors
papers.summary: Plug-and-play (PnP) methods for solving inverse problems have recently achieved strong performance by leveraging denoising priors based on powerful generative diffusion and flow models. However, existing diffusion- and flow-based PnP methods typically rely on stochastic renoise-denoise operations, which complicate the analysis of their convergence behavior. In this work, we identify and formalize the deterministic renoise-denoise operator underlying flow-based plug-and-play methods. This perspective reveals that these methods implicitly define a deterministic operator given by the expectation of a denoiser over the latent noise distribution. Building on this insight, we propose FlowADMM, a PnP algorithm that integrates the renoise-denoise operator into the classical alternating direction method of multiplier (ADMM) framework. We establish convergence guarantees for FlowADMM under weak Lipschitz conditions on the underlying flow network, and extend the analysis to non-stationary time schedules. Empirically, FlowADMM achieves state-of-the-art performance among flow-based PnP methods on a range of inverse problems, including denoising, deblurring, super-resolution, and inpainting, while requiring fewer data consistency evaluations than prior approaches.

ai.tldr: 提出FlowADMM，将基于流的重噪-去噪算子集成到ADMM框架中，实现可收敛的即插即用逆问题求解。
ai.motivation: 现有基于扩散和流的即插即用方法依赖随机重噪-去噪操作，收敛性分析困难。
ai.method: 形式化确定性重噪-去噪算子，并将其嵌入经典ADMM框架，在弱Lipschitz条件下建立收敛保证。
ai.result: 在去噪、去模糊、超分辨、修复等逆问题上达到当前最优性能，且数据一致性评估次数更少。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种收敛性有保证的即插即用逆问题求解方法，适用于多种图像复原任务。

idea_spark.idea: 将流模型的重噪-去噪过程视为确定性算子，并利用ADMM框架实现收敛性分析。
idea_spark.risk: 弱Lipschitz条件可能对某些流网络不成立，影响收敛保证的普适性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从随机操作中提取确定性结构，为生成模型在逆问题中的理论分析提供新视角。

idea_spark.inspiration: 从随机操作中提取确定性结构，为生成模型在逆问题中的理论分析提供新视角。

en.tldr: Proposes FlowADMM, integrating a flow-based renoise-denoise operator into the ADMM framework for convergent plug-and-play inverse problem solving.
en.motivation: Existing diffusion- and flow-based plug-and-play methods rely on stochastic renoise-denoise operations, complicating convergence analysis.
en.method: Formalizes the deterministic renoise-denoise operator and embeds it into the classical ADMM framework, establishing convergence guarantees under weak Lipschitz conditions.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art performance among flow-based PnP methods on denoising, deblurring, super-resolution, and inpainting, with fewer data consistency evaluations.
en.help_to_user: Provides a convergent plug-and-play method for inverse problems applicable to various image restoration tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Treat the renoise-denoise process of flow models as a deterministic operator and leverage the ADMM framework for convergence analysis.
idea_spark.risk: Weak Lipschitz conditions may not hold for some flow networks, limiting the generality of convergence guarantees.
idea_spark.inspiration: Extracting deterministic structure from stochastic operations offers a new perspective for theoretical analysis of generative models in inverse problems.

papers.title: C2L-Net: A Data-Driven Model for State-of-Charge Estimation of Lithium-Ion Batteries During Discharge
papers.summary: Accurate state-of-charge (SOC) estimation is critical for the safe and efficient operation of lithium-ion batteries in battery management systems (BMS). Although data-driven approaches can effectively capture nonlinear battery dynamics, many existing methods rely on long historical input sequences, resulting in high computational cost and introducing padding-induced positional bias at the beginning of drive cycles. To address these limitations, we propose C2L-Net, a novel context-to-latest data-driven framework for realistic online SOC estimation using only a short historical window (20 s). Unlike existing short-receptive-field or long-history models, the proposed framework explicitly separates contextual encoding from latest-measurement updating, enabling both efficient temporal modeling and rapid adaptation to dynamic battery states. The proposed model incorporates a chunk-based feature extraction mechanism that combines Theta Attention Pooling with a Fourier-based Seasonality Basis to capture local temporal patterns while reducing sequence length. A causal context encoder, integrating a gated recurrent unit (GRU) with Causal Cosine Attention, models temporal dependencies without information leakage. Furthermore, a latest-measurement decoder, inspired by recursive filtering, updates the contextual state using the most recent measurement, enhancing responsiveness to dynamic operating conditions. Extensive experiments on a public lithium-ion battery drive-cycle dataset under multiple fixed-temperature conditions demonstrate that the proposed method achieves state-of-the-art or competitive accuracy while significantly improving computational efficiency. In particular, C2L-Net achieves up to 60 times faster inference and requires fewer parameters than recent data-driven baselines, while maintaining robust performance across unseen driving profiles.

ai.tldr: 提出C2L-Net，一种仅需20秒短历史窗口即可在线估计锂离子电池荷电状态的数据驱动模型，在保持高精度的同时实现高达60倍的推理加速。
ai.motivation: 现有数据驱动SOC估计方法依赖长历史输入序列，导致计算成本高且存在填充引入的位置偏差，不适合实时在线应用。
ai.method: 将上下文编码与最新测量更新显式分离：采用基于块的Theta注意力池化和傅里叶季节基提取局部模式，用门控循环单元结合因果余弦注意力建模时序依赖，最后用递归滤波启发的解码器利用最新测量更新上下文状态。
ai.result: 在公开数据集上，C2L-Net在多个固定温度条件下达到最先进或相当的精度，参数更少，推理速度比近期基线方法快60倍，且对未见驾驶轮廓保持鲁棒。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何用短历史窗口实现高效、准确的在线SOC估计，适用于资源受限的电池管理系统。

idea_spark.idea: 将递归滤波思想与注意力机制结合，设计轻量级解码器，使模型能快速适应动态变化。
idea_spark.risk: 短历史窗口可能在某些极端工况下丢失长期依赖信息，需验证对深度放电或快速充放电场景的适应性。

en.tldr: Proposes C2L-Net, a data-driven model that estimates lithium-ion battery state-of-charge online using only a 20-second short historical window, achieving up to 60x faster inference while maintaining high accuracy.
en.motivation: Existing data-driven SOC estimation methods rely on long historical input sequences, leading to high computational cost and padding-induced positional bias, which are unsuitable for real-time online applications.
en.method: Explicitly separates contextual encoding from latest-measurement updating: uses chunk-based Theta Attention Pooling and Fourier Seasonality Basis for local pattern extraction, a causal context encoder with GRU and Causal Cosine Attention for temporal dependencies, and a recursive-filtering-inspired decoder to update the context state with the latest measurement.
en.result: On a public dataset under multiple fixed-temperature conditions, C2L-Net achieves state-of-the-art or competitive accuracy with fewer parameters, up to 60x faster inference than recent baselines, and robust performance on unseen driving profiles.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to achieve efficient and accurate online SOC estimation with a short historical window, suitable for resource-constrained battery management systems.

idea_spark.idea: Combine recursive filtering ideas with attention mechanisms to design a lightweight decoder that enables rapid adaptation to dynamic changes.
idea_spark.risk: Short historical window may lose long-term dependency information under extreme operating conditions; needs validation for deep discharge or fast charge/discharge scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws inspiration from Kalman filtering in control theory by explicitly modeling the state update step as a learnable module to enhance online responsiveness.

papers.title: DegBins: Degradation-Driven Binning for Depth Super-Resolution
papers.summary: Depth super-resolution (DSR) aims to recover a high-resolution (HR) depth map from its low-resolution (LR) counterpart. With color image guidance, this task is typically formulated as learning the residual between HR and LR in a low-dimensional feature space. However, this additive formulation is insufficient to accurately capture the complex relationship between HR and LR, especially under spatially varying degradations. In this paper, we introduce DegBins, a novel DSR framework that leverages degradation-driven binning to adaptively enhance residual modeling. Specifically, DegBins reformulates the regression-based DSR as a hybrid classification-regression problem, where the residual depth is represented as a linear combination of discrete depth bins weighted by their learned probability distribution, yielding more flexible and expressive representations. Furthermore, DegBins models the degradation relationship between HR and LR in a high-dimensional feature space, enabling adaptive bin range adjustment and probability optimization conditioned on local degradation characteristics. To progressively improve reconstruction quality, DegBins adopts a multi-stage refinement scheme, where each stage performs finer-grained bin partitioning and probability updating based on the former estimation. This coarse-to-fine design facilitates more accurate depth recovery, particularly in regions with severe degradations or complex structural variations. Extensive experiments across five benchmarks demonstrate that DegBins consistently outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods in terms of accuracy, robustness, and generalization.

ai.tldr: 提出DegBins框架，将深度超分辨率重构为混合分类-回归问题，通过退化驱动的分箱和渐进式细化提升精度。
ai.motivation: 现有方法在低维特征空间学习残差，难以应对空间变化的退化，导致复杂关系建模不足。
ai.method: 将残差深度表示为离散深度箱的线性组合，在高维特征空间自适应调整箱范围和概率，并采用多阶段细化方案。
ai.result: 在五个基准数据集上，精度、鲁棒性和泛化性均优于现有方法。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种处理退化不均匀场景的深度超分辨率新思路，可应用于图像增强、3D重建等任务。

idea_spark.idea: 将连续回归转化为离散箱的加权组合，结合退化感知的箱调整，提升模型对复杂退化的适应性。
idea_spark.risk: 分箱数量和范围的选择可能影响性能，需针对不同退化类型进行调优。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受深度超分辨率中残差学习局限性的启发，借鉴分类-回归混合思想，利用退化信息动态调整表示空间。

en.tldr: Proposes DegBins, a depth super-resolution framework that reformulates the task as a hybrid classification-regression problem via degradation-driven binning and multi-stage refinement.
en.motivation: Existing methods learn residuals in low-dimensional feature space, insufficient for spatially varying degradations and complex HR-LR relationships.
en.method: Represents residual depth as a linear combination of discrete depth bins with learned probabilities, adapts bin ranges and probabilities in high-dimensional feature space, and employs multi-stage coarse-to-fine refinement.
en.result: Outperforms state-of-the-art methods on five benchmarks in accuracy, robustness, and generalization.
en.help_to_user: Offers a novel approach for depth super-resolution under non-uniform degradation, applicable to image enhancement and 3D reconstruction.

idea_spark.idea: Convert continuous regression into weighted combination of discrete bins with degradation-aware adjustment for better adaptability.
idea_spark.risk: Choice of bin count and range may affect performance and require tuning for different degradation types.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by limitations of residual learning in depth super-resolution, hybrid classification-regression with degradation information dynamically shapes the representation space.

papers.title: A Cold Diffusion Approach for Percussive Dereverberation
papers.summary: Most recent advances in audio dereverberation focus almost exclusively on speech, leaving percussive and drum signals largely unexplored despite their importance in music production. Percussive dereverberation poses distinct challenges due to sharp transients and dense temporal structure. In this work, we propose a cold diffusion framework for dereverberating stereo drum stems (downmixes), modeling reverberation as a deterministic degradation process that progressively transforms anechoic signals into reverberant ones. We investigate two reverse-process parameterizations, Direct (next-state) and a Delta-normalized residual (velocity-style) prediction, and implement the framework using both a UNet and a diffusion Transformer backbone. The models are trained and evaluated on curated datasets comprising both acoustic and electronic drum recordings, with reverberation generated using a combination of synthetic and real room impulse responses. Extensive experiments on in-domain and fully out-of-domain test sets demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms strong score-based and conditional diffusion baselines, evaluated using signal-based and perceptual metrics tailored to percussive audio.

ai.tldr: 提出冷扩散框架用于立体声鼓组信号的去混响，在合成和真实房间脉冲响应数据集上优于基线方法。
ai.motivation: 现有音频去混响主要针对语音，打击乐和鼓信号因尖锐瞬态和密集时间结构而未被充分研究。
ai.method: 将混响建模为确定性退化过程，采用直接预测和Delta归一化残差两种反向参数化，基于UNet和扩散Transformer实现。
ai.result: 在域内和完全域外测试集上，所提方法在信号和感知指标上一致优于强基线。
ai.help_to_user: 为音乐制作中的鼓组去混响提供有效工具，提升混音质量。

en.tldr: Proposes a cold diffusion framework for stereo drum stem dereverberation, outperforming baselines on synthetic and real room impulse response datasets.
en.motivation: Existing audio dereverberation focuses on speech, leaving percussive and drum signals underexplored due to sharp transients and dense temporal structure.
en.method: Models reverberation as a deterministic degradation process, using direct and delta-normalized residual reverse parameterizations with UNet and diffusion Transformer backbones.
en.result: Consistently outperforms strong score-based and conditional diffusion baselines on in-domain and out-of-domain test sets using percussive-tailored metrics.
en.help_to_user: Provides an effective tool for drum dereverberation in music production, improving mix quality.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining cold diffusion with conditional control for adaptive dereverberation across different reverb types.
idea_spark.risk: May incur high computational cost for extreme non-stationary noise or real-time requirements.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the success of cold diffusion in image restoration, adapted to audio for modeling degradation processes.

papers.title: Demystifying Deep Reinforcement Learning: A Neuro-Symbolic Framework for Interpretable Open RAN Automation
papers.summary: Open Radio Access Networks (O-RAN) are increasingly adopting data-driven control through Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) to optimize complex tasks such as network slicing and mobility management. However, the deployment of DRL in carrier-grade networks is hindered by its inherent opacity and stochastic execution, which limit operator trust, auditability, and safe deployment. Existing explainable AI (XAI) approaches primarily provide post-hoc insights and fail to produce executable, interpretable policies suitable for operational environments. In this paper, we present DeRAN, a neuro-symbolic framework that bridges the gap between DRL performance and operational transparency by distilling black-box DRL policies into human-readable symbolic representations. DeRAN introduces a concept-driven abstraction layer that transforms high-dimensional network telemetry into a compact set of semantically meaningful features, enabling interpretable policy learning. Building on the semantically grounded concepts, DeRAN synthesizes symbolic policies using deep symbolic regression (DSR) for continuous control and neurally guided differentiable logic (NUDGE) for discrete decision-making. We implement DeRAN on a live 5G O-RAN testbed and evaluate it on two representative use cases. Experimental results demonstrate that DeRAN achieves 78\% and 87\% of DRL's cumulative rewards in the two use cases, while offering interpretability and auditability by design. Source code is available at https://github.com/Jadejavu/A-Neuro-Symbolic-Framework-for-Interpretable-Open-RAN-Automation

ai.tldr: 提出DeRAN神经符号框架，将DRL黑盒策略蒸馏为可读符号表示，在5G O-RAN测试床上实现可解释自动化。
ai.motivation: DRL在O-RAN中性能优异但缺乏可解释性，现有XAI方法仅提供事后解释，无法生成可执行的可解释策略。
ai.method: 通过概念驱动抽象层将高维遥测数据压缩为语义特征，结合深度符号回归（连续控制）和可微逻辑（离散决策）合成符号策略。
ai.result: 在两种用例中分别达到DRL累积奖励的78%和87%，同时提供内在可解释性和可审计性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解DRL决策逻辑，提升运营商对网络自动化系统的信任和安全性。

idea_spark.idea: 将概念抽象层与符号回归结合，实现从高维输入到可读策略的端到端蒸馏。
idea_spark.risk: 符号策略可能牺牲部分性能，且概念定义依赖领域知识。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受神经符号AI和可解释机器学习启发，将黑盒模型转化为白盒规则。

en.tldr: Proposes DeRAN, a neuro-symbolic framework that distills black-box DRL policies into human-readable symbolic representations, achieving interpretable O-RAN automation on a live 5G testbed.
en.motivation: DRL excels in O-RAN but lacks interpretability; existing XAI methods only provide post-hoc insights and cannot produce executable interpretable policies.
en.method: Uses a concept-driven abstraction layer to compress high-dimensional telemetry into semantic features, then synthesizes symbolic policies via deep symbolic regression (continuous control) and differentiable logic (discrete decisions).
en.result: Achieves 78% and 87% of DRL's cumulative rewards in two use cases while offering interpretability and auditability by design.
en.help_to_user: Helps operators understand DRL decision logic, enhancing trust and safety in network automation systems.

idea_spark.idea: Combines concept abstraction with symbolic regression for end-to-end distillation from high-dimensional input to readable policies.
idea_spark.risk: Symbolic policies may sacrifice some performance, and concept definition relies on domain expertise.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by neuro-symbolic AI and interpretable machine learning, transforming black-box models into white-box rules.

papers.title: ASIA: an Autonomous System Identification Agent
papers.summary: Over the years, research in system identification has provided a rich set of methods for learning dynamical models, together with well-established theoretical guarantees. In practice, however, the choice of model class, training algorithm, and hyperparameter tuning is still largely left to empirical trial-and-error, requiring substantial expert time and domain experience. Motivated by recent advances in agentic artificial intelligence, we present ASIA, a framework that delegates this iterative search to a large language model acting as an autonomous coding agent. Building on existing agentic platforms, ASIA closes the loop between hypothesis, implementation, and evaluation without human intervention, requiring only a plain-English description of the identification problem. We conduct an empirical study of ASIA on two system identification benchmarks and analyse the agent's search behaviour, the architectures and training strategies it discovers, and the quality of the resulting models. We also discuss the potential of the approach and its current limitations, including implicit test leakage, reduced methodological transparency, and reproducibility concerns.

ai.tldr: 提出ASIA框架，利用大语言模型作为自主编码代理，自动完成系统辨识中的模型选择、训练和调参，无需人工干预。
ai.motivation: 传统系统辨识依赖专家经验和反复试错，耗时且门槛高。
ai.method: 基于现有代理平台，让大语言模型闭环执行假设、实现和评估，仅需自然语言描述问题。
ai.result: 在两个基准上验证了ASIA的有效性，分析了搜索行为、发现的架构和训练策略，同时指出了测试泄露、透明度低和可复现性差等局限。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户自动完成系统辨识流程，降低对领域专家的依赖。

idea_spark.idea: 将大语言模型作为自主代理，闭环执行系统辨识的假设、实现和评估。

idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴代理AI的闭环思想，将专家经验自动化，减少人工干预。

en.tldr: ASIA is a framework that uses a large language model as an autonomous coding agent to automate system identification, including model selection, training, and hyperparameter tuning, requiring only a plain-English problem description.
en.motivation: Traditional system identification relies on expert trial-and-error, which is time-consuming and requires domain expertise.
en.method: Building on existing agentic platforms, ASIA closes the loop between hypothesis, implementation, and evaluation using a large language model without human intervention.
en.result: Empirical study on two benchmarks shows ASIA's effectiveness, with analysis of search behavior, discovered architectures, and training strategies, while noting limitations such as test leakage, reduced transparency, and reproducibility concerns.
en.help_to_user: Automates the system identification pipeline, reducing the need for domain experts.

idea_spark.idea: Use a large language model as an autonomous agent to close the loop of hypothesis, implementation, and evaluation in system identification.
idea_spark.risk: Risks include implicit test leakage, lack of methodological transparency, and reproducibility issues.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by recent advances in agentic AI to automate expert-driven iterative processes.

papers.title: Product-of-Gaussian-Mixture Diffusion Models for Joint Nonlinear MRI Reconstruction
papers.summary: Recently, diffusion models have attracted considerable attention for magnetic resonance image reconstruction due to their high sample quality. However, most existing methods rely on large networks with opaque time-conditioning mechanisms, and require offline coil sensitivity estimation. This results in limited interpretability of the reconstruction process and reduced flexibility in the acquisition setup. To address these limitations, we jointly reconstruct the image and the coil sensitivities by combining the parameter-efficient product-of-Gaussian-mixture diffusion model as an image prior with a classical smoothness prior on the coil sensitivities. The proposed method is fast and robust to both contrast and anatomical distribution shifts as well as changing k-space trajectories. Finally, we propose a more expressive parameterization of the image prior which improves results in denoising and magnetic resonance image reconstruction.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于高斯混合乘积扩散模型的联合非线性MRI重建方法，同时重建图像和线圈灵敏度，无需离线估计。
ai.motivation: 现有扩散模型用于MRI重建时依赖大型网络、不透明的时间条件机制和离线线圈灵敏度估计，导致可解释性差、采集设置灵活性低。

ai.result: 方法快速，对对比度、解剖分布变化及k空间轨迹变化均具有鲁棒性，且更优的图像先验参数化提升了去噪和MRI重建效果。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种可解释、灵活且鲁棒的MRI重建方案，适用于不同采集设置和分布偏移场景。

idea_spark.risk: 联合优化可能增加计算复杂度，且平滑先验假设可能不适用于快速变化的线圈灵敏度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 参数高效先验与经典正则化结合，提升模型可解释性和鲁棒性。

en.tldr: Proposes a joint nonlinear MRI reconstruction method using a product-of-Gaussian-mixture diffusion model as an image prior, simultaneously reconstructing the image and coil sensitivities without offline estimation.
en.motivation: Existing diffusion models for MRI reconstruction rely on large networks with opaque time-conditioning and offline coil sensitivity estimation, leading to limited interpretability and reduced flexibility in acquisition setup.
en.method: Combines a parameter-efficient product-of-Gaussian-mixture diffusion model as an image prior with a classical smoothness prior on coil sensitivities for joint reconstruction.
en.result: The method is fast and robust to contrast and anatomical distribution shifts as well as changing k-space trajectories, with improved denoising and reconstruction performance via a more expressive image prior parameterization.
en.help_to_user: Provides an interpretable, flexible, and robust MRI reconstruction approach suitable for various acquisition setups and distribution shifts.

idea_spark.idea: Jointly optimize coil sensitivity smoothness prior with diffusion model to avoid offline calibration.
idea_spark.risk: Joint optimization may increase computational complexity, and the smoothness assumption may not hold for rapidly varying coil sensitivities.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining parameter-efficient priors with classical regularization enhances model interpretability and robustness.

papers.title: ConFixGS: Learning to Fix Feedforward 3D Gaussian Splatting with Confidence-Aware Diffusion Priors in Driving Scenes
papers.summary: Feedforward 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) often struggles in trajectory-based sparse-view driving scenes. Existing Gaussian repair methods mainly target optimization-based 3DGS, while diffusion-based repair is typically restricted to iterative refinement near observed viewpoints, leaving feedforward 3DGS repair underexplored. We propose ConFixGS, a plug-and-play method that learns to fix feedforward 3DGS with confidence-aware diffusion priors. Starting from a pretrained feedforward model, ConFixGS generates diffusion-enhanced local pseudo-targets and validates them through reprojection-based cross-checking against support views. The resulting dense confidence maps guide refinement, enhancing reliable details while suppressing hallucinated or inconsistent evidence. On Waymo, nuScenes, and KITTI, ConFixGS improves challenging novel view synthesis, with PSNR gains of up to 3.68 dB and FID reduced by nearly half. Our results highlight confidence-aware fusion of generative priors and support-view consistency as a key principle for robust feedforward 3D driving scene reconstruction.

ai.tldr: 提出ConFixGS，一种即插即用方法，利用置信度感知的扩散先验修复前馈3D高斯泼溅在驾驶场景中的稀疏视图重建问题。
ai.motivation: 前馈3DGS在轨迹稀疏的驾驶场景中表现不佳，现有修复方法多针对优化型3DGS或局限于观测视角附近，前馈3DGS修复尚未充分探索。
ai.method: 从预训练前馈模型出发，生成扩散增强的局部伪目标，通过重投影交叉验证与支持视图比对，得到密集置信度图，引导细化过程，增强可靠细节并抑制幻觉或不一致证据。
ai.result: 在Waymo、nuScenes和KITTI上，PSNR提升高达3.68 dB，FID降低近一半，显著改善新视角合成质量。
ai.help_to_user: 为驾驶场景中的前馈3DGS提供了一种即插即用的修复方案，可提升稀疏视图下的重建鲁棒性和质量。

idea_spark.idea: 利用扩散模型生成伪目标，并通过重投影交叉验证构建置信度图，引导前馈模型的自适应细化。
idea_spark.risk: 扩散模型生成伪目标可能引入偏差，交叉验证的阈值选择需谨慎，计算开销可能增加。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将生成先验与几何一致性校验结合，以置信度图作为桥梁，平衡细节增强与幻觉抑制。

en.tldr: Proposes ConFixGS, a plug-and-play method that leverages confidence-aware diffusion priors to repair feedforward 3D Gaussian Splatting in sparse-view driving scenes.
en.motivation: Feedforward 3DGS struggles in trajectory-based sparse-view driving scenes; existing repair methods target optimization-based 3DGS or are limited to iterative refinement near observed viewpoints, leaving feedforward 3DGS repair underexplored.
en.method: Starting from a pretrained feedforward model, generates diffusion-enhanced local pseudo-targets, validates them via reprojection-based cross-checking against support views, and produces dense confidence maps to guide refinement, enhancing reliable details while suppressing hallucinated or inconsistent evidence.
en.result: On Waymo, nuScenes, and KITTI, PSNR gains up to 3.68 dB and FID reduced by nearly half, significantly improving novel view synthesis.
en.help_to_user: Provides a plug-and-play repair method for feedforward 3DGS in driving scenes, improving reconstruction robustness and quality under sparse views.

idea_spark.idea: Use diffusion models to generate pseudo-targets and build confidence maps via reprojection cross-checking to guide adaptive refinement of feedforward models.
idea_spark.risk: Diffusion-generated pseudo-targets may introduce bias; cross-checking threshold selection requires care; computational overhead may increase.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combine generative priors with geometric consistency checking, using confidence maps as a bridge to balance detail enhancement and hallucination suppression.

papers.title: ATLAS: Efficient Out-of-Core Inference for Billion-Scale Graph Neural Networks
papers.summary: Graph Neural Network (GNN) inference on billion-scale graphs is critical for domains like fintech and recommendation systems. Full-graph inference on these large graphs can be challenging due to high communication costs in distributed settings and high I/O costs in disk-backed Out-of-Core (OOC) settings. Existing OOC systems, operating across disk and memory, primarily focus on GNN training and perform poorly for full-graph inference due to massive read amplification, irregular I/O, and memory pressure. We present ATLAS, a disk-based GNN inference framework that enables efficient full-graph, layer-wise inference on graphs whose topologies, features and intermediate embeddings exceed the available memory on single machines. ATLAS replaces gather-based execution with a broadcast-based model that enables sequential, single-pass streaming reads of features and embeddings per layer. A tiered memory-disk hierarchy with minimum-pending-message eviction, graph reordering and a GPU-accelerated pipeline sustains high throughput within $128$ GiB RAM and $2$ TiB SSD. Across out-of-core graphs with up to $4$B edges and $550$ GiB features and multiple GNN architectures, ATLAS improves end-to-end inference time by $\approx12$--$30\times$ over State-of-the-Art (SOTA) OOC baselines on a single workstation, while remaining within $\approx5\%$ when features fit in memory.

ai.tldr: 提出ATLAS，一种基于磁盘的图神经网络推理框架，在单机128GB内存和2TB SSD上高效处理十亿级图的全图推理。
ai.motivation: 现有磁盘支持的图神经网络系统主要针对训练设计，在全图推理时面临读放大、不规则I/O和内存压力等问题。
ai.method: 采用广播执行模型替代收集执行，实现每层特征和嵌入的顺序单遍流式读取；设计分层内存-磁盘层次结构、最小待处理消息驱逐策略、图重排序和GPU加速流水线。
ai.result: 在高达40亿边和550GB特征的超大规模图上，相比最先进基线提升约12-30倍推理速度；当特征可装入内存时，性能差距在5%以内。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户在单机资源受限环境下高效完成十亿级图神经网络的推理任务。

idea_spark.idea: 利用图重排序和最小待处理消息驱逐策略优化I/O模式，减少随机访问。
idea_spark.risk: 广播模型可能不适用于某些需要高度聚合的图结构。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将训练优化的OOC系统改造为推理专用，启发其他领域类似迁移。

zh.motivation: 现有磁盘支持的图神经网络系统主要针对训练设计，在全图推理时面临读放大、不规则I/O和内存压力等问题。

en.tldr: ATLAS is a disk-based GNN inference framework that efficiently performs full-graph inference on billion-scale graphs using a single machine with 128GB RAM and 2TB SSD.
en.motivation: Existing out-of-core GNN systems are optimized for training and suffer from read amplification, irregular I/O, and memory pressure during full-graph inference.
en.method: Replaces gather-based execution with a broadcast-based model for sequential single-pass streaming reads; employs a tiered memory-disk hierarchy, minimum-pending-message eviction, graph reordering, and a GPU-accelerated pipeline.
en.result: Achieves 12-30x speedup over state-of-the-art baselines on graphs with up to 4B edges and 550GB features; within 5% of in-memory performance when features fit in RAM.
en.help_to_user: Enables efficient billion-scale GNN inference on a single workstation with limited memory and disk resources.

idea_spark.idea: Optimize I/O patterns using graph reordering and minimum-pending-message eviction to reduce random access.
idea_spark.risk: The broadcast model may not suit graph structures requiring heavy aggregation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Adapting training-oriented OOC systems for inference inspires similar cross-domain optimizations.

papers.title: KAN Text to Vision? The Exploration of Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks for Multi-Scale Sequence-Based Pose Animation from Sign Language Notation
papers.summary: Sign language production from symbolic notation offers a scalable route to accessible sign animation. We present KANMultiSign, a multi-scale sequence generator that translates HamNoSys notation into two-dimensional human pose sequences. Our framework makes two complementary contributions. First, we introduce a coarse-to-fine generation strategy with multi-scale supervision: the model is first guided by an intermediate body--hand--face scaffold to encourage global structural coherence, and then refines fine-grained hand articulation to improve finger-level detail. Second, we investigate integrating Kolmogorov--Arnold Network modules into a Transformer backbone, using learnable univariate function primitives to model the highly non-linear mapping from discrete phonological symbols to continuous body kinematics with a compact parameterization. Experiments on multiple public corpora spanning Polish, German, Greek, and French sign languages show consistent reductions in dynamic time warping based joint error compared with a strong notation-to-pose baseline, while using substantially fewer parameters. Controlled ablations further indicate that KAN-based variants substantially reduce parameter count while maintaining competitive performance when coupled with multi-scale supervision, rather than serving as the main driver of accuracy gains. These findings position multi-scale supervision as the key mechanism for improving notation-conditioned pose generation, with KAN offering a compact alternative for efficient modeling. Our code will be publicly available.

ai.tldr: 提出KANMultiSign框架，利用KAN模块和多尺度监督从符号标注生成人体姿态动画。
ai.motivation: 符号语言标注到姿态动画的映射高度非线性，现有方法参数多、细节不足。
ai.method: 采用粗到细生成策略，先构建身体-手-脸骨架，再细化手指；在Transformer中集成KAN模块学习非线性映射。
ai.result: 在多种手语数据集上，动态时间规整误差一致降低，参数更少；多尺度监督是精度提升的关键。
ai.help_to_user: 为符号语言动画生成提供高效、参数紧凑的解决方案。

idea_spark.idea: 探索KAN在其他非线性映射任务中的应用，如语音到口型动画。
idea_spark.risk: KAN模块在复杂场景下可能不如传统Transformer稳定，需更多调参。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将符号语言的结构化先验（如身体-手-脸分层）与紧凑网络结合，启发其他结构化输出任务。

en.tldr: Proposes KANMultiSign, a framework using KAN modules and multi-scale supervision to generate pose animations from sign language notation.
en.motivation: The mapping from symbolic notation to pose animation is highly nonlinear; existing methods are parameter-heavy and lack fine detail.
en.method: Employs a coarse-to-fine generation strategy with a body-hand-face scaffold, and integrates KAN modules into a Transformer backbone to learn the nonlinear mapping.
en.result: Consistent reduction in dynamic time warping joint error across multiple sign language corpora with fewer parameters; multi-scale supervision is the key driver of accuracy.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient, parameter-compact solution for sign language animation generation.

idea_spark.idea: Explore KAN in other nonlinear mapping tasks, such as speech-to-lip animation.
idea_spark.risk: KAN modules may be less stable than traditional Transformers in complex scenarios, requiring more tuning.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining structured priors (e.g., body-hand-face hierarchy) with compact networks inspires other structured output tasks.

papers.title: Measurement-Adapted Eigentask Representations for Photon-Limited Optical Readout
papers.summary: Optical readout in low-light imaging is fundamentally limited by measurement noise, including photon shot noise, detector noise, and quantization error. In this regime, downstream inference depends not only on the optical front end, but also on how noisy high-dimensional sensor measurements are represented before classification or decision-making. Here we show that eigentasks provide a measurement-adapted representation for optical sensor outputs by ordering readout features according to their resolvability under noise. Using experimental data from a lens-based optical imaging system and a reanalysis of published data from a single-photon-detection neural network, we find that eigentask representations frequently outperform standard baselines including principal component analysis and filtering-based compression. The advantage is most pronounced in photon-limited, few-shot, and higher-difficulty classification regimes. In few-shot MPEG-7 classification, for example, the advantage over other methods reaches about 10 percentage points as the number of classes increases. In these settings, eigentasks yield more informative low-dimensional features and improve sample-efficient downstream learning. These results identify measurement-adapted representation as a promising strategy for optical inference when photon budget, acquisition time, and task complexity are constrained.

ai.tldr: 提出测量自适应本征任务表示，用于光子受限光学读出，在低光成像中提升分类性能。
ai.motivation: 低光成像中测量噪声（光子散粒噪声、探测器噪声、量化误差）严重限制下游推理，传统表示方法（如PCA）未考虑噪声可分辨性。
ai.method: 利用本征任务按噪声可分辨性排序光学传感器输出特征，生成测量自适应表示。
ai.result: 在光子受限、少样本和高难度分类场景中，本征任务表示优于PCA和滤波压缩，在MPEG-7少样本分类中准确率提升约10个百分点。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解在光子预算有限时如何设计更有效的特征表示，提升低光成像下的分类性能。

idea_spark.idea: 将本征任务与深度学习结合，自动学习噪声自适应特征排序。
idea_spark.risk: 本征任务计算可能依赖精确噪声模型，实际噪声分布复杂时性能可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受噪声可分辨性启发，可探索其他任务驱动的特征选择策略。

zh.motivation: 低光成像中测量噪声（光子散粒噪声、探测器噪声、量化误差）严重限制下游推理，传统表示方法（如PCA）未考虑噪声可分辨性。

idea_spark.risk: 本征任务计算可能依赖精确噪声模型，实际噪声分布复杂时性能可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受噪声可分辨性启发，可探索其他任务驱动的特征选择策略。

en.tldr: Proposes measurement-adapted eigentask representations for photon-limited optical readout, improving classification in low-light imaging.
en.motivation: Measurement noise (photon shot noise, detector noise, quantization error) fundamentally limits downstream inference in low-light imaging; conventional representations like PCA ignore noise resolvability.
en.method: Eigentasks order optical sensor output features by their resolvability under noise, yielding measurement-adapted representations.
en.result: Eigentask representations outperform PCA and filtering-based compression in photon-limited, few-shot, and high-difficulty classification; e.g., ~10 percentage point gain in few-shot MPEG-7 classification.
en.help_to_user: Helps design more effective feature representations under limited photon budgets, improving classification in low-light imaging.

idea_spark.idea: Combine eigentasks with deep learning to automatically learn noise-adaptive feature ordering.
idea_spark.risk: Eigentask computation may rely on accurate noise models; performance could degrade under complex real-world noise.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by noise resolvability, explore other task-driven feature selection strategies.

papers.title: SCALAR: A Neurosymbolic Framework for Automated Conjecture and Reasoning in Quantum Circuit Analysis
papers.summary: In this paper, we present SCALAR (Symbolic Conjecture and LLM-Assisted Reasoning), a neurosymbolic framework for automated conjecture generation in quantum circuit analysis built on top of the CUDA-Q open source framework. The system integrates quantum simulation, symbolic conjecture generation, and LLM-based interpretation. We evaluate SCALAR on 82 MaxCut instances from the MQLib benchmark dataset and extend the analysis to 2,000 randomly generated graphs across four topologies: regular, Erdos-Renyi, Barabasi-Albert, and Watts-Strogatz. The framework generates conjectured bounds relating optimal QAOA parameters to graph invariants, including known relationships such as periodicity constraints on the phase separation parameter $γ$. SCALAR also recovers previously reported parameter transfer phenomena across structurally similar instances. Additionally, the system identifies correlations between graph structural features and optimization landscape properties, which we characterize through invariant-based descriptors. Using CUDA-Q tensor network simulator, we scale experiments to instances of up to 77 qubits. We discuss the accuracy, generality, and limitations of the generated conjectures, including sensitivity to graph class and quantum circuit depth.

ai.tldr: 提出SCALAR框架，结合量子模拟、符号猜想生成与LLM解释，自动分析量子电路并生成关于QAOA参数与图不变量的猜想。
ai.motivation: 量子电路分析中手动猜想生成效率低，需要自动化方法辅助发现参数与图结构间的关系。
ai.method: 基于CUDA-Q框架，集成量子模拟器、符号猜想生成模块和LLM解释器，对MaxCut实例及多种随机图进行实验。
ai.result: 成功生成关于最优QAOA参数与图不变量（如周期约束）的猜想，复现参数迁移现象，并识别图结构与优化景观的相关性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户自动发现量子电路中的模式与关系，加速量子算法设计与优化。

idea_spark.idea: 将符号猜想生成与LLM结合，实现从数据到可解释猜想的闭环。
idea_spark.risk: 猜想可能对特定图类或电路深度敏感，泛化性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受神经符号方法在科学发现中应用的启发，将LLM作为解释与验证工具。

en.tldr: SCALAR is a neurosymbolic framework that integrates quantum simulation, symbolic conjecture generation, and LLM interpretation to automatically generate conjectures about QAOA parameters and graph invariants in quantum circuit analysis.
en.motivation: Manual conjecture generation in quantum circuit analysis is inefficient; automated methods are needed to discover relationships between parameters and graph structures.
en.method: Built on CUDA-Q, the system combines a quantum simulator, symbolic conjecture generator, and LLM interpreter, tested on MaxCut instances and random graphs of various topologies.
en.result: Generated conjectured bounds linking optimal QAOA parameters to graph invariants (e.g., periodicity constraints), recovered parameter transfer phenomena, and identified correlations between graph features and optimization landscapes.
en.help_to_user: Helps users automatically discover patterns and relationships in quantum circuits, accelerating algorithm design and optimization.

idea_spark.idea: Combine symbolic conjecture generation with LLMs to create a closed loop from data to interpretable conjectures.
idea_spark.risk: Conjectures may be sensitive to graph class or circuit depth; generalization needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by neurosymbolic approaches in scientific discovery, using LLMs as interpretation and verification tools.

papers.title: How Much is Brain Data Worth for Machine Learning?
papers.summary: If a person can solve a task, can measuring their brain make it easier to train a model to solve that task too? Recent NeuroAI work suggests that supplementing task training with neural recordings can modestly improve model performance and robustness. However, it is unclear when there should be a benefit from using neural data and how much benefit to expect. We formulate this question mathematically, and begin to address it theoretically using a simple, analytically tractable linear gaussian model of task targets and neural recordings. For a multimodal estimator trained on both brain data and task labels, we derive scaling laws for how performance scales with the numbers of brain and task samples. From these laws we derive relative value and exchange rates between brain samples and task samples, quantifying how much extra task samples neural data is worth as a function of task-brain alignment, neural and task noise, latent dimension, and brain data sample size. We also analyze test distribution shift, to identify conditions where brain-regularized learning can produce substantial robustness gains through learned invariances. Finally, under a fixed collection budget, we characterize the regimes in which brain data is worth collecting. Our results provide a foundation for understanding how valuable brain data could be for improving machine learning.

ai.tldr: 本文通过线性高斯模型推导了脑数据对机器学习性能提升的数学规律，量化了脑样本与任务样本的等价关系。
ai.motivation: 现有神经AI工作表明脑数据可提升模型性能，但缺乏理论指导何时以及多少收益。
ai.method: 构建多模态估计器，推导脑数据和任务标签的缩放定律，计算脑样本与任务样本的交换率。
ai.result: 脑数据的价值取决于任务-脑对齐、噪声、潜在维度等因素；在特定条件下脑数据可带来鲁棒性增益。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究者理解在何种情况下收集脑数据对机器学习有价值，以及如何量化其收益。

idea_spark.idea: 利用脑数据学习不变性，提升模型对分布偏移的鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 理论模型假设线性高斯，实际脑数据非线性且噪声复杂，结论可能受限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发在有限预算下优化数据采集策略，优先收集高对齐脑数据。

en.tldr: This paper mathematically derives scaling laws for how brain data improves machine learning, quantifying the exchange rate between brain samples and task samples.
en.motivation: Recent NeuroAI work shows neural data can modestly improve model performance, but lacks theoretical guidance on when and how much benefit to expect.
en.method: Uses a linear Gaussian model to derive scaling laws for a multimodal estimator trained on both brain data and task labels, computing relative value and exchange rates.
en.result: The value of brain data depends on task-brain alignment, noise, latent dimension, etc.; under certain conditions, brain data can yield substantial robustness gains via learned invariances.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers understand when collecting brain data is worthwhile for machine learning and how to quantify its benefits.

idea_spark.idea: Use brain data to learn invariances, improving model robustness to distribution shift.
idea_spark.risk: The theoretical model assumes linear Gaussian, while real brain data is nonlinear and noisy, potentially limiting conclusions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires optimizing data collection strategies under a fixed budget, prioritizing high-alignment brain data.

papers.title: Active Testing of Large Language Models via Approximate Neyman Allocation
papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) require reliable evaluation from pre-training to test-time scaling, making evaluation a recurring rather than one-off cost. As model scales grow and target tasks increasingly demand expert annotators, both the compute and labeling costs needed for each evaluation rise rapidly. Active testing aims to alleviate this bottleneck by approximating the evaluation result from a small but informative subset of the evaluation pool. However, existing approaches primarily target classification and break down on generative tasks. We introduce a novel active testing algorithm tailored to generative tasks. Our method leverages semantic entropy from surrogate models to stratify the evaluation pool and then conducts approximate Neyman allocation based on signals extracted from these surrogates. Across multiple language and multimodal benchmarks and a range of surrogate-target model pairs, our method significantly improves on baselines and closely tracks Oracle-Neyman, delivering up to 28\% MSE reduction over Uniform Sampling and an average of 22.9\% budget savings.

ai.tldr: 提出一种针对生成任务的主动测试方法，利用代理模型的语义熵进行分层，并采用近似内曼分配，显著降低评估成本。
ai.motivation: 大语言模型评估成本高昂，现有主动测试方法主要针对分类任务，无法有效处理生成任务。
ai.method: 使用代理模型计算语义熵对评估池分层，然后基于代理信号进行近似内曼分配，选择最具信息量的子集。
ai.result: 在多个语言和多模态基准上，相比均匀采样，均方误差降低最高28%，平均节省22.9%预算，接近Oracle-Neyman性能。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户以更低成本高效评估大语言模型在生成任务上的表现。

idea_spark.idea: 结合语义熵和分层抽样，可进一步优化为动态分配策略，适应不同任务难度。
idea_spark.risk: 代理模型的选择可能影响分层质量，需确保代理与目标模型语义一致性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将统计抽样理论（内曼分配）与语义理解结合，为高效评估提供新思路。

en.tldr: Proposes an active testing method for generative tasks, leveraging semantic entropy from surrogate models for stratification and approximate Neyman allocation, significantly reducing evaluation costs.
en.motivation: Evaluating large language models is costly; existing active testing methods focus on classification and fail on generative tasks.
en.method: Stratifies the evaluation pool using semantic entropy from surrogate models, then applies approximate Neyman allocation based on surrogate signals to select informative subsets.
en.result: On multiple language and multimodal benchmarks, achieves up to 28% MSE reduction over uniform sampling and average 22.9% budget savings, closely tracking Oracle-Neyman.
en.help_to_user: Helps users efficiently evaluate large language models on generative tasks at lower cost.

idea_spark.idea: Combining semantic entropy with stratified sampling could be further optimized into dynamic allocation strategies adapting to task difficulty.
idea_spark.risk: Quality of stratification depends on surrogate model choice; semantic consistency between surrogate and target is crucial.
idea_spark.inspiration: Integrating statistical sampling theory (Neyman allocation) with semantic understanding offers a new paradigm for efficient evaluation.

papers.title: LLM Translation of Compiler Intermediate Representation
papers.summary: GCC and LLVM underpin much of modern software infrastructure, relying on distinct Intermediate Representations (IRs) to drive optimizations and code generation. However, the semantic and structural differences between these IRs create significant barriers for cross-toolchain interaction, limiting the reuse of compiler frontends, backends, and optimization pipelines across programming languages and compilation ecosystems. Traditional rule-based translators have attempted to bridge this gap, but their complexity and maintenance cost have hindered practical adoption. In this context, Large Language Models (LLMs) appear to be an emerging technology that offers a data-driven alternative, capable of learning complex mappings between heterogeneous compiler IRs directly from sufficiently representative examples. To explore this approach, this paper presents IRIS-14B, a 14-billion-parameter transformer model fine-tuned to translate GIMPLE (as emitted by GCC) to LLVM IR (as emitted by LLVM). The model is trained on paired IRs extracted from C sources and evaluated on the GIMPLE-to-LLVM IR transformation applied to IRs derived from real-world C code and competitive programming problems. To the best of our knowledge, IRIS-14B is the first model trained explicitly for IR-to-IR translation. It outperforms the accuracy of widely used models, including the largest state-of-the-art open models available today, ranging from 13 to 1,000 billion parameters, by up to 44 percentage points. The proposed transformation supports the integration of LLMs as complementary components within hybrid neuro-symbolic compiler architectures, where models such as IRIS-14B act as interoperability layers enabling cross-toolchain workflows without modifying existing compiler passes, while traditional compiler infrastructure continues to perform deterministic compilation and optimization.

ai.tldr: 提出IRIS-14B模型，将GCC的GIMPLE IR翻译为LLVM IR，在跨工具链互操作中显著优于现有大模型。
ai.motivation: GCC和LLVM使用不同的中间表示（IR），导致跨工具链交互困难，传统规则翻译器复杂且维护成本高。
ai.method: 微调14B参数的Transformer模型IRIS-14B，在配对的GIMPLE和LLVM IR数据上训练，实现IR到IR的翻译。
ai.result: 在真实C代码和编程竞赛问题的IR翻译任务中，IRIS-14B准确率比现有13B-1000B参数模型高出最多44个百分点。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何利用LLM桥接不同编译器IR，实现前端、后端和优化管线的跨生态复用。

idea_spark.idea: 将LLM作为混合神经符号编译器架构中的互操作层，保留传统编译器的确定性优化，同时实现跨工具链工作流。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量配对IR数据，模型可能对未见过的IR模式泛化不足；大模型推理延迟可能影响编译效率。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴神经机器翻译的思路，将编译器IR视为一种形式语言，用序列到序列模型学习其映射关系。

en.tldr: Proposes IRIS-14B, a model that translates GCC's GIMPLE IR to LLVM IR, outperforming large models by up to 44 percentage points in cross-toolchain interoperability.
en.motivation: GCC and LLVM use distinct Intermediate Representations (IRs), hindering cross-toolchain interaction; traditional rule-based translators are complex and costly to maintain.
en.method: Fine-tunes a 14-billion-parameter Transformer model (IRIS-14B) on paired GIMPLE and LLVM IR data for IR-to-IR translation.
en.result: On IR translation tasks from real-world C code and competitive programming problems, IRIS-14B achieves up to 44 percentage points higher accuracy than existing models with 13B to 1000B parameters.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how LLMs can bridge different compiler IRs, enabling reuse of frontends, backends, and optimization pipelines across ecosystems.

idea_spark.idea: Deploy LLMs as interoperability layers in hybrid neuro-symbolic compiler architectures, preserving deterministic compilation while enabling cross-toolchain workflows.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on high-quality paired IR data; may generalize poorly to unseen IR patterns; inference latency could impact compilation speed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws from neural machine translation, treating compiler IRs as formal languages and learning mappings via sequence-to-sequence models.

papers.title: Primal-Dual Guided Decoding for Constrained Discrete Diffusion
papers.summary: Discrete diffusion models generate structured sequences by progressively unmasking tokens, but enforcing global property constraints during generation remains an open challenge. We propose primal-dual guided decoding, an inference-time method that formulates constrained generation as a KL-regularised optimisation problem and solves it online via adaptive Lagrangian multipliers. At each denoising step, the method modifies token logits through an additive, constraint-dependent bias, with multipliers updated by mirror descent based on constraint violation. The bias arises as the optimal KL-regularised projection of the constraint, so the constrained distribution remains as close as possible to the model's unconstrained distribution while still satisfying the constraint. The method requires no retraining and no additional model evaluations beyond standard sampling, supports multiple simultaneous constraints, and provides formal bounds on constraint violation. We evaluate our approach on topical text generation, molecular design, and music playlist generation, showing that a single algorithm instantiated via domain-specific scoring functions improves constraint satisfaction while preserving relevant domain-specific quality metrics.

ai.tldr: 提出一种推理时方法，通过自适应拉格朗日乘子在线求解KL正则化优化问题，在离散扩散模型中施加全局属性约束，无需重新训练。
ai.motivation: 离散扩散模型能逐步生成结构化序列，但如何在生成过程中强制执行全局属性约束仍是一个开放挑战。
ai.method: 将约束生成建模为KL正则化优化问题，在每一步去噪时通过镜像下降更新拉格朗日乘子，产生加性约束偏置来修正token logits。
ai.result: 在主题文本生成、分子设计和音乐播放列表生成任务上，该方法在保持领域质量指标的同时提高了约束满足度。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需重新训练即可在离散扩散模型中施加多种全局约束的通用推理时解码策略。

idea_spark.idea: 将约束偏置视为可学习的投影算子，或许能进一步加速推理或处理更复杂的约束。
idea_spark.risk: 拉格朗日乘子的更新可能不稳定，尤其在约束冲突严重时；多约束间的权衡需要仔细调参。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受原始-对偶优化与扩散模型结合的启发，可探索将约束信息直接注入模型训练阶段。

zh.result: 在主题文本生成、分子设计和音乐播放列表生成任务上，该方法在保持领域质量指标的同时提高了约束满足度。
