idea_spark.risk: 拉格朗日乘子的更新可能不稳定，尤其在约束冲突严重时；多约束间的权衡需要仔细调参。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受原始-对偶优化与扩散模型结合的启发，可探索将约束信息直接注入模型训练阶段。

en.tldr: Proposes an inference-time method that solves a KL-regularised optimisation problem online via adaptive Lagrangian multipliers to enforce global property constraints in discrete diffusion models without retraining.
en.motivation: Discrete diffusion models generate structured sequences by progressively unmasking tokens, but enforcing global property constraints during generation remains an open challenge.
en.method: Formulates constrained generation as a KL-regularised optimisation problem and solves it online via adaptive Lagrangian multipliers, modifying token logits with an additive constraint-dependent bias updated by mirror descent.
en.result: On topical text generation, molecular design, and music playlist generation, the method improves constraint satisfaction while preserving domain-specific quality metrics.
en.help_to_user: Provides a general inference-time decoding strategy to impose multiple global constraints in discrete diffusion models without retraining.

idea_spark.idea: Treating the constraint bias as a learnable projection operator might further accelerate inference or handle more complex constraints.
idea_spark.risk: Lagrangian multiplier updates may become unstable under severe constraint violations; trade-offs among multiple constraints require careful tuning.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the combination of primal-dual optimization and diffusion models, one could explore injecting constraint information directly into the model training phase.

papers.title: Cplus2ASP: Computing Action Language C+ in Answer Set Programming
papers.summary: We present Version 2 of system Cplus2ASP, which implements the definite fragment of action language C+. Its input language is fully compatible with the language of the Causal Calculator Version 2, but the new system is significantly faster thanks to modern answer set solving techniques. The translation implemented in the system is a composition of several recent theoretical results. The system orchestrates a tool chain, consisting of f2lp, clingo, iclingo, and as2transition. Under the incremental execution mode, the system translates a C+ description into the input language of iclingo, exploiting its incremental grounding mechanism. The correctness of this execution is justified by the module theorem extended to programs with nested expressions. In addition, the input language of the system has many useful features, such as external atoms by means of Lua calls and the user interactive mode. The system supports extensible multi-modal translations for other action languages, such as B and BC, as well.

ai.tldr: 介绍Cplus2ASP系统第二版，实现动作语言C+的确定片段，通过现代回答集求解技术显著提升性能。
ai.motivation: 提升动作语言C+推理系统的效率，利用增量执行和模块定理保证正确性。
ai.method: 将C+描述翻译为iclingo输入语言，组合f2lp、clingo、iclingo、as2transition工具链，支持Lua外部原子和用户交互模式。
ai.result: 新系统比Causal Calculator第二版更快，支持多模态翻译扩展至其他动作语言如B和BC。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户高效地使用动作语言C+进行推理，并支持交互式开发和扩展。

idea_spark.idea: 将动作语言C+的推理与增量回答集求解结合，实现高效且可扩展的推理引擎。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖特定工具链（如clingo），可能限制跨平台兼容性；增量执行对复杂动作描述的正确性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用现代回答集求解技术优化传统动作语言推理，启发其他逻辑形式化系统的性能提升。

en.tldr: Introduces Version 2 of Cplus2ASP, implementing the definite fragment of action language C+ with significantly improved performance via modern answer set solving techniques.
en.motivation: To improve the efficiency of reasoning in action language C+ by leveraging incremental execution and the module theorem for correctness.
en.method: Translates C+ descriptions into iclingo input language, orchestrating a tool chain of f2lp, clingo, iclingo, and as2transition, with support for Lua external atoms and user interactive mode.
en.result: The new system is significantly faster than the Causal Calculator Version 2 and supports extensible multi-modal translations for other action languages like B and BC.
en.help_to_user: Helps users perform efficient reasoning with action language C+ and supports interactive development and extensibility.

idea_spark.idea: Combine action language C+ reasoning with incremental answer set solving for an efficient and scalable inference engine.
idea_spark.risk: Dependence on specific tool chains (e.g., clingo) may limit cross-platform compatibility; correctness of incremental execution for complex action descriptions needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging modern answer set solving techniques to optimize traditional action language reasoning inspires performance improvements in other logical formalisms.

papers.title: DiagnosticIQ: A Benchmark for LLM-Based Industrial Maintenance Action Recommendation from Symbolic Rules
papers.summary: Monitoring complex industrial assets relies on engineer-authored symbolic rules that trigger based on sensor conditions and prompt technicians to perform corrective actions. The bottleneck is not detection but response: translating rules into maintenance steps requires asset-specific knowledge gained through years of practice. We investigate whether LLMs can serve as decision support for this rule-to-action step and introduce \ours{}, a benchmark of 6{,}690 expert-validated multiple-choice questions from 118 rule-action pairs across 16 asset types. We contribute (i) a symbolic-to-MCQA pipeline normalizing rules to Disjunctive Normal Form with embedding-based distractor sampling, (ii) five variants probing distinct failure modes (Pro, Pert, Verbose, Aug, Rationale), and (iii) a benchmark of 29 LLMs and 4 embedding baselines. A human evaluation (9 practitioners, mean 45.0\%) confirms \ours{} requires specialist knowledge beyond operational experience. Three findings stand out. The frontier has closed: the top three LLMs lie within one Macro point, with Bradley-Terry Elo placing claude-opus-4-6 30 points above the next model. Yet \ours{}\,Pro exposes brittleness, with every model losing 13--60\% relative accuracy under distractor expansion. \ours{}\,Aug exposes pattern-matching: under condition inversion, frontier models still select the original answer 49--63\% of the time. The deployment bottleneck is not capability but calibration: frontier models handle template-style fault detection but break under structural perturbation.

ai.tldr: 提出DiagnosticIQ基准，包含6690个专家验证的多选题，评估LLM从符号规则推荐工业维护动作的能力。
ai.motivation: 工业资产监控依赖工程师编写的符号规则，但将规则转化为维护步骤需要多年经验，LLM可作为决策支持工具。
ai.method: 构建符号到MCQA的流水线，将规则归一化为析取范式，使用嵌入采样干扰项，设计五个变体（Pro、Pert、Verbose、Aug、Rationale）探测不同失败模式。
ai.result: 顶级LLM性能接近，但Pro变体下所有模型相对准确率下降13-60%；Aug变体下前沿模型在条件反转时仍49-63%选择原答案。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户了解LLM在工业维护决策中的能力与局限，特别是对结构扰动的脆弱性。

idea_spark.idea: 利用LLM生成对抗性干扰项，构建更鲁棒的工业维护决策支持系统。
idea_spark.risk: LLM可能依赖模式匹配而非真正理解规则，导致在结构变化时失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合符号推理与LLM的混合系统，以增强对规则结构变化的鲁棒性。

en.tldr: Introduces DiagnosticIQ, a benchmark of 6,690 expert-validated multiple-choice questions evaluating LLMs for industrial maintenance action recommendation from symbolic rules.
en.motivation: Monitoring industrial assets relies on symbolic rules, but translating rules into maintenance steps requires years of experience; LLMs could serve as decision support.
en.method: A symbolic-to-MCQA pipeline normalizes rules to Disjunctive Normal Form with embedding-based distractor sampling, and five variants probe distinct failure modes (Pro, Pert, Verbose, Aug, Rationale).
en.result: Top LLMs perform similarly, but under Pro variant all models lose 13-60% relative accuracy; under Aug variant frontier models still select original answer 49-63% of the time under condition inversion.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand LLM capabilities and limitations in industrial maintenance decision-making, especially brittleness under structural perturbations.

idea_spark.idea: Use LLMs to generate adversarial distractors for building more robust industrial maintenance decision support systems.
idea_spark.risk: LLMs may rely on pattern matching rather than true understanding, failing under structural changes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Hybrid systems combining symbolic reasoning with LLMs to enhance robustness against rule structure variations.

papers.title: GenMed: A Pairwise Generative Reformulation of Medical Diagnostic Tasks
papers.summary: Data-driven medical AI is traditionally formulated as a discriminative mapping from input $X$ to output $Y$ via a learned function $f$, which does not generalize well across heterogeneous data and modalities encountered in real-world clinical settings. In this work, we propose a fundamentally different, generative paradigm. We model the joint distribution $P(X,Y)$ using diffusion models and reframe inference as a test-time output optimization problem. By guiding the generative process to match observed inputs, our framework enables flexible, gradient-based conditioning at inference time without architectural changes or retraining, effectively supporting arbitrary and previously unseen combinations of observations. Extensive experiments demonstrate strong performance across standard and cross-modality medical image segmentation, few-shot segmentation with only 2 or 4 training samples, degraded-input segmentation, shape completion from sparse and partial observations, and zero-shot application to demonstrate generality. To support these evaluations, we curated and released a large-scale text-shape dataset derived from MedShapeNet. Our results highlight the versatility of generative joint modeling as a foundation for reusable, task-agnostic medical AI systems.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于扩散模型的生成式医学诊断框架，将推理转化为测试时输出优化问题，无需重新训练即可处理任意观测组合。
ai.motivation: 传统判别式医学AI在异构数据和模态下泛化能力差，无法适应真实临床场景。
ai.method: 用扩散模型建模联合分布P(X,Y)，在推理时通过梯度引导生成过程匹配观测输入，实现灵活的条件化推理。
ai.result: 在标准/跨模态分割、少样本分割、退化输入分割、形状补全和零样本任务上表现优异，并发布了大规模文本-形状数据集。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需重新训练即可适应多种医学诊断任务的通用生成式AI方案。

idea_spark.idea: 利用扩散模型的联合分布建模能力，将任意观测组合作为条件，实现任务无关的推理。
idea_spark.risk: 生成式模型的计算开销较大，实时性可能受限；对观测噪声敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将判别任务转化为生成式优化问题，启发其他领域（如药物发现、基因组学）的通用AI设计。

en.tldr: Proposes a generative diagnostic framework using diffusion models, reframing inference as test-time output optimization to handle arbitrary observation combinations without retraining.
en.motivation: Traditional discriminative medical AI generalizes poorly across heterogeneous data and modalities in real-world clinical settings.
en.method: Models joint distribution P(X,Y) with diffusion models and guides generation via gradient-based conditioning to match observed inputs at inference time.
en.result: Achieves strong performance on standard/cross-modality segmentation, few-shot segmentation, degraded-input segmentation, shape completion, and zero-shot tasks; releases a large-scale text-shape dataset from MedShapeNet.
en.help_to_user: Offers a versatile generative AI solution adaptable to various medical diagnostic tasks without retraining.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage joint distribution modeling of diffusion models to condition on arbitrary observations for task-agnostic inference.
idea_spark.risk: High computational cost may limit real-time use; sensitive to observation noise.
idea_spark.inspiration: Transforming discriminative tasks into generative optimization problems inspires general AI design in other fields like drug discovery and genomics.

papers.title: Harmonized Feature Conditioning and Frequency-Prompt Personalization for Multi-Rater Medical Segmentation
papers.summary: Multi-rater medical image segmentation captures the inherent ambiguity of clinical interpretation, where diagnostic boundaries vary across experts and imaging devices. Existing approaches often reduce this diversity to consensus labels or treat rater differences as noise, resulting in overconfident and poorly calibrated models. We propose a harmonized probabilistic framework that disentangles acquisition artifacts from genuine annotator variability through adaptive feature conditioning and frequency-domain personalization. A lightweight Harmonizer Network implicitly models scanner-specific artifacts and performs dynamic feature modulation to standardize latent representations, ensuring that uncertainty reflects anatomy rather than noise. To represent rater-specific styles, we introduce a novel High-Frequency Prompt Modules that operate in the spectral domain to encode annotator-dependent boundary precision and textural sensitivity. These prompts adaptively modulate harmonized features to produce personalized yet anatomically consistent segmentations. Furthermore, a Generalized Energy Distance based regularization aligns the generative distribution with empirical annotation variability, promoting diversity where experts disagree and consensus where they converge. Experiments on LIDC-IDRI and NPC-170 show SOTA aggregated and individualized segmentation, with notable GED reductions and improved Dice scores, especially on noisy cases. Beyond accuracy, the model exhibits clinically meaningful uncertainty. Confidence rises in agreement regions and declines in ambiguous areas, supporting its use as a reliable and interpretable tool for multi-expert clinical workflows.

ai.tldr: 提出一种协调概率框架，通过自适应特征调节和频域个性化，分离采集伪影与标注者变异，实现多评分者医学图像分割。
ai.motivation: 现有方法将评分者差异视为噪声或简化为共识标签，导致模型过度自信且校准不良。
ai.method: 使用轻量协调网络隐式建模扫描仪伪影并进行动态特征调制；引入高频提示模块在谱域编码标注者边界精度和纹理敏感性；采用广义能量距离正则化对齐生成分布与经验标注变异。
ai.result: 在LIDC-IDRI和NPC-170上取得最先进的聚合与个性化分割，GED显著降低，Dice提升，尤其在噪声病例上；不确定性在一致区域升高、模糊区域降低。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解多专家标注中的固有歧义，提供校准良好的分割结果和临床有意义的不确定性估计。

idea_spark.idea: 将频域提示与特征调节结合，分离设备伪影和标注者风格，实现个性化且解剖一致的分割。
idea_spark.risk: 频域提示可能过度拟合特定标注者风格，需验证泛化性；轻量协调网络对未知扫描仪伪影的鲁棒性待评估。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受频域分析在风格迁移中的启发，将标注者差异视为高频风格信息，通过谱域调制实现个性化。

en.tldr: A harmonized probabilistic framework disentangles acquisition artifacts from annotator variability via adaptive feature conditioning and frequency-domain personalization for multi-rater medical segmentation.
en.motivation: Existing methods treat rater differences as noise or reduce them to consensus labels, leading to overconfident and poorly calibrated models.
en.method: A lightweight Harmonizer Network models scanner-specific artifacts and performs dynamic feature modulation; High-Frequency Prompt Modules encode annotator-dependent boundary precision in the spectral domain; Generalized Energy Distance regularization aligns the generative distribution with empirical annotation variability.
en.result: State-of-the-art aggregated and individualized segmentation on LIDC-IDRI and NPC-170, with notable GED reductions and improved Dice scores, especially on noisy cases; confidence rises in agreement regions and declines in ambiguous areas.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand inherent ambiguity in multi-expert annotations, providing well-calibrated segmentations and clinically meaningful uncertainty estimates.

idea_spark.idea: Combine frequency-domain prompts with feature conditioning to separate device artifacts and annotator styles, enabling personalized yet anatomically consistent segmentation.
idea_spark.risk: Frequency-domain prompts may overfit specific annotator styles; generalization to unseen scanner artifacts needs evaluation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by frequency-domain analysis in style transfer, treating annotator variability as high-frequency style information and modulating it in the spectral domain for personalization.

papers.title: Guided Streaming Stochastic Interpolant Policy
papers.summary: Inference-time guidance is essential for steering generative robot policies toward dynamic objectives without retraining, yet existing methods are largely confined to chunk-based architectures that exhibit high latency and lack the reactivity needed for test-time preference alignment or obstacle avoidance. In this work, we formally derive the optimal guidance term for Stochastic Interpolants (SI) by analyzing the value function's time evolution via the Backward Kolmogorov Equation, establishing a modified drift that theoretically guarantees sampling from a target distribution. We apply this framework to real-time control through the Streaming Stochastic Interpolant Policy (SSIP), which generalizes the deterministic Streaming Flow Policy (SFP). Unifying this guidance law with the streaming architecture enables fast and reactive control. To support diverse deployment needs, we propose two complementary mechanisms: training-free Stochastic Trajectory Ensemble Guidance (STEG) that computes gradients on-the-fly for zero-shot adaptation, and training-based Conditional Critic Guidance (CCG) for amortized inference. Empirical evaluations demonstrate that our guided streaming approach significantly outperforms conventional chunk-based policies in reactivity and provides superior, physically valid guidance for dynamic, unstructured environments.

ai.tldr: 提出了一种基于随机插值的引导式流式策略，通过理论推导最优引导项，实现实时、反应式的机器人控制，并支持零样本适应和摊销推理。
ai.motivation: 现有推理时引导方法局限于高延迟的块式架构，无法满足动态目标对齐或避障所需的实时反应性。
ai.method: 利用向后Kolmogorov方程分析价值函数的时间演化，推导出随机插值的最优引导项，并统一到流式架构中；提出无训练的随机轨迹集成引导和有训练的条件评论家引导两种机制。
ai.result: 引导式流式策略在反应性上显著优于传统块式策略，在动态非结构化环境中提供更优且物理有效的引导。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在不重新训练的情况下，通过推理时引导实现机器人策略的实时偏好对齐和避障。

idea_spark.idea: 将随机插值引导与流式架构结合，实现零样本适应和摊销推理，为机器人策略提供高效、反应式的在线调整能力。
idea_spark.risk: 训练式引导可能依赖特定任务数据，泛化到全新环境时需验证；无训练引导的计算开销在资源受限平台上可能较高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受强化学习中值函数与最优控制理论的启发，将随机插值的引导项与价值函数时间演化联系起来。

en.tldr: Proposes a guided streaming policy based on stochastic interpolants, deriving the optimal guidance term theoretically for real-time reactive robot control, supporting zero-shot adaptation and amortized inference.
en.motivation: Existing inference-time guidance methods are confined to high-latency chunk-based architectures, lacking the reactivity needed for dynamic objective alignment or obstacle avoidance.
en.method: Analyzes the time evolution of the value function via the Backward Kolmogorov Equation to derive the optimal guidance term for Stochastic Interpolants, unifying it with a streaming architecture; proposes training-free Stochastic Trajectory Ensemble Guidance and training-based Conditional Critic Guidance.
en.result: The guided streaming policy significantly outperforms conventional chunk-based policies in reactivity and provides superior, physically valid guidance for dynamic, unstructured environments.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to achieve real-time preference alignment and obstacle avoidance for robot policies via inference-time guidance without retraining.

idea_spark.idea: Combining stochastic interpolant guidance with a streaming architecture enables zero-shot adaptation and amortized inference, offering efficient, reactive online adjustment for robot policies.
idea_spark.risk: Training-based guidance may rely on task-specific data and requires validation for novel environments; training-free guidance may incur computational overhead on resource-constrained platforms.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by value functions and optimal control theory in reinforcement learning, linking the guidance term of stochastic interpolants to the temporal evolution of the value function.

papers.title: Offline Preference Optimization for Rectified Flow with Noise-Tracked Pairs
papers.summary: Existing preference datasets for text-to-image models typically store only the final winner/loser images. This representation is insufficient for rectified flow (RF) models, whose generation is naturally indexed by a specific prior noise sample and follows a nearly straight denoising trajectory. In contrast, prior DPO-style alignment for diffusion models commonly estimates trajectories using an independent forward noising process, which can be mismatched to the true reverse dynamics and introduces unnecessary variance. We propose Prior Noise-Aware Preference Optimization (PNAPO), an off-policy alignment framework specialized for rectified flow. PNAPO augments preference data by retaining the paired prior noises used to generate each winner/loser image, turning the standard (prompt, winner, loser) triplet into a sextuple. Leveraging the straight-line property of RF, we estimate intermediate states via noise-image interpolation, which constrains the trajectory estimation space and yields a tighter surrogate objective for preference optimization. In addition, we introduce a dynamic regularization strategy that adapts the DPO regularization based on (i) the reward gap between winner and loser and (ii) training progress, improving stability and sample efficiency. Experiments on state-of-the-art RF T2I backbones show that PNAPO consistently improves preference metrics while substantially reducing training compute.

ai.tldr: 提出PNAPO框架，通过保留生成图像时的噪声对，利用整流流直线特性进行偏好优化，提升文本到图像模型的偏好指标并减少训练计算量。
ai.motivation: 现有偏好数据集仅存储最终胜负图像，不适用于整流流模型；而传统DPO方法使用独立前向加噪过程估计轨迹，与真实反向动力学不匹配且引入方差。
ai.method: 将偏好数据扩充为六元组（提示、胜者噪声、胜者图像、败者噪声、败者图像），利用整流流直线性通过噪声-图像插值估计中间状态，并引入动态正则化策略调整DPO正则化强度。
ai.result: 在先进整流流文本到图像骨干上，PNAPO一致提升偏好指标，同时大幅降低训练计算量。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户更高效地对整流流文本到图像模型进行偏好对齐，减少训练成本。

idea_spark.idea: 利用整流流直线性，将噪声对作为偏好数据的一部分，实现更紧的代理目标。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖整流流直线性假设，若模型偏离直线轨迹则效果可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从噪声对中提取轨迹信息，启发其他生成模型利用生成路径的几何特性进行优化。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户更高效地对整流流文本到图像模型进行偏好对齐，减少训练成本。

idea_spark.risk: 依赖整流流直线性假设，若模型偏离直线轨迹则效果可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从噪声对中提取轨迹信息，启发其他生成模型利用生成路径的几何特性进行优化。

en.tldr: Proposes PNAPO, an off-policy alignment framework for rectified flow that retains paired prior noises and leverages straight-line interpolation for tighter preference optimization, improving metrics and reducing compute.
en.motivation: Existing preference datasets store only final images, insufficient for rectified flow; prior DPO methods use independent forward noising that mismatches reverse dynamics and adds variance.
en.method: Augments preference data to sextuples (prompt, winner noise, winner image, loser noise, loser image), estimates intermediate states via noise-image interpolation using RF's straight-line property, and introduces dynamic regularization based on reward gap and training progress.
en.result: Consistently improves preference metrics on state-of-the-art RF T2I backbones while substantially reducing training compute.
en.help_to_user: Helps users align rectified flow text-to-image models more efficiently with less training cost.

idea_spark.idea: Exploit straight-line property of rectified flow to incorporate noise pairs into preference data for tighter surrogate objective.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on straight-line assumption; performance may degrade if model deviates from linear trajectory.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using noise pairs to capture trajectory information inspires leveraging geometric properties of generation paths for optimization in other models.

papers.title: When (and How) to Trust the Expert: Diagnosing Query-Time Expert-Guided Reinforcement Learning
papers.summary: Many continuous-control problems ship with a competent but suboptimal controller (a tuned PID, a hand-designed gait). A growing family of methods uses such controllers as queryable experts during RL, but each method has been proposed in isolation, on a different benchmark, without imperfect-expert testing. We harmonize the comparison on a shared SAC backbone, common HPO and evaluation protocols, 100/50 seeds per (env, method), and a degradation sweep over expert undertuning, action bias, and observation noise. The comparison surfaces three failure modes single-paper evaluations miss: (F1) a critic blind spot under argmax-plus-bootstrap that drags IBRL below no-expert SAC on experts close to the no-expert-RL ceiling (RL-near-ceiling, distinct from the absolute physical ceiling); (F2) residual saturation on far-from-optimal experts; and (F3) warm-start buffer poisoning that collapses training-time-handoff methods under deployment-time expert undertuning. No single method dominates: each wins on one task-structure regime and fails predictably elsewhere; on RL-near-ceiling experts (FourTank, GlassFurnace) no query-time method clears the expert within our 1M-step budget, leaving open whether this is a fundamental wall or a budget effect. We convert the spread into a testable decision rule keyed on three pre-training observables (expert quality, task termination, perturbation type). The benchmark, taxonomy, and decision rule are the primary contribution; we additionally describe EDGE, a softmax-over-ensemble-LCB design point used to demonstrate that both axes the taxonomy points to (gate form, scoring rule) are individually exploitable.

ai.tldr: 本文系统比较了查询式专家引导强化学习方法，发现三种单篇论文未发现的失败模式，并提出了基于预训练可观测量的决策规则。
ai.motivation: 现有专家引导RL方法各自独立提出，缺乏统一比较和对不完美专家的测试，导致实际部署时可能失败。
ai.method: 在共享SAC骨干、统一超参数优化和评估协议下，对多种方法进行大规模实验（每环境每方法100/50种子），并引入专家欠调、动作偏差和观测噪声的退化扫描。
ai.result: 识别出三种失败模式：评论家盲点、残差饱和、热身缓冲区污染；无单一方法在所有场景占优；在接近无专家RL上限的专家上，所有查询式方法均未在1M步内超越专家。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解何时以及如何信任专家引导RL，提供可操作的决策规则以避免常见失败。

idea_spark.idea: 设计基于软最大集成LCB的EDGE方法，证明门控形式和评分规则两个维度均可独立利用。
idea_spark.risk: 决策规则可能依赖特定基准，泛化到新任务需验证；1M步预算限制可能掩盖长期效果。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从系统比较中提炼简单可测试的规则，启发更鲁棒的专家集成策略。

en.tldr: This paper systematically compares query-time expert-guided RL methods, uncovers three failure modes missed by single-paper evaluations, and proposes a decision rule based on pre-training observables.
en.motivation: Existing expert-guided RL methods are proposed in isolation without unified comparison or imperfect-expert testing, leading to potential deployment failures.
en.method: Large-scale experiments on a shared SAC backbone with common HPO and evaluation protocols (100/50 seeds per env/method), plus a degradation sweep over expert undertuning, action bias, and observation noise.
en.result: Identifies three failure modes: critic blind spot, residual saturation, warm-start buffer poisoning; no single method dominates; on RL-near-ceiling experts, no query-time method surpasses the expert within 1M steps.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand when and how to trust expert-guided RL, providing actionable decision rules to avoid common failures.

idea_spark.idea: Designs EDGE, a softmax-over-ensemble-LCB method, demonstrating that both gate form and scoring rule dimensions are individually exploitable.
idea_spark.risk: The decision rule may be benchmark-specific; generalization to new tasks requires validation; the 1M-step budget may mask long-term effects.
idea_spark.inspiration: Extracting simple testable rules from systematic comparison inspires more robust expert integration strategies.

papers.title: MolSight: Molecular Property Prediction with Images
papers.summary: Every molecule ever synthesised can be drawn as a 2D skeletal diagram, yet in modern property prediction this universally available representation has received less focus in favour of molecular graphs, 3D conformers, or billion-parameter language models, each imposing its own computational and data-engineering overhead. We present $\textbf{MolSight}$, the first systematic large-scale study of vision-based Molecular Property Prediction (MPP). Using 10 vision architectures, 7 pre-training strategies, and $2\,M$ molecule images, we evaluate performance across 10 downstream tasks spanning physical-property regression, drug-discovery classification, and quantum-chemistry prediction. To account for the wide variation in structural complexity across pre-training molecules, we further propose a $\textbf{chemistry-informed curriculum}$: five structural complexity descriptors partition the corpus into five tiers of increasing chemical difficulty, consistently outperforming non-curriculum baselines. We show that a single rendered bond-line image, processed by a vision encoder, is sufficient for competitive molecular property prediction, i.e. $\textit{chemical insight from sight alone}$. The best curriculum-trained configuration achieves the top result on $\textbf{5 of 10}$ benchmarks and top two on $\textbf{all 10}$, at $\textbf{$\textit{80$\times$ lower}$}$ FLOPs than the nearest multi-modal competitor.

ai.tldr: 提出MolSight，首次系统研究基于图像的分子性质预测，使用10种视觉架构和化学信息课程学习，在10个基准任务上以80倍更低的计算量取得领先结果。
ai.motivation: 现有分子性质预测依赖分子图、3D构象或大语言模型，计算和数据工程开销大；而所有分子均可绘制为2D骨架图，但该通用表示未被充分利用。
ai.method: 使用10种视觉架构和7种预训练策略，在200万分子图像上训练；提出化学信息课程，根据5个结构复杂度描述符将语料分为5个难度层级，逐步训练。
ai.result: 最佳课程训练配置在10个基准任务中5个取得第一，全部进入前二，计算量仅为最近多模态竞品的1/80。
ai.help_to_user: 展示了一种低计算成本的分子性质预测新范式，适合资源受限场景，可快速部署于药物发现和量子化学任务。

idea_spark.idea: 将分子图像与图神经网络结合，利用视觉编码器提取全局结构特征，同时用图网络捕捉局部原子连接，实现互补表示。
idea_spark.risk: 图像表示可能丢失立体化学信息，对需要3D构象的任务（如对映体选择性预测）效果有限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类化学家通过观察结构式推断性质的启发，验证了纯视觉输入在科学预测中的潜力，可推广至其他化学可视化任务。

en.tldr: MolSight is the first systematic large-scale study of vision-based molecular property prediction, using 10 vision architectures and a chemistry-informed curriculum to achieve top results on 10 benchmarks with 80× lower FLOPs.
en.motivation: Existing molecular property prediction relies on molecular graphs, 3D conformers, or large language models, each with high computational and data engineering costs; yet every molecule can be drawn as a 2D skeletal diagram, a universally available representation that remains underexplored.
en.method: We evaluate 10 vision architectures and 7 pre-training strategies on 2M molecule images, and propose a chemistry-informed curriculum that partitions the corpus into five tiers of increasing structural complexity using five descriptors.
en.result: The best curriculum-trained configuration achieves top-1 on 5 of 10 benchmarks and top-2 on all 10, at 80× lower FLOPs than the nearest multi-modal competitor.
en.help_to_user: Demonstrates a low-computation paradigm for molecular property prediction, suitable for resource-constrained settings and rapid deployment in drug discovery and quantum chemistry tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Combine molecular images with graph neural networks: use a vision encoder for global structural features and a graph network for local atomic connectivity, enabling complementary representations.
idea_spark.risk: Image representations may lose stereochemical information, limiting effectiveness for tasks requiring 3D conformations, such as enantioselectivity prediction.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by how human chemists infer properties from structural diagrams, this work validates the potential of pure visual input in scientific prediction, extendable to other chemical visualization tasks.

papers.title: Two Ways to De-Bias an LLM-as-a-Judge: A Continuous-Score Comparison of Hierarchical Bayesian Calibration and Neural-ODE Score Transport
papers.summary: [Abridged] Using a Large Language Model (LLM) as an automatic rater (LLM-as-a-judge) is cheap but potentially biased: some judges run lenient, others strict, the middle of the scale gets compressed, and verbose answers may be over-rewarded. A common remedy is post-hoc calibration: leave the cheap judge in place and, on a modest set of paired anchors, fit a transformation from raw judge scores to an estimate of the human rating. We compare two correctors that take opposing views on how this mapping should be modeled: a parametric, small-anchor hierarchical Bayesian linear correction with per-score uncertainty, and a non-parametric Neural-ODE (FFJORD) score-transport flow. Both are run head-to-head on UltraFeedback fine-grained_score (1700 paired examples, 200 held out), with calibration split into three operational sub-questions: population-mean recovery, per-item accuracy, and distributional-shape match.   The headline result is that the choice between methods is primarily a data-budget question. Both correctors close the raw $+0.71$-point mean offset to within $\pm 0.08$ of the GPT-4 reference, at 100 and at 1500 anchors. Past that, the methods swap roles. With 100 anchors, the linear corrector reconstructs the human-score distribution roughly twice as well by KL divergence (0.031 vs. 0.058) and ties the flow on MAE. With 1500 anchors the flow wins on every metric (MAE 0.320 vs. 0.359, Pearson 0.922 vs. 0.896, KL 0.026 vs. 0.037). The Bayesian linear corrector saturates well below 1500 anchors: residual $\tanh$-shaped non-linearity is, by construction, structure a linear correction cannot fit. The flow keeps improving as labels grow. We translate these findings into an explicit decision rule for production deployments.

ai.tldr: 比较了两种后校准方法（层次贝叶斯线性校正和神经ODE分数传输）来消除LLM作为评判者的偏差，发现方法选择取决于数据预算。
ai.motivation: LLM作为自动评分器成本低但存在偏差，需要后校准来估计人类评分。
ai.method: 在UltraFeedback数据集上，使用层次贝叶斯线性校正（参数化、小锚点）和神经ODE分数传输（非参数化）进行对比实验。
ai.result: 100个锚点时线性校正更优，1500个锚点时神经ODE全面胜出；线性校正饱和，神经ODE随标签增加持续改进。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户根据可用锚点数量选择最合适的去偏方法。

idea_spark.idea: 结合两种方法：小数据时用线性校正，大数据时用神经ODE。
idea_spark.risk: 线性校正无法拟合非线性残差，神经ODE需要更多数据。
idea_spark.inspiration: 数据预算决定方法选择，启发自适应校准策略。

zh.motivation: LLM作为自动评分器成本低但存在偏差，需要后校准来估计人类评分。

idea_spark.risk: 线性校正无法拟合非线性残差，神经ODE需要更多数据。
idea_spark.inspiration: 数据预算决定方法选择，启发自适应校准策略。

en.tldr: Compares two post-hoc calibration methods (hierarchical Bayesian linear correction and Neural-ODE score transport) to debias LLM-as-a-judge, finding method choice depends on data budget.
en.motivation: LLM as automatic rater is cheap but biased; post-hoc calibration is needed to estimate human ratings.
en.method: Head-to-head comparison on UltraFeedback dataset using parametric hierarchical Bayesian linear correction and non-parametric Neural-ODE score transport.
en.result: With 100 anchors, linear correction is better; with 1500 anchors, Neural-ODE wins on all metrics; linear correction saturates, Neural-ODE improves with more labels.
en.help_to_user: Helps users choose the best debiasing method based on available anchor count.

idea_spark.idea: Combine both methods: linear correction for small data, Neural-ODE for large data.
idea_spark.risk: Linear correction cannot fit nonlinear residuals; Neural-ODE requires more data.
idea_spark.inspiration: Data budget determines method choice, inspiring adaptive calibration strategies.

papers.title: Data-driven Circuit Discovery for Interpretability of Language Models
papers.summary: Circuit discovery aims to explain how language models (LMs) implement a specific task by localizing and interpreting a circuit, a computational subgraph responsible for the LM's behavior. Existing circuit discovery methods are hypothesis-driven; they first informally define a task with a dataset, and then apply a circuit discovery algorithm over that dataset to obtain a single circuit. This imposes two strong assumptions: that the LM implements the task with a single circuit, and that the dataset adequately represents the task as humans understand it. We systematically test these assumptions across four previously studied tasks and find that even minor dataset variations that preserve task semantics can produce circuits with low edge overlap and cross-dataset faithfulness. More strikingly, when applied to a mixed dataset with two distinct tasks whose separately discovered circuits have near-zero cross-faithfulness, existing methods still return a single circuit with high faithfulness across both tasks. This indicates that current methods discover dataset-specific circuits, rather than general task circuits. We propose Data-driven Circuit Discovery (DCD), a new discovery framework that drops both assumptions: instead of returning a single circuit for a dataset, DCD first clusters examples in the dataset by how similarly the model processes them and discovers a separate circuit for each group. This allows distinct mechanisms to appear separately rather than merged into a single circuit; each circuit explains its group, not the full task. Experiments show that DCD discovers multiple circuits per dataset, each more faithful to its group than a single circuit discovered by existing methods. Broadly, DCD lets the data reveal mechanistic structure within LMs, rather than relying on human-defined task boundaries that may not align with how models organize their computation.

ai.tldr: 提出数据驱动的电路发现方法（DCD），通过聚类示例并分别为每组发现电路，避免现有方法将不同机制合并为单一电路的问题。
ai.motivation: 现有电路发现方法假设模型用单一电路实现任务且数据集充分代表任务，但实验表明这些假设不成立，导致发现的是数据集特定电路而非通用任务电路。
ai.method: DCD 先根据模型处理示例的相似性对数据集进行聚类，再为每个聚类分别发现一个电路。
ai.result: DCD 在每个数据集中发现多个电路，每个电路对其所属聚类的忠实度高于现有方法发现的单一电路。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解语言模型内部可能存在多种并行机制，而非单一任务电路，从而更准确地解释模型行为。

idea_spark.idea: 利用模型内部表示或注意力模式的相似性自动划分数据子集，发现模型实际使用的不同计算路径。
idea_spark.risk: 聚类结果可能不稳定或难以解释，需要验证聚类是否对应有意义的机制差异。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从数据驱动角度重新审视解释性方法，让模型自身揭示其计算组织方式，而非依赖人类预设的任务边界。

zh.tldr: 提出数据驱动的电路发现方法（DCD），通过聚类示例并分别为每组发现电路，避免现有方法将不同机制合并为单一电路的问题。
zh.motivation: 现有电路发现方法假设模型用单一电路实现任务且数据集充分代表任务，但实验表明这些假设不成立，导致发现的是数据集特定电路而非通用任务电路。

zh.result: DCD 在每个数据集中发现多个电路，每个电路对其所属聚类的忠实度高于现有方法发现的单一电路。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解语言模型内部可能存在多种并行机制，而非单一任务电路，从而更准确地解释模型行为。

idea_spark.risk: 聚类结果可能不稳定或难以解释，需要验证聚类是否对应有意义的机制差异。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从数据驱动角度重新审视解释性方法，让模型自身揭示其计算组织方式，而非依赖人类预设的任务边界。

en.tldr: Proposes Data-driven Circuit Discovery (DCD) that clusters examples by model processing similarity and discovers a separate circuit per cluster, avoiding the merging of distinct mechanisms into a single circuit.
en.motivation: Existing circuit discovery methods assume a single circuit per task and that the dataset adequately represents the task, but experiments show these assumptions fail, yielding dataset-specific rather than task-general circuits.
en.method: DCD first clusters examples in the dataset based on how similarly the model processes them, then discovers a separate circuit for each cluster.
en.result: DCD discovers multiple circuits per dataset, each more faithful to its cluster than a single circuit from existing methods.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand that language models may employ multiple parallel mechanisms rather than a single task circuit, enabling more accurate model interpretation.

idea_spark.idea: Use similarity in model internal representations or attention patterns to automatically partition data subsets and discover distinct computational paths actually used by the model.
idea_spark.risk: Clustering results may be unstable or hard to interpret; validation is needed to ensure clusters correspond to meaningful mechanistic differences.
idea_spark.inspiration: Re-examine interpretability methods from a data-driven perspective, letting the model reveal its own computational organization rather than relying on human-defined task boundaries.

papers.title: Normalization Equivariance for Arbitrary Backbones, with Application to Image Denoising
papers.summary: Normalization Equivariance (NE), equivariance to global contrast and brightness transforms, improves robustness to distribution shift in image-to-image prediction. Existing methods enforce this prior by constraining internal layers to NE-compatible families, limiting compatibility with standard components such as attention and LayerNorm, and adding runtime cost. We characterize the full NE function class: a function is NE if and only if it admits a normalize-process-denormalize factorization. This turns exact NE enforcement, for the ideal wrapper, from an internal architectural constraint into an input-output parameterization problem, allowing a parameter-free wrapper (WNE) to enforce NE around any backbone, including transformers. In a single-noise mismatch diagnostic for blind denoising, the wrapper improves CNN and transformer robustness with no measurable GPU overhead; architectural NE baselines incur up to a 1.6x slowdown.

ai.tldr: 提出归一化等变性（NE）的完整函数类刻画，并设计无参数包装器WNE，使任意骨干网络（包括Transformer）都能满足NE，在盲去噪中提升鲁棒性且无GPU开销。
ai.motivation: 现有NE方法通过约束内部层实现，限制了与注意力、LayerNorm等标准组件的兼容性，并增加运行时成本。
ai.method: 证明函数是NE当且仅当可分解为归一化-处理-反归一化形式，从而将NE强制从内部架构约束转化为输入输出参数化问题，设计无参数包装器WNE。
ai.result: 在单噪声失配诊断中，WNE使CNN和Transformer鲁棒性提升，无额外GPU开销；而架构级NE基线速度最多降低1.6倍。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种即插即用的方法，使任意图像到图像模型获得对全局对比度和亮度变化的等变性，增强分布偏移下的鲁棒性。

idea_spark.idea: 将NE包装器应用于视频去噪或低光照增强，利用时序或光照变化的等变性。
idea_spark.risk: 包装器可能对某些极端对比度/亮度变换效果有限，需验证在真实复杂光照下的表现。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受群等变性（如旋转等变）的输入输出参数化思路启发，将对称性约束从内部层移到外部包装。

en.tldr: Characterizes the full Normalization Equivariance (NE) function class and proposes a parameter-free wrapper (WNE) that enforces NE on any backbone, improving robustness in blind denoising with no GPU overhead.
en.motivation: Existing NE methods constrain internal layers, limiting compatibility with standard components like attention and LayerNorm, and increasing runtime cost.
en.method: Proves that a function is NE iff it admits a normalize-process-denormalize factorization, turning NE enforcement into an input-output parameterization problem, and designs a parameter-free wrapper WNE.
en.result: In a single-noise mismatch diagnostic for blind denoising, WNE improves CNN and transformer robustness with no measurable GPU overhead; architectural NE baselines incur up to 1.6x slowdown.
en.help_to_user: Provides a plug-and-play method to endow any image-to-image model with equivariance to global contrast and brightness transforms, enhancing robustness under distribution shift.

idea_spark.idea: Apply the NE wrapper to video denoising or low-light enhancement, leveraging equivariance to temporal or illumination changes.
idea_spark.risk: The wrapper may have limited effectiveness under extreme contrast/brightness transforms; real-world complex lighting needs validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by input-output parameterization of group equivariance (e.g., rotation equivariance), moving symmetry constraints from internal layers to an external wrapper.

papers.title: Set-Based Groupwise Registration for Variable-Length, Variable-Contrast Cardiac MRI
papers.summary: Quantitative cardiac magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) enables non-invasive myocardial tissue characterization but relies on robust motion correction within these variable-length, variable-contrast image sequences. Groupwise registration, which simultaneously aligns all images, has shown greater robustness than pairwise registration for motion correction. However, current deep-learning-based groupwise registration methods cannot generalize across MRI sequences: the architecture typically encodes input data as a fixed-length channel stack, which rigidly couples network design to protocol-specific sequence length, input ordering, and contrast dynamics. At inference time, any change in imaging protocols will render the network unusable. In this work, we introduce \emph{\AnyTwoReg}, a new set-based groupwise registration framework that takes a quantitative MRI sequence as an unordered set. This set formulation fundamentally decouples network design from sequence length and input ordering. By utilizing a shared encoder and correlation-guided feature aggregation, \emph{\AnyTwoReg} constructs a permutation-invariant canonical reference for registration, and learns a permutation-equivariant mapping from images to deformation fields. Additionally, we extract contrast-insensitive image features from an existing foundation model to handle extreme contrast variations. Trained exclusively on a single public $T_1$ mapping dataset (STONE, sequence length $L=11$), \AnyTwoReg generalizes to two unseen quantitative MRI datasets (MOLLI, ASL) with variable lengths ($L \in [11, 60]$) and different contrast dynamics. It achieves strong cross-protocol generalization in a zero-shot manner, and consistently improves downstream quantitative mapping quality. Notably, while designed for quantitative MRI sequences, our framework is directly applicable to Cine MRI sequences for inter-cardiac-phase registration.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于集合的组配准框架，可处理变长、变对比的心脏MRI序列，实现零样本跨协议泛化。
ai.motivation: 现有深度学习的组配准方法无法泛化到不同MRI序列，因为其网络架构将输入编码为固定长度的通道堆叠，与序列长度、输入顺序和对比度动态紧密耦合。
ai.method: 将定量MRI序列视为无序集合，利用共享编码器和相关性引导的特征聚合构建置换不变的规范参考，并学习置换等变的形变场映射；同时从现有基础模型中提取对比度不敏感特征。
ai.result: 仅在STONE数据集（序列长度11）上训练，即可零样本泛化到MOLLI和ASL数据集（序列长度11-60），并持续改善下游定量映射质量。
ai.help_to_user: 该框架可直接用于Cine MRI序列的帧间配准，为心脏MRI运动校正提供通用解决方案。

idea_spark.idea: 将基础模型提取的对比度不敏感特征与集合配准结合，可能进一步推广到多模态图像配准。
idea_spark.risk: 对极端对比度变化或噪声较大的序列，基础模型特征可能失效，需验证鲁棒性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用集合论思想解耦网络与序列长度，启发其他序列依赖任务的通用模型设计。

en.tldr: Proposes a set-based groupwise registration framework that handles variable-length, variable-contrast cardiac MRI sequences and achieves zero-shot cross-protocol generalization.
en.motivation: Existing deep learning groupwise registration methods cannot generalize across MRI sequences because their architecture encodes input as a fixed-length channel stack, tightly coupling network design to sequence length, input ordering, and contrast dynamics.
en.method: Treats a quantitative MRI sequence as an unordered set, uses a shared encoder and correlation-guided feature aggregation to build a permutation-invariant canonical reference, and learns a permutation-equivariant deformation mapping; additionally extracts contrast-insensitive features from a foundation model.
en.result: Trained only on the STONE dataset (sequence length 11), it generalizes zero-shot to MOLLI and ASL datasets (sequence lengths 11-60) and consistently improves downstream quantitative mapping quality.
en.help_to_user: The framework is directly applicable to Cine MRI sequences for inter-cardiac-phase registration, offering a general solution for cardiac MRI motion correction.

idea_spark.idea: Combining contrast-insensitive features from foundation models with set-based registration may further extend to multi-modal image registration.
idea_spark.risk: For sequences with extreme contrast variations or high noise, foundation model features may fail; robustness needs validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using set theory to decouple network from sequence length inspires general model design for other sequence-dependent tasks.

papers.title: Statistical Model Checking of the Keynes+Schumpeter Model: A Transient Sensitivity Analysis of a Macroeconomic ABM
papers.summary: Agent-based models (ABMs) are increasingly used in macroeconomics, but their analysis still often relies on ad hoc Monte Carlo campaigns with heterogeneous statistical effort across parameter settings. We show how statistical model checking (SMC), implemented through MultiVeStA, can provide a principled analysis layer for a realistic macroeconomic ABM without rewriting the simulator in a dedicated formalism. Our case study is the heuristic-switching Keynes+Schumpeter(K+S) model, analysed hrough a transient sensitivity campaign over one-parameter sweeps, two macro observables (unemployment and GDP growth), and one auxiliary micro-level probe (market share) on the post-warmup phase of a 600-step horizon. The analysis is driven by reusable temporal queries, observable-specific precision targets, and confidence-based stopping rules that automatically determine the simulation effort required by each configuration. Results show a clear contrast across parameter families: macro-financial and structural sweeps produce the strongest transient effects, whereas several heuristic-rule sweeps remain much weaker under the same precision policy. More broadly, the paper shows that SMC can support reproducible and informative quantitative analysis of substantively rich economic ABMs, while making uncertainty estimates and simulation cost explicit parts of the reported results.

ai.tldr: 展示了统计模型检验（SMC）如何为宏观经济主体基模型提供可重复、信息丰富的定量分析，以凯恩斯-熊彼特模型为例，通过瞬态敏感性分析揭示了不同参数族对宏观变量的影响差异。
ai.motivation: 宏观经济主体基模型的分析常依赖临时蒙特卡洛模拟，缺乏统一的统计框架，导致结果不可重复且不确定性不明确。
ai.method: 使用MultiVeStA实现统计模型检验，通过可重用的时间查询、观测特定精度目标和基于置信度的停止规则，自动确定每个参数配置所需的模拟努力。
ai.result: 宏观金融和结构参数扫描产生最强的瞬态效应，而多个启发式规则扫描在相同精度策略下效应较弱。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解统计模型检验如何提升宏观经济主体基模型分析的严谨性和可重复性，并明确不确定性估计和模拟成本。

idea_spark.idea: 将基于置信度的停止规则与多目标优化结合，自动平衡多个观测变量的精度与计算资源。
idea_spark.risk: 框架对模拟器接口要求较高，可能不适用于封闭或黑箱模型。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受启发于软件工程中的统计模型检验，将其应用于经济学主体基模型，实现自动化、可重复的敏感性分析。

en.tldr: Demonstrates how statistical model checking (SMC) provides reproducible and informative quantitative analysis for macroeconomic agent-based models, using the Keynes+Schumpeter model as a case study with transient sensitivity analysis revealing differential effects across parameter families.
en.motivation: Analysis of macroeconomic ABMs often relies on ad hoc Monte Carlo campaigns without a unified statistical framework, leading to irreproducible results and unclear uncertainty.
en.method: Implements SMC via MultiVeStA, using reusable temporal queries, observable-specific precision targets, and confidence-based stopping rules to automatically determine simulation effort per configuration.
en.result: Macro-financial and structural parameter sweeps produce the strongest transient effects, while several heuristic-rule sweeps remain much weaker under the same precision policy.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how SMC can enhance rigor and reproducibility in macroeconomic ABM analysis, making uncertainty estimates and simulation cost explicit.

idea_spark.idea: Combine confidence-based stopping rules with multi-objective optimization to automatically balance precision and computational resources across multiple observables.
idea_spark.risk: The framework requires a certain level of simulator interface, which may not be applicable to closed or black-box models.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by statistical model checking in software engineering, applied to economic ABMs for automated and reproducible sensitivity analysis.

papers.title: HYPERPOSE: Hyperbolic Kinematic Phase-Space Attention for 3D Human Pose Estimation
papers.summary: We introduce HYPERPOSE, a novel 3D human pose estimation framework that performs spatio-temporal reasoning entirely within the Lorentz model of hyperbolic space $\mathbb{H}^d$ to natively preserve the hierarchical tree topology of the human skeleton. Current state-of-the-art pose estimators aim to capture complex joint dynamics by relying on transformers and graph convolutional networks. Since these architectures operate exclusively in Euclidean space which fundamentally mismatches the inherent tree structure of the human body, these methods inevitably suffer from exponential volume distortion and struggle to maintain structural coherence. To this end, we depart from flat spaces and aim to improve geometric fidelity with Hyperbolic Kinematic Phase-Space Attention (HKPSA), natively embedding complex joint relationships without distortion, alongside a multi-scale windowed hyperbolic attention mechanism that efficiently models temporal dynamics in $O(TW)$ complexity. Furthermore, to overcome the well-known instability of training non-Euclidean manifolds, HYPERPOSE introduces a novel Riemannian loss suite and an uncertainty-weighted curriculum, enforcing physical geodesic constraints like bone length and velocity consistency. Extensive evaluations on the Human3.6M and MPI-INF-3DHP datasets demonstrate that HYPERPOSE achieves state-of-the-art structural and temporal coherence, significantly reducing both volume distortion and velocity error, while establishing new state-of-the-art benchmarks in overall positional accuracy.

ai.tldr: 提出HYPERPOSE框架，在双曲空间中进行时空推理，以保持人体骨架的树状拓扑结构，在Human3.6M和MPI-INF-3DHP上达到最先进水平。
ai.motivation: 现有方法在欧氏空间中操作，与人体骨架的树状结构不匹配，导致体积失真和结构一致性差。
ai.method: 在Lorentz模型的双曲空间中设计HKPSA注意力机制，结合多尺度窗口化双曲注意力和黎曼损失套件与不确定性加权课程。
ai.result: 显著降低体积失真和速度误差，在位置精度上建立新基准。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用双曲几何提升3D人体姿态估计的结构和时序一致性。

idea_spark.idea: 将双曲空间中的相位空间注意力与图神经网络结合，用于动态场景中的姿态预测。
idea_spark.risk: 双曲空间训练不稳定，需要精心设计的损失函数和课程学习。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从人体骨架的树状拓扑出发，启发将非欧几何用于其他层次结构数据的深度学习。

zh.tldr: 提出HYPERPOSE框架，在双曲空间中进行时空推理，以保持人体骨架的树状拓扑结构，在Human3.6M和MPI-INF-3DHP上达到最先进水平。

zh.method: 在Lorentz模型的双曲空间中设计HKPSA注意力机制，结合多尺度窗口化双曲注意力和黎曼损失套件与不确定性加权课程。

idea_spark.risk: 双曲空间训练不稳定，需要精心设计的损失函数和课程学习。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从人体骨架的树状拓扑出发，启发将非欧几何用于其他层次结构数据的深度学习。

en.tldr: HYPERPOSE performs spatio-temporal reasoning in hyperbolic space to preserve human skeleton tree topology, achieving state-of-the-art results on Human3.6M and MPI-INF-3DHP.
en.motivation: Existing methods operate in Euclidean space, mismatching the tree structure of the human body, causing volume distortion and poor structural coherence.
en.method: Designs HKPSA attention in the Lorentz model of hyperbolic space, with multi-scale windowed hyperbolic attention and a Riemannian loss suite with uncertainty-weighted curriculum.
en.result: Significantly reduces volume distortion and velocity error, setting new benchmarks in positional accuracy.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how hyperbolic geometry improves structural and temporal coherence in 3D human pose estimation.

idea_spark.idea: Combine hyperbolic phase-space attention with graph neural networks for pose prediction in dynamic scenes.
idea_spark.risk: Training in hyperbolic space is unstable, requiring carefully designed loss functions and curriculum learning.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the tree topology of the human skeleton, suggesting non-Euclidean geometry for hierarchical data deep learning.

papers.title: Tight Lower Bounds on The Single-Error Detection Threshold for Analog Error-Correcting Codes
papers.summary: Analog error-correcting codes (Analog ECCs) for approximate vector-matrix multiplication have been extensively studied as means to achieve fault-tolerant in-memory computation. The theoretical foundations for such coding schemes, particularly the characterization of their correction capabilities via the height profile, have been well established in recent literature. In this paper, we focus on the case of single-error detection Analog ECCs. Among several open problems related to this case proposed by Ron M. Roth in [1], Problem 1 asks:   "Identify the values of $k$ and $n$ for which every linear $[n, k]$ code $\mathcal{C}$ over $\mathbb{R}$ satisfies: $$\mathsf{h}_1(\mathcal{C}):=\max_{\boldsymbol{c}\in \mathcal{C}\setminus{\{\boldsymbol{0}\}}}\mathsf{h}_1(\boldsymbol{c})\geq \Big\lceil \frac{k}{n-k} \Big\rceil.\text{"}$$ Here, for any $\boldsymbol{x}\in\mathbb{R}^n$, $\mathsf{h}_1(\boldsymbol{x})$ represents the ratio between the largest and second largest absolute values of $\boldsymbol{x}$'s entries.   As the simplest special case of Problem 1 (with $n-k=2$), the following problem was posed as Problem 2 in [1]:   "Must every $(n-2)$-dimensional subspace of $\mathbb{R}^n$, $n$ even, contain a nonzero vector in which the ratio between the largest and second largest absolute values of its entries is at least $(n/2)-1$?"   These problems directly pertain to the lower bounds on the single-error detection threshold for Analog ECCs: Problem 1 corresponds to arbitrary $n-k$ and Problem 2 corresponds to $n-k=2$. In this paper, we provide an affirmative answer to Problem 2 and a rigorous proof using theories related to convex optimization. Furthermore, we extend our analytical method to show that the lower bound in Problem 1 is tight for the case where $n-k$ divides $k$. Our results fill the gap in the lower bound theory of thresholds for single-error detection in Analog ECCs.

ai.tldr: 本文解决了模拟纠错码中单错误检测阈值下界的两个公开问题，给出了问题2的肯定回答，并证明了问题1在特定条件下的紧性。
ai.motivation: 模拟纠错码在近似向量-矩阵乘法中用于实现容错内存计算，其单错误检测阈值下界存在未解决的公开问题。
ai.method: 利用凸优化理论，对问题2给出严格证明，并将分析方法推广到问题1中n-k整除k的情形。
ai.result: 问题2得到肯定回答；问题1的下界在n-k整除k时是紧的。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解模拟纠错码单错误检测能力的理论下界，为设计更优的容错编码方案提供依据。

idea_spark.idea: 利用高度轮廓的几何性质，将检测阈值问题转化为凸优化中的极值问题。
idea_spark.risk: 推广到一般n-k情形可能面临更复杂的组合结构。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从线性代数与凸优化的交叉视角，重新审视编码理论中的经典问题。

en.tldr: This paper solves two open problems on lower bounds for single-error detection thresholds in analog error-correcting codes, affirmatively answering Problem 2 and proving tightness of Problem 1 when n-k divides k.
en.motivation: Analog ECCs are used for fault-tolerant in-memory computation via approximate matrix-vector multiplication, and open problems exist regarding their single-error detection threshold lower bounds.
en.method: Convex optimization theory is employed to rigorously prove Problem 2, and the analysis is extended to show tightness of Problem 1 when n-k divides k.
en.result: Problem 2 is answered affirmatively; the lower bound in Problem 1 is tight when n-k divides k.
en.help_to_user: Provides theoretical lower bounds for single-error detection in analog ECCs, aiding the design of more reliable coding schemes.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage geometric properties of height profiles to reformulate detection thresholds as convex optimization extremal problems.
idea_spark.risk: Generalizing to arbitrary n-k may involve more complex combinatorial structures.
idea_spark.inspiration: Revisit classical coding theory problems through the lens of linear algebra and convex optimization.

papers.title: XTinyU-Net: Training-Free U-Net Scaling via Initialization-Time Sensitivity
papers.summary: While U-Net architectures remain the gold standard for medical image segmentation, their deployment in resource-constrained environments demands aggressive model compression. However, finding an optimally efficient configuration is computationally prohibitive, typically requiring exhaustive train-and-evaluate cycles to find the smallest model that maintains peak performance. In this paper, we introduce a training-free selection framework to automatically identify ultralightweight, dataset-specific U-Net configurations directly at initialization. We observe that systematically scaling down U-Net channel width induces a sharp transition from a stable performance plateau to representational capacity collapse. To pinpoint this boundary without training, we propose a Jacobian-based sensitivity metric that scores discrete, width-capped U-Net variants using a small set of unlabeled images. By analyzing the total variation of this sensitivity curve, we isolate the smallest stable configuration, which we denote as XTinyU-Net. Evaluated across six diverse medical datasets within the nnU-Net framework, XTinyU-Net achieves segmentation accuracy comparable to the heavy nnU-Net baseline with 400x-1600x fewer parameters, and outperforms contemporary lightweight architectures while utilizing 5x-72x fewer parameters. Code is publicly accessible on https://github.com/alvinkimbowa/nntinyunet.git.

ai.tldr: 提出一种无需训练的U-Net缩放方法，通过初始化时的雅可比敏感性度量自动找到超轻量配置，在保持分割精度的同时大幅减少参数量。
ai.motivation: U-Net在医学图像分割中表现优异，但部署到资源受限环境需要激进压缩，而传统方法需大量训练才能找到最优小模型，计算成本过高。
ai.method: 在初始化阶段，用少量无标签图像计算不同宽度U-Net变体的雅可比敏感性曲线，通过总变差分析定位性能稳定到崩溃的临界点，选出最小稳定配置XTinyU-Net。
ai.result: 在六个医学数据集上，XTinyU-Net与nnU-Net基线精度相当，参数量减少400-1600倍；比当代轻量架构参数少5-72倍。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户在资源受限场景下快速获得超轻量、高精度的U-Net分割模型，无需训练即可确定最优通道宽度。

idea_spark.idea: 将雅可比敏感性曲线与贝叶斯优化结合，自动搜索更复杂的架构超参数（如深度、卷积核大小）。
idea_spark.risk: 敏感性度量可能对数据集噪声敏感，且仅考虑初始化状态，未覆盖训练动态对最终性能的影响。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从模型初始化时的“相变”现象出发，用无标签数据快速评估容量边界，启发其他无需训练的模型选择方法。

en.tldr: A training-free U-Net scaling method that automatically identifies ultralightweight configurations via Jacobian-based sensitivity at initialization, achieving comparable accuracy with 400x-1600x fewer parameters.
en.motivation: U-Net excels in medical segmentation but deploying it in resource-constrained settings requires aggressive compression; existing methods need costly train-and-evaluate cycles to find the smallest effective model.
en.method: At initialization, compute Jacobian-based sensitivity curves for U-Net variants with different channel widths using a few unlabeled images, then locate the smallest stable configuration (XTinyU-Net) via total variation analysis of the sensitivity curve.
en.result: On six medical datasets, XTinyU-Net matches nnU-Net baseline accuracy while using 400x-1600x fewer parameters, and outperforms contemporary lightweight architectures with 5x-72x fewer parameters.
en.help_to_user: Enables users to quickly obtain an ultralightweight yet accurate U-Net segmentation model for resource-constrained environments without any training.

idea_spark.idea: Combine the Jacobian sensitivity curve with Bayesian optimization to automatically search more complex architectural hyperparameters like depth and kernel size.
idea_spark.risk: The sensitivity metric may be sensitive to dataset noise and only considers initialization, ignoring training dynamics that affect final performance.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging the 'phase transition' phenomenon at initialization to quickly assess capacity boundaries with unlabeled data inspires other training-free model selection methods.

papers.title: Is DRL-based MAC Ready for Underwater Acoustic Networks? Exploring Its Practicality in Real Field Experiments
papers.summary: Medium Access Control (MAC) protocols rely on neighbor and environment information to design collision-free access rules for Underwater Acoustic Networks (UANs). Acquiring this information suffers from high communication overhead due to the unique underwater acoustic channel characteristics, such as long propagation delay, spatiotemporal variations in communication quality, and high attenuation. Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) is promising to circumvent the UANs' physical constraints and provide a low-overhead solution for underwater MAC protocols, since it can decide access rules based on real-time observation without extra information exchange. However, the unique underwater acoustic channel characteristics impose significant challenges on observation acquisition, training time, and the balance of multiple reward factors for DRL-based MAC protocols. Most existing methods remain at the theoretical level: (1) they design partial intelligent agents failing to achieve fully autonomous access; (2) they assume unreasonable simulation scenarios, weakening the effects of underwater acoustic channel characteristics on MAC protocols. To enhance the practicality of DRL-based MAC protocols, we first analyze the application challenges of DRL in UANs through real field experiments. Based on the above challenges, we propose a DRL-based MAC protocol that considers observation loss and balances multiple reward factors to achieve efficient Entire Autonomous access in the UAN (EA-MAC). To further explore the feasibility of DRL-based MAC protocols, we implement EA-MAC and other state-of-the-art protocols on underwater acoustic modems and evaluate their performance in real field experiments. Experimental results demonstrate that EA-MAC can adaptively determine the scheduling sequence for each node, enabling high-throughput and fair communication in a straightforward manner for UANs.

ai.tldr: 通过真实水下实验，验证了基于深度强化学习的MAC协议EA-MAC在水声网络中的实用性，实现了高吞吐量和公平通信。
ai.motivation: 现有基于DRL的MAC协议大多停留在理论层面，未充分考虑水声信道特性带来的观测获取、训练时间和多奖励平衡挑战，缺乏实际部署验证。
ai.method: 首先通过真实实验分析DRL在水声网络中的应用挑战，然后提出EA-MAC协议，该协议考虑观测丢失并平衡多个奖励因子，实现完全自主接入。
ai.result: 在水声调制解调器上实现EA-MAC并与现有协议对比，实验表明EA-MAC能自适应确定节点调度序列，实现高吞吐量和公平通信。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户了解DRL在水声MAC协议中的实际可行性，以及EA-MAC如何解决观测丢失和奖励平衡问题。

idea_spark.idea: 将EA-MAC中的奖励平衡机制与多智能体协作框架结合，探索分布式水下节点间的联合调度优化。
idea_spark.risk: 真实水声环境复杂多变，EA-MAC的泛化能力可能受限，需更多场景实验验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从真实实验中发现DRL在UAN中的关键挑战，启发设计更鲁棒的观测补偿和自适应训练策略。

idea_spark.idea: 将EA-MAC中的奖励平衡机制与多智能体协作框架结合，探索分布式水下节点间的联合调度优化。

en.tldr: Through real underwater experiments, we validate the practicality of a DRL-based MAC protocol (EA-MAC) for underwater acoustic networks, achieving high throughput and fair communication.
en.motivation: Existing DRL-based MAC protocols remain theoretical, neglecting challenges from underwater acoustic channel characteristics such as observation acquisition, training time, and reward balancing, and lack real-world deployment.
en.method: We first analyze DRL application challenges in UANs via real field experiments, then propose EA-MAC that handles observation loss and balances multiple reward factors for fully autonomous access.
en.result: EA-MAC is implemented on underwater acoustic modems and compared with state-of-the-art protocols; experiments show it adaptively determines node scheduling sequences for high throughput and fairness.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the practical feasibility of DRL for underwater MAC and how EA-MAC addresses observation loss and reward balancing.

idea_spark.idea: Combine EA-MAC's reward balancing with multi-agent collaboration frameworks to explore joint scheduling optimization among distributed underwater nodes.
idea_spark.risk: Real underwater environments are highly variable; EA-MAC's generalization may be limited and requires more scenario validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Key challenges identified from real experiments inspire the design of more robust observation compensation and adaptive training strategies.

papers.title: Reducing Annotation Burden for Femoral Cartilage Segmentation in Knee MRI via Cross-Sequence Transfer Learning
papers.summary: Purpose: To develop and evaluate cross-sequence transfer learning for automatic femoral cartilage segmentation, testing bidirectional transfer between dual-echo steady-state (DESS) and sagittal proton density-weighted 3D fast spin-echo (Cube) sequences. Materials and Methods: We optimized a modified 2D U-Net on 507 DESS images from the Osteoarthritis Initiative (OAI). We then established same-sequence baselines using subject-level cross-validation on a subset of 44 OAI DESS images and 44 Cube images acquired at the Istituto Ortopedico Rizzoli, Bologna, Italy. Each subset included 22 non-lesioned and 22 lesioned subjects. Finally, we performed transfer learning across sequences by fine-tuning the pretrained models on the target sequence with increasing training set sizes to study convergence, while keeping validation and test sets fixed. Segmentations were evaluated using Dice similarity coefficient (DSC) and average surface distance (ASD). Lesion effects were assessed with two-sided Mann-Whitney U tests with Bonferroni correction. Results: Same-sequence training yielded higher accuracy on DESS than Cube (DSC, $0.900$ vs $0.830$; $P < .001$). Cube-to-DESS transfer matched DESS performance (DSC, $0.903 \pm 0.032$ vs $0.900 \pm 0.027$), reaching a performance plateau at 9 training subjects. DESS-to-Cube yielded a lower combined DSC ($0.802 \pm 0.049$ vs $0.830 \pm 0.042$), reaching a plateau at 24 training subjects. Lesions did not affect DESS ($P \ge .39$) but reduced Cube accuracy (DSC, $0.805$ vs $0.856$; $P < .001$). Conclusion: Transfer learning across sequences can substantially reduce target-sequence annotation requirements for femoral cartilage segmentation, but performance is direction- and sequence-dependent, and the effects of lesions on segmentation may vary across MRI sequences.

ai.tldr: 提出跨序列迁移学习以减少膝关节MRI中股骨软骨分割的标注负担，在DESS和Cube序列间双向迁移，发现Cube到DESS迁移效果与同序列训练相当，而反向迁移效果略差。
ai.motivation: 膝关节MRI中不同序列的软骨分割需要大量标注数据，跨序列迁移学习有望利用已有标注序列减少目标序列的标注需求。
ai.method: 在507张OAI DESS图像上优化改进的2D U-Net，然后在44张DESS和44张Cube图像上建立同序列基线，最后通过微调预训练模型进行跨序列迁移学习，逐步增加训练集大小研究收敛性。
ai.result: Cube到DESS迁移达到与DESS同序列训练相当的DSC（0.903 vs 0.900），仅需9个训练样本即达平台；DESS到Cube迁移DSC较低（0.802 vs 0.830），需24个样本达平台；病变不影响DESS分割但降低Cube精度。
ai.help_to_user: 如果您需要分割膝关节MRI中的股骨软骨但缺乏目标序列的标注数据，本方法可显著减少标注工作量，但需注意迁移方向依赖性和病变影响。

idea_spark.idea: 探索多源序列联合迁移或自监督预训练以进一步提升DESS到Cube等困难方向的迁移性能。
idea_spark.risk: 病变对某些序列（如Cube）分割精度影响显著，需在临床应用中评估病变样本的迁移鲁棒性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用公开数据集（如OAI）预训练模型，再通过少量目标序列样本微调，可快速部署到新序列的临床研究。

en.tldr: Proposes cross-sequence transfer learning to reduce annotation burden for femoral cartilage segmentation in knee MRI, testing bidirectional transfer between DESS and Cube sequences, finding Cube-to-DESS transfer matches same-sequence performance while reverse transfer is slightly worse.
en.motivation: Cartilage segmentation across different MRI sequences requires extensive annotations; cross-sequence transfer learning can leverage existing annotated sequences to reduce target sequence annotation requirements.
en.method: Optimized a modified 2D U-Net on 507 OAI DESS images, established same-sequence baselines on 44 DESS and 44 Cube images, then performed transfer learning across sequences by fine-tuning pretrained models with increasing training set sizes.
en.result: Cube-to-DESS transfer achieved DSC comparable to DESS same-sequence training (0.903 vs 0.900), plateauing at 9 training subjects; DESS-to-Cube yielded lower DSC (0.802 vs 0.830), plateauing at 24 subjects; lesions did not affect DESS but reduced Cube accuracy.
en.help_to_user: If you need to segment femoral cartilage in knee MRI but lack target sequence annotations, this method can substantially reduce annotation effort, but be aware of direction dependency and lesion effects.

idea_spark.idea: Explore multi-source sequence joint transfer or self-supervised pretraining to improve performance for difficult directions like DESS-to-Cube.
idea_spark.risk: Lesions significantly affect segmentation accuracy for certain sequences (e.g., Cube); clinical deployment must evaluate transfer robustness on pathological samples.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage public datasets (e.g., OAI) for pretraining, then fine-tune with a small number of target sequence samples for rapid deployment in clinical studies.

papers.title: Polygon-mamba: Retinal vessel segmentation using polygon scanning mamba and space-frequency collaborative attention
papers.summary: Retinal vessel segmentation is crucial for diagnosis and assessment of ocular diseases. Notably, segmentation of small retinal vessels has been consistently recognized as a challenging and complex task. To tackle this challenge, we design a hybrid CNN-Mamba fusion network that integrates polygon scanning mamba and space-frequency collaborative attention mechanism for the detection of small vessels. Considering that the traditional mamba architecture with horizontal-vertical scanning may compromise the topological integrity of target structures and result in local discontinuities in small retinal vessels, we present a polygon scanning visual state space model (PS-VSS) to identify small vessel structural features by multi-layer reverse scanning way. Which effectively preserves pixels connectivity, thereby substantially mitigating the loss of information pertaining to small vessels. Furthermore, as we all known that the spatial domain prioritizes positional and structural information, while the frequency domain emphasizes global perception and local detail components, a space-frequency collaborative attention mechanism (SFCAM) is introduced within the skip connection to extract efficient features from the spatial and frequency domains. This strategy empowers the model to dynamically enhance the key features while effectively suppressing clutters. To assess the efficacy of our model, it was tested on three publicly available datasets: DRIVE, STARE, and CHASE_DB1. Compared to manual annotations, our model demonstrated F1 scores of 0.8283, 0.8282, and 0.8251, Area Under Curve (AUC) values of 0.9806, 0.9840, and 0.9866, and Sensitivity (SE) values of of 0.8268, 0.8314, and 0.8484 across three datasets, respectively. The effectiveness of our model was validated through both visual inspection and quantitative analysis.

ai.tldr: 提出一种混合CNN-Mamba融合网络，结合多边形扫描Mamba和空间-频率协同注意力机制，用于视网膜小血管分割。
ai.motivation: 传统Mamba的水平-垂直扫描会破坏小血管的拓扑完整性，导致局部不连续；空间域和频率域信息未充分协同利用。
ai.method: 设计多边形扫描视觉状态空间模型（PS-VSS）通过多层反向扫描保持像素连通性；在跳跃连接中引入空间-频率协同注意力机制（SFCAM）动态增强关键特征并抑制杂波。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种高精度的视网膜小血管分割方法，可辅助眼科疾病诊断。

en.tldr: Proposes a hybrid CNN-Mamba fusion network integrating polygon scanning mamba and space-frequency collaborative attention for retinal small vessel segmentation.
en.motivation: Traditional mamba's horizontal-vertical scanning compromises topological integrity of small vessels, causing local discontinuities; spatial and frequency domain information is not fully synergized.
en.method: Designs a polygon scanning visual state space model (PS-VSS) to preserve pixel connectivity via multi-layer reverse scanning; introduces a space-frequency collaborative attention mechanism (SFCAM) in skip connections to dynamically enhance key features and suppress clutter.
en.result: On DRIVE, STARE, and CHASE_DB1 datasets, achieves F1 scores of 0.8283, 0.8282, 0.8251, AUC of 0.9806, 0.9840, 0.9866, and sensitivity of 0.8268, 0.8314, 0.8484, respectively.
en.help_to_user: Provides a high-accuracy method for retinal small vessel segmentation, aiding ocular disease diagnosis.

idea_spark.idea: Extend space-frequency collaborative attention to multi-scale feature fusion, or combine with Transformer for better global modeling.
idea_spark.risk: Polygon scanning may increase computational complexity; trade-off between efficiency and accuracy needs consideration.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by Mamba's strength in long-sequence modeling, combined with CNN for medical image segmentation, and innovated scanning path to preserve topology.

papers.title: Threat Modelling using Domain-Adapted Language Models: Empirical Evaluation and Insights
papers.summary: Large Language Models(LLMs) are increasingly explored for cybersecurity applications such as vulnerability detection. In the domain of threat modelling, prior work has primarily evaluated a number of general-purpose Large Language Models under limited prompting settings. In this study, we extend the research area of structured threat modelling by systematically evaluating domain-adapted language models of different sizes to their general counterparts. We use both LLMs and Small Language Models(SLMs) that were domain adapted to telecommunications and cybersecuirty. For the structured threat modelling, we selected the widely used STRIDE approach and the application area is 5G security.   We present a comprehensive empirical evaluation using 52 different configurations (on 8 different language models) to analyze the impact of 1) domain adaptation, 2) model scale, 3) decoding strategies (greedy vs. stochastic sampling), and 4) prompting technique on STRIDE threat classification. Our results show that domain-adapted models do not consistently outperform their general-purpose counterparts, and decoding strategies significantly affect model behavior and output validity. They also show that while larger models generally achieve higher performance, these gains are neither consistent nor sufficient for reliable threat modelling. These findings highlight fundamental limitations of current LLMs for structured threat modelling tasks and suggest that improvements require more than additional training data or model scaling, motivating the need for incorporating more task-specific reasoning and stronger grounding in security concepts.   We present insights on invalid outputs encountered and present suggestions for prompting tailored specifically for STRIDE threat modelling.

ai.tldr: 本研究系统评估了领域自适应语言模型在结构化威胁建模（STRIDE）中的表现，发现领域自适应模型并不总是优于通用模型，解码策略显著影响输出有效性，且模型规模带来的性能提升不稳定。
ai.motivation: 现有研究主要评估通用大语言模型在有限提示设置下的威胁建模能力，缺乏对领域自适应模型、不同规模模型及解码策略的系统比较。
ai.method: 使用8种语言模型（含领域自适应和通用模型）在52种配置下，评估STRIDE威胁分类任务，分析领域自适应、模型规模、解码策略和提示技术的影响。
ai.result: 领域自适应模型未一致优于通用模型；解码策略显著影响输出有效性；大模型性能提升不稳定，不足以实现可靠威胁建模。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解当前语言模型在结构化威胁建模中的局限性，并提供针对STRIDE的提示设计建议。

idea_spark.idea: 结合任务特定推理和更强的安全概念基础，而非仅依赖更多数据或模型缩放。
idea_spark.risk: 领域自适应可能引入偏见或过拟合，导致在未见过的威胁模式上表现下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 探索混合模型架构，将语言模型与符号推理或知识图谱结合，提升威胁建模的可靠性。

en.tldr: This study systematically evaluates domain-adapted language models for structured threat modeling (STRIDE), finding that domain adaptation does not consistently outperform general models, decoding strategies significantly affect output validity, and gains from larger models are inconsistent.
en.motivation: Prior work mainly evaluated general-purpose LLMs under limited prompting settings, lacking systematic comparison of domain-adapted models, different scales, and decoding strategies.
en.method: Evaluated 8 language models (including domain-adapted and general) across 52 configurations on STRIDE threat classification, analyzing impact of domain adaptation, model scale, decoding strategy, and prompting technique.
en.result: Domain-adapted models do not consistently outperform general counterparts; decoding strategies significantly affect output validity; larger model performance gains are inconsistent and insufficient for reliable threat modeling.

idea_spark.inspiration: Explore hybrid architectures combining language models with symbolic reasoning or knowledge graphs to improve threat modeling reliability.

papers.title: REPTILES: Repeated Tiles of Sargantana, a RISC-V multicore based on OpenPiton
papers.summary: Chip industry continues advancing and expanding modern computing systems, resulting in more complex multi-core processors. Conversely, academic projects face scalability challenges due to limited resources, highlighting the need for open-source frameworks that enable innovation and knowledge sharing. Recently, several open-source proposals have emerged, offering flexible and scalable designs, but fail to meet the performance demands of modern High-Performance Computing (HPC) applications. In this project, we present REPTILES, an open-source RISC-V multicore framework based on OpenPiton\thanks. REPTILES interconnects multiple Sargantana cores with the memory hierarchy of OpenPiton. Moreover, we present the new features incorporated in Sargantana and OpenPiton designs to improve the performance of HPC applications. We demonstrate that REPTILES presents suitable scalability, achieving a speedup of 3.1x on average with 4 cores. Additionally, we show that Sargantana's new features increase the performance of vector addition benchmark in a 9.3x.

ai.tldr: 提出基于OpenPiton和Sargantana核的开源RISC-V多核框架REPTILES，实现4核平均3.1倍加速，向量加法性能提升9.3倍。
ai.motivation: 现有开源多核设计难以满足高性能计算应用的需求，学术项目受限于资源，需要可扩展的开源框架。
ai.method: 将多个Sargantana核通过OpenPiton的内存层次结构互连，并引入新特性优化HPC性能。
ai.result: 4核平均加速3.1倍，向量加法基准测试性能提升9.3倍。
ai.help_to_user: 提供可扩展的开源RISC-V多核设计参考，适用于HPC研究和教学。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将REPTILES框架与专用加速器（如GPU或FPGA）集成，进一步提升HPC性能。
idea_spark.risk: 开源社区维护和持续优化可能面临资源不足，性能提升依赖具体应用场景。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴OpenPiton的模块化设计，构建更灵活的开源多核平台。

en.method: Interconnects multiple Sargantana cores with OpenPiton's memory hierarchy and introduces new features for HPC optimization.
en.result: 3.1x average speedup with 4 cores; 9.3x performance gain in vector addition benchmark.

papers.title: Democratizing Measurement of Critical Mobile Infrastructure: Security and Privacy in an Increasingly Centralized Communication Ecosystem
papers.summary: Cellular networks serve as the backbone of global communication, providing critical access to telephony and the Internet, often in regions lacking alternatives. However, the growing complexity of these networks, driven by architectural innovations (e.g., Voice over IP, eSIMs) and commercial dynamics (e.g., roaming, virtual operators, zero-rating), remains poorly understood due to the lack of open, scalable, and geographically diverse measurement tools and independent measurement studies.   Moreover, access to mobile networks today is no longer limited to the traditional radio interface. Technologies like Voice-over-WiFi (VoWiFi) offer alternative connectivity paths via third-party Internet infrastructure, extending operator reach into environments with limited cellular coverage. At the same time, over-the-top (OTT) messaging services such as WhatsApp and Signal have become central to modern communication, accounting for a substantial share of global messaging and voice traffic while bypassing traditional operator-controlled channels entirely.   This dissertation addresses these challenges by introducing new approaches for independent, scalable, and reproducible measurements of mobile communication systems without requiring cooperation from network or platform operators. We design, implement, and open-source measurement platforms that enable controlled experiments across cellular radio networks, operator-provided services, and OTT messaging applications.

ai.tldr: 本论文提出独立、可扩展的移动通信系统测量方法，无需运营商合作，并开源测量平台。
ai.motivation: 蜂窝网络日益复杂，但缺乏开放、可扩展的测量工具和独立研究，导致安全与隐私问题难以评估。
ai.method: 设计并实现开源测量平台，支持对蜂窝无线网络、运营商服务和OTT消息应用进行受控实验。
ai.result: 提供了独立、可复现的测量方法，涵盖VoWiFi、eSIM、零费率等新技术场景。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员和用户理解移动基础设施的安全与隐私风险，促进透明度和问责制。
