papers.title: Model-based Dynamic 3D MRI Reconstructions using Neural Fields and Tensor Product Expansions
papers.summary: Conventional MRI reconstruction methods treat images and coil sensitivities as discrete objects, leading to high memory demands and limited structural awareness that hamper effective regularization. These limitations hinder accurate reconstruction in highly undersampled scenarios, such as dynamic 3D cardiac magnetic resonance (CMR). We introduce a discretization-free, memory-efficient, model-based framework for dynamic 2D and 3D MRI reconstruction from highly undersampled data. We represent magnetization and coil sensitivities as continuous objects -- differentiable functions -- using tensor products of univariate neural fields. This tensor product structure enables scalable optimization in high-dimensional spatiotemporal settings. Our method outperforms state-of-the-art model-based reconstructions in dynamic 2D and 3D MR settings, preserving structure and motion even under aggressive undersampling (e.g., acceleration factor 16).

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于神经场和张量积展开的无离散化、内存高效的动态3D MRI重建方法，在高度欠采样下优于现有方法。
ai.motivation: 传统MRI重建将图像和线圈灵敏度视为离散对象，内存需求高且缺乏结构感知，难以在高度欠采样动态3D心脏MRI中准确重建。
ai.method: 将磁化强度和线圈灵敏度表示为连续可微函数，使用单变量神经场的张量积，实现高维时空下的可扩展优化。
ai.result: 在动态2D和3D MRI中，即使在加速因子16的极端欠采样下，仍能保持结构和运动细节，性能优于现有模型驱动方法。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何利用神经场和张量积实现连续、内存高效的动态MRI重建，适用于加速扫描和高质量成像。

idea_spark.idea: 将神经场与张量积结合，在保持连续性的同时降低计算复杂度，为高维重建提供新思路。
idea_spark.risk: 神经场训练可能对超参数敏感，且张量积结构在极高维度下可能面临可扩展性挑战。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受神经辐射场（NeRF）启发，将隐式神经表示引入MRI重建，但通过张量积分解避免全连接网络的冗余计算。

en.tldr: A discretization-free, memory-efficient model-based framework using neural fields and tensor product expansions for dynamic 3D MRI reconstruction, outperforming state-of-the-art under high undersampling.
en.motivation: Conventional MRI reconstruction treats images and coil sensitivities as discrete objects, causing high memory demands and limited structural awareness, which hinders accurate reconstruction in highly undersampled dynamic 3D cardiac MRI.
en.method: Represent magnetization and coil sensitivities as continuous differentiable functions via tensor products of univariate neural fields, enabling scalable optimization in high-dimensional spatiotemporal settings.
en.result: Outperforms state-of-the-art model-based reconstructions in dynamic 2D and 3D MRI, preserving structure and motion even under aggressive undersampling (e.g., acceleration factor 16).
en.help_to_user: Provides insight into using neural fields and tensor products for continuous, memory-efficient dynamic MRI reconstruction, beneficial for accelerated scanning and high-quality imaging.

idea_spark.idea: Combining neural fields with tensor products maintains continuity while reducing computational complexity, offering a new approach for high-dimensional reconstruction.
idea_spark.risk: Training neural fields may be sensitive to hyperparameters, and the tensor product structure could face scalability issues in very high dimensions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by neural radiance fields (NeRF), the method introduces implicit neural representations to MRI reconstruction but avoids redundant computation of fully connected networks via tensor product decomposition.

papers.title: CausalGS: Learning Physical Causality of 3D Dynamic Scenes with Gaussian Representations
papers.summary: Learning a physical model from video data that can comprehend physical laws and predict the future trajectories of objects is a formidable challenge in artificial intelligence. Prior approaches either leverage various Partial Differential Equations (PDEs) as soft constraints in the form of PINN losses, or integrate physics simulators into neural networks; however, they often rely on strong priors or high-quality geometry reconstruction. In this paper, we propose CausalGS, a framework that learns the causal dynamics of complex dynamic 3D scenes solely from multi-view videos, while dispensing with the reliance on explicit priors. At its core is an inverse physics inference module that decouples the complex dynamics problem from the video into the joint inference of two factors: the initial velocity field representing the scene's kinematics, and the intrinsic material properties governing its dynamics. This inferred physical information is then utilized within a differentiable physics simulator to guide the learning process in a physics-regularized manner. Extensive experiments demonstrate that CausalGS surpasses the state-of-the-art on the highly challenging task of long-term future frame extrapolation, while also exhibiting advanced performance in novel view interpolation. Crucially, our work shows that, without any human annotation, the model is able to learn the complex interactions between multiple physical properties and understand the causal relationships driving the scene's dynamic evolution, solely from visual observations.

ai.motivation: 现有方法依赖强先验或高质量几何重建，难以从视频中直接学习物理规律。
ai.method: 通过逆物理推理模块解耦初始速度场和材料属性，并利用可微物理模拟器以物理正则化方式引导学习。
ai.result: 在长期未来帧外推和新视角插值任务上超越现有方法，且无需人工标注即可学习多物理属性间的复杂交互。

idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴因果推断中的解耦思想，将物理先验隐式编码于网络结构而非显式约束。

en.tldr: Proposes CausalGS, a framework that learns causal dynamics of complex 3D dynamic scenes solely from multi-view videos without explicit priors.
en.motivation: Existing methods rely on strong priors or high-quality geometry reconstruction, making it hard to learn physical laws directly from video.
en.method: An inverse physics inference module decouples the initial velocity field and intrinsic material properties, then a differentiable physics simulator guides learning with physics regularization.
en.result: Outperforms state-of-the-art on long-term future frame extrapolation and novel view interpolation, learning complex interactions between multiple physical properties without human annotation.
en.help_to_user: Provides a framework to automatically extract physical causality from visual observations, useful for dynamic scene prediction and rendering.

idea_spark.idea: Decompose scene dynamics into kinematic (initial velocity field) and dynamic (material properties) interpretable factors.
idea_spark.risk: Modeling multi-object interactions in complex scenes may be limited, and the differentiable simulator incurs high computational cost.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws on causal inference's disentanglement idea, encoding physical priors implicitly in network structure rather than explicit constraints.

papers.title: Cross-Modal Semantic-Enhanced Diffusion Framework for Diabetic Retinopathy Grading
papers.summary: Automated grading of diabetic retinopathy (DR) faces several critical challenges: subtle inter-grade visual distinctions in fine-grained lesion patterns, distributional discrepancies induced by heterogeneous imaging devices and acquisition conditions, and the inherent inability of purely visual approaches to exploit clinical semantic knowledge. In this paper, we propose CLIP-Guided Semantic Diffusion (CGSD), a DR grading framework that synergistically integrates vision-language pretraining with diffusion probabilistic modeling. We adopt a domain-specific vision-language model tailored for DR grading as the semantic guidance module and adapt it to the target domain via Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), effectively bridging the distributional gap between the pretrained model and the target dataset with only a minimal number of trainable parameters. Building on this foundation, we construct a cross-modal semantic conditioning vector by computing the dot product between image features and the text description features of each DR grade, yielding a joint representation that simultaneously encodes visual content and clinical-grade semantics. This vector serves as the conditioning signal for the diffusion denoising network, replacing the structurally complex dual-branch visual prior employed in existing diffusion-based classification methods. Experiments on the APTOS 2019 dataset demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves an accuracy of 87.5% and a macro-averaged F1 score of 0.731, outperforming a variety of representative methods. Ablation studies further validate the independent contribution of each constituent module.

ai.tldr: 提出CLIP引导的语义扩散框架（CGSD），通过视觉-语言预训练与扩散概率建模协同，解决糖尿病视网膜病变分级中的细微病变区分、设备分布差异及缺乏临床语义知识的问题。
ai.motivation: 现有纯视觉方法难以捕捉细粒度病变模式、受成像设备差异影响且无法利用临床语义知识。
ai.method: 采用领域特定视觉-语言模型作为语义引导模块，通过LoRA适配目标域；构建跨模态语义条件向量（图像特征与各等级文本描述特征的点积）作为扩散去噪网络的条件信号。
ai.result: 在APTOS 2019数据集上准确率87.5%，宏平均F1分数0.731，优于多种代表性方法。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种融合视觉与临床语义的糖尿病视网膜病变自动分级方法，可辅助医生提高诊断效率与一致性。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将扩散模型与多模态大语言模型结合，实现更丰富的临床知识注入与可解释性。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量文本描述；领域适配可能需额外标注数据；计算资源需求较高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用点积融合视觉与文本特征作为条件信号，简化了传统双分支结构，为多模态融合提供新思路。

en.tldr: Proposes CLIP-Guided Semantic Diffusion (CGSD) framework that synergizes vision-language pretraining with diffusion probabilistic modeling to address fine-grained lesion distinction, device distribution shifts, and lack of clinical semantics in diabetic retinopathy grading.
en.motivation: Existing purely visual methods struggle with subtle inter-grade differences, are affected by imaging device heterogeneity, and cannot leverage clinical semantic knowledge.
en.method: Uses a domain-specific vision-language model as semantic guidance module adapted via LoRA; constructs a cross-modal semantic conditioning vector (dot product of image features and text description features for each DR grade) as the conditioning signal for the diffusion denoising network.
en.result: Achieves 87.5% accuracy and 0.731 macro F1 on APTOS 2019, outperforming various representative methods.
en.help_to_user: Provides an automated DR grading method that integrates visual and clinical semantics, potentially aiding clinicians in improving diagnostic efficiency and consistency.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining diffusion models with multimodal large language models for richer clinical knowledge injection and interpretability.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on high-quality text descriptions; domain adaptation may require additional labeled data; high computational resource demand.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using dot product to fuse visual and text features as conditioning signal simplifies traditional dual-branch structures, offering a new perspective for multimodal fusion.

papers.title: MegaScale-Omni: A Hyper-Scale, Workload-Resilient System for MultiModal LLM Training in Production
papers.summary: As the foundational component of versatile AI applications, training an multimodal large language model (MLLM) relies on multimodal datasets with dynamic modality mixture proportions and sample length distributions. However, existing MLLM systems remain inefficient under dynamic workloads, due to statically coupled decisions of resource allocation and model parallelization between encoders and the LLM backbone. This paper presents MegaScale-Omni, an industrial-grade MLLM training system tailored for dynamic workload adaption and hyper-scale deployment. MegaScale-Omni is built upon the training scheme of encoder-LLM multiplexing with three key innovations: (1) Decoupled parallelism strategies with long-short sequence parallelism for encoders to process variable-length samples, and full-fledged 5D parallelism for the LLM backbone, both organized under a communication-efficient parallelization layout. (2) Unified encoder-LLM representations for flexible, extensible colocation, and a new paradigm of encoder-LLM joint pipeline with workload resilience. (3) Workload balancing techniques via decentralized grouped reordering in data loaders and adaptive resharding from encoder to LLM ranks. MegaScale-Omni is deployed as the foundation of our in-house large-scale MLLM training tasks with thousands of GPUs. Our experimental results demonstrate $1.27\times$-$7.57\times$ throughput improvement under production-grade dynamic workloads, as compared to four state-of-the-art systems.

ai.tldr: 提出MegaScale-Omni，一个面向多模态大模型训练的工业级系统，通过解耦并行策略、统一编码器-LLM表示和负载均衡技术，在动态工作负载下实现1.27倍至7.57倍的吞吐量提升。
ai.motivation: 现有MLLM训练系统在动态多模态数据集（如混合比例和样本长度变化）下效率低下，因为资源分配和模型并行化策略是静态耦合的。
ai.method: 三大创新：1）编码器采用长短序列并行，LLM骨干采用5D并行，并组织成通信高效的并行布局；2）统一编码器-LLM表示实现灵活共置，以及具有工作负载弹性的联合流水线；3）通过数据加载器中的去中心化分组重排序和编码器到LLM的自适应重分片实现负载均衡。
ai.result: 在数千GPU的生产环境中，与四个最先进系统相比，吞吐量提升1.27倍至7.57倍。
ai.help_to_user: 如果你需要训练多模态大模型并面临动态数据分布和扩展性问题，该系统提供了解耦并行和负载均衡的实用方案。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将编码器-LLM联合流水线范式应用于更广泛的视觉-语言模型或多任务学习。
idea_spark.risk: 系统复杂度高，实现和调优成本较大；动态负载均衡可能引入额外开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从生产环境动态工作负载出发，设计弹性并行和负载均衡机制，启发我们关注实际部署中的非理想条件。

en.tldr: Proposes MegaScale-Omni, an industrial-grade MLLM training system that achieves 1.27x-7.57x throughput improvement under dynamic workloads via decoupled parallelism, unified encoder-LLM representation, and workload balancing.
en.motivation: Existing MLLM systems are inefficient under dynamic multimodal datasets (e.g., varying modality mixture and sample lengths) due to statically coupled resource allocation and model parallelization.
en.method: Three key innovations: (1) Decoupled parallelism with long-short sequence parallelism for encoders and full 5D parallelism for LLM backbone under a communication-efficient layout; (2) Unified encoder-LLM representations for flexible colocation and a joint pipeline with workload resilience; (3) Workload balancing via decentralized grouped reordering in data loaders and adaptive resharding from encoder to LLM ranks.
en.result: Deployed on thousands of GPUs, achieving 1.27x-7.57x throughput improvement over four state-of-the-art systems under production dynamic workloads.
en.help_to_user: If you train multimodal LLMs facing dynamic data distributions and scaling challenges, this system offers practical solutions with decoupled parallelism and load balancing.

idea_spark.idea: Explore applying the encoder-LLM joint pipeline paradigm to broader vision-language models or multi-task learning.
idea_spark.risk: High system complexity may increase implementation and tuning costs; dynamic load balancing could introduce overhead.
idea_spark.inspiration: Starting from production dynamic workloads to design elastic parallelism and load balancing inspires focusing on non-ideal conditions in real deployments.

papers.title: Enhancing Consistency Models for Multi-Agent Trajectory Prediction
papers.summary: Diffusion models for multi-agent trajectory prediction are limited by iterative denoising, which causes inference latency that hinders their use in time-critical settings like autonomous driving. Fast-sampling variants using DDIM and informed initial noise distributions partially alleviate this issue, but they either fail to achieve true single-step generation or are constrained by the chosen noise distribution. Consistency Models (CMs) offer high-quality one-step generation by mapping noise directly to data, but are difficult to train from scratch . We propose ECTraj, an enhanced CM pipeline with improved training and conditional generation for trajectory prediction. Our framework extends the student-teacher consistency training scheme: the student produces standard outputs, while the teacher explicitly fuses its predictions with parts of the ground truth to give stronger supervision. We also exploit CMs' direct denoising for top-K multi-shot generation during training. Combining conditional generation with this enhanced consistency objective yields faster inference and improved prediction accuracy, establishing competitive new benchmarks on the large-scale Argoverse 2 dataset.

ai.tldr: 提出ECTraj，通过增强一致性模型实现多智能体轨迹预测的单步生成，在Argoverse 2上取得竞争性基准。
ai.motivation: 扩散模型迭代去噪导致推理延迟，不适合自动驾驶等时间敏感场景；现有快速采样方法无法实现真正单步生成或受限于噪声分布。
ai.method: 扩展学生-教师一致性训练：教师显式融合预测与部分真值提供更强监督；利用一致性模型直接去噪进行训练时top-K多轮生成。
ai.result: 在Argoverse 2数据集上实现更快推理和更高预测精度，建立新的竞争性基准。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种高效的单步轨迹预测方法，适用于自动驾驶等实时应用。

idea_spark.idea: 将一致性模型与条件生成结合，利用top-K多轮生成提升训练多样性。
idea_spark.risk: 教师融合真值可能引入过拟合风险，需谨慎设计融合比例。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从扩散模型加速需求出发，借鉴一致性模型直接映射特性，创新训练范式。

idea_spark.inspiration: 从扩散模型加速需求出发，借鉴一致性模型直接映射特性，创新训练范式。

en.tldr: Proposes ECTraj, an enhanced consistency model pipeline for single-step multi-agent trajectory prediction, achieving competitive benchmarks on Argoverse 2.
en.motivation: Diffusion models suffer from inference latency due to iterative denoising, unsuitable for time-critical settings like autonomous driving; existing fast samplers fail to achieve true single-step generation or are constrained by noise distribution.
en.method: Extends student-teacher consistency training: teacher explicitly fuses predictions with ground truth for stronger supervision; leverages direct denoising of consistency models for top-K multi-shot generation during training.
en.result: Achieves faster inference and improved prediction accuracy on Argoverse 2, establishing new competitive benchmarks.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient single-step trajectory prediction method suitable for real-time applications like autonomous driving.

idea_spark.risk: Teacher fusion with ground truth may introduce overfitting risk; careful design of fusion ratio is needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the need to accelerate diffusion models, leverages the direct mapping property of consistency models to innovate training paradigms.

papers.title: Cloud Performance Decomposition for Long-Term Performance Engineering: A Case Study
papers.summary: Cloud performance fluctuates due to factors such as resource contention and workload changes. These factors can be short-term, seasonal, or long-term. Their effects are often intertwined in performance traces, making performance management difficult. Prior work on cloud performance engineering used time-series decomposition to separate these factors. However, existing approaches rely on basic decomposition methods that may miss key variation patterns and fail on traces with complex or intermittent patterns, limiting their usefulness across diverse cloud deployments.   To address this limitation, we propose two time-series decomposition techniques for cloud performance engineering: a hybrid/manual method and a fully automatic method. Through a case study of 11 serverless functions, we show that both approaches can successfully and consistently reveal trends and seasonal cycles, such as weekly and quarterly patterns, which are otherwise obscured.   As an evaluation and application of the decomposition, we used the decomposed components to predict future performance, yielding mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) values of only 1.8\% (hybrid) and 2.1\% (automatic), significantly outperforming basic time-series methods and deep learning. We further show that decomposition insights can guide practical resource allocation. Using decomposition-informed scaling on AWS, we reduced latency variability by over 60\% and maximum latency by 10\%. Similar experiments on benchmarks on AWS confirmed that seasonal patterns and performance gains generalize beyond our case study. Notably, our findings demonstrate that even a single performance trace contains rich actionable information for guiding cloud management decisions.

ai.tldr: 提出两种时间序列分解方法（混合/手动和全自动），通过11个无服务器函数的案例研究，成功揭示被掩盖的趋势和季节周期，并用于性能预测和资源分配，显著降低延迟变异性和最大延迟。
ai.motivation: 现有云性能工程依赖的基础分解方法可能遗漏关键变化模式，在复杂或间歇性模式的时间序列上表现不佳，限制了其在不同云部署中的实用性。
ai.method: 提出混合/手动和全自动两种时间序列分解技术，用于分离云性能迹线中的短期、季节性和长期因素。
ai.result: 在11个无服务器函数上，分解后性能预测MAPE分别为1.8%（混合）和2.1%（自动），优于基础方法和深度学习；基于分解的AWS扩缩容将延迟变异性降低超过60%，最大延迟降低10%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解云性能迹线中的隐藏模式，并利用分解结果进行更准确的性能预测和资源分配决策。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将分解结果与强化学习结合，实现自适应资源调度。
idea_spark.risk: 分解方法可能对超短期或非周期性噪声敏感，需进一步验证鲁棒性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 单一性能迹线包含丰富可操作信息，启发从少量数据中提取管理决策依据。

zh.method: 提出混合/手动和全自动两种时间序列分解技术，用于分离云性能迹线中的短期、季节性和长期因素。
zh.result: 在11个无服务器函数上，分解后性能预测MAPE分别为1.8%（混合）和2.1%（自动），优于基础方法和深度学习；基于分解的AWS扩缩容将延迟变异性降低超过60%，最大延迟降低10%。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解云性能迹线中的隐藏模式，并利用分解结果进行更准确的性能预测和资源分配决策。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将分解结果与强化学习结合，实现自适应资源调度。
idea_spark.risk: 分解方法可能对超短期或非周期性噪声敏感，需进一步验证鲁棒性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 单一性能迹线包含丰富可操作信息，启发从少量数据中提取管理决策依据。

en.tldr: Proposes two time-series decomposition methods (hybrid/manual and fully automatic) for cloud performance engineering. A case study on 11 serverless functions reveals hidden trends and seasonal cycles, enabling accurate performance prediction and resource allocation, reducing latency variability by over 60% and maximum latency by 10% on AWS.
en.motivation: Existing cloud performance engineering relies on basic decomposition methods that may miss key variation patterns and fail on complex or intermittent traces, limiting their usefulness across diverse cloud deployments.
en.method: Proposes hybrid/manual and fully automatic time-series decomposition techniques to separate short-term, seasonal, and long-term factors in cloud performance traces.
en.result: On 11 serverless functions, decomposed components yield MAPE of 1.8% (hybrid) and 2.1% (automatic) for future performance prediction, outperforming basic methods and deep learning. Decomposition-informed scaling on AWS reduces latency variability by over 60% and maximum latency by 10%.
en.help_to_user: Helps users uncover hidden patterns in cloud performance traces and leverage decomposition results for more accurate performance prediction and resource allocation decisions.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining decomposition results with reinforcement learning for adaptive resource scheduling.
idea_spark.risk: Decomposition methods may be sensitive to very short-term or non-periodic noise; robustness needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: A single performance trace contains rich actionable information, inspiring decision-making from limited data.

papers.title: DySurface: Consistent 4D Surface Reconstruction via Bridging Explicit Gaussians and Implicit Functions
papers.summary: While novel view synthesis (NVS) for dynamic scenes has seen significant progress, reconstructing temporally consistent geometric surfaces remains a challenge. Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF) and 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) offer powerful dynamic scene rendering capabilities; however, relying solely on photometric optimization often leads to geometric ambiguities. This results in discontinuous surfaces, severe artifacts, and broken surfaces over time. To address these limitations, we present DySurface, a novel framework that bridges the effectiveness of explicit Gaussians with the geometric fidelity of implicit Signed Distance Functions (SDFs) in dynamic scenes. Our approach tackles the structural discrepancy between the forward deformation of 3DGS ($canonical \rightarrow dynamic$) and the backward deformation required for volumetric SDF rendering ($dynamic \rightarrow canonical$). Specifically, we propose the VoxGS-DSDF branch that leverages deformed Gaussians to construct a dynamic sparse voxel grid, providing explicit geometric guidance to the implicit SDF field. This explicit anchoring effectively regularizes the volumetric rendering process, significantly improving surface reconstruction quality, with watertight boundaries and detailed representations. Quantitative and qualitative experiments demonstrate that DySurface significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in geometric accuracy metrics while maintaining competitive rendering performance.

ai.tldr: 提出DySurface框架，结合显式高斯与隐式SDF，实现动态场景中时间一致的4D表面重建。
ai.motivation: 现有动态场景渲染方法（NeRF、3DGS）仅依赖光度优化，导致几何歧义、表面不连续和随时间断裂。
ai.method: 通过VoxGS-DSDF分支，将变形高斯构建的动态稀疏体素网格作为显式几何引导，约束隐式SDF场的体渲染过程。
ai.result: 在几何精度指标上显著超越基线，同时保持竞争性渲染性能，生成水密边界和细节表面。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种在动态场景中同时获得高质量渲染和精确几何表面的方法。

idea_spark.idea: 利用显式高斯引导隐式SDF，解决前向变形与后向渲染之间的结构不一致性。
idea_spark.risk: 体素网格分辨率与计算开销的平衡可能限制高分辨率实时应用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从3DGS的显式表示与NeRF的隐式几何中取长补短，启发其他混合表示方法的设计。

zh.motivation: 现有动态场景渲染方法（NeRF、3DGS）仅依赖光度优化，导致几何歧义、表面不连续和随时间断裂。

en.tldr: DySurface bridges explicit Gaussians and implicit SDFs for temporally consistent 4D surface reconstruction in dynamic scenes.
en.motivation: Existing dynamic scene rendering methods (NeRF, 3DGS) rely solely on photometric optimization, leading to geometric ambiguities, discontinuous surfaces, and temporal breakage.
en.method: A VoxGS-DSDF branch uses deformed Gaussians to construct a dynamic sparse voxel grid, providing explicit geometric guidance to regularize the implicit SDF field during volumetric rendering.
en.result: Significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in geometric accuracy while maintaining competitive rendering performance, producing watertight boundaries and detailed surfaces.
en.help_to_user: Offers a method to achieve both high-quality rendering and accurate geometry in dynamic scenes.

idea_spark.idea: Use explicit Gaussians to guide implicit SDFs, resolving the structural mismatch between forward deformation and backward rendering.
idea_spark.risk: The trade-off between voxel grid resolution and computational cost may limit high-resolution real-time applications.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining strengths of 3DGS explicit representation and NeRF implicit geometry inspires design of other hybrid representations.

papers.title: Quantifiable Uncertainty: A Stochastic Consensus Multi-Agent RAG Framework for Robust Malware Detection
papers.summary: While contemporary deep learning malware detectors define a dominant defense paradigm, their sophistication also exposes them to novel structural evasion attacks, a limitation we attribute to their inherent inability to express epistemic uncertainty. To address this challenge, we present MAGMA, a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework that decouples malware analysis into semantic code retrieval and probabilistic verification. In contrast to monolithic classifiers, MAGMA employs a dual-stream embedding scheme over assembly and pseudo-code representations to isolate Decision-Critical Functions (DCFs) from the noise of dead code. We further introduce a Stochastic Consistency Ensemble, in which multiple instances of the same reasoning agent independently evaluate the retrieval set under non-deterministic sampling. From this ensemble, we derive two complementary metrics: Function Evidence Strength (FES), a weighted aggregation of retrieval confidence, and the Evidence Conflict Score (ECS), defined as the Shannon entropy of the ensemble's predictive distribution. We show that elevated ECS values serve as an effective proxy for structural ambiguity, enabling the system to implement a principled ``reject-option'' policy. Extensive evaluation demonstrates that MAGMA achieves a 98.4% detection rate, substantially exceeding existing solutions.

ai.tldr: 提出MAGMA框架，通过随机一致性集成和检索增强生成实现鲁棒的恶意软件检测，达到98.4%的检测率。
ai.motivation: 现有深度学习恶意软件检测器无法表达认知不确定性，易受结构性规避攻击。
ai.method: 采用双流嵌入方案分离决策关键函数，引入随机一致性集成，推导函数证据强度和证据冲突分数，实现拒绝选项策略。
ai.result: 检测率达98.4%，显著优于现有方案。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过不确定性量化提升恶意软件检测的鲁棒性。

idea_spark.idea: 利用检索增强生成和不确定性度量实现可解释的拒绝决策。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量代码表示和检索集，可能受对抗性检索干扰。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将认知不确定性引入安全检测，启发更可靠的防御机制。

en.tldr: Proposes MAGMA, a stochastic consensus multi-agent RAG framework for robust malware detection, achieving 98.4% detection rate.
en.motivation: Current deep learning malware detectors lack epistemic uncertainty expression, making them vulnerable to structural evasion attacks.
en.method: Uses dual-stream embedding to isolate decision-critical functions, introduces stochastic consistency ensemble, derives Function Evidence Strength and Evidence Conflict Score, and implements a reject-option policy.
en.result: Detection rate of 98.4%, significantly outperforming existing solutions.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how uncertainty quantification enhances robustness in malware detection.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage retrieval-augmented generation and uncertainty metrics for interpretable rejection decisions.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on high-quality code representations and retrieval sets, potentially vulnerable to adversarial retrieval.
idea_spark.inspiration: Introducing epistemic uncertainty into security detection inspires more reliable defense mechanisms.

papers.title: PaceVGGT: Pre-Alternating-Attention Token Pruning for Visual Geometry Transformers
papers.summary: Visual Geometry Transformer (VGGT) is a strong feed-forward model for multiple 3D tasks, but its Alternating-Attention (AA) stack scales quadratically in the total token count, making long clips expensive.   Existing token-reduction accelerators operate inside AA, leaving the patch grid that enters AA uncompressed.   We introduce PaceVGGT, a pre-AA token pruning framework that prunes DINO patch tokens before the first AA block of a frozen VGGT. PaceVGGT trains a lightweight Token Scorer that estimates per-token importance from DINO features. The scorer is first distilled against an AA-internal attention target from the unpruned backbone, then refined under downstream camera, depth, and point-map losses.   A per-frame keep budget fixes the backbone-visible sequence length, while an importance-adaptive merge/prune assignment preserves residual content from high-saliency frames under a fixed total merge budget.   A Feature-guided Restoration module reconstructs the dense spatial grid required by the prediction heads.   On ScanNet-50 and 7-Scenes, PaceVGGT remains on the reconstruction quality--latency frontier while reducing inference latency.   On ScanNet-50, it reduces latency by \(5.1\times\) over unmodified VGGT at \(N=300\) and \(1.47\times\) over LiteVGGT at \(N=1000\).   These results identify pre-AA pruning as a viable acceleration route for frozen VGGT-style geometry transformers.

ai.tldr: 提出PaceVGGT，在冻结的VGGT第一个交替注意力块之前剪枝DINO patch token，实现5.1倍加速且保持重建质量。
ai.motivation: VGGT的交替注意力堆栈随token总数二次增长，长序列推理昂贵；现有加速方法在AA内部操作，未压缩进入AA的patch网格。
ai.method: 训练轻量Token Scorer基于DINO特征估计token重要性，先蒸馏AA内部注意力目标，再经下游损失微调；采用每帧固定保留预算和重要性自适应合并/剪枝分配，最后用特征引导恢复模块重建密集空间网格。
ai.result: 在ScanNet-50上比原始VGGT延迟降低5.1倍（N=300），比LiteVGGT降低1.47倍（N=1000），处于重建质量-延迟前沿。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需修改VGGT主干即可显著加速3D几何变换器推理的实用剪枝方案。

idea_spark.idea: 利用DINO特征作为通用重要性信号，结合蒸馏和下游损失微调，实现任务感知的token剪枝。
idea_spark.risk: 剪枝可能丢失细粒度几何细节，尤其在低保留预算下；依赖DINO特征可能引入领域偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将剪枝时机从注意力内部提前到输入阶段，配合自适应合并/剪枝分配，为加速冻结模型提供新思路。

idea_spark.risk: 剪枝可能丢失细粒度几何细节，尤其在低保留预算下；依赖DINO特征可能引入领域偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将剪枝时机从注意力内部提前到输入阶段，配合自适应合并/剪枝分配，为加速冻结模型提供新思路。

en.tldr: PaceVGGT prunes DINO patch tokens before the first alternating-attention block of a frozen VGGT, achieving 5.1× speedup while maintaining reconstruction quality.
en.motivation: VGGT's alternating-attention stack scales quadratically with total token count, making long clips expensive; existing accelerators operate inside AA without compressing the input patch grid.
en.method: A lightweight Token Scorer estimates per-token importance from DINO features, first distilled against an AA-internal attention target, then refined with downstream losses. A per-frame keep budget and importance-adaptive merge/prune assignment are used, followed by a Feature-guided Restoration module to reconstruct the dense spatial grid.
en.result: On ScanNet-50, latency is reduced by 5.1× over VGGT (N=300) and 1.47× over LiteVGGT (N=1000), staying on the quality-latency frontier.
en.help_to_user: Provides a practical pruning method to significantly accelerate frozen 3D geometry transformers without modifying the backbone.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage DINO features as a universal importance signal, combined with distillation and downstream loss fine-tuning for task-aware token pruning.
idea_spark.risk: Pruning may lose fine-grained geometric details, especially under low keep budgets; reliance on DINO features may introduce domain bias.
idea_spark.inspiration: Moving pruning from inside attention to the input stage, with adaptive merge/prune assignment, offers a new direction for accelerating frozen models.

papers.title: CapVector: Learning Transferable Capability Vectors in Parametric Space for Vision-Language-Action Models
papers.summary: This paper proposes a novel approach to address the challenge that pretrained VLA models often fail to effectively improve performance and reduce adaptation costs during standard supervised finetuning (SFT). Some advanced finetuning methods with auxiliary training objectives can improve performance and reduce the number of convergence steps. However, they typically incur significant computational overhead due to the additional losses from auxiliary objectives. To simultaneously achieve the enhanced capabilities of auxiliary training with the simplicity of standard SFT, we decouple the two objectives of auxiliary-objective SFT within the parameter space, namely, enhancing general capabilities and fitting task-specific action distributions. To deliver the goal, we only need to train the model to converge on a small-scale task set using two distinct training strategies, resulting in two finetuned models. The parameters' difference between the two models can then be interpreted as capability vectors provided by auxiliary objectives. These vectors are then merged with pretrained parameters to form a capability-enhanced meta model. Moreover, when standard SFT is augmented with a lightweight orthogonal regularization loss, the merged model attains performance comparable to auxiliary finetuned baselines with reduced computational overhead. Internal and external experiments demonstrate that our capability vectors (1) are effective and versatile across diverse models, (2) can generalize to novel environments and embodiments out of the box.

ai.tldr: 提出CapVector方法，在参数空间中解耦辅助训练目标，通过参数差异构建能力向量，实现高效迁移微调。
ai.motivation: 预训练VLA模型在标准监督微调中性能提升有限且收敛慢，现有辅助目标方法计算开销大。
ai.method: 在小型任务集上用两种策略训练得到两个微调模型，取其参数差作为能力向量，合并到预训练参数中形成增强元模型，并辅以轻量正交正则化。
ai.result: 能力向量有效且通用，能零样本泛化到新环境和实体，计算开销低于辅助微调基线。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种低计算成本的VLA模型微调方案，提升性能并减少适配成本。

idea_spark.idea: 将辅助目标的能力解耦为参数空间中的向量，通过模型合并实现高效增强。
idea_spark.risk: 能力向量的通用性可能受限于训练任务集的代表性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 参数差异作为能力表示，可启发其他多目标学习中的知识解耦与复用。

zh.tldr: 提出CapVector方法，在参数空间中解耦辅助训练目标，通过参数差异构建能力向量，实现高效迁移微调。
zh.motivation: 预训练VLA模型在标准监督微调中性能提升有限且收敛慢，现有辅助目标方法计算开销大。

zh.result: 能力向量有效且通用，能零样本泛化到新环境和实体，计算开销低于辅助微调基线。
zh.help_to_user: 提供一种低计算成本的VLA模型微调方案，提升性能并减少适配成本。

idea_spark.idea: 将辅助目标的能力解耦为参数空间中的向量，通过模型合并实现高效增强。
idea_spark.risk: 能力向量的通用性可能受限于训练任务集的代表性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 参数差异作为能力表示，可启发其他多目标学习中的知识解耦与复用。

en.tldr: Proposes CapVector, which decouples auxiliary training objectives in parameter space, uses parameter differences as capability vectors, and merges them for efficient transfer finetuning.
en.motivation: Pretrained VLA models underperform in standard SFT with slow convergence; existing auxiliary methods incur high computational cost.
en.method: Train two models on a small task set with two strategies, compute parameter difference as capability vectors, merge with pretrained parameters, and add lightweight orthogonal regularization.
en.result: Capability vectors are effective and versatile, generalize zero-shot to new environments and embodiments, with lower overhead than auxiliary baselines.
en.help_to_user: Offers a low-cost finetuning approach for VLA models that improves performance and reduces adaptation effort.

idea_spark.idea: Represent auxiliary objective capabilities as parameter-space vectors and merge for efficient enhancement.
idea_spark.risk: Generality of vectors may depend on representativeness of training task set.
idea_spark.inspiration: Parameter differences as capability representations can inspire knowledge decoupling and reuse in multi-objective learning.

papers.title: The Direct Integration Theorem: A Rigorous Framework for Consistent Discrete Solutions of the Inverse Radon Problem
papers.summary: This paper presents a novel Direct Integration Theorem (DIT), derived as a non-trivial corollary of the classical Central Slice Theorem (CST). The DIT provides a mathematically consistent transition from the continuous to the discrete domain - a fundamental challenge in computed tomography - thereby eliminating the need for frequency-domain interpolation without resorting to conventional ramp-filtering. The proposed approach circumvents two principal limitations inherent in traditional methods: (i) the zero-frequency singularity and spectral distortions introduced by the mandatory ramp-filtering step, and (ii) discretization inaccuracies associated with frequency-domain interpolation. Based on the DIT, we develop a rigorous framework for consistent discrete solutions of the inverse Radon problem. Mathematical modeling demonstrates that this approach achieves quasi-exact reconstruction, with errors constrained solely by sampling parameters and grid geometry. Furthermore, while Filtered Back Projection (FBP) inherently distorts the variance of the reconstructed image, the DIT-based algorithm preserves it. Comparative simulations confirm that the proposed method eliminates common artifacts, such as intensity cupping, and consistently outperforms FBP in terms of PSNR, SSIM, and reprojection fidelity, faithfully restoring the original image's statistical characteristics.

ai.tldr: 本文提出直接积分定理，为逆拉东问题提供从连续到离散的一致框架，无需频域插值和斜坡滤波，实现准精确重建。
ai.motivation: 传统滤波反投影方法存在零频奇异性和频域插值误差，且会扭曲重建图像的统计特性。
ai.method: 基于中心切片定理推导直接积分定理，建立离散域的一致求解框架，避免斜坡滤波和频域插值。
ai.result: 重建误差仅受采样参数和网格几何限制，保持图像方差，消除杯状伪影，PSNR、SSIM和重投影保真度优于FBP。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解CT重建中离散化误差的来源及一种无滤波反投影的替代方案。

idea_spark.idea: 将直接积分定理与深度学习结合，学习最优离散化策略。
idea_spark.risk: 实际CT系统中非理想采样和噪声可能影响定理的适用性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从连续到离散的严格数学转换可启发其他成像模态的离散算法设计。

idea_spark.idea: 将直接积分定理与深度学习结合，学习最优离散化策略。
idea_spark.risk: 实际CT系统中非理想采样和噪声可能影响定理的适用性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从连续到离散的严格数学转换可启发其他成像模态的离散算法设计。

en.tldr: This paper proposes the Direct Integration Theorem, providing a consistent continuous-to-discrete framework for the inverse Radon problem without frequency-domain interpolation or ramp-filtering, achieving quasi-exact reconstruction.
en.motivation: Traditional Filtered Back Projection suffers from zero-frequency singularity, frequency-domain interpolation errors, and distortion of reconstructed image statistics.
en.method: Derive the Direct Integration Theorem as a corollary of the Central Slice Theorem, establishing a consistent discrete solution framework that bypasses ramp-filtering and frequency-domain interpolation.
en.result: Reconstruction error is bounded only by sampling parameters and grid geometry; preserves image variance, eliminates cupping artifacts, and outperforms FBP in PSNR, SSIM, and reprojection fidelity.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the source of discretization errors in CT reconstruction and an alternative to filtered back projection.

idea_spark.idea: Combine the Direct Integration Theorem with deep learning to learn optimal discretization strategies.
idea_spark.risk: Non-ideal sampling and noise in practical CT systems may affect the theorem's applicability.
idea_spark.inspiration: The rigorous continuous-to-discrete mathematical transformation can inspire discrete algorithm design for other imaging modalities.

papers.title: Categorical Message Passing Language (CaMPL) for programmers
papers.summary: Categorical Message Passing Language (CaMPL) is a functional-style concurrent programming language whose semantics is in category theory, more specifically, linear actegories. Its core programming feature is message passing along typed communication channels between concurrent processes. CaMPL also supports controlled non-determinism via 'races' which allow processes to adapt dynamically while they are running, higher-order processes which pass other processes as messages, and custom channel datatypes called protocols and coprotocols which allow one to define infinite channel types or implement session types.   The type system of CaMPL arises from a Curry-Howard-Lambek-like correspondence for concurrent programming, established by Cockett and Pastro in their paper titled "The logic of message passing". This type system ensures that a formal CaMPL program, i.e., one which does not allow general recursion, will never become deadlocked or livelocked. In this article, we explore the type system of CaMPL, custom channel types, and controlled non-determinism using code examples after briefly introducing its mathematical underpinnings.

ai.tldr: CaMPL是一种基于范畴论（线性作用范畴）的函数式并发编程语言，通过类型化通信通道实现消息传递，并支持受控非确定性、高阶进程及自定义通道类型。
ai.motivation: 为并发编程提供一种基于范畴论语义的类型安全语言，避免死锁和活锁。
ai.method: 利用线性作用范畴建立消息传递逻辑，通过Curry-Howard-Lambek对应设计类型系统，并引入协议/共协议定义无限通道类型。
ai.result: 形式化CaMPL程序（禁止一般递归）保证无死锁和活锁，支持受控非确定性和高阶进程。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助程序员理解如何用范畴论思想编写安全、无死锁的并发程序。

idea_spark.idea: 利用协议/共协议机制实现会话类型，可扩展至微服务通信协议的形式化验证。
idea_spark.risk: 范畴论门槛较高，可能限制实际采用；禁止一般递归可能降低表达力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受Cockett和Pastro的“消息传递逻辑”启发，将范畴论直接嵌入语言设计。

en.tldr: CaMPL is a functional-style concurrent language based on category theory (linear actegories), using typed channels for message passing, with controlled non-determinism, higher-order processes, and custom channel types.
en.motivation: To provide a type-safe concurrent language grounded in categorical semantics that guarantees freedom from deadlock and livelock.
en.method: Leverages linear actegories to formalize message-passing logic, designs a type system via Curry-Howard-Lambek correspondence, and introduces protocols/coprotocols for infinite channel types.
en.result: Formal CaMPL programs (without general recursion) are deadlock- and livelock-free, while supporting controlled non-determinism and higher-order processes.
en.help_to_user: Helps programmers write safe, deadlock-free concurrent programs using categorical principles.

idea_spark.idea: Protocols/coprotocols enable session types, which could be extended to formal verification of microservice communication protocols.
idea_spark.risk: High entry barrier due to category theory; banning general recursion may limit expressiveness.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by Cockett and Pastro's 'logic of message passing', embedding category theory directly into language design.

papers.title: Fetal Brain Imaging: A Composite Neural Network Approach for Keyframe Detection in Ultrasound Videos
papers.summary: This article presents a novel approach to keyframe detection in ultrasound videos, with a particular focus on fetal brain imaging. The proposed model is a composite neural network architecture that combines a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) with a Recurrent Neural Network (RNN). The CNN extracts spatial features from individual video frames, while the RNN captures temporal dependencies between consecutive frames within each video sequence.   The proposed model may improve the efficiency and accuracy of fetal brain ultrasound analysis, thereby supporting earlier detection, diagnosis, and treatment planning for selected fetal brain conditions.

ai.tldr: 提出一种结合CNN和RNN的复合神经网络，用于超声视频中胎儿脑部关键帧检测。
ai.motivation: 提高胎儿脑部超声分析的效率和准确性，支持早期诊断和治疗规划。
ai.method: 使用CNN提取单帧空间特征，RNN捕获帧间时序依赖，构建复合神经网络。
ai.result: 可能提升关键帧检测性能，但未提供具体量化结果。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户自动识别超声视频中的关键帧，减少人工筛选工作量。

idea_spark.idea: 结合注意力机制或自监督学习，进一步提升模型对模糊帧的鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 缺乏大规模标注数据集时，模型泛化能力可能受限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索将时序建模与空间注意力结合，用于动态器官的实时监测。

en.tldr: Proposes a composite neural network combining CNN and RNN for keyframe detection in fetal brain ultrasound videos.
en.motivation: To improve efficiency and accuracy of fetal brain ultrasound analysis, supporting early diagnosis and treatment planning.
en.method: Uses CNN for spatial feature extraction from individual frames and RNN for capturing temporal dependencies across consecutive frames.
en.result: May enhance keyframe detection performance, but no quantitative results are provided.
en.help_to_user: Helps users automatically identify keyframes in ultrasound videos, reducing manual screening effort.

idea_spark.idea: Integrate attention mechanisms or self-supervised learning to improve robustness against blurry frames.
idea_spark.risk: Generalization may be limited without large-scale annotated datasets.
idea_spark.inspiration: Explore combining temporal modeling with spatial attention for real-time monitoring of dynamic organs.

papers.title: Permit: Permission-Aware Representation Intervention for Controlled Generation in Large Language Models
papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in enterprise settings where they handle sensitive documents and user context, raising acute concerns over security and controllability. Conventional access control regulates whether information is accessible to the model, yet leaves how the model uses that information at generation time largely unconstrained: once sensitive content enters the context, outputs may still drift beyond a user's authorized scope. We present Permit, a novel permission-aware representation intervention framework that closes this gap by enforcing fine-grained control directly on the model's hidden states. Through exploratory analysis, we find that permission conditions induce hidden-state shifts that are (i) separable across permissions and (ii) concentrated in a small set of dominant directions. Permit exploits this geometry in two stages: it first identifies a permission-sensitive subspace from activation differences across permission conditions, and then performs lightweight interventions within this subspace to steer generation, with two concrete instantiations (offset-based and gated). Both operate atop a frozen backbone with only a handful of permission-specific parameters, achieving precise control with minimal overhead. Experimental results demonstrate that Permit performs better than the state-of-the-art method across multiple permission settings while driving information leakage to near zero, achieving over 18% F1-score improvement with >98% fewer trainable parameters.

ai.tldr: 提出Permit框架，通过干预大语言模型的隐藏状态实现细粒度的权限感知生成控制，在保持模型冻结的同时大幅降低信息泄露。
ai.motivation: 现有访问控制仅决定信息是否进入模型上下文，但无法约束模型在生成时如何使用这些信息，导致敏感内容可能超出用户授权范围。
ai.method: 通过探索性分析发现权限条件在隐藏状态中产生可分离且集中在少数主导方向上的偏移，据此识别权限敏感子空间，并在该子空间内进行轻量级干预（偏移或门控方式）。
ai.result: 在多个权限设置下优于现有方法，F1分数提升超过18%，可训练参数减少98%以上，信息泄露接近零。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在不修改模型参数的情况下，通过隐藏状态干预实现精确的权限控制，适用于企业敏感文档处理场景。

idea_spark.idea: 利用隐藏状态中权限条件的几何结构，通过轻量级干预实现高效控制，避免重新训练整个模型。
idea_spark.risk: 干预可能影响模型原有生成质量，且权限敏感子空间的识别依赖于探索性分析，可能不适用于所有模型。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从权限条件在隐藏状态中的可分离性出发，启发其他安全控制任务（如隐私保护、内容过滤）采用类似的空间干预策略。

zh.tldr: 提出Permit框架，通过干预大语言模型的隐藏状态实现细粒度的权限感知生成控制，在保持模型冻结的同时大幅降低信息泄露。

idea_spark.idea: 利用隐藏状态中权限条件的几何结构，通过轻量级干预实现高效控制，避免重新训练整个模型。
idea_spark.risk: 干预可能影响模型原有生成质量，且权限敏感子空间的识别依赖于探索性分析，可能不适用于所有模型。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从权限条件在隐藏状态中的可分离性出发，启发其他安全控制任务（如隐私保护、内容过滤）采用类似的空间干预策略。

en.tldr: Proposes Permit, a permission-aware representation intervention framework that enforces fine-grained control over LLM generation by steering hidden states, achieving near-zero information leakage with minimal overhead.
en.motivation: Conventional access control only regulates information access but not how the model uses it during generation, leading to potential unauthorized content leakage.
en.method: Identifies a permission-sensitive subspace from hidden-state shifts across permission conditions, then applies lightweight interventions (offset or gated) within this subspace on a frozen backbone.
en.result: Outperforms state-of-the-art across multiple permission settings, with >18% F1 improvement and >98% fewer trainable parameters, reducing information leakage to near zero.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to achieve precise permission control via hidden-state intervention without modifying model parameters, suitable for enterprise sensitive document handling.

idea_spark.idea: Leverages the geometric structure of permission conditions in hidden states for efficient control via lightweight intervention, avoiding full model retraining.
idea_spark.risk: Interventions may affect generation quality, and subspace identification relies on exploratory analysis that may not generalize to all models.
idea_spark.inspiration: The separability of permission conditions in hidden states inspires similar subspace intervention strategies for other security control tasks like privacy protection or content filtering.

papers.title: On-Policy Distillation with Best-of-N Teacher Rollout Selection
papers.summary: On-policy distillation (OPD), which supervises a student on its own sampled trajectories, has emerged as a data-efficient post-training method for improving reasoning while avoiding the reward dependence of reinforcement learning and the catastrophic forgetting often observed in standard supervised fine-tuning. However, standard OPD typically computes teacher supervision under noisy student-generated contexts and often relies on a single stochastic teacher rollout per prompt. As a result, the supervision signal can be high-variance: the sampled teacher trajectory can be incorrect, uninformative, or poorly matched to the student's current reasoning behavior. To address this limitation, we propose BRTS, a Best-of-N Rollout Teacher Selection framework for on-policy distillation. BRTS augments standard student-context OPD with a teacher-context supervision branch constructed from the curated teacher trajectory. Rather than distilling from the first sampled teacher rollout, BRTS samples a small pool of teacher trajectories and selects the auxiliary trajectory using a simple priority rule: correctness first, student alignment second. When multiple correct teacher trajectories are available, BRTS chooses the one most aligned with the student's current behavior; when unconditioned teacher samples fail on harder prompts, it invokes a ground-truth-conditioned recovery step to elicit a natural derivation. The selected trajectory is then used to provide reliable teacher-context supervision inside the OPD loop, augmented with an auxiliary loss on the teacher trajectory. Experiments on AIME 2024, AIME 2025, and AMC 2023 show that BRTS improves over standard OPD on challenging reasoning benchmarks, with the largest gains on harder datasets. Our code is available at https://github.com/BWGZK-keke/BRTS.

ai.tldr: 提出BRTS框架，通过从多个教师轨迹中挑选最佳辅助轨迹，改进同策略蒸馏中教师监督信号的质量，提升推理能力。
ai.motivation: 标准同策略蒸馏使用单一随机教师轨迹，监督信号方差大，可能包含错误或无信息内容，限制推理提升。
ai.method: BRTS采样少量教师轨迹，按正确性优先、学生对齐次之的规则选择辅助轨迹，并在同策略蒸馏循环中加入教师上下文监督分支和辅助损失。
ai.result: 在AIME 2024、AIME 2025和AMC 2023上，BRTS优于标准同策略蒸馏，尤其在更难的数据集上提升最大。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过精心选择教师轨迹来提升蒸馏效果，避免高方差监督信号。

idea_spark.idea: 探索动态调整采样池大小或引入学生置信度作为选择权重，进一步优化轨迹选择。
idea_spark.risk: 采样多个教师轨迹增加计算开销，可能不适用于资源受限场景。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受强化学习中best-of-N采样启发，将类似思想引入蒸馏流程以提升信号质量。

zh.tldr: 提出BRTS框架，通过从多个教师轨迹中挑选最佳辅助轨迹，改进同策略蒸馏中教师监督信号的质量，提升推理能力。
zh.motivation: 标准同策略蒸馏使用单一随机教师轨迹，监督信号方差大，可能包含错误或无信息内容，限制推理提升。

zh.result: 在AIME 2024、AIME 2025和AMC 2023上，BRTS优于标准同策略蒸馏，尤其在更难的数据集上提升最大。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过精心选择教师轨迹来提升蒸馏效果，避免高方差监督信号。

idea_spark.idea: 探索动态调整采样池大小或引入学生置信度作为选择权重，进一步优化轨迹选择。
idea_spark.risk: 采样多个教师轨迹增加计算开销，可能不适用于资源受限场景。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受强化学习中best-of-N采样启发，将类似思想引入蒸馏流程以提升信号质量。

en.tldr: Proposes BRTS, a Best-of-N Rollout Teacher Selection framework that improves on-policy distillation by selecting the best auxiliary teacher trajectory, enhancing reasoning performance.
en.motivation: Standard on-policy distillation uses a single stochastic teacher rollout, leading to high-variance supervision signals that can be incorrect or uninformative, limiting reasoning gains.
en.method: BRTS samples a small pool of teacher trajectories and selects the auxiliary trajectory using a priority rule: correctness first, student alignment second. It adds a teacher-context supervision branch and an auxiliary loss within the OPD loop.
en.result: BRTS outperforms standard OPD on AIME 2024, AIME 2025, and AMC 2023, with the largest improvements on harder datasets.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how careful teacher trajectory selection can improve distillation quality and reduce variance in supervision signals.

idea_spark.idea: Explore dynamically adjusting the pool size or incorporating student confidence as a selection weight to further optimize trajectory choice.
idea_spark.risk: Sampling multiple teacher trajectories increases computational cost, which may be prohibitive in resource-limited settings.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by best-of-N sampling in reinforcement learning, applying a similar idea to distillation to improve signal quality.

papers.title: S2FT: Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning in Sparse Spectrum Domain
papers.summary: Parameter Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) is a key technique for adapting a large pretrained model to downstream tasks by fine-tuning only a small number of parameters. Recent methods based on Fourier transforms have further reduced the fine-tuned parameters scale by only fine-tuning a few spectral coefficients. Its basic assumption is that the weight change δW is a spatial-domain matrix with a sparse spectrum. However, in this paper, we observe that the spectrum of weight change is not sparse, but instead distributed like power-uniform. This fact implies that fine-tuning only a few spectral coefficients is insufficient to accurately model the weight change with uniform spectrum. To address this issue, we propose to seek an invertible transformation that can transform a latent spatial-domain matrix with sparse spectrum to the weight change, and then perform PEFT on such sparse spectrum domain with few spectral coefficients, called S2FT. To seek such transformation, we first pre-estimate a coarse weight change as a prior. Then, inspired by that sparse spectrum often correspond to locally smooth spatial structures, we regard this transformation as a row and column rearrangement operation on the pre-estimated weight change that smooth spatial structures while keep the structure information of neurons. Finally, we propose to solve the rearrangement search problem in a simple nearest neighbor search manner, thereby obtaining the invertible transformation. Extensive results show our S2FT achieves superior performance by only using 0.08% training parameters.

ai.tldr: 提出S2FT方法，通过可逆变换将稀疏谱域映射到权重变化，仅用0.08%参数微调即达优异性能。
ai.motivation: 现有傅里叶PEFT假设权重变化谱稀疏，但实际呈均匀分布，导致仅微调少数谱系数建模不准确。
ai.method: 先预估计粗权重变化作为先验，再通过行列重排操作（最近邻搜索）得到可逆变换，在稀疏谱域微调少量系数。
ai.result: 仅用0.08%训练参数即取得优于现有方法的性能。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种新的参数高效微调思路，适用于大模型下游任务适配。

idea_spark.idea: 利用预估计权重变化的结构先验，通过重排操作获得稀疏谱表示。
idea_spark.risk: 重排搜索的计算开销可能较大，且对先验估计质量敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将权重变化视为可重排的平滑结构，启发其他基于结构先验的微调方法。

zh.tldr: 提出S2FT方法，通过可逆变换将稀疏谱域映射到权重变化，仅用0.08%参数微调即达优异性能。
zh.motivation: 现有傅里叶PEFT假设权重变化谱稀疏，但实际呈均匀分布，导致仅微调少数谱系数建模不准确。
zh.method: 先预估计粗权重变化作为先验，再通过行列重排操作（最近邻搜索）得到可逆变换，在稀疏谱域微调少量系数。
zh.result: 仅用0.08%训练参数即取得优于现有方法的性能。
zh.help_to_user: 提供一种新的参数高效微调思路，适用于大模型下游任务适配。

idea_spark.idea: 利用预估计权重变化的结构先验，通过重排操作获得稀疏谱表示。
idea_spark.risk: 重排搜索的计算开销可能较大，且对先验估计质量敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将权重变化视为可重排的平滑结构，启发其他基于结构先验的微调方法。

en.tldr: Proposes S2FT, which uses an invertible transformation to map a sparse spectrum domain to weight changes, achieving superior performance with only 0.08% training parameters.
en.motivation: Existing Fourier-based PEFT assumes sparse spectrum of weight changes, but actual spectrum is uniform, making fine-tuning few spectral coefficients insufficient.
en.method: Pre-estimates a coarse weight change as prior, then applies row/column rearrangement via nearest neighbor search to obtain an invertible transformation, fine-tuning few coefficients in the sparse spectrum domain.
en.result: Achieves superior performance using only 0.08% training parameters.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new parameter-efficient fine-tuning approach suitable for adapting large models to downstream tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage structural prior from pre-estimated weight changes via rearrangement to obtain sparse spectrum representation.
idea_spark.risk: Rearrangement search may incur high computational cost and is sensitive to prior estimation quality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Treating weight changes as rearrangeable smooth structures inspires other structure-prior-based fine-tuning methods.

papers.title: UIESNN: A Scale-Aware Spiking Network for Underwater Image Enhancement
papers.summary: Underwater image enhancement (UIE) is a practically important yet underexplored application of spiking neural networks (SNNs), where the dominant degradations are large-scale and low-frequency, such as wavelength-dependent colour casts and scattering-induced veiling. Existing SNN restoration designs rely on locally bounded spiking perception, which can limit global correction and lead to saturated or inconsistent representations. To address these challenges, we propose a scale-aware SNN framework for UIE named UIESNN. At its core is a Multi-scale Pooling LIF Block (MPLB) that injects hierarchical multi-scale pooling responses into membrane dynamics, thereby enlarging the effective receptive field while preserving fine-grained details and inducing heterogeneous scale-dependent activations. Building on MPLB, we design a spiking residual architecture that integrates frequency decomposition and attention-based refinement in a fully spike-driven pipeline. Extensive experiments on the EUVP and LSUI benchmarks demonstrate that UIESNN achieves state-of-the-art performance among SNN-based methods, delivering improved colour fidelity and spatial coherence with competitive energy cost.

ai.tldr: 提出一种尺度感知的脉冲神经网络UIESNN，用于水下图像增强，通过多尺度池化LIF模块扩大感受野并保持细节，在EUVP和LSUI基准上达到SNN方法最优性能。
ai.motivation: 现有SNN修复方法依赖局部有界脉冲感知，难以进行全局校正，导致水下图像颜色失真和表示不一致。
ai.method: 设计多尺度池化LIF模块（MPLB），将层次化多尺度池化响应注入膜动力学，扩大有效感受野；基于MPLB构建脉冲残差架构，集成频率分解和注意力细化，实现全脉冲驱动流水线。
ai.result: 在EUVP和LSUI基准上，UIESNN在SNN方法中达到最优，颜色保真度和空间一致性提升，且能效有竞争力。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种高效、低能耗的水下图像增强方案，适用于资源受限的水下机器人或实时处理场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将脉冲神经网络与多尺度池化结合，利用膜动力学实现全局-局部特征融合，避免传统SNN感受野受限问题。
idea_spark.risk: 多尺度池化可能引入额外计算开销，需验证在极端资源约束下的实时性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受生物视觉系统中尺度选择性启发，将池化响应直接调制神经元膜电位，实现尺度感知脉冲编码。

en.tldr: Proposes UIESNN, a scale-aware spiking neural network for underwater image enhancement, using a Multi-scale Pooling LIF Block to enlarge receptive fields while preserving details, achieving state-of-the-art SNN performance on EUVP and LSUI benchmarks.
en.motivation: Existing SNN restoration methods rely on locally bounded spiking perception, limiting global correction and causing color casts and inconsistent representations in underwater images.
en.method: Designs a Multi-scale Pooling LIF Block (MPLB) that injects hierarchical multi-scale pooling responses into membrane dynamics to enlarge effective receptive fields; builds a spiking residual architecture with frequency decomposition and attention-based refinement in a fully spike-driven pipeline.
en.result: UIESNN achieves state-of-the-art performance among SNN methods on EUVP and LSUI benchmarks, with improved color fidelity and spatial coherence at competitive energy cost.
en.help_to_user: Provides an energy-efficient underwater image enhancement solution suitable for resource-constrained underwater robots or real-time processing.

idea_spark.idea: Combines spiking neural networks with multi-scale pooling, using membrane dynamics to fuse global and local features, overcoming the limited receptive field of traditional SNNs.
idea_spark.risk: Multi-scale pooling may introduce additional computational overhead; real-time performance under extreme resource constraints needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by scale selectivity in biological visual systems, directly modulating neuron membrane potential with pooling responses to achieve scale-aware spiking encoding.

papers.title: VeloGauss: Learning Physically Consistent Gaussian Velocity Fields from Videos
papers.summary: In this paper, we aim to jointly model the geometry, appearance, and physical information of 3D scenes solely from dynamic multi-view videos, without relying on any physical priors. Existing works typically employ physical losses merely as soft constraints or integrate physical simulations into neural networks; however, these approaches often fail to effectively learn complex motion physics. Although modeling velocity fields holds the potential to capture authentic physical information, due to the lack of appropriate physical constraints, current methods are unable to correctly learn the interaction mechanisms between rigid and non-rigid particles. To address this, we propose VeloGauss, designed to learn the physical properties of complex dynamic 3D scenes without physical priors. Our method learns the velocity field for each Gaussian particle by introducing a Physics Code and a Particle Dynamics System, and ultimately incorporates Global Physical Constraints to ensure the physical consistency of the scene. Extensive experiments on four public datasets demonstrate that our method outperforms achieves state-of-the-art performance in both Novel View Interpolation and Future Frame Extrapolation tasks.

ai.tldr: 提出VeloGauss，从多视角视频中联合学习3D场景的几何、外观和物理信息，无需物理先验，通过物理编码和粒子动力学系统学习每个高斯粒子的速度场，并施加全局物理约束，在新视角插值和未来帧外推任务上达到最优。
ai.motivation: 现有方法仅将物理损失作为软约束或集成物理模拟到神经网络，难以有效学习复杂运动物理，且无法正确学习刚性与非刚性粒子间的交互机制。
ai.method: 为每个高斯粒子引入物理编码和粒子动力学系统学习速度场，最终施加全局物理约束保证场景物理一致性。
ai.result: 在四个公开数据集上，新视角插值和未来帧外推任务均取得最优性能。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需物理先验即可从视频中学习场景物理一致性的方法，可用于动态场景重建和预测。

idea_spark.idea: 将物理约束以可学习编码形式嵌入高斯粒子，结合全局约束，实现无先验的物理一致性学习。
idea_spark.risk: 对复杂场景中粒子间交互的建模可能仍不够精细，全局约束可能过度简化局部物理细节。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从物理模拟中借鉴粒子系统思想，但用可学习编码替代显式物理方程，启发无先验物理学习的新范式。

idea_spark.risk: 对复杂场景中粒子间交互的建模可能仍不够精细，全局约束可能过度简化局部物理细节。

en.tldr: Proposes VeloGauss to jointly learn geometry, appearance, and physical information of 3D scenes from multi-view videos without physical priors, using a Physics Code and Particle Dynamics System to learn velocity fields for each Gaussian particle, with Global Physical Constraints achieving state-of-the-art on novel view interpolation and future frame extrapolation.
en.motivation: Existing methods use physical losses only as soft constraints or integrate physical simulations into networks, failing to effectively learn complex motion physics and interaction mechanisms between rigid and non-rigid particles.
en.method: Introduces a Physics Code and Particle Dynamics System for each Gaussian particle to learn velocity fields, and applies Global Physical Constraints to ensure physical consistency.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art performance on both novel view interpolation and future frame extrapolation tasks across four public datasets.
en.help_to_user: Provides a method to learn physically consistent 3D scenes from videos without physical priors, useful for dynamic scene reconstruction and prediction.

idea_spark.idea: Embed physical constraints as learnable codes into Gaussian particles, combined with global constraints, to achieve prior-free physically consistent learning.
idea_spark.risk: Modeling of particle interactions may still be coarse for complex scenes; global constraints might oversimplify local physical details.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrows particle system ideas from physics simulation but replaces explicit equations with learnable codes, inspiring a new paradigm for prior-free physical learning.

papers.title: NICE FACT: Diagnosing and Calibrating VLMs in Quantitative Reasoning for Kinematic Physics
papers.summary: The ability to derive precise spatial and physical insights is a cornerstone of vision-language models (VLMs), yet their poor performances in related spatial intelligence tasks such as physical reasoning remain a fundamental barrier. The community critically lacks a scientific analysis revealing whether VLMs faithfully reach answers or plausibly make guesses. This work aims to provide a fundamental understanding of how VLMs perceive the physical world, and utilize physical laws, while assessing the reliability of model confidence. We propose NICE and FACT, a dual-diagnostic paradigm that explicitly decomposes quantitative reasoning for kinematic physics: FACT diagnoses visual fidelity, physical law comprehension, and temporal grounding. NICE studies our novel neighborhood-informed calibration method and novel metrics to evaluate and calibrate confidence reliability. Evaluated across 6 latest state-of-the-art VLMs, we uncover that models fail to identify visual preconditions or utilize necessary physical laws to reach answers. This work highlights and establishes a standardized diagnostic paradigm to guide the development of faithful, physically-grounded VLMs.

ai.tldr: 提出NICE和FACT双诊断范式，系统评估和校准视觉语言模型在运动学物理定量推理中的视觉保真度、物理定律理解和时间定位能力，发现现有模型在视觉前提识别和物理定律应用上存在根本缺陷。
ai.motivation: 视觉语言模型在物理推理等空间智能任务中表现不佳，但缺乏对其推理过程是否忠实或仅是猜测的科学分析。
ai.method: 设计FACT诊断视觉保真度、物理定律理解和时间定位；NICE提出邻域感知校准方法和新指标评估置信度可靠性。
ai.result: 在6个最新视觉语言模型上发现模型无法识别视觉前提或利用必要物理定律得出答案。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究者诊断和校准视觉语言模型在物理定量推理中的可靠性，指导开发更忠实、基于物理的模型。

idea_spark.idea: 结合物理模拟器生成可控的视觉场景，系统测试模型对物理定律的依赖程度。
idea_spark.risk: 诊断结果可能过度依赖特定物理场景，泛化性需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用邻域感知校准方法可推广至其他需要置信度可靠性的多模态任务。

idea_spark.risk: 诊断结果可能过度依赖特定物理场景，泛化性需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用邻域感知校准方法可推广至其他需要置信度可靠性的多模态任务。

en.tldr: Proposes NICE and FACT, a dual-diagnostic paradigm to systematically evaluate and calibrate VLMs in quantitative kinematic physics reasoning, revealing fundamental failures in visual precondition identification and physical law application.
en.motivation: VLMs perform poorly in spatial intelligence tasks like physical reasoning, but lack scientific analysis on whether they faithfully reason or merely guess.
en.method: FACT diagnoses visual fidelity, physical law comprehension, and temporal grounding; NICE introduces a neighborhood-informed calibration method and novel metrics for confidence reliability.
en.result: On 6 state-of-the-art VLMs, models fail to identify visual preconditions or utilize necessary physical laws to reach answers.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers diagnose and calibrate VLM reliability in quantitative physical reasoning, guiding development of faithful, physically-grounded models.

idea_spark.idea: Combine with physics simulators to generate controlled visual scenes and systematically test model reliance on physical laws.
idea_spark.risk: Diagnostic results may be overly specific to certain physical scenarios; generalizability needs validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: The neighborhood-informed calibration method can be extended to other multimodal tasks requiring confidence reliability.

papers.title: Light Cone Consistency: Toward a Unified Theory of Consistency in Message-Passing Systems
papers.summary:
Every distributed system -- databases, networks, postal services, CPU caches -- is a message-passing system. Every message-passing system is a growing causal log observed by a set of observers. We present Light Cone Consistency (LCC), a framework that describes every known consistency model as a configuration of three constraints on each observer's visible sub-DAG: causal closure $C(\mathrm{deps})$, fork resolution $O(π)$, and timeliness $R(δ)$, plus an orthogonal return-value function $F$. We map 85 configurations, covering all 50+ named models from Viotti and Vukolic's taxonomy, with caveats for fork-based and probabilistic models.   We show that three impossibility results of distributed computing -- CAP, FLP, and AFC -- each constrain exactly one pair of parameters, and prove they are minimal and independent. Our central result is the observation that these three constraints are fully entangled: violation of any one surface cascades to the other two, because restoring any parameter requires messages -- and those messages are subject to all three constraints. The three parameters and their pairwise impossibility surfaces form a fully connected triangle. Every distributed system must exit the triangle by relaxing at least one parameter. The triangle activates only when the system is in use: $C 
eq \mathrm{none}$, $O 
eq \mathrm{trivial}$, or $R 
eq \mathrm{absent}$ each introduces a constraint that exposes the system to the surfaces. A system that demands nothing -- or writes far slower than its propagation delay -- is trivially linearizable. We identify open problems including a conjectured fourth surface (log locality), undiscovered constraints, and the universality of the safety-liveness fork as the consequence of crossing any boundary.

ai.tldr: 提出光锥一致性（LCC）框架，用三个约束（因果闭包、分叉解决、及时性）统一描述所有已知一致性模型，并揭示CAP、FLP、AFC三个不可能性结果构成一个完全纠缠的三角形。
ai.motivation: 现有分布式一致性模型种类繁多，缺乏统一的理论框架来理解它们之间的关系和基本限制。
ai.method: 将每个观察者可见的子DAG上的三个约束（C、O、R）和一个返回值函数F进行参数化配置，映射了Viotti和Vukolic分类中的50多个命名模型。
ai.result: 三个不可能性结果（CAP、FLP、AFC）各自约束一对参数，且这些参数完全纠缠：违反任何一个都会级联影响其他两个，系统必须放松至少一个参数才能退出三角形。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员和工程师理解一致性模型的设计空间、基本不可能性及其内在联系，指导系统设计中的权衡决策。

idea_spark.idea: 探索第四个表面（日志局部性）以及安全-活性分叉作为跨越任何边界的普遍后果，可能发现新的不可能性结果。
idea_spark.risk: 框架的完备性依赖于对现有模型的映射，可能遗漏尚未发现的约束或模型。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从相对论中的光锥概念获得灵感，将分布式系统的因果结构类比为时空中的光锥，从而统一看似不同的不一致性来源。

zh.method: 将每个观察者可见的子DAG上的三个约束（C、O、R）和一个返回值函数F进行参数化配置，映射了Viotti和Vukolic分类中的50多个命名模型。
zh.result: 三个不可能性结果（CAP、FLP、AFC）各自约束一对参数，且这些参数完全纠缠：违反任何一个都会级联影响其他两个，系统必须放松至少一个参数才能退出三角形。

idea_spark.risk: 框架的完备性依赖于对现有模型的映射，可能遗漏尚未发现的约束或模型。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从相对论中的光锥概念获得灵感，将分布式系统的因果结构类比为时空中的光锥，从而统一看似不同的不一致性来源。

en.tldr: Proposes Light Cone Consistency (LCC), a framework that unifies all known consistency models via three constraints (causal closure, fork resolution, timeliness) and reveals that CAP, FLP, and AFC impossibility results form a fully entangled triangle.
en.motivation: Existing distributed consistency models are diverse and lack a unified theoretical framework to understand their relationships and fundamental limitations.
en.method: Parameterizes each observer's visible sub-DAG with three constraints (C, O, R) and a return-value function F, mapping over 50 named models from Viotti and Vukolic's taxonomy.
en.result: Three impossibility results (CAP, FLP, AFC) each constrain one pair of parameters, and these parameters are fully entangled: violating any one cascades to the others, forcing systems to relax at least one parameter to exit the triangle.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers and engineers understand the design space of consistency models, fundamental impossibilities, and their interconnections, guiding trade-off decisions in system design.

idea_spark.idea: Explore the conjectured fourth surface (log locality) and the safety-liveness fork as a universal consequence of crossing any boundary, potentially discovering new impossibility results.
idea_spark.risk: The framework's completeness depends on mapping existing models; it may miss yet undiscovered constraints or models.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the light cone concept from relativity, analogizing the causal structure of distributed systems to spacetime light cones to unify seemingly different sources of inconsistency.

papers.title: The Carrier Pigeon Internet Protocol: An Algorithmic (and Lighthearted) Perspective
papers.summary: The theoretical model behind the pigeon post as a link layer in a communication network was introduced by Shannon (under the guise of studying One-Time Pads for cryptography). That is, to send a one-hop message to $v$, a node $u$ needs a mail pigeon bred and raised at $v$. When sending a message using a pigeon to $v$, node $u$ loses the pigeon. To send another message to $v$, node $u$ needs another pigeon of $v$. It has been demonstrated that the communication bandwidth achievable with pigeon post can exceed that of networks using other media. This has already motivated the introduction of Internet standards that allow the use of pigeons as Internet link-layer media.   In this paper, we begin to fill in the missing piece: designing algorithms for breeding and scheduling pigeons to meet a given communication demand efficiently, minimizing the number of pigeons required. We consider singlehop, 2-hop, and multihop pigeon use. While the singlehop variant admits a simple characterization, both the 2-hop and the multihop variants are NP-hard. For the latter variants, we present a polynomial-time algorithm based on demand aggregation that achieves a 2-approximation for the number of pigeons used. We believe that this pigeon-based perspective offers both amusing and instructive insights into network design and hopefully, into ornithology.

ai.tldr: 本文从算法角度研究信鸽互联网协议，提出鸽群繁殖与调度算法，在单跳、两跳和多跳场景下最小化所需鸽子数量，并给出两跳和多跳问题的2-近似算法。
ai.motivation: 现有研究已证明信鸽通信的带宽潜力，但缺乏高效的鸽群繁殖与调度算法来满足通信需求并最小化鸽子数量。
ai.method: 针对单跳、两跳和多跳场景分别建模，单跳有简单刻画，两跳和多跳问题被证明是NP难的，并基于需求聚合提出多项式时间2-近似算法。
ai.result: 单跳问题可精确求解；两跳和多跳问题为NP难，所提算法达到2-近似比。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何在非传统通信介质（如信鸽）中设计高效调度算法，并启发网络协议设计的幽默与教学视角。

idea_spark.idea: 利用需求聚合思想设计近似算法，可应用于其他NP难的资源分配问题。
idea_spark.risk: 实际部署中鸽子的生物特性（如飞行速度、耐力）未被建模，算法可能过于理想化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将生物行为与网络协议结合，为低带宽或高延迟环境提供创新通信方案。

zh.tldr: 本文从算法角度研究信鸽互联网协议，提出鸽群繁殖与调度算法，在单跳、两跳和多跳场景下最小化所需鸽子数量，并给出两跳和多跳问题的2-近似算法。
zh.motivation: 现有研究已证明信鸽通信的带宽潜力，但缺乏高效的鸽群繁殖与调度算法来满足通信需求并最小化鸽子数量。
zh.method: 针对单跳、两跳和多跳场景分别建模，单跳有简单刻画，两跳和多跳问题被证明是NP难的，并基于需求聚合提出多项式时间2-近似算法。
zh.result: 单跳问题可精确求解；两跳和多跳问题为NP难，所提算法达到2-近似比。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何在非传统通信介质（如信鸽）中设计高效调度算法，并启发网络协议设计的幽默与教学视角。

idea_spark.idea: 利用需求聚合思想设计近似算法，可应用于其他NP难的资源分配问题。
idea_spark.risk: 实际部署中鸽子的生物特性（如飞行速度、耐力）未被建模，算法可能过于理想化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将生物行为与网络协议结合，为低带宽或高延迟环境提供创新通信方案。

en.tldr: This paper algorithmically studies the Carrier Pigeon Internet Protocol, proposing pigeon breeding and scheduling algorithms to minimize the number of pigeons needed for single-hop, 2-hop, and multi-hop communication, achieving a 2-approximation for the NP-hard variants.
en.motivation: Previous work demonstrated the bandwidth potential of pigeon post but lacked efficient algorithms for breeding and scheduling pigeons to meet communication demands while minimizing pigeon count.
en.method: Models single-hop, 2-hop, and multi-hop scenarios; single-hop admits a simple characterization, while 2-hop and multi-hop are NP-hard; a polynomial-time 2-approximation algorithm based on demand aggregation is presented.
en.result: Single-hop is exactly solvable; 2-hop and multi-hop are NP-hard, with the proposed algorithm achieving a 2-approximation ratio.
en.help_to_user: Provides insights into designing efficient scheduling algorithms for unconventional communication media (e.g., carrier pigeons) and offers an amusing yet instructive perspective on network design.

idea_spark.idea: The demand aggregation approach for approximation algorithms can be applied to other NP-hard resource allocation problems.
idea_spark.risk: Biological characteristics of pigeons (e.g., flight speed, endurance) are not modeled, making the algorithm potentially too idealized for real deployment.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining biological behavior with network protocols inspires innovative communication solutions for low-bandwidth or high-latency environments.

papers.title: A Reconfigurable Multiplier Architecture for Error-Resilient Applications in RISC-V Core
papers.summary: Neural Networks (NNs) have been widely adopted due to their outstanding efficacy and adaptability across computer vision and deep learning applications. The optimization of NNs is necessary to enable their deployment on energy constrained embedded devices, where the limited available energy poses a significant challenge for efficient inference. This paper presents a runtime reconfigurable multiplier architecture integrated into the RISC-V core, targeting energy efficient neural network inference and edge AI applications. The proposed multiplier supports adaptability for exact and approximate computation with multiple configurable accuracy levels via a dedicated mulscr, enabling fine-grained energy accuracy control within a standard processor pipeline. The proposed design achieves 44%-52% and 62%-68% power reduction in exact and approximate modes respectively, while maintaining the computational performance of 1.89 DMIPS/MHz. Evaluations on error-tolerant workloads including 2d convolution and matrix multiplication demonstrate up to 63% reduction in energy consumption, with the proposed design achieving 1.21 pJ/instruction for matrix multiplication, confirming its effectiveness for energy-constrained edge AI deployments.

ai.tldr: 提出一种运行时可重构乘法器架构，集成于RISC-V内核，支持精确与近似计算模式切换，在边缘AI推理中实现44%-68%功耗降低和最高63%能耗节省。
ai.motivation: 神经网络在资源受限的嵌入式设备上部署时，能量效率是关键瓶颈。现有处理器缺乏细粒度能量-精度控制机制。
ai.method: 设计可重构乘法器，通过专用mulscr指令支持多种精度级别，在标准处理器流水线内实现精确/近似计算动态切换。
ai.result: 精确模式功耗降低44%-52%，近似模式降低62%-68%；矩阵乘法能耗降至1.21 pJ/指令，计算性能保持1.89 DMIPS/MHz。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在RISC-V处理器中通过可重构乘法器实现能量高效的神经网络推理，适用于边缘AI设备。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将可重构精度控制扩展到整个数据路径（如内存访问、激活函数），实现端到端能量-精度权衡。
idea_spark.risk: 近似计算可能引入不可预测的精度损失，需针对具体应用验证误差容忍度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受神经网络对误差的鲁棒性启发，将近似计算与硬件可重构性结合，实现动态能量优化。
