en.tldr: A runtime reconfigurable multiplier architecture integrated into a RISC-V core supports exact and approximate computation modes, achieving 44%-68% power reduction and up to 63% energy savings for edge AI inference.
en.motivation: Neural network deployment on energy-constrained embedded devices faces energy efficiency challenges; existing processors lack fine-grained energy-accuracy control.
en.method: A reconfigurable multiplier with multiple configurable accuracy levels is controlled via a dedicated mulscr instruction, enabling dynamic switching between exact and approximate modes within a standard processor pipeline.
en.result: Power reduction of 44%-52% in exact mode and 62%-68% in approximate mode; matrix multiplication energy of 1.21 pJ/instruction while maintaining 1.89 DMIPS/MHz performance.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to achieve energy-efficient neural network inference on RISC-V processors using a reconfigurable multiplier for edge AI devices.

idea_spark.idea: Explore extending reconfigurable precision control to the entire data path (e.g., memory access, activation functions) for end-to-end energy-accuracy trade-offs.
idea_spark.risk: Approximate computing may introduce unpredictable accuracy loss; error tolerance must be validated for specific applications.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by neural networks' robustness to errors, combining approximate computing with hardware reconfigurability enables dynamic energy optimization.

papers.title: Accelerating Compound LLM Training Workloads with Maestro
papers.summary: Compound LLM training workloads-such as knowledge distillation and multimodal LLM (MLLM) training-are gaining prominence. These typically comprise heterogeneous components differing in parameter scale, execution mode (forward-only or full forward-backward), and sequence length. Besides, component activation can be data-dependent: in MLLM training, modality-specific parts activate only when inputs contain corresponding modalities, causing dynamic computational paths and irregular runtime workloads. Conventional frameworks, designed for monolithic models, cannot handle the dual heterogeneity-static (across components) and dynamic (runtime). By enforcing one-size-fits-all training configurations across components and ignoring input-induced variations, they suffer suboptimal throughput and poor GPU utilization. In this paper, we introduce Maestro, a section-centric training framework that addresses both challenges. Maestro first restructures the workload into a coarse-grained section graph. Each section independently configures its parallelism strategy, micro-batch size, and data-parallel degree-enabling fine-grained, component-aware resource allocation to tackle static heterogeneity. To tackle runtime irregularity, Maestro introduces a wavefront scheduling algorithm that dynamically reorders input samples to orchestrate concurrent section execution while preserving cross-section data dependencies. This maximizes inter-section parallelism and minimizes stalls, boosting hardware utilization. Deployed in production for millions of GPU hours, Maestro reduces GPU consumption by ~40% on key workloads-including knowledge distillation and MLLM training-validating its real-world impact.

ai.tldr: 提出Maestro框架，通过分节图和波前调度算法，解决复合LLM训练中的静态异构和动态不规则问题，在百万GPU小时生产中降低约40% GPU消耗。
ai.motivation: 现有框架无法处理复合LLM训练中组件间的静态异构（参数规模、执行模式、序列长度差异）和运行时动态不规则（数据依赖的组件激活），导致吞吐量低、GPU利用率差。
ai.method: 将工作负载重构为粗粒度分节图，每个分节独立配置并行策略、微批大小和数据并行度；引入波前调度算法动态重排输入样本，协调分节并发执行并保持数据依赖。
ai.result: 在知识蒸馏和多模态LLM训练等关键负载上，GPU消耗降低约40%，验证了实际效果。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过细粒度资源分配和动态调度优化复合LLM训练效率。

idea_spark.idea: 将训练任务分解为独立配置的分节，并利用波前调度动态平衡并发与依赖，实现高效资源利用。
idea_spark.risk: 分节划分粒度可能影响调度灵活性，波前调度在极端动态场景下可能引入额外开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从数据流计算和并行数据库的调度策略中获得启发，将训练视为有依赖的粗粒度任务图。

zh.tldr: 提出Maestro框架，通过分节图和波前调度算法，解决复合LLM训练中的静态异构和动态不规则问题，在百万GPU小时生产中降低约40% GPU消耗。

zh.result: 在知识蒸馏和多模态LLM训练等关键负载上，GPU消耗降低约40%，验证了实际效果。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过细粒度资源分配和动态调度优化复合LLM训练效率。

idea_spark.idea: 将训练任务分解为独立配置的分节，并利用波前调度动态平衡并发与依赖，实现高效资源利用。
idea_spark.risk: 分节划分粒度可能影响调度灵活性，波前调度在极端动态场景下可能引入额外开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从数据流计算和并行数据库的调度策略中获得启发，将训练视为有依赖的粗粒度任务图。

en.tldr: Maestro uses a section-centric framework and wavefront scheduling to handle static and dynamic heterogeneity in compound LLM training, reducing GPU consumption by ~40% in production.
en.motivation: Existing frameworks fail to handle static heterogeneity (parameter scale, execution mode, sequence length) and dynamic irregularity (data-dependent activation) in compound LLM training, causing low throughput and poor GPU utilization.
en.method: Restructures workload into a coarse-grained section graph with independent parallelism, micro-batch size, and data-parallel degree per section; introduces wavefront scheduling to dynamically reorder input samples for concurrent execution while preserving data dependencies.
en.result: Achieves ~40% GPU reduction on key workloads like knowledge distillation and multimodal LLM training in production.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how fine-grained resource allocation and dynamic scheduling can optimize compound LLM training efficiency.

idea_spark.idea: Decompose training into independently configurable sections and use wavefront scheduling to balance concurrency and dependencies for efficient resource utilization.
idea_spark.risk: Section granularity may affect scheduling flexibility; wavefront scheduling could introduce overhead in extremely dynamic scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by dataflow computing and parallel database scheduling, treating training as a coarse-grained task graph with dependencies.

papers.title: PaperFit: Vision-in-the-Loop Typesetting Optimization for Scientific Documents
papers.summary: A LaTeX manuscript that compiles without error is not necessarily publication-ready. The resulting PDFs frequently suffer from misplaced floats, overflowing equations, inconsistent table scaling, widow and orphan lines, and poor page balance, forcing authors into repetitive compile-inspect-edit cycles. Rule-based tools are blind to rendered visuals, operating only on source code and log files. Text-only LLMs perform open-loop text editing, unable to predict or verify the two-dimensional layout consequences of their changes. Reliable typesetting optimization therefore requires a visual closed loop with verification after every edit. We formalize this problem as Visual Typesetting Optimization (VTO), the task of transforming a compilable LaTeX paper into a visually polished, page-budget-compliant PDF through iterative visual verification and source-level revision, and introduce a five-category taxonomy of typesetting defects to guide diagnosis. We present PaperFit, a vision-in-the-loop agent that iteratively renders pages, diagnoses defects, and applies constrained repairs. To benchmark VTO, we construct PaperFit-Bench with 200 papers across 10 venue templates and 13 defect types at different difficulty. Extensive experiments show that PaperFit outperforms all baselines by a large margin, establishing that bridging the gap from compilable source to publication-ready PDF requires vision-in-the-loop optimization and that VTO constitutes a critical missing stage in the document automation pipeline.

ai.tldr: 提出视觉排版优化问题，并设计PaperFit智能体，通过视觉闭环迭代修复LaTeX排版缺陷，显著提升PDF质量。
ai.motivation: LaTeX编译无错不代表出版就绪，常见排版缺陷（浮动体错位、公式溢出、孤行等）迫使作者反复手动调整，现有规则工具和纯文本LLM无法感知二维布局后果。
ai.method: 形式化定义视觉排版优化（VTO），构建五类缺陷分类法；PaperFit智能体迭代渲染页面、诊断缺陷并执行受限修复。
ai.result: 在包含200篇论文、10种模板、13类缺陷的PaperFit-Bench上，PaperFit大幅超越所有基线。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助科研作者自动优化LaTeX文档排版，减少手动调校工作量，提升投稿效率。

idea_spark.idea: 将排版缺陷诊断与修复建模为视觉-语言联合任务，利用渲染反馈指导源级编辑。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量渲染和缺陷标注数据，对罕见或复杂缺陷可能泛化不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴计算机视觉中的闭环控制思想，将排版优化从开环文本编辑升级为视觉验证驱动的迭代过程。

idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量渲染和缺陷标注数据，对罕见或复杂缺陷可能泛化不足。

en.tldr: Proposes Visual Typesetting Optimization (VTO) and PaperFit, a vision-in-the-loop agent that iteratively renders pages, diagnoses defects, and applies constrained repairs, significantly improving PDF quality.
en.motivation: A compilable LaTeX manuscript is not necessarily publication-ready; common defects (misplaced floats, overflowing equations, widows/orphans) force repetitive manual cycles, while rule-based tools and text-only LLMs cannot perceive 2D layout consequences.
en.method: Formalizes VTO with a five-category defect taxonomy; PaperFit iteratively renders pages, diagnoses defects, and applies constrained source-level repairs via visual verification.
en.result: On PaperFit-Bench (200 papers, 10 venue templates, 13 defect types), PaperFit outperforms all baselines by a large margin.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers automatically optimize LaTeX document layout, reducing manual tuning and improving submission efficiency.

idea_spark.idea: Model typesetting defect diagnosis and repair as a vision-language joint task, using rendered feedback to guide source-level edits.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on high-quality rendering and defect annotation data; may generalize poorly to rare or complex defects.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrows closed-loop control from computer vision, upgrading typesetting optimization from open-loop text editing to vision-verification-driven iteration.

papers.title: Debugging the Debuggers: Failure-Anchored Structured Recovery for Software Engineering Agents
papers.summary: Software engineering agents are increasingly deployed in evaluable engineering environments, yet post-failure recovery remains costly, manual, and ad hoc. Existing systems expose traces or generate follow-up feedback, but they do not convert heterogeneous runtime evidence into grounded, bounded recovery guidance for a subsequent attempt. We present PROBE, a failure-anchored framework for structured recovery in software engineering agents. PROBE organizes failed-run telemetry into structured evidence, structured diagnosis, and bounded recovery guidance through a Telemetry Layer, a Diagnosis Layer, and a Guidance Gate. The Telemetry Layer preserves fine-grained runtime signals, the Diagnosis Layer fuses cross-signal evidence into grounded diagnoses, and the Guidance Gate produces diagnosis-derived guidance only when it is evidence-grounded, actionable, and within the scope of agent-side behavior.   We evaluate PROBE across three settings: repository-level software repair, enterprise workflow recovery, and AIOps service mitigation. On 257 initially unresolved cases, PROBE achieves 65.37% Top-1 diagnosis accuracy and a 21.79% recovery rate, outperforming the strongest non-PROBE baseline by 43.58 and 12.45 percentage points. The results reveal a diagnosis-recovery gap: accurate diagnosis is necessary but insufficient unless translated into bounded guidance that a subsequent attempt can execute and verify. Beyond controlled evaluation, a Microsoft IcM prototype shows that PROBE can attach as a non-intrusive side channel to existing service-diagnosis workflows without changing the agent policy, toolset, or execution budget. These results suggest that telemetry-grounded, failure-anchored recovery can improve post-failure recoverability under realistic engineering constraints.

ai.tldr: 提出PROBE框架，通过故障锚定的结构化恢复，将运行时遥测转化为有界恢复指导，在软件工程代理中提升恢复率。
ai.motivation: 现有软件工程代理在故障后恢复成本高、手动且临时，缺乏将异构运行时证据转化为有界恢复指导的方法。
ai.method: PROBE包含遥测层、诊断层和指导门：遥测层保留细粒度信号，诊断层融合跨信号证据，指导门仅在证据充分、可操作且限于代理行为时输出指导。
ai.result: 在257个未解决案例上，Top-1诊断准确率65.37%，恢复率21.79%，分别比最强基线高43.58和12.45个百分点；Microsoft IcM原型验证了非侵入式集成。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何系统化地利用故障运行时数据生成可执行的恢复指导，提升软件工程代理的鲁棒性。

idea_spark.idea: 将遥测层与诊断层分离，并引入指导门确保输出可执行，避免无效指导。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量遥测数据，若信号噪声大或缺失，诊断和指导可能不准确。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从软件工程代理的故障恢复问题出发，启发设计更通用的故障诊断与恢复架构。

zh.tldr: 提出PROBE框架，通过故障锚定的结构化恢复，将运行时遥测转化为有界恢复指导，在软件工程代理中提升恢复率。

idea_spark.idea: 将遥测层与诊断层分离，并引入指导门确保输出可执行，避免无效指导。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量遥测数据，若信号噪声大或缺失，诊断和指导可能不准确。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从软件工程代理的故障恢复问题出发，启发设计更通用的故障诊断与恢复架构。

en.tldr: Proposes PROBE, a failure-anchored structured recovery framework that converts runtime telemetry into bounded recovery guidance for software engineering agents, improving recovery rates.
en.motivation: Existing post-failure recovery for software engineering agents is costly, manual, and ad hoc, lacking methods to convert heterogeneous runtime evidence into grounded, bounded guidance.
en.method: PROBE comprises a Telemetry Layer (preserves fine-grained signals), a Diagnosis Layer (fuses cross-signal evidence), and a Guidance Gate (outputs guidance only when evidence-grounded, actionable, and within agent scope).
en.result: On 257 unresolved cases, achieves 65.37% Top-1 diagnosis accuracy and 21.79% recovery rate, outperforming the strongest baseline by 43.58 and 12.45 percentage points; Microsoft IcM prototype validates non-intrusive integration.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how to systematically leverage failure runtime data to generate executable recovery guidance, enhancing robustness of software engineering agents.

idea_spark.idea: Separate telemetry and diagnosis layers, and introduce a guidance gate to ensure outputs are actionable, avoiding ineffective guidance.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on high-quality telemetry data; noisy or missing signals may lead to inaccurate diagnosis and guidance.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the failure recovery problem in software engineering agents, it suggests designing more general fault diagnosis and recovery architectures.

papers.title: Oracle Poisoning: Corrupting Knowledge Graphs to Weaponise AI Agent Reasoning
papers.summary: We define Oracle Poisoning, an attack class in which an adversary corrupts a structured knowledge graph that AI agents query at runtime via tool-use protocols, causing incorrect conclusions through correct reasoning. Unlike prompt injection, Oracle Poisoning manipulates the data agents reason over, not their instructions. We demonstrate six attack scenarios against a production 42-million-node code knowledge graph, providing the first empirical demonstration of knowledge graph poisoning against a production-scale agentic system, distinct from CTI embedding poisoning. Primary evaluation uses real SDK tool-use across nine models from three providers (N=30 per model), where models autonomously invoke a graph query tool and reason from results. The result is unambiguous: every tested model trusts poisoned data at 100% at moderate attacker sophistication(L2), with 269 valid trials (of 270) accepting fabricated security claims under directed queries. Under open-ended prompts, trust drops to 3-55%, confirming prompt framing as a confound; we report both conditions. An attacker sophistication gradient reveals discrete break points, a minimum skill at which trust flips from 0% to 100%, reframing the attack as a question not of whether but of how much. A controlled delivery-mode comparison shows that inline evaluation produces false negatives: GPT-5.1 shows 0% trust inline but 100% under both simulated and real agentic tool-use, demonstrating that delivery mode is a first-order confound. We evaluate five defences; read-only access control eliminates the direct mutation vector, while the remaining four are partial and model-dependent. Analysis of four additional platforms suggests the attack may generalise across the knowledge-graph ecosystem.

ai.tldr: 提出Oracle Poisoning攻击，通过污染知识图谱使AI代理在正确推理下得出错误结论，实验证明所有模型在中等攻击复杂度下100%信任被污染数据。
ai.motivation: 现有攻击多关注提示注入，忽略了代理系统在运行时查询结构化知识图谱时可能被数据污染的风险。
ai.method: 定义攻击类别，在4200万节点的生产级代码知识图谱上实施六种攻击场景，通过真实SDK工具调用测试九个模型，并比较内联评估与代理工具使用的差异。
ai.result: 中等攻击复杂度下所有模型100%信任被污染数据；开放提示下信任降至3-55%；内联评估产生假阴性；只读访问控制可消除直接变异向量。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解AI代理系统在知识图谱层面的安全脆弱性，以及如何设计防御措施。

idea_spark.idea: 探索在知识图谱中植入隐蔽的推理陷阱，使代理在特定查询下产生系统性错误。
idea_spark.risk: 攻击者可能利用此方法操纵AI代理在关键决策中的输出，造成严重后果。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发设计更鲁棒的知识图谱验证机制，以及代理推理过程中的数据可信度评估方法。

en.tldr: Proposes Oracle Poisoning, an attack that corrupts knowledge graphs to make AI agents draw incorrect conclusions despite correct reasoning; experiments show 100% trust in poisoned data at moderate attacker sophistication.
en.motivation: Existing attacks focus on prompt injection, overlooking the risk of data poisoning in structured knowledge graphs queried by AI agents at runtime.
en.method: Defines the attack class, implements six scenarios on a production 42-million-node code knowledge graph, tests nine models via real SDK tool-use, and compares inline evaluation with agentic tool-use.
en.result: 100% trust in poisoned data at moderate attacker sophistication; trust drops to 3-55% under open-ended prompts; inline evaluation yields false negatives; read-only access control eliminates the direct mutation vector.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the security vulnerabilities of AI agent systems at the knowledge graph level and how to design defenses.

idea_spark.idea: Explore implanting hidden reasoning traps in knowledge graphs to cause systematic errors in agent queries.
idea_spark.risk: Attackers could manipulate AI agent outputs in critical decisions, leading to severe consequences.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires the design of more robust knowledge graph validation mechanisms and data trustworthiness assessment during agent reasoning.

papers.title: Smart Contract Security Beyond Detection
papers.summary: Smart contract security has progressed from vulnerability detection toward a broader research agenda that includes semantic reasoning, automated repair, adversarial robustness, and real-time exploit detection. This paper develops a capstone-oriented research narrative around four directions: foundation-model-based smart contract semantics and vulnerability reasoning [1], automated smart contract repair with formal guarantees [2], adversarial learning for robust malicious contract and transaction detection [3], and real-time transaction-level exploit detection at blockchain scale [4]. We connect these directions to two recent studies that characterize the current frontier: a diagnostic analysis of where smart contract security analyzers fall short [5] and a scalable real-time system for malicious Ethereum transaction detection [6]. The resulting framework is intended to help students formulate capstone projects that are technically grounded, empirically measurable, and aligned with contemporary smart contract security research.

ai.tldr: 本文提出一个以顶点项目为导向的研究框架，涵盖基于基础模型的语义推理、自动修复、对抗鲁棒性和实时漏洞检测四个方向，旨在帮助学生设计技术扎实的智能合约安全项目。
ai.motivation: 智能合约安全研究已从单纯的漏洞检测扩展到更广泛的议程，需要系统化的框架来指导学生的顶点项目。
ai.method: 通过整合四个研究方向（基础模型语义推理、自动修复、对抗学习、实时交易级漏洞检测）并关联两项前沿研究，构建一个技术扎实、可衡量的研究框架。
ai.result: 形成一个顶点项目框架，使学生能够开展与当代智能合约安全研究一致的项目。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解智能合约安全的前沿研究方向，并为其顶点项目提供结构化的指导。

idea_spark.idea: 可以设计一个结合基础模型和形式化验证的智能合约自动修复系统，并评估其在不同漏洞类型上的效果。
idea_spark.risk: 基础模型可能产生误报或漏报，形式化验证的扩展性有限，实时检测系统可能面临高吞吐量下的性能瓶颈。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受论文中“连接四个方向”的启发，可进一步探索如何将对抗学习与实时检测结合，以增强对未知攻击的防御能力。

en.tldr: This paper develops a capstone-oriented research framework covering foundation-model-based semantic reasoning, automated repair, adversarial robustness, and real-time exploit detection, aiming to help students formulate technically grounded smart contract security projects.
en.motivation: Smart contract security research has expanded beyond vulnerability detection to a broader agenda, requiring a systematic framework to guide student capstone projects.
en.method: By integrating four research directions (foundation-model semantic reasoning, automated repair, adversarial learning, real-time transaction-level exploit detection) and linking two recent studies, a technically grounded and empirically measurable framework is constructed.
en.result: A capstone project framework is formed, enabling students to undertake projects aligned with contemporary smart contract security research.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand frontier research directions in smart contract security and provides structured guidance for their capstone projects.

idea_spark.idea: One could design an automated smart contract repair system combining foundation models and formal verification, and evaluate its effectiveness across different vulnerability types.
idea_spark.risk: Foundation models may produce false positives/negatives, formal verification may have scalability issues, and real-time detection systems may face performance bottlenecks under high throughput.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the paper's 'connecting four directions', one could further explore how to combine adversarial learning with real-time detection to enhance defense against unknown attacks.

papers.title: Intelligent Autonomous Orchestration for Distributed Cloud Resources using Complex-Stability Analysis
papers.summary: In modern distributed cloud environments, efficient resource allocation is required as traditional scaling mechanisms are often subject to cloud thrashing due to network-induced latencies. In this paper, we propose C-SAS (Complex-Stability Aware Scaling), an intelligent autonomous orchestration framework that leverages complex analytic methods to achieve system-wide equilibrium. In contrast to heuristic-based models, C-SAS acts as a stability-aware agent, converting telemetry noise into a deterministic "Safety Envelope" on the $s$-plane using the Argument Principle and Rouché's Theorem. The algorithm smartly suppresses oscillatory scaling operations that would otherwise degrade performance, by computing a real-time Analytic Stability Index (ASI). The experimental results show that C-SAS reduces VM flapping by 94\%, and achieves 96\% resource efficiency, significantly outperforming standard PID and ML-based autonomous agents. Our results suggest that future resilient autonomous cloud infrastructures will require AI-driven orchestrators with built-in formal stability constraints.

ai.tldr: 提出C-SAS框架，利用复分析方法实现分布式云资源的智能自主编排，有效抑制云抖动并提高资源效率。
ai.motivation: 传统伸缩机制因网络延迟导致云抖动，资源分配效率低下。
ai.method: 基于幅角原理和Rouché定理，将遥测噪声转换为s平面上的确定性“安全包络”，并计算实时解析稳定性指数（ASI）。
ai.result: 虚拟机抖动减少94%，资源效率达96%，优于PID和ML方法。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何利用复分析理论解决云资源编排中的稳定性问题。

idea_spark.idea: 将复分析中的零点/极点判据用于实时系统稳定性监控。
idea_spark.risk: 复分析计算开销可能在高频场景下成为瓶颈。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从控制理论中的s平面分析获得启发，将数学定理直接用于工程决策。

en.tldr: Proposes C-SAS, an intelligent autonomous orchestration framework using complex analysis to suppress cloud thrashing and improve resource efficiency in distributed cloud environments.
en.motivation: Traditional scaling mechanisms suffer from cloud thrashing due to network-induced latencies, leading to inefficient resource allocation.
en.method: Leverages the Argument Principle and Rouché's Theorem to convert telemetry noise into a deterministic 'Safety Envelope' on the s-plane, computing a real-time Analytic Stability Index (ASI).
en.result: Reduces VM flapping by 94% and achieves 96% resource efficiency, outperforming PID and ML-based agents.
en.help_to_user: Provides insight into applying complex analysis for stability-aware orchestration in cloud systems.

idea_spark.idea: Use zero/pole criteria from complex analysis for real-time stability monitoring in systems.
idea_spark.risk: Computational overhead of complex analysis may become a bottleneck in high-frequency scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by s-plane analysis in control theory, applying mathematical theorems directly to engineering decisions.

papers.title: Sketch-based Access Control: A Multimodal Interface for Translating User Preferences into Intent-Aligned Policies
papers.summary: Developing simple and expressive access controls -- interfaces to specify policies that define who should have access to resources and under what circumstances -- is a longstanding challenge in usable security. We present Sketch-based Access Control (SBAC), a sketch-based, AI-assisted access control authoring system that combines the expressive power of sketching with the interpretive capabilities of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) to support the interpretation and validation of policy specifications as they are iteratively refined. Through a formative study with 14 participants, we identified three design requirements and developed a human-AI collaborative workflow composed of three stages -- Specify, Analyze, and Test -- enabled by the system's ability to maintain and interpret evolving access control specifications. In a user evaluation with 14 participants grounded in their real-world access control scenarios, we found the system and the workflow helped participants progressively refine initially underspecified preferences into more complete and precise policies -- surfacing gaps they had not anticipated, resolving ambiguities through dialogue, and validating policy behavior through concrete scenarios.

ai.tldr: 提出基于草图的访问控制（SBAC），结合草图与多模态大语言模型，帮助用户将偏好逐步转化为精确策略。
ai.motivation: 现有访问控制界面难以让非专家用户准确表达策略意图，导致策略不完整或歧义。
ai.method: 通过三阶段人机协作流程（指定、分析、测试），利用MLLM解释草图并迭代完善策略。
ai.result: 用户评估表明，SBAC帮助参与者发现未预见的漏洞、消除歧义，并生成更完整精确的策略。
ai.help_to_user: 提供直观的草图交互和AI辅助解释，降低策略制定门槛，提升策略质量。

idea_spark.idea: 将草图解释与策略验证结合，通过具体场景测试自动发现策略漏洞。
idea_spark.risk: MLLM可能误解草图意图，导致策略错误；用户可能过度依赖AI而忽略关键细节。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用多模态输入（草图+对话）逐步细化模糊需求，为可解释AI在安全策略中的应用提供新思路。

en.tldr: Proposes Sketch-based Access Control (SBAC), combining sketching with multimodal large language models to help users iteratively refine preferences into precise policies.
en.motivation: Existing access control interfaces fail to help non-expert users accurately express policy intent, leading to incomplete or ambiguous policies.
en.method: A three-stage human-AI collaborative workflow (Specify, Analyze, Test) where MLLMs interpret sketches and support iterative refinement.
en.result: User evaluation showed SBAC helped participants uncover unanticipated gaps, resolve ambiguities, and produce more complete and precise policies.
en.help_to_user: Provides intuitive sketch interaction and AI-assisted interpretation, lowering the barrier to policy authoring and improving policy quality.

idea_spark.idea: Combine sketch interpretation with policy validation, using concrete scenarios to automatically detect policy gaps.
idea_spark.risk: MLLMs may misinterpret sketch intent, leading to erroneous policies; users may over-rely on AI and overlook critical details.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using multimodal input (sketch + dialogue) to progressively refine vague requirements offers a new approach for explainable AI in security policy.

papers.title: Engineering Economy: A New Paradigm for Escaping the Middle-Income Trap
papers.summary: This paper introduces the concept of Engineering Economy as a new paradigm for understanding and managing macroeconomic policy in middle-income countries seeking to escape the middle-income trap. Drawing on Turkiye's post-2001 economic trajectory and South Korea's successful transition from a low-income to a high-income economy, the study argues that conventional frameworks whether the Washington Consensus's market liberalization prescriptions or the institutionalist critique alone are insufficient. Instead, it proposes treating the economy as a dynamic control system requiring continuous calibration rather than static equilibrium. The paper develops a road-surface metaphor (highway, side-road, off-road) to characterize different global economic regimes and presents eleven interconnected policy pillars spanning venture capital formation, regulatory sandboxes, technology-focused industrial policy, and human capital development. By synthesizing insights from endogenous growth theory (Romer), institutional economics (Acemoglu), the catching-up literature (Lee), cybernetic systems theory (Wiener), and Schumpeterian creative destruction, the framework reconceptualizes macroeconomic instruments through control-engineering analogies: interest rates as energy gradients, fiscal policy as energy flow, exchange rates as balance motors, and regulation as adaptive suspension. The analysis demonstrates that Turkiye's structural challenge is not merely institutional weakness but a systemic absence of R&D demand from its dominant enterprise structures, creating a vicious cycle that conventional reforms cannot break. Seven specific opportunity windows arising from US-China technological rivalry are identified, and a phased implementation roadmap is proposed.

ai.tldr: 提出“工程经济”范式，将经济视为动态控制系统，结合土耳其和韩国案例，为中等收入国家摆脱陷阱提供政策框架。
ai.motivation: 现有华盛顿共识或制度主义框架不足以解释和解决中等收入陷阱问题。
ai.method: 通过道路表面隐喻（高速公路、侧路、越野）描述全球经济体制，并构建十一个相互关联的政策支柱。
ai.result: 识别出土耳其结构性挑战在于企业缺乏研发需求，并指出美中技术竞争带来的七个机会窗口。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解中等收入陷阱的工程控制视角，以及如何设计系统性政策组合。

idea_spark.idea: 可尝试用类似控制论框架分析其他中等收入国家（如巴西、马来西亚）的经济转型路径。
idea_spark.risk: 过度简化经济复杂性，忽略政治和社会因素。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从控制工程与经济增长理论的交叉中获取政策设计灵感。

idea_spark.risk: 过度简化经济复杂性，忽略政治和社会因素。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从控制工程与经济增长理论的交叉中获取政策设计灵感。

en.tldr: Proposes 'Engineering Economy' as a paradigm treating the economy as a dynamic control system, using Turkey and South Korea cases to offer a policy framework for middle-income countries.
en.motivation: Existing Washington Consensus or institutionalist frameworks are insufficient to explain and address the middle-income trap.
en.method: Uses a road-surface metaphor (highway, side-road, off-road) to characterize global economic regimes and builds eleven interconnected policy pillars.
en.result: Identifies Turkey's structural challenge as a lack of R&D demand from dominant enterprises, and points to seven opportunity windows from US-China technological rivalry.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the middle-income trap from a control-engineering perspective and how to design systemic policy packages.

idea_spark.idea: Could try applying a similar cybernetic framework to analyze economic transitions in other middle-income countries like Brazil or Malaysia.
idea_spark.risk: Oversimplifying economic complexity and neglecting political and social factors.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws inspiration from the intersection of control engineering and growth theory for policy design.

papers.title: Amortized Asynchronous Byzantine Reliable Broadcast with Optimal Resilience
papers.summary: Byzantine Reliable Broadcast (BRB) is a fundamental primitive in distributed computing and cryptographic systems. Reducing the communication complexity of BRB protocols remains an important research direction. However, most work focuses on synchronous networks, with limited attention to the more challenging setting of network \textit{asynchrony}.   Achieving sub-quadratic communication for asynchronous BRB typically requires probabilistic approaches that sacrifice optimal $f=\frac{n}{3}$ resilience. In this work, we present a multi-shot BRB algorithm for asynchronous networks that maintains optimal resilience through an underutilized technique: \textit{amortization}. Our protocol structures BRB across multiple rounds, where each round provides incremental additive guarantees. Once these initial rounds complete, each subsequent BRB instance requires only a single additional round.   This amortization strategy achieves asymptotic optimal $O(n|m|)$ message complexity when messages are sufficiently large, with $Ω(n)$ round complexity in the worst case. Under favorable conditions, an optimistic delivery path reduces the round complexity to $Ω(1)$.

ai.tldr: 提出一种在异步网络中保持最优容错性的多轮拜占庭可靠广播协议，通过摊销技术实现渐近最优的消息复杂度。
ai.motivation: 现有异步BRB协议要么牺牲最优容错性，要么通信复杂度高，需要一种同时保持最优容错性和亚二次通信复杂度的方案。
ai.method: 利用摊销技术，将BRB协议结构化为多轮，每轮提供增量保证，后续实例只需额外一轮，从而降低平均通信开销。
ai.result: 在消息足够大时达到渐近最优的O(n|m|)消息复杂度，最坏情况下轮复杂度为Ω(n)，乐观情况下可降至Ω(1)。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何在异步分布式系统中设计高效且容错的广播协议。

idea_spark.idea: 将摊销与乐观执行路径结合，在良好条件下大幅降低延迟。
idea_spark.risk: 摊销依赖多轮交互，在消息较小或网络高度异步时可能收益有限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴了同步网络中摊销技术的成功应用，将其适配到更具挑战的异步环境。

zh.motivation: 现有异步BRB协议要么牺牲最优容错性，要么通信复杂度高，需要一种同时保持最优容错性和亚二次通信复杂度的方案。

zh.result: 在消息足够大时达到渐近最优的O(n|m|)消息复杂度，最坏情况下轮复杂度为Ω(n)，乐观情况下可降至Ω(1)。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何在异步分布式系统中设计高效且容错的广播协议。

idea_spark.risk: 摊销依赖多轮交互，在消息较小或网络高度异步时可能收益有限。

en.tldr: A multi-shot Byzantine Reliable Broadcast algorithm for asynchronous networks that maintains optimal resilience via amortization, achieving asymptotically optimal message complexity.
en.motivation: Existing asynchronous BRB protocols either sacrifice optimal resilience or have high communication complexity; a scheme that maintains both optimal resilience and sub-quadratic communication is needed.
en.method: Structures BRB across multiple rounds using amortization, where each round provides incremental guarantees and subsequent instances require only one extra round, reducing average communication overhead.
en.result: Achieves asymptotically optimal O(n|m|) message complexity for sufficiently large messages, with worst-case round complexity Ω(n) and optimistic delivery reducing it to Ω(1).
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how to design efficient and fault-tolerant broadcast protocols in asynchronous distributed systems.

idea_spark.idea: Combine amortization with an optimistic execution path to significantly reduce latency under favorable conditions.
idea_spark.risk: Amortization relies on multi-round interaction, which may yield limited benefits for small messages or highly asynchronous networks.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by successful applications of amortization in synchronous networks, adapted to the more challenging asynchronous setting.

papers.title: WATSON: Leveraging Data Watchpoints for Shadow Stack Protection on Embedded Systems
papers.summary: Embedded and Internet-of-Things (IoT) devices play a critical role in modern life. Their software and firmware, often developed in memory-unsafe languages like C, are susceptible to memory safety vulnerabilities that can lead to control-flow hijacking attacks. Shadow stack is a defense mechanism against control-flow hijacking that targets return addresses. However, existing shadow stack solutions for embedded systems have the following limitations. First, they lack system-wide protection, particularly for interrupts and exceptions. Second, they introduce high performance overhead. Third, they depend on security extensions like a trusted execution environment, which are not universally available on embedded devices. Finally, they rely on hardware features that have inherent configurable constraints, which pose compatibility challenges when integrating security mechanisms that require similar hardware support.   To overcome these limitations, we present WATSON, an efficient and effective shadow stack solution. It leverages a standard hardware debug unit named data watchpoints for shadow stack protection on embedded systems. To prevent unauthorized access to the shadow stack, WATSON leverages the address-matching features of the debug unit to enforce the write protection of the shadow stack. Additionally, WATSON is compatible with compiler options to enforce forward-edge control-flow integrity.   We implemented a prototype of WATSON on the ARM CortexM architecture, and the concept also applies to other platforms. The introduced overhead is 7.33% and 1.81% on BEEBS and CoreMark-Pro benchmarks, respectively. We also evaluate WATSON on exception handling and two real-world applications, observing negligible performance overhead and a worst-case code size overhead of 2.11%. Furthermore, our security evaluation demonstrates that WATSON effectively prevents attacks.

ai.tldr: 提出WATSON，利用硬件数据监视点实现嵌入式系统影子栈保护，克服现有方案性能高、依赖特定硬件等局限。
ai.motivation: 现有嵌入式影子栈方案缺乏系统级保护（如中断/异常）、性能开销大、依赖可信执行环境等硬件特性，兼容性差。
ai.method: 利用标准调试单元的数据监视点地址匹配功能，对影子栈实施写保护，并与编译器选项结合实现前向边控制流完整性。
ai.result: 在ARM Cortex-M上原型实现，BEEBS和CoreMark-Pro基准测试开销分别为7.33%和1.81%，异常处理和实际应用开销可忽略，代码大小开销最大2.11%，安全评估证明有效防御攻击。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助嵌入式开发者了解一种轻量级、无需特殊硬件的影子栈保护方案，可用于提升IoT设备安全性。

idea_spark.idea: 将调试硬件用于运行时安全监控，避免引入专用安全扩展，降低部署门槛。
idea_spark.risk: 数据监视点数量有限，可能无法保护多个影子栈区域；调试单元在部分低端MCU上可能缺失。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用现有硬件调试功能实现安全机制，减少对专用安全硬件的依赖，是一种低成本安全增强思路。

idea_spark.idea: 将调试硬件用于运行时安全监控，避免引入专用安全扩展，降低部署门槛。
idea_spark.risk: 数据监视点数量有限，可能无法保护多个影子栈区域；调试单元在部分低端MCU上可能缺失。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用现有硬件调试功能实现安全机制，减少对专用安全硬件的依赖，是一种低成本安全增强思路。

en.tldr: Proposes WATSON, a shadow stack protection for embedded systems using hardware data watchpoints, overcoming limitations of existing solutions in performance, system-wide coverage, and hardware dependency.
en.motivation: Existing shadow stack solutions for embedded systems lack system-wide protection (e.g., interrupts/exceptions), incur high performance overhead, rely on security extensions like TEE, and face compatibility issues due to configurable hardware constraints.
en.method: Leverages address-matching features of a standard debug unit (data watchpoints) to enforce write protection on the shadow stack, and integrates with compiler options for forward-edge control-flow integrity.
en.result: Prototype on ARM Cortex-M achieves 7.33% and 1.81% overhead on BEEBS and CoreMark-Pro benchmarks, negligible overhead on exception handling and real-world applications, worst-case code size overhead of 2.11%, and effective attack prevention in security evaluation.
en.help_to_user: Helps embedded developers understand a lightweight, hardware-agnostic shadow stack protection scheme to enhance IoT device security.

idea_spark.idea: Repurpose debug hardware for runtime security monitoring, avoiding dedicated security extensions and lowering deployment barriers.
idea_spark.risk: Limited number of data watchpoints may restrict protection of multiple shadow stack regions; debug unit may be absent in low-end MCUs.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging existing hardware debug features for security mechanisms reduces reliance on specialized security hardware, offering a low-cost security enhancement approach.

papers.title: SL5 Standard for AI Security
papers.summary: Security Level 5 (SL5) is a security posture for AI systems that could plausibly thwart top-priority operations by the world's most cyber-capable institutions: those with extensive resources, state-level infrastructure, and expertise years ahead of the public state of the art. The SL5 terminology originates from the RAND Corporation's 2024 report "Securing AI Model Weights". Frontier AI development requires use-case-specific, productivity-optimised and updateable AI datacenter security standards. This first revision of the SL5 standard focuses on requirements with long lead times: interventions that must be planned years in advance, such as facility construction, hardware procurement, and organizational capability development. We prioritize these requirements because preserving optionality for SL5 by 2028/2029 requires starting now. These capabilities cannot be retrofitted on short notice when the need becomes urgent. Some requirements represent significant departures from current day standard practice. We believe bold measures are necessary for this level of security and see clear opportunities to apply optimization pressure to existing and novel solutions to customize them for the AI industry and address the practical operational requirements as much as possible. Our organization exists to begin paving this path. Some requirements approximate government security capabilities where private-sector approaches may be insufficient. We identify these gaps and note where government involvement may ultimately be necessary.

ai.tldr: 本文定义了AI系统的安全等级5（SL5），针对能抵御顶级网络机构攻击的防护姿态，重点提出需长期规划的基础设施、硬件采购和组织能力要求。
ai.motivation: 前沿AI系统面临国家级对手的威胁，现有安全标准不足，需提前数年规划关键干预措施。
ai.method: 基于RAND报告，识别并优先制定需长期准备的安全要求，包括设施建设、硬件采购和组织能力发展。
ai.result: 提出SL5标准初版，强调不可事后补救的长期措施，并指出需政府介入的领域。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解前沿AI系统的高安全等级要求及其实施路径。

idea_spark.idea: 开发AI安全成熟度模型，结合政府与私营部门协作框架。
idea_spark.risk: 标准可能过于严格，增加开发成本；政府介入可能引发隐私和监管问题。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴核设施安全等级思路，为AI数据中心定制防护标准。

en.tldr: This paper defines Security Level 5 (SL5) for AI systems, a posture to thwart top-tier cyber adversaries, focusing on long-lead-time requirements such as facility construction, hardware procurement, and organizational capability development.
en.motivation: Frontier AI systems face threats from nation-state actors; current security standards are insufficient, requiring years of advance planning for critical interventions.
en.method: Based on the RAND report, identify and prioritize long-lead-time security requirements, including infrastructure, hardware, and organizational capabilities.
en.result: Proposes the first revision of the SL5 standard, emphasizing non-retrofittable measures and identifying gaps where government involvement is necessary.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand high-security requirements for frontier AI systems and their implementation pathways.

idea_spark.idea: Develop an AI security maturity model with a government-private sector collaboration framework.
idea_spark.risk: Standards may be overly strict, increasing costs; government involvement may raise privacy and regulatory concerns.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draw inspiration from nuclear facility security levels to tailor protection standards for AI data centers.

papers.title: Edge-Cloud Collaborative Pothole Detection via Onboard Event Screening and Federated Temporal Segmentation
papers.summary: Road potholes threaten driving safety and increase infrastructure maintenance costs, while large-scale and timely pothole detection remains challenging in urban road networks. Vehicle-mounted vibration sensing offers a low-cost and scalable solution, however, continuous transmission of raw acceleration streams causes high communication overhead. Also, vibration patterns induced by potholes are often confused with those caused by manholes, speed bumps, and other local road structures. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an edge-cloud collaborative pothole detection framework based on onboard vibration event screening and federated temporal segmentation. At the vehicle side, a Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM)-based module adaptively models background vibration and screens candidate abnormal events from continuous acceleration streams. The onboard module acts as a lightweight high-recall filter and uploads only compact candidate event segments with their contextual information. At the server side, pothole detection is formulated as a point-wise temporal segmentation task. A 1D Attention U-Net is developed to distinguish potholes from vibration-similar road events by capturing multi-scale temporal features and preserving event boundary information. Furthermore, the model is trained under a federated learning framework to exploit distributed multi-vehicle data while accommodating non-IID vehicle data distributions. Experiments on multi-vehicle vibration sensing data show that the proposed framework reduces unnecessary data transmission from smooth road segments and improves fine-grained pothole detection under both centralized and federated settings.

ai.tldr: 提出一种边云协同的坑洼检测框架，通过车载振动事件筛选和联邦时序分割，降低通信开销并提升检测精度。
ai.motivation: 道路坑洼威胁行车安全，现有振动传感方案存在高通信开销和坑洼与井盖、减速带等振动模式混淆的问题。
ai.method: 车载端用高斯混合模型自适应筛选异常振动事件，仅上传候选片段；服务端用1D Attention U-Net进行点式时序分割，并在联邦学习框架下利用多车非独立同分布数据训练。
ai.result: 实验表明该框架减少了平滑路段的不必要数据传输，并在集中和联邦设置下提升了细粒度坑洼检测性能。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户了解如何利用边缘-云端协同和联邦学习实现低开销、高精度的道路坑洼实时检测。

idea_spark.idea: 将GMM背景建模与注意力U-Net结合，实现轻量级边缘筛选与云端精细分割的协同。
idea_spark.risk: 联邦学习下非独立同分布数据可能导致模型收敛慢或性能下降，需设计更鲁棒的聚合策略。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将边缘筛选与联邦学习结合，用于其他传感器数据（如声音、图像）的异常检测与分割。

en.tldr: Proposes an edge-cloud collaborative pothole detection framework using onboard vibration event screening and federated temporal segmentation to reduce communication overhead and improve detection accuracy.
en.motivation: Road potholes threaten driving safety; existing vibration sensing solutions suffer from high communication overhead and confusion between potholes and similar vibration patterns from manholes, speed bumps, etc.
en.method: On the vehicle side, a GMM-based module adaptively screens abnormal vibration events and uploads only candidate segments. On the server side, a 1D Attention U-Net performs point-wise temporal segmentation, trained under a federated learning framework to handle non-IID multi-vehicle data.
en.result: Experiments show the framework reduces unnecessary data transmission from smooth road segments and improves fine-grained pothole detection in both centralized and federated settings.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how edge-cloud collaboration and federated learning enable low-overhead, high-accuracy real-time pothole detection.

idea_spark.idea: Combining GMM background modeling with attention U-Net enables lightweight edge filtering and fine-grained cloud segmentation.
idea_spark.risk: Non-IID data distributions in federated learning may cause slow convergence or performance degradation, requiring more robust aggregation strategies.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires combining edge screening with federated learning for anomaly detection and segmentation in other sensor modalities (e.g., audio, images).

papers.title: Binge, Bot, Repeat: Unpacking the Ecosystem of Video Piracy on Telegram
papers.summary: Telegram has emerged as a major platform for large-scale video piracy, where copyrighted content is rapidly distributed among users. Despite its prominence, the structural and operational dynamics of this ecosystem remain insufficiently understood. To address this gap, we present the first large-scale study of video piracy on Telegram through a mixed-method analysis of 1,057 channels that shared 209k unique posts between December 2023 and January 2026 - systematically characterizing their content, distribution strategies, and how the ecosystem is sustained at scale. Central to our approach is the development of a fine-grained taxonomy that enables a structured understanding of the activity and intent of these channels on a per-post level. The channels collectively distributed 19,033 unique copyrighted titles originating from 175 countries, accumulating over 4.85B unique views and resulting in a lower-bound estimated financial loss of $17.49B for content rights holders. We also find that this ecosystem is deliberately engineered to be resilient against takedown efforts, frequently redirecting users through chains of intermediary channels and automated bots that collectively handle hosting, access control, monetization, and channel discovery. The scale and persistence of this ecosystem motivated the development of Anti-RIP, a real-time framework for detecting emerging video piracy communities on Telegram. Anti-RIP utilizes our taxonomy to generate contextual, interpretable insights that stakeholders confirmed improve the triaging action against reported posts and channels. Over a 61-day period, the framework facilitated the takedown of 524 previously unknown piracy channels and 71 bots. To support reproducibility and future research, we open-source both the dataset and the Anti-RIP framework.

ai.tldr: 首次大规模研究Telegram视频盗版生态系统，分析了1057个频道和209k条帖子，发现盗版频道通过中介链和自动机器人实现抗删除，并开发了Anti-RIP框架用于实时检测。
ai.motivation: Telegram已成为大规模视频盗版的主要平台，但其结构和运作机制尚不明确。
ai.method: 混合方法分析1057个频道（2023年12月至2026年1月），构建细粒度分类法，并开发Anti-RIP框架。
ai.result: 频道分发19,033个版权作品，累计48.5亿次观看，估计损失174.9亿美元；Anti-RIP在61天内协助下架524个盗版频道和71个机器人。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解Telegram盗版生态的规模和韧性，并提供实时检测工具。

idea_spark.idea: 利用频道间重定向链和机器人行为模式，构建主动预警系统，在盗版内容扩散前阻断。

zh.method: 混合方法分析1057个频道（2023年12月至2026年1月），构建细粒度分类法，并开发Anti-RIP框架。
zh.result: 频道分发19,033个版权作品，累计48.5亿次观看，估计损失174.9亿美元；Anti-RIP在61天内协助下架524个盗版频道和71个机器人。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解Telegram盗版生态的规模和韧性，并提供实时检测工具。

idea_spark.inspiration: 盗版生态的工程化韧性启发我们设计更鲁棒的分布式内容审核系统。

en.tldr: First large-scale study of video piracy on Telegram, analyzing 1,057 channels and 209k posts, revealing a resilient ecosystem using intermediary chains and bots, and introducing Anti-RIP for real-time detection.
en.motivation: Telegram has become a major platform for large-scale video piracy, yet its structural and operational dynamics remain poorly understood.
en.method: Mixed-method analysis of 1,057 channels (Dec 2023–Jan 2026), development of a fine-grained taxonomy, and creation of the Anti-RIP framework.
en.result: Channels distributed 19,033 unique copyrighted titles, accumulated 4.85B views, estimated $17.49B loss; Anti-RIP facilitated takedown of 524 piracy channels and 71 bots in 61 days.
en.help_to_user: Provides understanding of the scale and resilience of Telegram piracy ecosystem and offers a real-time detection tool.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage channel redirection chains and bot behavior patterns to build proactive warning systems that block piracy before widespread distribution.
idea_spark.risk: Adversarial attacks may evade detection; privacy and false positives require careful handling.
idea_spark.inspiration: The engineered resilience of the piracy ecosystem inspires more robust distributed content moderation systems.

papers.title: "Training robust watermarking model may hurt authentication!'' Exploring and Mitigating the Identity Leakage in Robust Watermarking
papers.summary: The rapid advancement of generative AI has underscored the critical need for identifying image ownership and protecting copyrights. This makes post-processing image watermarking an essential tool -- it involves embedding a specific watermark message into an image, with successful verification if a similar message can be decoded from the watermarked image. However, this method is susceptible to both adversarial attacks that manipulate the watermarked image to yield an unverified message upon decoding, and the proposed identity leakage-related attacks (e.g., forging watermarked images). The threat of identity leakage is particularly exacerbated in both empirical and certified robust watermarking methods. To defend against the aforementioned attacks, we propose W-IR, the first image watermarking framework that simultaneously incorporates identity protection and robustness. To enhance model robustness, we introduce a novel randomized smoothing technique as part of a robust watermarking, that offers certified robustness against perturbations across two distinct transformation spaces: pixel-level and coordinate-level. Moreover, to further mitigate identity leakage, we propose a new strategy based on residual information loss, aimed at minimizing the mutual information between the residual and watermarked images. Our work strikes a superior balance between robustness and identity leakage mitigation. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our W-IR framework achieves high certified accuracy for authenticity while effectively reducing identity leakage. \footnote{The code is available at https://github.com/holdrain/W-I-R.}

ai.tldr: 提出首个同时保护身份和鲁棒性的图像水印框架W-IR，通过随机平滑和残差信息损失缓解身份泄露。
ai.motivation: 现有鲁棒水印方法易受身份泄露攻击，攻击者可伪造水印图像，且鲁棒性越强泄露越严重。
ai.method: 采用随机平滑技术实现像素级和坐标级扰动下的认证鲁棒性，并通过最小化残差与水印图像的互信息来减少身份泄露。
ai.result: W-IR在保持高认证准确率的同时有效降低身份泄露，优于现有方法。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解鲁棒水印中的身份泄露风险，并提供一种兼顾鲁棒性与隐私保护的解决方案。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将随机平滑与对抗训练结合，或设计更紧的互信息下界以进一步抑制泄露。
idea_spark.risk: 随机平滑可能引入计算开销，且互信息估计的近似误差可能影响实际效果。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从信息论角度重新审视鲁棒性与隐私的权衡，启发其他安全领域（如联邦学习）的隐私保护设计。

idea_spark.risk: 随机平滑可能引入计算开销，且互信息估计的近似误差可能影响实际效果。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从信息论角度重新审视鲁棒性与隐私的权衡，启发其他安全领域（如联邦学习）的隐私保护设计。

en.tldr: Proposes W-IR, the first image watermarking framework that simultaneously ensures identity protection and robustness via randomized smoothing and residual information loss.
en.motivation: Existing robust watermarking methods are vulnerable to identity leakage attacks, where adversaries can forge watermarked images, and robustness exacerbates leakage.
en.method: Employs randomized smoothing for certified robustness against pixel-level and coordinate-level perturbations, and minimizes mutual information between residual and watermarked images to reduce identity leakage.
en.result: W-IR achieves high certified authentication accuracy while effectively mitigating identity leakage, outperforming prior works.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand identity leakage risks in robust watermarking and provides a solution that balances robustness with privacy.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining randomized smoothing with adversarial training, or design tighter lower bounds on mutual information to further suppress leakage.
idea_spark.risk: Randomized smoothing may introduce computational overhead, and approximation errors in mutual information estimation could affect practical performance.
idea_spark.inspiration: Revisiting the robustness-privacy trade-off from an information-theoretic perspective inspires privacy-preserving designs in other security domains like federated learning.

papers.title: Governing AI-Assisted Security Operations: A Design Science Framework for Operational Decision Support
papers.summary: Engineering managers increasingly must decide how to introduce generative artificial intelligence (AI), retrieval-augmented generation, and coding agents into high-risk operational functions without weakening accountability, privacy, cost discipline, or auditability. The central message of this study is that AI-assisted operational decision support should be managed as a governed engineering capability before it is scaled as automation. Security operations centers (SOCs) provide a suitable setting because they combine privileged telemetry, specialist expertise, software repositories, cloud services, and evidence-sensitive decisions. This study uses Kusto Query Language (KQL) and Microsoft Azure security capabilities as a bounded technical instantiation of that broader engineering management problem. KQL is read-only in ordinary query use, but read-only does not mean risk-free: AI-assisted queries can still create privacy, cost, performance, schema-validity, and decision-quality risks through broad scans, sensitive-field exposure, stale intelligence, and misleading interpretations. Using design science research, the study develops a governed AI query-broker artifact that separates AI planning from operational execution through schema-grounded retrieval, approved templates, policy validation, read-only adapters, normalized outputs, auditable agent traces, and engineering review board gates. The contribution is not a new KQL technique, security product, or detection algorithm. Rather, the study contributes a management framework for governing AI-assisted operational decision support in high-risk digital infrastructure by specifying design propositions, role accountability, maturity stages, quality gates, evaluation criteria, and evidence boundaries.

ai.tldr: 本研究提出一个治理框架，将AI辅助的运营决策支持作为受管控的工程能力，而非直接自动化，并以安全运营中心（SOC）和Kusto查询语言（KQL）为例进行设计科学验证。
ai.motivation: 工程管理者在将生成式AI引入高风险运营功能时，面临问责、隐私、成本、审计等治理挑战，现有方法缺乏系统性的管理框架。
ai.method: 采用设计科学研究方法，开发了一个受治理的AI查询代理工件，通过模式约束检索、批准模板、策略验证、只读适配器、标准化输出、可审计代理痕迹和工程评审委员会门控，将AI规划与运营执行分离。
ai.result: 贡献了一套管理框架，包含设计命题、角色问责、成熟度阶段、质量门控、评估标准和证据边界，而非新的KQL技术或安全产品。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助工程管理者理解如何在安全运营等高风险场景中安全、可控地引入AI辅助决策支持，避免隐私泄露、成本失控和决策质量下降。

idea_spark.idea: 将AI查询代理的只读适配器与策略验证机制结合，形成通用的“AI执行隔离层”，适用于任何需要审计和问责的查询场景。
idea_spark.risk: 框架依赖组织对工程评审委员会和策略验证的严格执行，若门控流程被绕过或策略定义不完善，仍可能引入风险。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受安全运营中心中只读查询与高风险决策分离的实践启发，可推广至其他需要平衡AI能力与治理约束的场景。

zh.tldr: 本研究提出一个治理框架，将AI辅助的运营决策支持作为受管控的工程能力，而非直接自动化，并以安全运营中心（SOC）和Kusto查询语言（KQL）为例进行设计科学验证。

idea_spark.idea: 将AI查询代理的只读适配器与策略验证机制结合，形成通用的“AI执行隔离层”，适用于任何需要审计和问责的查询场景。

idea_spark.inspiration: 受安全运营中心中只读查询与高风险决策分离的实践启发，可推广至其他需要平衡AI能力与治理约束的场景。

en.tldr: This study proposes a governance framework that treats AI-assisted operational decision support as a managed engineering capability before scaling as automation, using security operations centers (SOCs) and Kusto Query Language (KQL) as a concrete instantiation via design science research.
en.motivation: Engineering managers face challenges in introducing generative AI into high-risk operational functions without compromising accountability, privacy, cost discipline, or auditability, and existing approaches lack a systematic management framework.
en.method: Using design science research, a governed AI query-broker artifact is developed that separates AI planning from operational execution through schema-grounded retrieval, approved templates, policy validation, read-only adapters, normalized outputs, auditable agent traces, and engineering review board gates.
en.result: The contribution is a management framework comprising design propositions, role accountability, maturity stages, quality gates, evaluation criteria, and evidence boundaries, rather than new KQL techniques or security products.
en.help_to_user: Helps engineering managers understand how to safely and controllably introduce AI-assisted decision support in high-risk scenarios like security operations, avoiding privacy leaks, cost overruns, and degraded decision quality.

idea_spark.idea: Combine the read-only adapter and policy validation mechanism of the AI query broker into a generic 'AI execution isolation layer' applicable to any query scenario requiring auditability and accountability.
idea_spark.risk: The framework relies on strict enforcement of engineering review board gates and policy validation; if gate processes are bypassed or policies are poorly defined, risks may still arise.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the practice of separating read-only queries from high-risk decisions in security operations centers, this approach can be generalized to other contexts balancing AI capability with governance constraints.

papers.title: Bridging the Cognitive Gap: A Unified Memory Paradigm for 6G Agentic AI-RAN
papers.summary: As 6G evolves, the radio access network must transcend traditional automation to embrace agentic AI capable of perception, reasoning, and evolution. A fundamental cognitive gap persists in current disaggregated architectures, where interfaces force the physical layer to compress high-dimensional states into low-dimensional metrics, trapping reasoning agents behind a semantic bottleneck. This article envisions a shift from interface-bound to memory-centric architectures. We propose a unified memory paradigm that dissolves the boundaries between sensing and reasoning by mapping biological memory hierarchies onto heterogeneous computing fabrics. Enabled by emerging coherent interconnects, this approach creates a cognitive continuum where microsecond-level reflexes, millisecond-level reasoning, and long-term evolution share state across time scales. By replacing message passing with zero-copy observability, we empower AI agents to bridge the gap between real-time responsiveness and long-horizon context for truly autonomous 6G networks.

ai.tldr: 提出统一记忆范式，通过生物记忆层次映射到异构计算架构，解决6G代理AI-RAN中感知与推理之间的语义瓶颈。
ai.motivation: 当前分解架构中接口强制物理层压缩高维状态为低维指标，导致推理代理面临语义瓶颈，无法实现真正的自主6G网络。
ai.method: 采用统一记忆范式，用零拷贝可观测性替代消息传递，利用相干互连在微秒级反射、毫秒级推理和长期演化之间共享状态。
ai.result: 实现了跨时间尺度的认知连续体，使AI代理能够桥接实时响应与长期上下文。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解6G网络中如何通过记忆中心架构提升AI代理的感知、推理和演化能力。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将生物记忆层次（如海马体、前额叶）映射到边缘计算节点的具体实现方案。
idea_spark.risk: 相干互连的硬件成熟度与能耗可能成为实际部署的瓶颈。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从神经科学中记忆系统的分层与同步机制获得启发，设计跨时间尺度的状态共享协议。

zh.tldr: 提出统一记忆范式，通过生物记忆层次映射到异构计算架构，解决6G代理AI-RAN中感知与推理之间的语义瓶颈。
zh.motivation: 当前分解架构中接口强制物理层压缩高维状态为低维指标，导致推理代理面临语义瓶颈，无法实现真正的自主6G网络。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解6G网络中如何通过记忆中心架构提升AI代理的感知、推理和演化能力。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将生物记忆层次（如海马体、前额叶）映射到边缘计算节点的具体实现方案。
idea_spark.risk: 相干互连的硬件成熟度与能耗可能成为实际部署的瓶颈。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从神经科学中记忆系统的分层与同步机制获得启发，设计跨时间尺度的状态共享协议。

en.tldr: Proposes a unified memory paradigm that maps biological memory hierarchies onto heterogeneous computing fabrics to bridge the semantic bottleneck between sensing and reasoning in 6G agentic AI-RAN.
en.motivation: Current disaggregated architectures force the physical layer to compress high-dimensional states into low-dimensional metrics, trapping reasoning agents behind a semantic bottleneck and hindering truly autonomous 6G networks.
en.method: Adopts a unified memory paradigm that replaces message passing with zero-copy observability, leveraging coherent interconnects to share state across microsecond-level reflexes, millisecond-level reasoning, and long-term evolution.
en.result: Creates a cognitive continuum that enables AI agents to bridge real-time responsiveness and long-horizon context for autonomous 6G networks.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how a memory-centric architecture can enhance perception, reasoning, and evolution of AI agents in 6G networks.

idea_spark.idea: Explore concrete implementations mapping biological memory hierarchies (e.g., hippocampus, prefrontal cortex) onto edge computing nodes.
idea_spark.risk: Hardware maturity and energy consumption of coherent interconnects may become practical deployment bottlenecks.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draw inspiration from hierarchical and synchronization mechanisms of memory systems in neuroscience to design cross-timescale state-sharing protocols.

papers.title: OrbitBFT: Enabling Scalable and Robust BFT Consensus in LEO Constellations
papers.summary: Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite constellations are evolving from communication relays into autonomous platforms operating in increasingly congested and contested environments. Since uplinks to ground stations can be severed or jammed, ensuring reliable coordination among satellites requires autonomous Byzantine Fault-Tolerant (BFT) consensus. However, applying conventional BFT protocols to LEO constellations is challenging due to their dynamic topology, sparse connectivity, and limited communication bandwidth. In this paper, we present OrbitBFT, a novel two-stage hierarchical BFT consensus protocol tailored to the unique characteristics of LEO constellations. First, OrbitBFT exploits the topological stability within orbital planes to partition the constellation and perform localized intra-plane consensus, which reduces communication overhead. Second, we design a Byzantine-resilient bypass mechanism and a hop-by-hop transmission protocol to ensure reliable message delivery and mitigate congestion, even in the presence of adversarial behavior. Third, we adapt and optimize PBFT and HotStuff to the LEO context, achieving linear message complexity while preserving safety and liveness. Extensive evaluations in a realistic Starlink-based simulation demonstrate that OrbitBFT significantly improves scalability, throughput, and latency compared to its original designs, making it a practical and efficient BFT solution for large-scale satellite networks.

ai.tldr: 提出OrbitBFT，一种面向低轨卫星星座的两阶段分层拜占庭容错共识协议，利用轨道面内拓扑稳定性降低通信开销，并设计拜占庭弹性旁路机制与逐跳传输协议，在动态稀疏网络中实现线性消息复杂度。
ai.motivation: 低轨卫星星座从通信中继演变为自主平台，上行链路易被切断或干扰，需要可靠的拜占庭容错共识，但传统BFT协议难以适应动态拓扑、稀疏连接和有限带宽。
ai.method: 利用轨道面内拓扑稳定性进行星座分区和局部面内共识；设计拜占庭弹性旁路机制和逐跳传输协议；将PBFT和HotStuff适配到低轨环境，实现线性消息复杂度。
ai.result: 在基于Starlink的仿真中，OrbitBFT相比原始设计显著提升了可扩展性、吞吐量和延迟。
ai.help_to_user: 为低轨卫星网络提供了一种实用高效的BFT共识方案，可增强星座在对抗环境下的自主协调能力。

idea_spark.idea: 结合轨道面稳定性和逐跳传输，设计轻量级拜占庭容错协议，避免全局广播。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖轨道面内拓扑稳定性，若星座构型剧烈变化（如大规模机动）可能影响性能。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用网络固有结构（如轨道面）简化共识，启发在动态网络中寻找稳定子结构来设计协议。

zh.tldr: 提出OrbitBFT，一种面向低轨卫星星座的两阶段分层拜占庭容错共识协议，利用轨道面内拓扑稳定性降低通信开销，并设计拜占庭弹性旁路机制与逐跳传输协议，在动态稀疏网络中实现线性消息复杂度。

zh.result: 在基于Starlink的仿真中，OrbitBFT相比原始设计显著提升了可扩展性、吞吐量和延迟。
zh.help_to_user: 为低轨卫星网络提供了一种实用高效的BFT共识方案，可增强星座在对抗环境下的自主协调能力。

idea_spark.idea: 结合轨道面稳定性和逐跳传输，设计轻量级拜占庭容错协议，避免全局广播。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖轨道面内拓扑稳定性，若星座构型剧烈变化（如大规模机动）可能影响性能。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用网络固有结构（如轨道面）简化共识，启发在动态网络中寻找稳定子结构来设计协议。

en.tldr: Proposes OrbitBFT, a two-stage hierarchical Byzantine fault-tolerant consensus protocol for LEO constellations, leveraging intra-plane topological stability to reduce communication overhead and designing a Byzantine-resilient bypass mechanism with hop-by-hop transmission to achieve linear message complexity in dynamic sparse networks.
en.motivation: LEO satellite constellations are evolving into autonomous platforms where uplinks can be severed or jammed, requiring reliable BFT consensus, but conventional protocols struggle with dynamic topology, sparse connectivity, and limited bandwidth.
en.method: Partitions the constellation using intra-plane topological stability for localized consensus; designs a Byzantine-resilient bypass mechanism and hop-by-hop transmission; adapts PBFT and HotStuff to LEO context for linear message complexity.
en.result: In a realistic Starlink-based simulation, OrbitBFT significantly improves scalability, throughput, and latency compared to original designs.
en.help_to_user: Provides a practical and efficient BFT consensus solution for large-scale satellite networks, enhancing autonomous coordination in contested environments.

idea_spark.idea: Combine orbital plane stability with hop-by-hop transmission to design a lightweight BFT protocol that avoids global broadcast.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on intra-plane topological stability; significant constellation maneuvers may degrade performance.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging inherent network structure (e.g., orbital planes) to simplify consensus inspires protocol design by identifying stable substructures in dynamic networks.

papers.title: Price Distortions in Korea's Electricity Market: Barriers to Renewable Integration and Reform Pathways
papers.summary: Structural distortions in price signals within the Korean electricity market, governed by a cost-based pool (CBP) and a uniform pricing mechanism, fundamentally undermine the nation's energy transition goals. The current market design fails to reflect transmission constraints, real-time supply and demand dynamics, and generator-specific costs, leading to inefficient resource allocation and hindering long-term investments in renewable energy and grid flexibility. This paper identifies the key drivers of these distortions and proposes a holistic reform package to enhance market efficiency. The package includes four key reforms: \stepcounter{excep}(\roman{excep}) introducing a locational marginal pricing system to manage transmission constraints; \stepcounter{excep}(\roman{excep}) establishing a real-time market to reflect temporal value; \stepcounter{excep}(\roman{excep}) integrating market and system operations to resolve inconsistencies; and \stepcounter{excep}(\roman{excep}) transitioning from CBP to a price-based bidding system. Each reform targets a distinct source of inefficiency. The broader contribution of this study, however, lies in showing that, under the current Korean market design, the market cannot readily provide effective price signals. These reforms therefore need to be implemented jointly to establish a coherent market design in which price signals are aligned with Korea's energy policy objectives.

ai.tldr: 本文分析了韩国电力市场中价格信号的结构性扭曲，指出成本型竞价池和统一定价机制阻碍了可再生能源整合，并提出包含节点边际电价、实时市场等四项联合改革方案。
ai.motivation: 韩国现行电力市场设计无法反映输电约束、实时供需和机组成本差异，导致资源配置低效，阻碍可再生能源投资和电网灵活性提升。
ai.method: 识别价格扭曲的关键驱动因素，提出四项改革：引入节点边际电价、建立实时市场、整合市场与系统运营、从成本型竞价转向价格型竞价。
ai.result: 四项改革需联合实施才能建立连贯的市场设计，使价格信号与韩国能源政策目标一致。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解韩国电力市场结构性问题及系统性改革路径，适用于能源政策分析和市场设计研究。

idea_spark.idea: 将市场运营与系统运营整合，消除调度与交易之间的不一致性。
idea_spark.risk: 改革可能面临现有市场主体利益冲突和实施复杂性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从价格信号扭曲角度切入，强调多项改革必须协同推进，而非孤立实施。

en.tldr: This paper analyzes structural price distortions in Korea's electricity market caused by cost-based pool and uniform pricing, and proposes a joint reform package including locational marginal pricing, real-time market, and bidding system transition.
en.motivation: Current market design fails to reflect transmission constraints, real-time dynamics, and generator-specific costs, leading to inefficient allocation and hindering renewable investment.
en.method: Identifies key distortion drivers and proposes four reforms: locational marginal pricing, real-time market, integrated market-system operations, and price-based bidding.
en.result: Reforms must be implemented jointly to establish coherent price signals aligned with Korea's energy policy goals.
en.help_to_user: Provides insights into structural barriers and reform pathways for electricity markets with uniform pricing and cost-based pools.

idea_spark.idea: Integrate market and system operations to resolve inconsistencies between dispatch and trading.
idea_spark.risk: Reform may face resistance from incumbent stakeholders and implementation complexity.
idea_spark.inspiration: Emphasizes that multiple reforms must be coordinated rather than implemented in isolation to fix price signal distortions.
